boardgame: Kommissar Rex: Das Kriminalspiel für Spürnasen
description:
Rex is running through Vienna and looking for the tracks of bad guys, which he must follow. In these places you will always find instructions that you must collect for further game.
Roll the dice and move.
The winner of the game is player who resolves properly the case.

An official Pardoes game

De villagers of Symbolica are in an uproar. All fairy tales are stolen from Pardoes' Star chamber, so now Pardoes has to travel across all six fairy tale lands to collect his fairy tales again. Of course the dark force who stole the fairy tales is after Pardoes as well. But Pardoes is a smart kid and confuses the dark force by duplicating himself while searching for the fairy tales. But only the real Pardoes can bring the fairy tales back to the Star chamber. Are you the real Pardoes?

The game is designed and illustrated by Henny Knoet

The game consists of one main board with the Star chamber and six small boards; the fairy tale lands. On each board you find four different kind of spaces:
- neutral spaces (nothing happens)
- question marks: if you answer the question right, you may roll the dice again
- Pardoes spaces: Solve a Pardoes task for example, move your pawn two spaces
- Fairy tale points: If you answer the question right, you'll get a fairy tale card

Goal of the game is to collect three different fairy tale cards from three different fairy tale lands and deliver them to the Star chamber

An official Pardoes game

The game consists of two games in one. On the back you can play memory, on the front you'll find the Pardoes game, a trivia game about Pardoes and themepark De Efteling.

Goal of the trivia game (also known as the horse jump game) is to finish your puzzle and collect three cards by crossing the gridded board in a chess knight like way and correctly answering questions. How far you can move in the chess knight like way is determined by two dice.

You can download more questions for the game from De Efteling website.

For the memory game you have to collect pairs of the same picture. The one with the most pairs wins

Contents: 
- 1 double-sided game board 
- 2 dice 
- 8 puzzles 
- 48 cards (24 pairs) 
- Rules
- The original 8 cardboard game pieces could be replaced by 8 miniatures you could collect from the local supermarket in 2001.

Witch's Coming! is a game about a Halloween night in which one player plays the Witch team and the other the villager team. The witches mingle with the children in costumes, enter the villager's houses, and cast curses. If all three witches manage to get into the villagers' houses before sunrise, the witch wins; if the villagers manage to stop that, the villagers win. To do this, the villager player needs to get all three children into the villagers' houses or chase away the witches until the sun rises.

The witch player and the villager player take alternating turns, starting with the witch. Once they've each taken a turn, they check whether someone has won, and if not, the game continues with the witch taking her turn first.

boardgame: Wrong Chemistry: Bonus Nobel Prize Scientists
description:
Wrong Chemistry comes in full circle with a special last deck: Bonus Nobel Prize Scientists. This deck includes 52 scientists. Each card has its own illustration but some of the scientists have the same abilities in order to keep the balance in the game. These scientists add one more variant to Wrong Chemistry.

As it's known from the "Expand Your Lab", now you can use the scientists during all the game up to 3 times. Their different abilities add some strategy in the original game. Using this game we have a new way to use the scientists. You shuffle the deck and if you want you can add the scientists from the expansion and then you deal 5 cards on the table faced up. When a player decides to use one of these scientists, then you draw another card form the deck. This way there are on the table always 5 scientists and their abilities change during the game.

boardgame: Dudley Dodo's Environment Game Pocket Edition
description:
Quiz game about environmental issues. Each player gets a Score Card (10 are provided) and a pencil. The questiuons are in the categories Water, Plants, Universe and Animals. You choose one of the subjects and a question is read out to you. If you get it right you mark of that icon in the left hand column of your score card. Next turn you can then ask for a question type that features an icon in a square of your score card that is adjacent to the last one you crossed off. This way you can make a (torturous) route across your score card to get to the right hand column and if you get there first, you win.

Game comes with 90 question cards, four questions on each card, plus 10 score cards, 2 pencils and a rubber (so that the score card can be used again.

This promo item features 2 new Predator tiles and a Pterodactyl for your first player marker!

The tiles function by replacing the two predator tiles that are already in the game, and make these new predators that much more destructive and vicious.

6 characters has a 5 cards each - head, shoulders, stomach, legs and feet. Collect all 5 cards of any character first by exchanging cards by other players to win the game.

Multiple other game variants possible including Happy families.

Firewall is a hacking themed game designed for 2-4 players. Each player is trying to hack into the same node by controlling the most ports. Only one hacker comes out on top.

Hackers take control of ports by placing the most packets on them. They can also play a firewall on a port to block all other players from playing on that port. If a player is restricted by a opponent's firewall they may place a backdoor next to it to also be able to play on that port. Crafty hackers can also use spikes to their advantage by removing a die from the game. If a player manages to play a second firewall of their color then that port is locked down and no player may play anything on it (including spikes).

Each player starts with ten dice. At the beginning of the game the first player rolls to see how many ports there will be (from 2-4). Next the first player rolls 2 dice and places one on an open port. The next player rolls 3 dice and places one on the board. Play continues with each player rolling 3 dice and placing one until all players are out of dice or all ports are locked down.

The player with the most ports controlled wins. If there is a tie for ports controlled then the tied players count their total number of packets on their controlled ports. Whoever has the most packets wins.

boardgame: Krosmaster: Arena – Argon Gass Exclusive Figure
description:
Argon Gass is an promotional figure for the game Krosmaster: Arena.

Foggernauts were originally humans, ones who could wield technomagic. They lived underwater for many centuries in their sunken homelands. Over time, the technomagic they used slowly started to convert them into living machines. The only human thing about them now is their spirit. And after many centuries, the Foggernaunts have risen from their watery prisons to claim the stasis crystals on the land, the source of their power and craving. And Argon Gass is one of them.

Argon Gass plays as a close range fighter, where his short range Sonartillery makes short work of his foe's armor. To assist him with his close range style, he has heavy armor and self repairing capabilities to shrug off attacks, letting him keep going where flesh and bones can't.

boardgame: Krosmaster: Arena – Count Frigost exclusive figure
description: An exclusive figure from the game Krosmaster: Arena

Roll & move game. 
Objective: be the first reaching space 65
 - moving by turns - 
You travel from number 1 to number 65 at the gameboard; ending on a pictured space follow instructions printed on lid inside.  
Meeples for 4 players are added, but more persons can join.
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Freakball Team Manager is a express game, based on Blood Bowl Team Manager by Jay Little.  The game was created for the 2013 Express/In a Tin Contest.

2-4 players roll dice to determine their roster of "freaks," and resolve events in a futuristic, American-Football game.  After each of 5 weeks in the game, the players can spend points to upgrade their team with better players, skills, or buy more fans of their team.  The player with the most fans at the end of the game wins.

War Command is a physical edition of a computer game.

It is a card game which borrows elements from tabletop RPGs and tactics games to create a new strategic experience.

Company information states; "It combines the deck building elements of a Trading Card Game with the strategic positioning of a battle field environment. Players move Character Cards around on a board, playing Support Cards from their hand in concert with Character movements and attacks in a mad race to destroy the other player's Commander Card first.

"The game takes place in the far future on the planet Haven, which, due to a cataclysmic wave of radiation, will soon be the last livable planet in the galaxy. Upon learning of the impending disaster, Humanity quickly scrapped what civilization remained on earth and took to the stars; arriving at the shielded planet just in time to survive the interstellar catastrophe. Unfortunately for them, they hadn't planned on its pulsing gravity field or violent magnetic storms. Doubly unfortunate for them, they weren't the only ones."

Pong Tactics is a strategy card game that adds new gameplay mechanics to the classic drinking game beer pong. 10 cups are set up on either side of an 8 foot table. One Formation card is selected to determine the formation of the 10 cups on both sides of the table and then the cards are set aside. Cups may be filled with water or beer (if players are of legal drinking age). The remainder of the game is Action Cards. These cards impact the normal beer pong rules in various ways, such as changing the shot, number of shots, cards in hand, blocking shots, determining the end of the game and much more. The game is played by teams of two players on each side of the table. Each player on a team takes one shot at the cups on the other side of the table; if they make the ball into a cup, it is removed and the player takes a drink either from their own drink or the cup. The goal is to remove all of your opponent's cups by making all the shots. The Action Cards can aid your team or hinder your opponent in achieving the end game goal.

Salt & Burn is a tabletop competitive action/adventure board game about hunting supernatural creatures. With 2-6 players, each person draws a randomly selected character card with health and damage stats, and a random vehicle card. There are 12 unique Angel, Human and Demon Hunter character cards, each with different stats for health and damage and different benefits based on species

Throughout the game you can collect item cards that modify your health or damage, and collect trunk cards which are like spells that you can save and cast during your turn to help you or hinder other hunters. If you die during the course of a fight, you will have to go to Purgatory until you roll a 3 and return to the start of the game without any of your worldly possessions.

Players travel the game board to hunt down monsters. Monsters are drawn at random along with other Event cards for an element of surprise. The more monsters you kill, the more lives you save. If you kill a particularly dangerous monster, you'll save more lives in the process, and win the game faster. The first hunter to save 10 lives wins the game.

description:
Smooth & Rifled is a Tactical level miniatures wargame system designed to recreate skirmish games with 28, 20 or 15mm miniatures. This very first edition covers the period from approximately 1700 to approximately 1900. The French and Indian War, the American Revolutionary War, the Napoleonic Wars, the American Civil War and the wars of the Colonial era are just some of the periods you can play with Smooth & Rifled.

The Smooth & Rifled rulebook contains the "nuts & bolts" of play. Each conflict covered by the game is available from the publisher as separate free pdf's.

description:
The game is about attacking the settlements of other vikings, sink their viking ships and plunder their gold. To win, one must collect 2 golden treasures on one's own settlement or sink all competing viking ships. [translation of box cover blurb]

Each turn, a player rolls two dice and moves either one of initially 7 ships a number of squares equal to the sum of the dice or two ships each according to one of the dice. While moving, ships must avoid squares with obtacles or islands and other ships, except on the final square where it can sink another player's ship or enter an island. When sinking a ship with a gold treasure or entering an island with one, a ship captures the treasure.

Game contents:

1 board
7 plastic viking ships in each of the 4 colours
4 golden plastic disks to represent treasures
2 normal 6-sided dice

Japanese cardgame. The title read as Tsukaima! that's mean "Familiar!" (but the Latin alphabet descibed as French:"Familier!")

The players role as familiars hunter in the fantasy world. Compete to get the most valuable familiars to be the winner.

The game is simple about drawing and collecting the set of familiars cards. If the familiars set is trended, it is worth more points at the end of the game.

From the game box:

"THIS IS THE HOME EDITION OF THE EXCITING T.V. GAME-BATTA HAI. BATTA HAI WAS DEVELOPED BY LYON S. WEIR OF HOLLYWOOD FOR THE ENTERTAINMENT INDUSTRY, BUT THIS PARTICULAR EDITION HAS BEEN DESIGNED BY HIM WITH HOME ENTERTAINMENT IN MIND. YOU, YOUR FAMILY, AND YOUR FRIENDS WILL ENJOY PLAYING IT OVER AND OVER, FOR EACH GAME WILL BE DIFFERENT FROM THE PREVIOUS ONE."

Batta Hai is a Bingo-like game with endless variations: Twelve different boards, forty nine different calls. Each board can be played in different ways, finding the way is the key to victory.
Once a piece has been called it may be arranged on the player's board, or set aside. Only the first piece called must remain in position for the duration of the game.

The player who correctly covers all the numbered and arrow squares and calls out "Batta Hai" wins the game because the same play piece, when called, may complete more than one board.

Puzzle game for the youngest children.
There are 8 puzzles with 4 puzzle pieces and in 4 colors.
In turn player rolls the soft ball with 4 colors and take the puzzle-tile from the pile with the color ball is showing.
He must place the tile to correct puzzle.

Trivia block with 120 questions and answers about our Earth where quickest correct answers score You a points.

Player with the most points wins.

The kingdom is at peace, protected by the Forces of Good, but they have grown lax and weak. The Forces of Evil gather to overthrow them. But who shall rule in Good’s place?  As a Force of Evil yourself, it is up to you to emerge from the conflict supreme.

Forces of Evil is a dungeon-building strategy game for 2-6 players.  You and your friends are working to defeat The Forces of Good.  You need to build a dungeon, hoard treasure, and recruit minions (as well as keep them happy).  However, The Forces of Good will rally against you and attack!  You must successfully defeat these ambitious adventurers or they will ruin your plans! At the same time, you must find ways to undermine the other Forces of Evil to insure your own victory.  When the last hero falls, the most powerful Force of Evil will rule the land!

Players use actions from cards placed on the gameboard to persue their strategy for winning. Players draw new cards to advance their position or hinder others'. Tokens are used to represent units in players' dungeons as well as heroes fighting against them. The combat element of the game is resolved using dice.

The game ends when either the last Castle Garrison falls or the last Card of Light is drawn, triggering Armageddon. Players are awarded bonus influence points for achieving certain strategic goals. Then each player tallies his power, wealth, and prestige as measured in influence, treasure, and defeated heroes. Whoever has the highest total claims rulership over the kingdom and wins the game!

boardgame: Death Before Dishonor: The Battle of Santiago Harbor
description:
“We saw what probably has not been witnessed since the days of the Armada, ships coming out for deadly battle, but dressed as for a regal parade or a festival day. From their shining black hulls, with huge golden figureheads bearing the crest and coat-of-arms of Spain, to the tops of their masts where fluttered proudly the immense silken flags…to the brightly colored awnings over their decks, they bespoke luxury and chivalry, and a proud defiance.
--George Edward Graham of the Associated Press as he witnessed the sortie of the Spanish Fleet from the Santiago Channel, July 3rd, 1898.

Death Before Dishonor is an introductory level wargame simulation on the naval actions that occurred near Santiago harbor during the Spanish-American War. The game design is meant to be simple and highly playable, yet one that portrays the historical challenges facing the combatants. Historically the US failed to bottle up the Spanish fleet, and was forced to undergo a lengthy blockade while the US Army fought the Spanish garrison for control of the city and port. The capture of key fortifications and the heights at Kettle and San Juan Hills compelled Admiral Pascual Cervera to sortie on July 3rd to prevent his ships from being sunk at their moorings by US artillery, or capture when the town surrendered. The US navy would sink all of the fleeing Spanish ships with little loss, scoring a lop-sided win.

Each copy of Death Before Dishonor is composed of the following:

•    One sheet of 61 single-sided counters.
•    One 8.5 by 11 map.
•    This set of rules.

Players will also need a one ten-sided die (D10).

boardgame: A Monstrous Fuss: The Battle of Wilson's Creek, August 10, 1861

description:
From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Be a hero valiantly coming to the aid of a stronghold as a vast army bears down on it! Use the resources of the stronghold and your own unique abilities to keep it from overwhelmed.

Build a deck of cards to play with as you go and do your best to defeat the foes as they appear.

Choose one of 3 heroes - Arpad of Lhun, Zsuzsa Slatewalker and Sandor the Wise - to help a besieged settlement.
Face one of 2 pre-built armies, the Goblin Horde or the Fire Lizard Crusade, or customise your own.
Set how challenging you want the defence to be.
Save Fort Briggs!

In Bulwark, players use "Heroism" to purchase cards for their hand, and play those cards to upgrade their hero and fight monsters.

description:
Army List Expansion for Vis Bellica.

Covers early civilization up to 1 BC.
Each army has a variable composition, with some restrictions, and are grouped by the period that army would have operated in.

Players select their army composition from the lists.  Units have points values and the players add units up to a points limit agreed to before the game.

description:
From the inside box cover:

"Flagship is a game as simple or as involved as the players are able to plan it.

Flagship is played on a special square Flagship playing board. The playing pieces used are nine (9) in number, being patterned after and given the functions of regular Naval units. In order of their importance, the pieces are:

1 Flagship
1 Aircraft Carrier
7 Destroyers

THE OBJECT of the game is to capture the Flagship of your opponent. When you capture the opponent's Flagship by jumping over it, you have won the game."

English translation
A game that successfully combines different elements of board game types and dynamics, a game which gives you multidimensional space, science fiction entertainment, and competition with cooperation.
Before a player can win a foothold on Mars many obstacles and adventures have to be endured.
Beware! Crossing the stargate, deciphering the Martian language, and avoiding the evil Dum Dum may make you completely forget about the outside world.

Learn the language of the Martians and take a trip into space.
Never to avoid the evil Dum Dum!


        


Polish:
Gra, która udanie łączy elementy typowe dla gry planszowej z dynamiką, jaką daje wielopoziomowa przestrzeń, rozrywkę z fantazją naukową, współzawodnictwo ze współpracą.
Zanim gracz zdobędzie swój przyczółek na Marsie, musi pokonać wiele przeszkód i przeżyć masę przygód.
Uwaga! Przejście przez gwiezdne wrota, odczytywanie znaczenia języka Marsjan, unikanie podstępów złowrogiego Dum Duma mogą gracza doprowadzić do takiego stanu, że zapomni o całym świecie.

Naucz się języka Marsjan i wybierz się w kosmiczną podróż.
Nie zapomnij unikać złowrogiego Dum Duma!

The Story

Triple-G is known to many different peoples and creatures by many different names, but most of them know it as the Grey-Green Giant.   It is a very large world located in the Interior of the Expanse, almost directly in the center of Trader's Void.  You would think then that it is a vast hub of activity, given its location, but alas it is not.  You see, for a very very very long time the Gibs and Nowans have slaughtered each other all across the Expanse, and a good-sized majority of that slaughter has happened on Triple-G.  All living things on Triple-G have lived under the constant yoke of their terrible war, until recently... when they vanished!  No one knows why, not that anyone even knows why they were fighting in the first place, but they left Triple-G, and according to rumors, they left the rest of the Expanse as well, and slinked off to their homeworlds to do Void knows what.  And who cares! Because they're gone, it's a miracle, and needless to say, there has been much celebrating.  It is an exciting time to be alive, to be a free person, to be an info collector, to be anyone on Triple-G, but unfortunately we must remember that it is also an ominous time.  Though the Gibs and Nowans have left, they leave behind many vacuums.  Vacuums of space and power that will be quickly filled by the unknown.  Already there are stirrings in the shadows of the townes, and though for the first time in a long long time there are brave travelers heading outside the gates, most never return.  When you consider what you are going to do now, you must exercise extreme caution, and realize the effect your actions will have.  Our eyes need to be wide open, and our minds sharp, both inside the gates, and out.  It is a delicate time in the Expanse, so we must tread delicately.

Kav Moggleberry, Expert Info Collector - Address to new travellers at the first traveller's guild annual raffle and bake sale

The Game

Tired of the cowards that huddle in fear in the stronghold of Gorth, you gear up and head outside the gates into the unknown landscape.  Explore ruins, find ancient destroyed cities, discover strange abandoned sites, experience new creatures, meet new cultures, but most importantly... find out more about the ominous unknown threat that whispers in the shadows, and threatens all of the free peoples of Triple-G!

Development

Currently playtesting Zone 1: The Green Hills of Gorthicon - Players must clear the Lost Pass before the Ominous Whispers track gets to 6.

Apocalypse is a new way of playing games of Warhammer 40,000. Allowing you to field as many miniatures as you like, in any combination. There really are no limits to what you can do.

FIGHTING AN APOCALYPSE BATTLE: The rules for setting up and playing games of Apocalypse; new Apocalypse missions and strategic assets; new rules such as Divine Intervention, and Sons of the Primarch; world ending Unnatural disasters; and advice on how to field all your miniatures in one gigantic collection.

APOCALYPSE FORMATIONS: Details of more than 100 devastating formations that feature the most powerful military assets of the 41st Millennium. With 11 distinct classes of formation, you can take your choice from the C'tan-powered Tesseract Vault to an entire Battle Company of Space Marines! For added convenience, these formations are available to purchase as 1-click formations.

MINIATURES SHOWCASE: Sumptuous photographs of super-heavy vehicles, gargantuan armies and heroic commanders to inspire your own collection.

WAR ZONE: ARMAGEDDON: Details one of the most monumental campaigns of the 41st Millennium, with unique special rules, formations and missions.

Peppered throughout the book is a host of additional background information to further inspire you.

Publisher Blurb

Death from the Skies brings the white-knuckle intensity of aerial combat in the 41st Millennium to your tabletop, featuring selected material from White Dwarf magazine, the Crusade of Fire supplement and exclusive new content. Exclusively available from games-workshop.com, this 72-page, full colour softback compendium book is crammed with background information, gaming ideas and hobby inspiration to help you bring the battle for air superiority to life.

It includes: a detailed account of the Battle for Cardrim, featuring White Scars Space Marines, Necrons and Orks locked in a deadly battle for control of a vital airfield; four new Air War missions that showcase the lightning-fast nature of aerial warfare; optional rules for dogfighting and veteran fighter aces for your Warhammer 40,000 games; and a showcase of beautifully painted Flyers from the 'Eavy Metal collection.

There are rules for using the Stormraven Gunship with a Codex: Space Marines and Black Templars army; updated rules for the range of Warhammer 40,000 Flyer models, including bestiary and army list entries for the following codexes: Black Templars, Blood Angels, Dark Eldar, Grey Knights, Imperial Guard, Necrons, Orks and Space Marines.

Publisher Blurb

Crusade of Fire is a campaign system for Warhammer 40,000 that enables you to join the campaign to control the Corvus Sub-sector. Whether you choose to join the Crusade of Fire itself, the foul Servants of Ruin or the bloodthirsty Prophets of War, the fate of the sub-sector lies in your hands.

This 96-page, full-colour hardcover book features fantastic artwork and a host of dynamic new rules. As well as the campaign system itself, the book contains rules that can be used in any Warhammer 40,000 game, from massive multi-player scenarios fought in low-gravity environments or in bunkers deep below the ground, to expanded rules for Flyers. It also features rules for playing games in the gladiatorial arenas of Commorragh as well as for fighting battles on the surface of a Daemon World.

Crusade of Fire also features the story of nine hobbyists as they play through the campaign, including detailed battle reports, fantastic army showcases and turn-by-turn accounts of their conquest

Send your wrestlers into the arena to fight under secret rules known only to your opponents – except when you set the rules, that is! Your winning weapons in this fun challenge that sends crazy characters into epic fights are tactics, intuition and bluffing – but in the end, it's all just a game!

Each round in Wild Arena – winner of the Cartamundi Award in Premio Archimede 2012 – the first player plays a Referee card (which shows how to win the round) face down and a Wrestler card (which has a value) face up. All other players in clockwise order then play a Wrestler card, trying to guess the rule. When everyone has played a Wrestler, the Referee card is revealed and the winner of the bout is determined!

Crime does not pay. In this game you are one of Beagle Boys. Goal is to perform the most burglaries in Duckburg as possible, before you are captured by coppers.

Game components are 2 dices (one with 2 red, 2 yellow, and 2 blue sides and normal d6), mission deck, tool deck, pawns for players (1 Beagle boy for each player) and coppers (numbered from 1 to 4).

It's a roll and move game, to complete a mission you need to be a right place with right tool and assignment.

After each round last player move copper, every time one player is eliminated one copper is added to board. Instead of a Beagle Boy (who is in jail), the eliminated player moves a copper.
 The player with most points after all Beagle Boys are captured win.

The object of the game is the first player/fox to get one chicken back to his den wins the game.
 How to play:

Place the chickens in the coop in the center of the board .
each player takes 3 foxes and places them in their den marked on board.

Throw a 6 or 1 to get a fox out of den.
Throw the dice and move on that number of squares in a clockwise direction,you are trying to get to the coop in the center of the board .once in the coop you can pick up a chicken and make your way home to the den,always moving in a clockwise direction.
If another player/fox lands on your fox they capture your chicken and you are returned to the den .

You may stay on any track and keep going around for strategic purposes e.g. to capture a chicken of your opponent.

boardgame: Forces of Warmachine: Convergence of Cyriss
description:
EMBRACE THE PERFECTION OF THE MACHINE


To those who worship Cyriss, no tenet is more sacred than the Fifth Harmonic—the belief that the Clockwork Goddess will manifest once the perfect machine is created across all of Caen. The key to this vessel’s completion lies in the geomantic fulcrums of power that cover the world, many in hostile territory. For centuries the cult has been amassing an immense mechanikal army, poised to take the final steps in readying for their goddess. The time has come for the Convergence to complete the Great Work, and they will let none stand in their way.

Devote yourself to mechanikal perfection and transcend the flesh with Forces of WARMACHINE: Convergence of Cyriss, featuring:

• Complete faction rules and profiles for fielding the Convergence of Cyriss in WARMACHINE Mk II, including Unbound formations.
• An in-depth look at the history and science of the Convergence of Cyriss and its enigmatic goddess.
• A painting guide to help you ready your forces for battle.
• Theme Force lists and rules, which allow you to create armies based on specialized forces from the Convergence.
LET NONE IMPERIL THE GREAT WORK!
 Available in softcover (PIP 1053) and hardcover (PIP 1054).

From the rulebook flavour text: The lightning fast reaction game with the little tiger and his friends for 2 to 6 players ages 5 and up. - The little tiger has a party and everybody has arrived: the little bear, the good looking goose, Guenter the frog and all the tiger friends. They eat, they laugh and dance with a lot of action. Of course they make photographs, as everyone wants a picture of him/her with friends to take home. Who gets the most photographs wins. And it works like this: Roll the dice, quickly look for the pictures and grab them fast.

Janosch Bildersuche (with Janosch being an artist and author of childrens books and Bildersuche meaning picture search) consists of 25 cards, each depicting two of Janoschs characters, and 2 6-sided dice that have stickers with these characters on them. All cards are put on the table for everybody to see. The dice are rolled. The first player to find the matching card - depicting the 2 characters that were rolled - touches it with one hand, and if correct gets to keep it. After all picture cards are rolled and found the player with the most cards is declared the winner.

Without a sound, or even a tremor, the fractured shards of many worlds have been haphazardly fused into new reality. The inhabitants are dazed and lost. The broken remains of each civilization now seek to rebuild. To even begin building a new home, each alien land must be tamed to suit the temperament and needs of your people.

Breath is a 2-4 express print-and-play area control game, inspired by Terra Mystica. And it fits in an Altoids tin!

The handful of cubes each player has are used both as workers who terraform the land as well as markers for settlements and bridges. When enough workers have been placed on a hex, the terrain can be changed to suit your people. Settlements placed on hexes that match your peoples needs can be joined together to build bigger settlements. When you run out of room to expand,  build bridges to reach the land across the river, or send your workers on ships to colonize the far reaches the world-spanning river.

Although larger settlements give more points at the end of the game, having many smaller settlements next to your opponents will give extra income during the game. Consider carefully which path you choose - a single large world-spanning settlement, or many tiny village-sized settlements.

Drawing from Michael Waitsman's Proteus rather than from Nomic-related card games, Más-Menos is a trick-taking game for two players and a 32-card deck constructed around two variable winning conditions: one applied to single tricks and another applied to the whole game. These conditions will change during play as tricks are won by cards of different suits.

Both players start out with a hand of 16 cards - after discarding three each they must determine the winning conditions that will apply from the first trick on. After that tricks are played; the suit of the winning card of each trick will switch one of the two currently valid conditions - red cards determine if highest or lowest wins a trick, black cards determine if more or less tricks win a game.

"Each player is an ambitious bee who wants to be the big-shot of the hive by building his or her own. Hives go up in value by having wide levels and tall stacks of honey. As soon as the Wax Stash includes the predetermined number of hexagons, the game ends. Whoever has built the most valuable hive (structure of hexagons) at the end of the game becomes the Big-Shot Bee and wins the game." (From the rulebook)

Bee Sneaky! is a game of double-double-think. With a light beehive theme, the only game components needed to play the game are generic transparent plastic yellow hexagons. Each round, a player secretly places a number of hexagons he wants to add to his hive in his left hand, and a number of hexagons in his right hand that corresponds to the specific way he wants to place them: either on the base-level of the hive, or on higher levels of the hive, or in stacks of "honey." But if several players choose the same action, only the player with the least number of hexagons to place gets to play the action!

This game is currently looking for a publisher. If interested, please contact the game's designer Corin A. Friesen at strategytree@verizon.net (or send a private message to username "Ambrose" on Board Game Geek).

Components:
• 300 small transparent plastic yellow hexagons in a hexagonal travel vinyl zipper case
• 1 rulebook

Other components for optional use:
• 42 cards
     - 24 action cards, 6 in each of the 4 types (Expand, Level-Up, Make-Honey, Outwit)
     - 18 number cards, 6 in each of the 3 numbers (1, 2, 3)
• 6 score cards
• 6 player aids

The Basics

Bakin' with Bacon is a card game for 2-4 players consisting of 2 decks of cards: The Bakin' with Bacon game deck and Bacon Playing Cards (a standard deck bacon-themed deck of 52 cards + 2 jokers + 2 extra cards). The game deck includes recipe cards, action cards, wild cards, and instruction cards.

Each recipe is made up of 4 ingredients, represented by specific cards from the standard deck. The goal is to collect all four ingredients to complete a recipe and then to complete all 3 recipes. The first person to complete all 3 recipes wins the game.

Gameplay

Each player begins with 7 cards held in his/her hand and 3 unique recipe cards face up on the table. Players take turns in a clockwise direction. At the beginning of a turn, a player draws one card from the deck. The player ends a turn in one of three ways: applying 4 ingredients to complete a recipe, playing an action card, or discarding an unwanted card into the discard pile.

When a recipe is completed, that recipe and the 4 cards used to complete the recipe are no longer in play, unless reactivated with an action card that specifically affects cards that are out of play.

At any point during the game, if a player draws the last remaining card from the deck, at the end of that player's turn, the discard pile is shuffled and then becomes the deck to draw from.

The game ends when one player completes all three of his/her recipes and is declared the winner.
publishers: The United States Playing Card Company

description: There are 52 cards with different letters on either side, 4 blank cards, and a tray with 5 bins for cards.  The cards are dealt evenly, players take turns placing letters in the trays until the last one is full -- making a valid word.  Then there are no turns: players just play one of their cards to the middle, replacing a letter with another letter to make another valid word.  When a player plays all his cards the round stops and he scores 1 point for each other card unplayed by the others.  The first to 25 points wins.

Dragon's gold is the best gold! The Dwarves know that, and so, when asked to make a golden dornik for the Trollgod, they set out to get some gold from Arngaxx the Dragon. Dragons hate to give up their gold, and they rather enjoy eating dwarves. There is no way the two sides can come to a mutually satisfying agreement.

The dwarf player moves his three dwarf pieces into the dragon's lair, using checkers like movement on a gridded map of the dragon's lair, and tries to carry off pieces of the dragon's gold. Meanwhile the dragon player moves the dragon, using checkers-like movement, in an attempt to eat the dwarves. The dwarf player wins if he gets three pieces of gold out of the dragon's lair. The dragon wins if he kills all three of the dwarves.
A new mechanism card exclusively for backers of the company's 2013 Kickstarter campaign.

Castles of the Franconian Switzerland is the exciting game of strategy and knowledge for the whole family. 
The goal of this game is to conquer castles and fortresses to liberate foreign.
Players roll the dice and move their pawn and in certain boxes try to conquer the castles by answering the questions about the castles included in the game, the Middle Ages and the Knights of existence in general. 
Who has occupied all the castles in the end, is the supreme lord of the castle of Franconian Switzerland, thus winning the game. Younger knights can make the event as pure dice game (without answering questions).

2-8 player word game that's all about 4-letter words.

Gameplay is simple: write down as many 4-letter words as you can think of within 30 seconds. The words you chose are based on certain criteria established by a roll of the Game Mode die, which includes 6 different game modes: Starts With, Ends With, Dual Rune, Rhymes With, Relation, and Weak Link. The first three modes are letter-based, and decided on the roll of a D20 letter die. The last three modes are word-based, and decided on the flip of a Word Card. Only 4-letter words are allowed (no plurals), and only unique words earn you a point (ie. if another player has the same word as you, neither of you earn a point for that word). First player to 44 points wins.

The game has unique custom packaging, in an engraved wooden box that measures on 3.5" x 5.5" (just a bit bigger than an index card!), and has a wood-and-glass timer, and wooden and bone-colored dice to keep with the old-world runic theme.

Race to Insanity is best suited for 5 players. One player is known as the Active Player, and they try to perform a combination of Challenges picked by other players from cards drawn from the deck. The Active Player earns points for successfully completed Challenges during their 1 minute round of play. Here is an example of a possible combination of Challenges during one round.

Blue Challenge (Vocal Lines):  Say three times fast – Molly munched on many muffins Monday

Pink Challenge (Actions & Motions):  Hop around like a monkey

Green Challenge (Facial Expressions):  Make a fish face
 Yellow Challenge (Vocal Impressions): Talk like a pirate

How many of these could you do in a minute?

BattleMage is a semi-cooperative deck-building board game. Players each assume the role of a BattleMage frantically slinging spells from atop their castle's walls in an attempt to stall an endless onslaught of invading Monsters for as long as possible.

The BattleMage who achieves the most honor by best defending their homeland will be awarded the title of Grand ArchMage and win the game!

Players will collect spell cards in a classic deck-building fashion using 3 separate resources. Each spell card has an immediate effect as well as a lasting Resonance effect. These abilities are used to try and destroy an everlasting onslaught of monsters approaching the castle walls which will move toward the castle each turn as determined by the roll of dice. Defeated monsters are harvested into players' decks by the Soul Crystals they leave behind, to even further boost each player's deck during play.

BattleMage is for 1-4 players (yes, there is a solitaire play variant!) and takes 45-60 minutes to play.

boardgame: Consetta and Her Wheel of Fate
description:
One player is familiar with the rules and acts as "Medium" and tells the fortunes of the other players, who must participate via various cards (there are several kinds, with numbers, symbols, and questions/answers) and their placement on an illustrated board that implements a kind of zodiac.

There is a fictional backstory of the Gypsy Princess Consetta of course, and the artwork of William Longyear is featured everywhere from the box cover to the instructions to the board and all the cards.

Mourinho and the Special Ones (MSO) is a trading card game based on the TV series of the same name. Two players build themselves a team of footballers and a draw deck of skills and tactics then go head-to-head against each other.

The game is played over two halves on a simplified playing area of lines (Forward, Midfield, Defense) and zones (Wing and Centre) that represent the Pitch.

When you are the attacking player, you will attempt to pass the ball around the pitch, avoiding tackles by the defending player, until you decide to try and score a goal. Skill and tactic cards are used to boost the attributes of footballers to make them better in these situations.

The winning team is the one that scores the most goals.

The game also incorporates Augmented Reality on the cards

This is a Deer Hunting Game tested and approved by Deer Hunters.
This game captures the essence of the hunt. Nearly every element of sitting in your tree stand waiting for the big buck to appear is woven into the fabric of this wonderful game. Players pick cards from 4 piles representing different locations in the woods, these cards include the deer, its orientation to the hunter and the range. When a player has all three he can shoot if he has the available tags. The deer are constantly moving and players need to make decisions about using an extra shot, buying an extra deer tag, or a scope.
Hunters can fall asleep, spook deer, and track blood trails if not a clean shot. Not only is it fun for hunters, but non hunters love it too. The player with the most weight wins. There is also two score modifiers, the Deer Magnet razz given to a player who misses his shot, and the Warden who visits once per game. 
There is also a legendary King (ghost) Buck that is so elusive the game provides a hall of fame certificate to record any hunter who manages to bag this enormous buck!

Welcome to the Eastlands of Dothmora, a realm riven by intrigue, feuding lords and warbands fighting in the secretive Shadow War. It is a land steeped in rune magic and blood, yet one where the various races – humans, elves, dwarfs, fauns, trolls, ogres, orcs, goblins and halflings – live in harmony, owing allegiance not to race but to one of the mighty factions that make up this war-torn kingdom.

Twelve Elements of War is a 28mm Tabletop skirmish wargame. A typical warband will include between 7 - 12 models (depending on your faction and style of play), activating one model at a time. Players alternate turns activating models, keeping both players engaged and thinking. Despite only activating one model at a time, it successfully simulates combined attacks with multiple models; the first attacks in a round weaken and lock-down a model, making it easier to finish off by attackers later in a round.

It uses a multiple D6 dice, one-roll system for all rolls during a battle. Roll a number of dice equal to the Model Statistic that you are using to attack/defend/employ, add bonus dice if you have an advantage of some type, like charging. Compare the result of this roll to the enemy model's statistic or enemy model's roll to see if you succeed. The dice system does not use charts or tables & is used for all rolls, making the game extremely easy to learn.

The broad range of Faction Lists each bring something special to the battlefield, and each faction choose from the 9 fantasy races. Each Faction is balanced to each other, despite having multiple fantasy races and at least 6 roles per Faction to choose from. Successfully employing a variety of different races performing melee, shooting or magic roles on the battlefield is the key to winning.

Over 20 different mission combinations ensures that each game of Twelve Elements of War will different from the next. Creating a balanced warband is only the start; quick thinking, using the right model at the right time & an assertive battleplan is the key to completing your objectives while denying your opponent theirs.

The fun with Twelve Elements of War doesn't finish after one game. Twelve Elements of War has a complete Campaign system that sees your models gain experience, better abilities, new skills, money and magical runic items as they play battles and also explore the world of Lumria acquiring strange and unique items. You will also have the option to visit a town after each game. In town you can choose to go to the Tavern to recruit new members and heroes, The Market to purchase new items and equipment, the Arena to watch pit fighting and maybe pick up a few pointers. Lastly the Temple, where you can prey to the gods to heal your wounds or ask for their blessing in future battles. In addition to visiting these places, you roll on one of four unique “random encounter charts”.

Games can be played on a vast variety of battlefield types, from cautious tactical advances in the snow covered Forest of Sighs, to fast paced skirmishes leaping from building top to building top in the sprawling Port City of Arcadia.

Lastly, with 100's of magical  item combinations to try out and over 36 unique runic spells to cast with your magic users, this game has a large amount of re-playability.

Factions in the core book include:

- The elite soldiers of Highguard, vying to restore their city-state to its former glory.
- The tough, guerrilla warriors of the Mining Guild, seeking to control valuable mineral resources across the continent.
- The Geniocracy of Toth, who strive to expand their knowledge of technology, science and magic – by sword’s edge if need be. 
- The sorcerers of the Shadow Hold, adepts at runic magic, and served by thrall workers and brainwashed fighters. They scour the land for lost runic artifacts and raid other factions to capture   slaves. 
- The stern Keepers of Talon Crag Citadel, who have vowed to the gods to hunt down all who dare toy with the more dangerous aspects of runic magic.

With more to follow n the future.

boardgame: Blind Alley: The Game of Mazes
description:
This game is somewhat similar to Milton Bradley's Pathfinder; it also has each player create a maze and then try to solve the opponent's maze by moving through it and being told when walls are encountered.

However, in Blind Alley the wall pieces come in a variety of shapes, each of which is a different color. When your piece hits a wall you are told what color it is, which may enable you to make deductions about where other walls are based on the shape of the piece. Information-marking walls are provided in the maze wall colors to allow easy tracking.

Bocce Dice is an action/dexterity game where players take turns rolling 4 standard six-sided dice towards a target Score Zone located on a rectangular play mat. Each player has their own Score Zone that gives them points, but the player can also attempt to reduce their opponent's points by attempting to knock their opponent's dice from the play mat. At the end of every round, the points are totaled and the player with the most points wins the round. The first player to win 10 rounds wins the game.

Mimikry is an abstract game in which you place shells on a grid of  6x6, leaving 3 spaces blank.
Next to every (horizontal) row is a number indicating how many spaces the shells in that row may move (horizontally, vertically and diagonally) to being able to end their movement on a blank space. Every shell that has moved to a blank space gets 'buried' under the sand (thus the 'mimicry' theme) and is turned over.
The game ends when all the shells have been turned over after being buried into a blank space. In that case you win.
When there are still shells left that can't be moved to a blank space you lose.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
If you like linguistics, syntax, or just awesomely offensive party games, this is for you!

From the games' web-site:

LTAG! is an absurd, irreverent card game based on Lexicalized Tree Adjoining Grammar
Compete and co-operate to generate offensive yet grammatical English sentences made of partial syntactic trees.
The first player to use up all of their cards wins!

Every Lexical Card in LTAG! is designed to successfully generate hilarious, deplorable, grammatical sentences by way of substitution, conjunction, adjunction, and more!

boardgame: Star Wars, The Clone Wars  3-Card Match Game
description:
"Match all characters with their action shot and vehicle to win! Different game rules included withint the pack."

Thirty double-size cards, making 10 sets of 3. Basically Memory with Trios instead of Pairs. Also included are rules to use the deck for a game of Donkey.
Advantage Tennis was a free print and play sports game by Adam Porter. It is no longer available.

A game with 8 figures with different colours on top. They look like atoms. Also a stack of 26 cards is part of the game. 26 Cards with different coloured balls.
1 Player throws the 8 figures. 1 Player draws a card.
All players call a numbre they think the figures match with the coloured balls on the card.
The player who guesses the right numbre get the card.
The player with the most cards wins the game.

Ringames Words is a fun and innovative game of linguistic competence, where vocabulary and visual dexterity are the protagonists.  Be the fastest to discover the best combination of letters to form the longest word.

The game is played on a board composed of seven concentric circles.  The circles are filled with letters of the alphabet and each letter is on one of two background colours.  The circles can all be rotated to help in forming words.  Each player in turn has ninety seconds (sixty in the advanced game) to form the largest possible word.  Words must be composed of letters with the same background colour that are in a straight line from centre to outside of the board or vice versa, or in the form of a v.  Once used a word cannot be repeated for the rest of the game.  The roll of a dice may require a player to take an “informative hexagon” with a goal for their turn.  If the goal is met, they will score bonus points, if not, their turn is void.  Scores range from one point for a four letter word to 50 points for an eleven letter word.  The game ends when a player completes their twelfth word.  Most points wins.

A note about playing time: it is highly dependent on the number of players and their experience with the game.  The estimate of 30 minutes is for a two-player game.  With the full complement of eight players, that could rise to 90 minutes.

description:
(Description from game publisher)

The great experiment that was the Republic of the Sphere has failed. Withdrawn behind the Fortress walls, the once-great power has become a silent, opaque remnant of its former glory. Without its influence, old hatreds have risen anew. As war once more rages across the Inner Sphere, new equipment strides across ancient battlefields. Technology, once stagnated by trade restrictions and peace treaties, now surges forward again, testing these new machines in the fierce crucible of war.

Technical Readout: 3145 introduces the latest wave of new battle armor, vehicle, ‘Mech, and aerospace units appearing in the Republic Armed Forces and across the Inner Sphere in the Dark Age era. Featuring new equipment described in Era Report: 3145 and Field Manual: 3145, this book brings players an update on the advancing technologies used in the battlefields of the thirty-second century.

boardgame: Ad-Man! A Game of Advertising Mascots
description:
Ad-Man! is a card game about advertising mascots. Playing the role of an advertising executive, players use original mascots to win product accounts to sell things like soda pop, baseball teams, and fabric detergent. The winner of the game has collected the most products. Different mascot types (like a dog, a cowboy, or silverware) are not restricted to selling just one thing. A snowman, for example, has been used to sell apples, dishwashing soap flakes, and the city of North Saint Paul, Minnesota.

The game consists of 60 core mascot cards, 20 expansion mascot cards, 31 product cards, 8 trump cards, rules, and 4 blank cards.

(The following is an abbreviated rules set. The full set of rules can be found on my website)
Product cards have a symbol, and only mascots with that same symbol are of use in winning the account for that product. Mascot cards have three symbols, each with a numerical value. Players take turns playing mascots with the appropriate symbol, vying to have the highest accumulated number value.

If a player has put any card into play during the round, at the conclusion they draw 1 fresh Mascot card. Players have the option to pass play at the beginning of a round, and may draw 2 Mascot cards

More information can be found at www.steverobbinsgames.com

Roll & Move game with 2 dice, d6 numbered and d6 with coloured arrows; 
Objective: Be the first getting out of a board - similar to a labyrinth.
 - acting by turns -
All players start at the same space. Move forward according rolled number; ending on a space with arrows use second die to determine diretion to move along.
Otherwise you may decide which way to go. 
First Player who escapes successfully wins.

Nevermoor is a tabletop miniature adventure/skirmish game. Players take control of a party of characters and fight through the deadly streets of Nevermoor to save the city from certain doom.
The Miniatures

Nevermoor utilizes 40mm pewter cast miniatures to represent the various characters within the game world. Each miniature is created with a mixture of hand sculpting and 3d modeling to create a true to life scale. Each character is designed by Creative Director, David Tepfer in a variety of poses and varying equipment to give each party (no matter the guild) a unique look. Initial designs, artwork, and sculpt pictures can all be found in the “Guild Models” section of this website.
The Game

Nevermoor is a small scale miniature game. Each party only consists of five models which are divided into one of three party roles:  Leader, Striker, or Tank. The small scale nature of the game allows for truly deep character construction. Each character can purchase upgrades throughout the game, gaining new attacks, skill, and equipment. All of this makes a perfectly paced game whose action slowly increases until it reaches its breaking point in the final few turns!
The World

The King of Nevermoor has gone mad! A mythic plague has taken hold of the city and corrupted the minds and bodies of its citizens. The plague is revered as a gift of the gods by the infected and turned these deformed monsters into “holy” zealots. In his madness, the King has sealed off the city to cleanse the uninfected. The city’s remaining guilds have taken to the streets in attempt to liberate the city but the mass hysteria and rioting has turned the city into a war zone.

The tabletop is packed with buildings, winding streets, and claustrophobic alleys. This unique layout makes playing Nevermoor feel like a massive game of cat and mouse. Hidden traps and ambushes are favored to the open fight in Nevermoor; favoring the clever planner over the brute.

Objective: 
be the first bringing home your 6 meeples
-moving by turns-
players rolls the die - moving forward during the very first beginning.
later on you may use the die to push your own meeples forward (the more at one space the better) or push back opponents' meeples.

remarks:
published during 1930's

Three Squares is a simple dice and chip game for players over the age of six.  The game consists of one die with various symbols and groups of three differently colored chips.  Each player starts the game with a group of three chips of the same color that are "their chips".  The object of the game is to win everyone else's chips.  Players accomplish this by rolling the die and following the rules based on what is rolled.  The die has three faces that are a simple orange square, one face with a yellow star, one face with the word "right" and one face with the word "left".
Every third square that is rolled during the game give the roller an opportunity to take a chips from someone.  Every time a star is rolled the player takes a chip from someone.  Rolling right or left changes the direction of play.
If a player loses all three of "their chips" they are out of the game and they must forfeit all of their chips and any others they have won to the player who took their last chip.
The winner is the player who is left with all the chips form all other players.
Setup take 15 seconds.  Games typically last 20 minutes or less.

The game is similar to the 1983 dice game LCR.

Predestination is a 2 player cooperative trick-taking cardgame.  It is a companion game to the 2 player competitive trick-taking cardgame Foreknowledge, with both being inspired by Romans 8 in the Bible.

In the game, the dealer player will be dealt a set of cards from the deck and then allocate the cards between themselves and the non-dealer player.  The dealer then arranges the cards in order they will play them, and play proceeds.

The object for the round is to make exactly the number of tricks that was revealed at the start of the round.  Play continues a set number of rounds, with each round consisting of a different number of tricks that need to be won.

In the advanced version, additional rules are added and change each round, to mix things up.

The game with "Elektronikrechner" - electronic calculator.

From the publishers description:
Goal: Who can build first an uninterrupted row of tokens on the game board, from start to finish? Who has at the same time brought his end sum by clever choosing numbers near Zero, so he wins the game.
Some tactics for an even more suspenseful game: Instead of cool calculating taking risks: All results change suddenly. Giving bad numbers to the other players. Blocking another players path.
Electronic calculator: With this exciting tactical game the electronic calculator is in high demand. With its help the best solutions can be found, when the best numbers have to be chosen. Also apart from the game You get useful services by the calculator. Effortless You can calculate everything that has to be calculated in every day life. Electronic calculator from Texas Instrumets.

boardgame: Star Trek Deck Building Game: The Next Generation – Release Date Ad Card – Jean-Luc Picard
description:
This promo card was released prior to the Star Trek Deck Building Game: The Next Generation Premier Edition to advertise the release date of said game on October 7, 2011.


    Name:* Jean-Luc Picard
    Cost:* 9
    Type:* Character
    Characteristics:* Federation | Human | Male | Ensign
    Effect:* When you complete a Mission or diplomacy a Starship, you may gain 1 card from starbase.
    Stats:* +2 speed | +2 attack | +3 diplomacy | - shield

description:
In ancient legend the mythic Tuatha dé Danann arrived on the shores of Ireland and there they met the mighty Fomor, they of chaos and nature. And there ensued at Mag Tuireadh a terrible battle for control of the lands and their treasures...

The Fomorian War is a card game for 3 or 4 players lasting about 30 minutes. Players secretly choose Tuatha deities to help them in battle as part of a clan as they fight for control of the lands and treasures of Ireland. They can also appeal to the Fomor for help of an underhanded sort or use the power of sacred items. Winning a battle for their clan gains Victory Points.

The game continues for a series of rounds or until all the lands and their treasures are held by one clan. The player with the most Victory Points is then declared the winner.

Needed to play - The deck of 54 cards and 2 eurocubes per player. Players may elect to keep score with pen and paper.

Play:

Each round of the game is played in the same order of phases.

Phase 1 - Battle Area. Depending on their chosen detiy, a player will choose which two cards in the Circle of Cards which will be the Battle Sites. The two cards chosen must show different Clan Holding Icons in the lower left. The cards must be adjacent to each other.

Phase 2 - Choose Tuatha Ally. The current start player will shuffle the Tuatha Deck and discard two cards face down to the center of the Circle of Cards, and then choose one of the remaining cards from the deck, keeping it secret from the other players. They will then pass the rest of the deck the player to their left to choose a card from those that remain. The deck will be passed this way until each player has chosen a card. Any remaining cards are discarded to the center.

Phase 3 - Reveal Warrior Cards. Each player, beginning with the current start player, will now play one or more Warrior Cards in front of them, face up for all other players to see. These Warrior Cards will add Battle Point Values, for their clans, to the respective Battle Site cards and their initial Battle Point Values.

Players of the same clan are trying to beat the combined Battle Point Value of the other clan.

Phase 4 - Reveal Tuatha Allies. Now each player will, in turn, reveal their hidden Tuatha Ally. The current start player will call out the numbers in order (from 1 to 7) and the player possessing that numbered card will reveal it and the power of that card may be used by the player. Tuatha allies are resolved in order.

Phase 5 - Resolve Battle. After the Tuatha, and Sacred Item and Fomor powers have been resolved, total the Battle Point Values for each clan and compare. The winner is the clan with the most Battle Points. The losing clan’s Battle Site card is flipped over to the other side to show the winning clan’s alignment icon. In the case of a tie, no Battle Site cards are flipped and no Victory Points are awarded in Phase 6.

Phase 6 - Victory Points. All players of the winning clan receive Victory Points as stated by the chart at the bottom the winning Battle Site card. Track Victory Points on the Score Card.

Phase 7 - Discard and Draw new Warrior Cards or add Cattle Herd.
Discard all used Warrior Cards to the discard pile.

Phase 8 - New Start Player. The player to the left of the current start player is now the new start player.

Game End:

The game ends in one of two ways:
A. After 8 rounds if playing with 4 players, or 9 rounds if playing with 3 players.
B. If all of the cards in the Circle show the same Clan Holding icon.

At this point the player with the most Victory Points is the winner. In the event of a tie the player with the most Cattle Herd Cards played is the winner.

boardgame: Eagle of Lille: Expansion for Bloody April
description:
The Eagle of Lille expansion to Bloody April (Air War over Arras France 1917) expands the air war to follow the career of Immelmann and some of the other early aces such as Oswald Boelcke and Lanoe Hawker. Seven new scenarios cover the period from August 1915 to early 1917 while including tables which randomize taskings and add replayability to the scenarios. 16 new Aircraft Data Cards are in this expansion.  They include German single seat scout aeroplanes; Fokker Eindecker E.1, Eindecker E.II/E.III and Fokker D.III. German two-seater aeroplanes; Albatros C.I, Albatros C.III, Aviatik C.1 and LVG C.II as well as flying battle plane the AEG G.II with a crew of four. The British get a bevy of new aeroplanes; Bristol Scout C, B.E.12, RE.7 and Vickers FB5 Gun Bus as well as French made Nieuport 16, Morane-Saulnier N, L and BB.

A new 8” x 11” map extension adds the off-map aerodrome of Treizennes and Merville as a now playable area to fly. A 25 1/2"x 11" map extension shows the German and Allies aerodrome east of Amiens. New Advanced rules for Bloody April such as Aeroplane Quick Turn and new rules for Eagle of Lille have been added.  Two new counter sheets of aeroplanes and informational markers are included to round out this folio expansion.  Take to the skies over France like never before and relive the battles that started air warfare as we know it today.

Ownership of Bloody April is required to play this expansion.

Contents:

    Rulebook & Playbook (28 pages)
    2 countersheets
    1 8 1/2 x 11" map (map 'N')
    1 25 1/2 x 11" map (map 'S')
    8 German Aircraft data card
    8 British Aircraft data card


(source: GMT webside & user's description)

description:
From Beasts of War;

“ArcWorlde, is a skirmish tabletop game, set in the fantasy realm of ArcWorlde. Although you can play it with any amount of players, and any amount of models, it’s usually with around fifteen models each, and features a model-by-model playing system.

"Each player takes turns to activate one of their models, in whatever order they choose. When a Captain (the leader of the force) or Hero (the minor characters) are activated the controlling player may activate a number of other models at the same time if they wish to do so. Up to six friendly models within 6” of the Captain or Hero’s base may be activated, performing their actions immediately as normal. In this way a player may co-ordinate their troops to attack their foes in waves but it comes at a price. Activating your models in larger groups can leave your opponent free reign at the end of the turn if all your models have acted.

"Other unique aspects of ArcWorlde are smaller “side-quests” within the main game itself. These are entirely optional, and by no means an essential component of the ArcWorlde game, but they add flavour and fun if the players so wish! There are Forgotten Tombs, where the player can delve inside to uncover lost treasure, or terrible evil. There are the Epic Duels, where ANY of the models on the board can engage in mighty combat, fighting to the death for glory and prestige. There are many more work-in-progress side quests, which will be added to the rulebook if the Kickstarter does as well as I hope!
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

boardgame: Bear in Underwear Backpack Adventure Board Game
description: Bear and his animal friends have found a backpack full of goodies. Players roll a jumbo multi-colored die to travel around the 3-D game board, through the forest and over the bridge, landing on colored spaces directing them to collect their animal’s favorite food, forest item and underwear. If a player lands on a space numbered from 1-3, they reach into the red box backpack to pick out that number of items that might match the items on their animal card. If they're lucky they'll grab one they need, otherwise they share it with the other players. First player to collect all the goodies for their animals wins in this silly game based on the books by Todd H. Doodler. No reading required. Everyone plays on every turn.

boardgame: Leviathans: Ship Quirks & Crew Abilities Card Deck
description:
The complexities of ship construction create unique design quirks that bring a flavor all their own to each leviathan, while the men and women who crew these airships bring their skills, passions and blood to elevate each vessel into legend.

Leviathans: Ship Quirks & Crew Abilities Card Deck is actually comprised of two decks: Ship Quirks and Crew Abilities, both of which feature cards with a positive or negative value. By using these cards, players can customize their airships prior to play, thereby having everyone start from differing circumstances each time you play. The rules recommend that when a player adds a positive Ship Quirk or Crew Ability that he take a similarly valued negative Quirk or Ability as well.

Hooch is a resource management card game for 3-6 players set during Prohibition. Each player controls a Syndicate, a groups of like minded ne’er-do-wells. The object of the game is to be the first to get the set amount of hooch before your rivals. But that’s easier said than done. First you’ve got to take control of a location, put up a still, and hire some muscle to defend it. The other syndicates are doing the same, but they’re also trying to stop you from becoming the most powerful crime lord in the city. They might go to war, sending their soldiers to take out your operation. Or maybe they’ll send a Button Man in to take out all your guys and then they can stroll in and take over the place themselves. Sure you’ll have your own Syndicate members to help you, but that won’t get you all the way. You’re going to need a public face as well. Maybe you should bribe a Judge. He can help you with all those pesky City Politics they might throw at you. Maybe a Mouthpiece to help the law bend your way. But something to keep in mind: you paid them off, maybe someone else might be able to pay more. Hooch is a game of alcohol, greed, backstabbing and violence. It’s the American way.

Star Borders: Aliens from designer Lance McMillan and developer Alan Emrich is a complete, 2-player stand-alone game that introduces two new factions, the Rhame Swarm (a.k.a., the “Bugs”) and the C1B0R6 Collective (a.k.a., the “Bots”) that are fighting to establish control of a contested region of the galaxy in the Star Borders universe.

This game is completely mate-able with Star Borders: Humanity and introduces a few new wrinkles to the universe, including the first 3-engine ships, ships that morph, and other strange goodies. Of course, there are new maps, counters, and compatible cards that can be retrofitted to Humanity so the design-your-own-scenarios possibilities have been increased exponentially.

Star Borders: Aliens features 7 new scenarios exploring the very unexpected war between the Bugs and the Bots, and the inclusion of a bonus scenario to allow players to bring in the forces from Humanity. The tension between the asymmetrical abilities of these fleets creates a great player tension on the board that you will long enjoy!

Complexity: 4 on a 9 scale
