description:
General Overview

Planet Defiant: Onslaught of Vanas is a cooperative sci-fi horror board game set in the far future where rare humans are capable of extraordinary feats due to technology and genetic manipulations. Earth is severely overpopulated and is searching for habitable planets to live on. With time being a factor several military groups are sent to far reaching ends of the galaxy to search for these planets. One such group comes upon a promising planet only to end up crash landing on the planet. After emerging from the wreckage they find the first alien race humanity has ever encountered but these creatures were not what they were hoping for and now they are in for the fight of their lives just to escape the planet... can they survive?

Game Mechanics & Features

Planet Defiant: Onslaught of Vanas will feature multiple factions fighting for survival against an aggressive alien species. Aliens will spawn every turn with more coming the longer you are playing. You will be fighting epic battles against multiple creatures and insanely powerful bosses to find the resources needed to escape the planet. Only with the help of your teammates can you hope to make it off the planet alive.

The Bullet points

Onslaught of Vanas supports 1-8 players that scales difficulty on the number of players

46 total miniatures

D10 (ten sided dice) for all combat and skill checks.

Over 500+ cards including every alien, character, weapon, and item in the game, all with amazing artwork

Large Game Board with 30 unique locations to visit.

A day and night system that completely change what you encounter and how you play.

Cooperative game play including cooperative fights, trading, leveling, and synergy system.

Customizable weapons and skill system to play your character how you want to.

Fun initiative system that changes how the battles play out.

Bosses who need teamwork and strategy to defeat.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Skirmish! The Scramble, players need to use their monsters to grab treasure that's been dropped by adventurers who fled the area.  Claim more than the other player – or any four treasures – and you win.

Each player has a small deck of numbered (1-9) monster cards, five spells and two dummy cards. (For players of uneven skill, the more experienced player can shuffle additional low-valued "newb" monsters into his deck.) Each player shuffles his deck, takes the two dummy cards in hand, then fills his hand to six cards. Players shuffle the seven-card treasure deck (valued 1-7), then lay out five cards face down.

A round starts with the players revealing the value of the treasure. Each player first lays out a single card face down, then they reveal these scout monsters and refill their hands. They then lay down two cards face down, then reveal them. A player can include one spell in this pair of cards, and the spell might, say, steal an opponent's weakest monster or kill all monsters other than the undead. Whoever now has the highest monster total claims the treasure.

The first player to win two treasures valued 1-4 claims a crown card worth 7 points. If a player claims four of the five treasures, he wins instantly; otherwise players tally their points after the end of five rounds to see who wins!

Wild Weather is a lightweight, portable card game for 2-4 players that plays in 15 mins.  Designed as part of the Standing in a Line Design Contest.

The aim of the game is to collect sets of cards that match a given objective card.

Players are dealt a hand of cards - all cards are displayed so all players may see the face in-play and have 4 Seasons - Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall and 4 Colours - Red, Blue, Yellow, Green, plus Symbols that are Black or White.

On a Turn the player Chooses a TYPE (Season or Colour) and an ACTION (Flip Over, Pass-Left, Pass-Right). ALL cards matching the nominated TYPE are affected, in all hands.

Play continues clockwise until any player meets the objective - they take the card and a new Objective is revealed.

Game ends when the 5th Objective Card is won - scores added and winner declared.

The game is also language independent and color-blind friendly.

A dice game that has nothing in common with Soccer except the name and terminology. The game consists of three dice which include one "Defending" die and two "Attacking" dice. The first player rolls his "Attacking" die and if "Shoot" is rolled then the 2nd player rolls the "Defending" die trying to roll a "Miss" result, whereupon the 2nd player now posses the ball and begins "attacking". The game continues a succession of PASS, TACKLE & SHOOT attacks and GOAL, CORNER & MISS defenses with possession passing between the players. Play continues until a predetermined score (agreed upon before the game) is reached.

boardgame: Heroes, Villains and Fiends: A Companion for In Her Majesty's Name
description:
Game description from the publisher:
A selection of Company Lists, scenarios and special rules designed to expand and develop the world of In Her Majesty’s Name. The Company Lists will introduce new, fully playable factions to the setting, from the Vatican’s monster hunters to revolutionaries and underworld organisations across Europe to Russia’s intelligence agents, American rail barons, Asian secret societies, and the inhabitants of darkest Africa. The scenarios and special rules further develop the base game, giving players more variety, and increasing the possibilities!

Chapter & Verse requires players to connect hexagonal tiles they have drawn to tiles in the play area in a manner similar to Dominos. To connect tiles players must match the numbers on the edge of the tiles played with the number on the tiles it will be connecting to. Some of the tiles have olive branches on their edges instead of numbers. Olive branches must connect to other olive branches. Since there are many olive branches, they make it easier to connect tiles.  There are also wild tiles that have edges that may count as any number or an olive branch. One other way tiles can be matched is if the player is able to combine the tiles to create one of the three verses on the Message Card he was given at the beginning of the game. For example, a player may have the verse Matthew 5:16 on his message card. He could connect a tile with 16 on one of its edges to a tile that has Matthew in its center and 5 on one its edges to create Matthew 5:16. 
The objective of the game is for the players to create all three verses on their message cards and use the clues in those verses to guess the secret word on the other side of the card. The first player to create all three verses and guess his secret word is the winner. Even if a player can guess the secret word before they complete all three of the verses he must complete the other verses to win.

The following is from the back of the box - An archeologist, Dr. S needs your help to decipher and uncover secret words. He needs assistance sifting through ancient tiles and linking them together to gain clues to a message. Dr. S has started a Log Book detailing the links between the tiles. According to the legend, when you recreate the messages using the ancient tiles you will uncover clues to a secret word(s). Find the tiles, build the links, read the writings and claim your reward of wisdom, one word at a time.

Nightmare is a Trading Card Game. ( TCG/CCG )

Set in a world of shared nightmares and featuring 13 different Phobias to deploy against your own subconscious and that of your enemies, Nightmare will test your ability to remain focused and escape your fears.

Deploy strategy, planning, and a bit of Delusion before you lose your soul forever in an experience that combines the familiar with the never before seen in a compelling new twist on TCG gaming.

What Makes Nightmare Different?
Nightmare isn't your typical cookie cutter clone.

Nightmare shares some very fundamental TCG conventions with other games but the core structure and setting is completely unique.

We're going to explain everything as we go along, but what you're in for can best be described as "wicked" in every sense of the word.

Nightmare will also feature Achievements, DLC, and many more world first features! There's even a Novel Length Storyline planned!

About the Game Structure
There are 121 different game cards.

Players design and build a single deck made up of a certain number of cards.

Card Types
Nightmare doesn't have "spells" and "creatures", it features Phobias, Entities, Delusions, Doubts, Uncertainties, Guilts, Synaptic Misfires, Paranoia's, and Entropies.

Game Overview
At the beginning of a game, players shuffle their decks (called “Mind”) thoroughly and draw cards.

Players decide who goes first, by random selection.Each player may place one Delusion per turn.

Play progresses through turns and stages until one player loses their all of their Lucidity, thus losing the game or achieves 100 Lucidity. If there are more than 2 players, the last player still holding on to some Lucidity is the winner and buys their sanity with the souls of their opponents or achieves 100 Lucidity.

This is just the surface. Players will have to contend with Madness, Repression, and Regression in their struggle to escape their Nightmare.

Black Hole, initially designed as a paper-and-pencil game, is played on a triangular board of 21 cells. Two players alternate turns placing a numbered disc of their color onto an empty space. Discs are numbered 1-10 and must be placed in numerical order. When all the discs have been placed, the game ends. The one board space that remains empty is the "Black Hole"; each player sums the values of his discs surrounding the Black Hole, and the player with the lower sum wins.

Black Hole includes two rule variants: In the first, players can place their discs in any order, and in the second players place their discs face down and shuffle them, drawing a disc at random each turn, then placing it.

I am Providence

1-4 players 
Worker placement
Variable player powers (advanced version)

I am Providence, sets the players in Arkham. Mass and its surrounding areas.

Players will place their workers at different locations to gain types of resources that will help fight the terrors that will visit Arkham.  You'll collect lore, arcana,  and research resources, in addition to gaining the trust of cultists who will help usher in some of the more advanced terrors to Arkham. You must know your enemy and their plans if you wish to defeat them.

The game will have a built in time mechanic that will unleash unspeakable terrors on the town unless the players choose to confront it first.

The eldritch deck will contain 3 levels of terror which will visit the town.  Each time the game is played the deck will vary, as a certain number of cards is added to each of the three levels, changing with the number of players. 
Example. There are 18 level 1 terrors, in a typical game there will be only 6 in the level 1 deck. When setting up the game players will randomly choose 6 terrors and shuffle them.  Ensuring that you'll always have a fresh, exciting game as you never know what horrors may come to town.

Battling an ancient evil one won't always be easy, but will undoubtably increase in difficulty as the game progresses.

When battle commences, a player will flip the top of the eldritch deck and have to have the corresponding resources to defeat the terror. 
Defeating the terrors that confront Arkham will earn you valuable Elder signs (victory points) as well as possibly gaining additional resources.  Encountering these ancient terrible entities won't be easy on your psyche though, defeating or even just encountering these awful creatures can affect your sanity.

Collect too many insanity cubes and one of your workers will be eliminated from the game and have to check in to Arkham Asylum.

The game progresses through all three levels of the Eldritch deck, culminating with a final battle with an Elder god.

When a player defeats the final Elder God in the deck the game ends and Arkham is temporarily safe.  The player who has gained the most Elder Signs will claim victory.

In the advanced game, players will become famous personalities from the Lovecraft Mythos, gaining benefits and even possibly penalties based on the chosen characters.

I am Providence will be deeply rooted in the Lovecraft Mythos, never straying from the original source material. Many authors have continued the work of HP Lovecraft, expanding his Mythos and building a world of terror. We will focus solely on his writing and works for the base game.  (Though who knows of we'll see a return to Arkham in expansions).

Artistically, I am Providence will capture the theme and essence of the late 60's and early 70's American International Pictures movie one sheets.  The goal is to present I am Providence as a lost movie title in their series of films that were based on the works of Poe, though their films "The Dunwhich  Horror', 'The Haunted Palace'  and 'Die, Monster, Die' borrowed heavy from Lovecraft.

The game includes 8 giant plastic dice. Each team takes 4 of these dice and rolls them into targets. Any dice that land inside the rings score the numbers shown on top. Be the first team to score 21 and win.

Star Trek HeroClix: Tactics 3 puts you in command of starships from the Star Trek universe, including such factions as the Federation, Klingons, Romulans, Cardassians, the Dominion, Ferengi, and Vulcans.  Tactics 3 adds four new factions: the Bajorans, the Kazon, Species 8472, and the Borg.

Tactics 1, 2, and 3 are intended as a standalone game system, 100% compatible with the HeroClix core rules system.  So even though Tactics operates by the same rules and Powers and Abilities card that other HeroClix miniature games are played with, it exists outside of the Modern and Golden Age tournament environments and is played as its own game. (e.g., no Spiderman vs. USS Enterprise. There's Star Trek HeroClix: Away Team for that.)

Tactics 3 features 28 pre-painted ships and will be available for sale in both a 4-ship starter product and single-figure blind boosters.

The Duke: Swashbuckler is a tile for the abstract strategy game The Duke that was initially available only to participants of The Duke tournament at Gen Con 2013. It is available as a light and dark tile with the same front side, but the two backs are a mirror reflection of each other.

boardgame: Zombie Mall: Brains on the Brain
description:
Zombie Mall Brains on the Brain is the first expansion to Zombie Mall and features integrating Zombie Players into the existing rules of Zombie Mall.  Now players can find themselves reborn as part of the zombie horde after dying, and continue play while attacking the remaining Human Players.

The same map tiles and tokens included with Zombie Mall are used with this expansion along with the addition of a few Zombie Player specific tokens - these tokens are the Zombie Player token used for tracking where in the mall the Zombie Player is located, as well as the new Dead Again marker for those times that the Zombie Player is beaten down by the human folk.

Zombie Mall Brains on the Brain is an expansion and is not a complete game.  The Zombie Mall Basic Rules, available for free Print & Play download, are required to play.

Components:
In addition to adding Zombie Players to the A Call For Help scenario in the basic game, Expansion 1 also includes:


     New Tokens needed by the Zombie Players
     Updated Quick Reference Sheet usable by both Human Players and Zombie Players
     New Player Stat Card usable by both Human Players and Zombie Players. This Stat Card also implements a Scoring Track for player stats and Victory Points which drastically reduces the number of printed tokens needed per player: the 90 tokens/player on page 4 of the Zombie Mall Basic Rules Tokens PDF can be reduced to 7.
     2 new scenarios.

"It is a world transformed, where things are not what they seem. It is a world of heroic Autobots and evil Decepticons. It is the world of, THE TRANSFORMERS!"

Hey guys, John Nemo here. Very excited to be presenting this game to everyone.

Transformers: G1 at war is a free print and play game of heavy metal war on a galactic scale. Miniatures Wargame ruleset.  Players have the ability to field a full battle group of transformers, Both Autobot and Decepticon, From Optimus Prime and Megatron, to Bumblebee and Ravage.

The goal of the game is simple: Skirmish level combat. Players move there forces across the map, to fight, move, transform, and do pretty much everything you think of from a Transformer Battle.

The master game, "More than meets the eye", deals mainly with the characters released in the toyline's initial year, 1984.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Pew! Pew! is a solitaire (i.e. 1 player) game designed to emulate the feel of old school space shooters.

You are the last and best hope for survival against a horde of Advanced Alien starships on their way to lay waste to your homeworld! But are you enough?

The game is played in turns. Each turn is broken up as follows: 
   Sequence of Play
   1) Spawn New Ships
   2) Player Actions
      1 AP - Fire a Pew!
      2 AP - Move Orthogonally
      3 AP - Move Diagonally
      +1 AP - Drop a Mine while moving
      ALL AP - Spawn a Heart
   3) Alien Ships Move
   4) Spawn Missiles
   5) Move Pews!
   6) Move Missiles
   7) Move Player Ship Forward

Meaning - "Hail Caesar, those who are about to die salute you"

Ave Caesar, morituri te salutant is a game of Gladiator combat in a Roman Gladiator arena.  It has 1 to 4 combatants "Gladiators", plus Beasts, and Slaves!
The mechanics are slightly similar to Combat in the Lists with a new hex tile game board for the arena, 7 direction movement, new character sheets with abilities, new weapons and armor, beasts; such as lions and bears, slaves "non-trained" combatants, new tactics, and all new images and graphics for cards and components.

Each Gladiator has nine equipment counter locations on his reference card:

Head
Left shoulder
Right shoulder
Chest
Groin
Right hand
Left hand
Right leg
Left leg

These [9] locations can hold [9] different pieces of equipment which can be picked up before the match.

These new counters make up part of the new mechanics of the game where the equipment and weapons are represented by counters and cards!  The counters fit into specific locations on the player’s reference cards and are distributed before the match through a process where each Gladiator takes turns picking a certain number of equipment pieces from an rack.  This process can leave the Gladiators with a mix-match of different types of equipment and weapons but also make for a different game each time its played.

Each equipment counter is also represented by a Equipment Defense point card, which is used to keep track of the amount of damage a piece of equipment takes during a combat session.  This new system makes up the other part of the new mechanics for Ave Caesar...  Each piece of equipment depending on its type will use up to three different D6 die to help record the amount of damage that piece of equipment can take before it becomes useless. Once the piece of equipment take its max damage allotment, it no longer prevents damage from hitting the player.

Once equipped, the Gladiators take turns moving, fighting, repositioning, devising tactics and strategies to maneuver into position for the best attack and defense. If a Gladiator is killed all his equipment “counters” are placed on top of his player piece and are available to any of the combatants in the arena!

Each weapon and piece of equipment has a card explaining the attack and defense characteristics.  Attach and Defense cards are used for combat to help devise a tactical strategy against other players.

This game will consist of the following components:
A combat arena
Gladiator player reference cards
Weapon cards
Equipment Defense point cards
Animal cards
Slave cards
Attack cards
Defense cards
Specialty type cards for combat and defense - Attack and Defense styles
Player game board pieces
Animal game board pieces 
Slave game board pieces
Movement cards
etc...
And a new rules book

In the Galaxy far, far away… Join Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala on the planet Geonosis, where they have to fight against ferocious monsters on the arena. Will they survive until remaining jedi knights arrive? Will Count Dooku be victorius this time?

The aim of the game is to defeat your opponents until the droids enter the arena.

Gameplay:
Players decide which side of force thay want to play.
They Choose their pawns and appropriate decks of cards. Both sides have 3 characters that can be freely distributed according to the number of players.
One player can play multiple characters, but they can not fight against each other. 
The dark side of the Force begins. 
Each player in his turn has the right to perform an action with only one character.
In player’s turn, players can perform two actions: movement and attack. They can also use a special action.
Every turn players draw two cards from the character deck and select one to be used.
The movement and attack points are indicated on the cards along with special actions.
Special actions allow to fortify attack, heal or move away. 
Damage is marked on special lenticular indicators.
Whoever elimates the other side of the force wins the game.

Stations Manned and Ready II is a downloadable set of tactical naval miniatures rules for ship models of 1/2400 or 1/6000 scale (though other scales can be accommodated).  Like the first edition of the rules, these rules are set at a squadron level, allowing relatively large battles to be fought on a normal table in 2 to 4 hours. In the 2nd Edition all the separate parts that were in the 1st Edition have been combined and the combat system revised in order that it follows a consistent structure.

The period covered by the rules has been backdated to ca. 1885, and the data sets available expanded even further than before. The data in the system is compatible throughout so ships from all periods could be matched against each other, though this may not be recommended.

Ship data is presented in sets for nations and time eras. The data sheets are fully prepared for you, so there is no need to fill out separate sheets. The only proviso is that when using smaller vessels you may have print several copies and the names will have to be entered individually by the player. Ships from cruiser upwards all have individual sheets. All data sheets take account of revisions of armament at various times.

Time scales are 6 minutes for a game turn, combat is regulated by the use of 5,000 yard range bands, which provide gun penetration values and torpedo hit modifiers. Armour protection is converted to an armour class on specific areas of the ship, taking account of the armour type and date. Damage represents those hits that cause significant damage to the target, and critical hits cause degradation of the combat effectiveness of ships. Command and crew quality are important and ships are commanded and crewed by men of mixed ability, generated using systems which players will recognise from other A&A titles.

The chance of critical damage is now keyed to the "size of the bang" involved, and the number of guns firing (in the case of ships). More guns means a more chances of causing critical damage, and larger shells/bombs mean a higher chance that critical damage will occur. Higher chances also mean that the damage effects may also be more extensive, because we have expanded the way in which the critical damage system works.

Play follows a sequence using initiative. The winner of initiative chooses which side will move a squadron first. Movement then alternates between the two sides. During Movement the slowest elements finish their movement first, so the order is all submarines, then all ships, finally all aircraft. The winner of initiative then shoots with a squadron first, and then play alternates. Combat actions ignore the type of element involved.

Movement takes into account the manoeuvrability of ships of different sizes. Gunnery takes into account the crew skill of the firing ship, interference by shooting from other vessels, the size of the target and the number of guns firing. Hits will cause structural damage and may also cause critical hits, which will degrade the combat effectiveness of the vessel. As ships get more damaged, there is a possible effect on morale, which may cause formations to withdraw to lick their wounds.

boardgame: Card Counting: A Strategic Player vs. Player Game
description:
Players place bets on their own game board. Stackem up high, or place smaller bet amounts. Part of the challenge is bringing in a profit that keeps your bankroll growing. Watch out, the person next to you may just overbet the same winning number resulting in you having a smaller payday then what you should have earned. It's just like betting horses. The odds of a card being turned over are determined by the cards you see face up. The payout odds however are determined by the bet value on the winning number.

Bankrupt your rivals before the end of a round, or have the most chips at the end of a round to win the game. It's that simple!

Card Pirates is a "pirates" card game for 2 - 4 players. Players take the role of an old fashioned pirate ship on the high seas, searching for treasure, avoiding storms, attacking other ships and attempting to win the favour of the island governors. The game is playable with just one pack of playing cards and a token to represent each player.

The playing cards are spread out face down in a grid representing the high seas, with a few kept in the players' hands. The Aces are reserved and placed last to represent islands with buried treasure. Players take turns to use the number cards in their hands to sail across the sea, discover new cards and attack other ships, and use their face cards to reclaim treasure and other higher ranking face cards from islands.

The game ends the moment all the treasure has been recovered from the islands. The winner is the player who has the best combination of Aces and face cards in their hand.

Introduction
Chichen itza is 2 player abstract tile laying game with a lite Mayan theme.

It is played on the spaces of a 6x6 board with 18 shared numbered tiles.

Object attain the highest score by having the most pyramids in your color along the longest unobstructed golden paths on the board.

Definitions golden paths- are unobstructed lines on the board that run from one side of the board to the other, which are formed when the board is full of pieces. From 1 to 6 such paths will form per game.

Play
One player is red the other blue. Players share 18 domino like tiles that have two pyramids red and blue affixed to one face of the tile.
Tiles are also numbered like so…

First number is Red and the second number is Blue(3,1)(3,2)(3,3)(2,1)(2,2)(2,3)(1,1)(1,2)(1,3)

First number is Blue and the second number is Red(3,1)(3,2)(3,3)(2,1)(2,2)(2,3)(1,1)(1,2)(1,3)

The 6x6 board starts out initially empty.

Next decide who plays first.

Play then alternates.

On your turn, cover two empty connected spaces on the board using one tile piece, while taking care not to leave a single empty space between the other tiles on the board.

The game ends when the board is full.

Scoring 
To score a game you simply add up all the numbers on the pyramids in your color that run along either side of the golden paths and only those pyramids that are adjacent to those paths are scored.

Note: some golden vertical and horizontal paths intersect, when this happens some of your pyramids will lie along these intersections in both directions, so do not count them twice when scoring.

The player with the highest score wins.

This package includes:

StarForce
The game is a simulation of events within a conjectural future history. It is played on a map which displays 74 star sytems in a three-dimensional "sphere" of space measuring roughly 40 light years in diameter, with our home system at the center. A grid of hexagons printed over the map is used to regulate movement and position of pieces.

Outreach
Simulates civilization development, intra-galactic exploration and combat, covering about one-third of the galaxy on a Grand Strategic scale.

StarSoldier
A tactical level simulation of combat between a small number of StarSoldiers, powerful future warriors - shades of Starship Troopers. Firmly rooted in SPI wargaming - along with the usual complexity and breadth of rules. One notable about the game is that it has simultaneous plotted movement - manageable since each player only controls 3 - 12 warriors. There are many scenarios and a campaign as well.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Mahta Mahti is a very different kind of card game.  It is challenging enough to interest adults and experienced gamers, but simple enough to appeal to kids old enough to put a thought into something.  It is designed for 3-10 players, ages 12+, although we've seen 10 year olds play quite well. Cards are played from the hand onto a play area that is 16 cards square; four rows of four cards. The novel nature of Mahta Mahti cards is that as uniquely square cards with matchable numbers on all four sides, each card is rotated to find the best match.  Even more unique is that the scoring landscape constantly changes as cards are played on top one another. Right card; right spot; best match; best score.  It's that simple. A little strategy, a little arithmetic and a lot of fun.  The kind of game that makes kids dream of college, not monsters.

Welcome to planet Dubious, home of the Dubious Blobs!

The most sacred currency here is ice cream and it is your job to hold onto yours whilst making your opponent lose theirs in this brand new card game.

The game itself is made up of 54 cards and can be played by both small kids and big kids alike; there really is no age limit when it comes to playing and there is also no limit of players that can join in depending on how many decks you have.

The rules are simple to understand and the game is very colorful and lighthearted; the main objective of it is for the player to enjoy themselves (or become glorious overlord of all the ice creams if you so prefer.)

Players play either attacks or defense once per turn. Some attacks steal ice cream, some destroy ice cream.

Players can choose to win the game by either removing, through destruction or theft the ice cream of the opponent, or they can achieve the highest honor of collecting all twenty ice cream, for maximum bragging rights.

3D Dominos is a game played with 32 rectangular prisms made up of two cubes fused together, then colored on the exposed faces in one of two colors. Under pressure of a sand timer, players try to match up faces on a growing three dimensional structure, maximizing their scores by matching the most faces in the most difficult places. In addition to the pieces and sand timer, the game includes a serpentine scoreboard and scoring tokens in four player colors.

From publisher description:

More Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

So, you've faced down goblins and fire lizards at Fort Briggs and think you're pretty tough? Well, try the Winter of Death on for size. More cards, more options, more opportunities to take a thrashing from a card game.

This expansion rounds out the basic Bulwark set with the following:


    A new hero - Wiola Wildborne, the huntress.
    2 new enemy armies to face - the Fallen Elf's Vengeance and the Winter of Death
    3 new locations to defend - the Citadel of Xajorkith, the Sylvan Heights and the town of Holtvaros.


The starter set of Bulwark is split into two products - this expansion and the base set.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Eschewing the narrow study and academic focus of the Council of Mages, Jakab learned from the example of the elves and mastered something resembling their traditional magic. Having travelled all over the world in the process, he is a seasoned adventurer and mighty opponent on the field of battle. He was named Startouched by his tutors for his celestial inspiration, and not for having a head full of sky. He says.

Welcome to Hard Mode

Jakab Startouched is a new Hero deck, fully compatible with the base game. It introduces a little randomness to the Hero supply piles, making for a less predictable start to the game. He is recommended for experienced Bulwark players.

boardgame: Devenez Président des états unis: too close to call
description:
Devenez Président des états unis is a game about the American presidential elections.

The goal of the game is to become president of the united states.

Each player can decide if he wants to throw one or two dices. The result is a number of votes he can assign to any state.
If you accrued the exact amount of votes requested in a state, you win the total of votes of this state, while your opponents get nothing.

The winner is the one getting a majority of the electoral college.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

No-one knows what dark secrets were whispered into the ear of magecraft's most promising student in generations. Whatever they were, they led him to reject the wisdom of his teachers and take short-cuts to power - ones that left him far less, or perhaps far more, than human. He is consumed with the desire to destroy the mages he felt were holding him back, at the head of an army of brainwashed followers.

You Are Not Ready To Face This

The Messianic Uprising is a challenging Foe deck that can run you out of Wounds without even putting any into your deck. Almost every card interacts the the others, leaving you with very little room for error. Worst of all, the Arcane Messiah himself becomes harder to kill and more dangerous the more of his minions are around.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

The Tower of Secret Lore is the acvademy where all mages are taught, and where the Council that keeps them in order meets and works. The main tower is host to an almost impossible number of floors, with huge buttresses and parapets reaching out from it and containing entire rooms themselves. Shining with crackling magelight, it is an unmistakeable landmark.

More Spells, More Problems

The Tower of Secret Lore is a new Location deck, fully compatible with other Bulwark decks. Its defence presents new challenges for experienced Bulwark players, but it equally suitable for new players. It has particular synergy with the new Hero decks Magister Kelemen, Magistra Csenge and Jakab Startouched.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Csenge was identified as a precocious talent my mages from the Council at a young age. Unfortunately, she did nopt take well to the dsicpline and ,eventually was expelled. After a number of years developing her talents in her own way she became a formidable battle mage, much sought after as a mercenary. The Council decided it was better to have her inside throwing balls of fire out, than outside throwing them in. She is now a full member of the Council, much to the chagrin of her former tutors.

Welcome to Hard Mode

Magistra Csenge is a new Hero deck, fully compatible with the base game. It introduces a new mechanic, mana, that makes forcing combinations of cards more critical than ever. She also takes time to develop his deck, making her hard to manage for short Start Phases. She is recommended for experienced Bulwark players.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Magister Kelemen is a peaceful water mage, consumed with furthering his knowledge of magic. Unfortunately, his attempts to conduct research always seem to be interrupted by monsters, bandits and invading armies. Consequently, he is one of the most powerful battle mages in the world. One day he hopes to set up a laboratory that isn't swarming with demons, on fire or both by the end of the first week.

Welcome to Hard Mode

Magister Kelemen is a new Hero deck, fully compatible with the base game. It introduces a new mechanic, mana, that makes forcing combinations of cards more critical than ever. He also takes time to develop his deck, making him hard to manage for short Start Phases. He is recommended for experienced Bulwark players.

A Candyland-style game where Batman, Superman, Green Lantern, and Flash try to get to the finish before the Joker gets to his goal.  Each character has a different super power to help their side win.


Calling all DC Super Friends. The Joker is up to his usual tricks, and he must be stopped! Superman, Batman, Green Lantern and The Flash team up, calling on their special powers X-ray vision, the Batarang, force fields and lightning speed to dodge The Joker's gags and stop him in his tracks before he reaches the getaway car. The Fun House game board is packed with preschool play power: a wacky rolling ball, secret messages, and a launching Batarang will draw young super heroes into the action!

boardgame: Forces of Evil: Diabolical Demon Promo Card
description:
The Diabolical Demon preview promo for Forces of Evil provides the following abilities:
Cruelty: Slay any number of your monsters.  Receive that number of influence.
Subjugation: When you recruit goblin-kin, receive one additional Goblin Shaman.

This set contains 240 cards and 2400 concepts, including more than 1000 new. Old concepts are combined with other, giving players that have played the basic game often no advantage.

With this upgrade kit you experience this spectacular party game for teams again.

Crilo is a dexterity based game for one or more players. it is played by rolling a Crilo ball over Crilo cards to gain points. The Crilo ball resembles a tennis ball, but is covered with a velcro loop texture. The Crilo cards are 15 discs with a velcro patch in the centre, each worth a different amount of points.

The goal is to get as many points as possible by getting the Crilo ball to stick to as many Crilo cards as possible.

In Pipifax, players need to find animals in the wild, and the first player to find an animal in all four sections of the wilds wins the game.

To set up, take the 24 animal tiles – which come in four colors with six animals appearing in each color – shuffle them face down, then place them in groups by color. On a turn, the player rolls the die, then turns over a tile of any color to try to find the animal that matches what's shown on the die. If she fails to do so, her turn ends; if she does find the animal, she rolls the die again and tries to find the matching animal in a different color group. This process continues until either she fails to find an animal, thereby ending her turn, or she finds one animal in each of the four color groups, in which case she wins!

The IQ game for 2/4 players, to prove your general knowledge is superior to that of the other playes.

Absorbing family game based on the TV series for 2-4 players.
Shaw Taylor as the Quizmaster with Gwynneth Tighe

Equipment: Board with the "Know All" Answer Disc, Spinner and Scoring Chart, 12 Cards (Print A, B, & C). Four Pegs, Four Coloured Counters, Four Identical Question Sheets with five sets of 50 Questions, marked Red, Green, Yellow, Blue and White.

Vaquero is played on a 12x12 square grid of circles, divided into four colored quadrants of 6x6 circles each, with the central four circles of those quadrants missing and replaced with the game's logo.  Each quadrant has a pattern of numbers 1-10 on its circles starting on its outer corner and also on its inner corner.  Each player then has 10 numbered discs, the same colors as the board sections, and several games are described involving placing those pieces on the board and achieving an objective such as moving all the pieces from one numbered set of circles to another.

Like other games from this designer/publisher, Vaquero is an abstract strategy game system branded with a Western theme.

Barbie needs a new outfit for her evening date with her girlfriends, so she wants to shop for a new top; new trousers or a skirt; new shoes; and a new handbag — and all of this in one afternoon.

To set up Barbie Shopping Day, lay out the sun with its rays, as well as all garment parts. On your turn, use the spinner and take the indicated garment. If the spinner shows the sun, you take away one of the rays and can either take a garment or must give back a garment. If you are the first to completely dress Barbie, you win. When the sun has lost all rays before that, there is no winner and all players put one garment on Barbie.

Stranded in shark infested waters, race to shore or find the items necessary for an immediate rescue. Play as a "team" or jump overboard and go it alone, overcoming torrential storms, blabbering parrots, pirate attacks, and simply losing your mind. Hoard food for an end game bonus. Oar Else! is a "cooperative" card game for 3-5 players, ages 8+.

Everyone starts together, adrift at sea in a lifeboat. The game can be played cooperatively by paddling to shore together in the boat, collecting cumulatively fifteen paddle cards. An alternate win is for the group to find three out of four items necessary for the boat's immediate rescue.

However, players also have the option to jump overboard and "go it alone", taking their paddling contribution with them in the hopes of either paddling to shore before the boat or by achieving their personal immediate rescue scenario.

Once the boat reaches shore, food cards are used to break-the-tie. When food cards are drawn during a player's turn, they do not get to keep the card. Instead, the others in the boat must bid on the card by using cards from their hand.

Thank you to Chris Baylis at the Games Gazette (http://www.gamesgazette.co.uk/gg/main.php?p=content/Board+Games/OAR+ELSE+card+game+fun.htm) for writing this great review of Oar Else!. I appreciate his honest and constructive feedback and have since revised the rules in the hopes to make learning the game easier.

Rules revision: 2.19.14: http://www.flatcar6studios.com/oarelse/pdf/oarelse_rules_2014_0219.pdf

Download and print the combined set-up card: http://www.flatcar6studios.com/oarelse/pdf/OarElse_CombinedSetupCard.pdf

In Hook 'em, players are trying to collect the most fish. Place all the fish in the pond. Roll all 4 dice and take one yellow fish for each fish on the dice. Increase the catch using the the net as a multiplier. Use the hook to take fish from other players. Bring the Catch home or continue to roll and catch more fish.  Play continues until there are no more fish in the pond. The player with the most fish wins.

The game box contains 1 Pond Board, 4 Dice, 1 Fishing Boat, 40 Yellow Fish, and 24 Orange Fish.

Rooked! is a Chess variant that changes the goal of the game. Instead of Checkmate, you have specific achievement you need to reach to win the game.

At the start of the game, each player draws five random achievement cards from their color deck. Each card has an achievement and a reward for completing that achievement. All game pieces move as normal and all rules are the same as Chess, though you no longer worry about check and checkmate. Your opponent also has five achievements, so you must be quick to complete yours before they complete theirs.

WBC-The Board Game is a game for 3-5 players, who will share the fun of the week-long World Boardgaming Championships, contesting convention events in a quest for Laurels and competing for the ultimate prize: The title of Caesar. Players must manage their time wisely.  They must remember to carve out a moment between games to sleep and eat while dodging convention pitfalls – including other competitors, unfortunate circumstances, and the occasional dreaded phone call from home.  After six days of gaming -- when the games are all packed up and the competitors are headed for home – WBC players compare accumulated Laurels.  Highest score is Caesar, a proven boardgaming champion worthy of praise!

The six turns of WBC-The Board Game, each one representing a day at the convention.  Each day/turn proceeds in the following sequence first the Day’s Events are determined, Players then Schedule their day (ie pick which Events to attend), next the Players in turn order select specific Events or spaces to resolve (ie allow Event attendees to compete in that Event or spend time at a recovery space such as Pool Side hoping to recover some much needed Energy or satiate their Hunger).  Finally, at the close of the day/turn all Players participate in one final Event, the Late Night Event, where success often comes at a price.

Additionally Players will be assigned an Alter Ego card that represents their “Player Type” at the convention.  Player types range from the Eurogamer, to the Emerging Talent, the Superior Players, or the Late Night King.  Each Player type provides two abilities to the Player, one a typical benefit common to the Alter Ego Type being played and one a unique benefit for that Alter Ego.

Finally, convention attendees compete at WBC for a variety of reasons.  WBC-The Board Game represents this with Convention Focus markers.  The Convention Focus is a secret objective the Player is attempting to complete by game’s end and depending upon their level of success rewards the Player with a number of additional Laurels.

Each copy of WBC-The Board Game includes the following:

- One 22-by-34” game board.
- W “What’s Your Game” deck, featuring many of the
games you’ll play at WBC.
- B “Build Your Skill” deck, featuring mostly
useful items which will aid your quest
to be Caesar.
- C “Competitors” deck, featuring many of the
competitors who will provide a helpful
boost -- or stand in your way --
throughout the week.
- 55 player cubes, 11 each in five colors.
- 15 dice, three each in five colors.
- 60 convention focus markers.
- 95 laurel tokens.
- Six two-sided alter-ego player aids
- One turn marker.
- One 12-page rulebook.

¡Welcome to Monza international circuit, where the best pilots of the world race for formula 1 great prize! As a real pilot, you'll have to use your reflexes and choose quickly the cards that allow you to accelerate and advance in the race. But you must be precise, you're running at a speed of 250 km/h and a bad decission could take you off the track. ¡Speed up, advance and win in this speedy tabletop game!

Contents:

54 80 x 80 mm cards
1 rulebook

¡Bienvenido al circuito internacional de Monza, donde los mejores pilotos del mundo compiten por el gran premio de la fórmula uno! Como un verdadero piloto, deberás utilizar tus reflejos y elegir rápidamente las cartas que te permitan acelerar y avanzar en la carrera. Pero deberás ser preciso, ya que corres a 250 kilómetros por hora y una mala decisión te hará salir de la pista. ¡Acelera, avanza y gana en este velocísimo juego de mesa!

Contenido del juego:

54 tarjetas de 80 x 80 mm
1 reglas de juego

Wrangled! is a fast-paced card game of Cowboys and Cattledrives for 2-4 players that takes about 30-45 minutes to play. Players can choose to Draw, Trade, Build, or Attack on their turns, using either Cow or Cowboy cards in their hands to score points on the Cattledrive that forms during the game. Have the most Claimed Cows at the end of the game to win!

(From the Publisher's website):

Build your fame and glory!
Viziers is a game of foresight set in a land of trade, combat and discovery. As a Vizier to the Sultan of Arpeya, you govern an Oasis on his behalf. With some planning, adaptability, and a good record of service, you're certain to become the next Grand Vizier!

Gain the Sultan's favor by developing a prosperous Oasis. Entice the trade caravans to your domain with livestock, weapons, food and artifacts but beware the raiders and conquerors who will surely seek to steal your wealth! Build academies to make discoveries, strongholds to protect your Oasis and palaces to curry favor with the nobility. Every once in a while make an effort to set a rival back - but take care that you don't suffer the same fate!

Do you have the foresight to lay a successful path to victory, or the creativity to adapt to the changing needs of your Oasis? Can you stay a step ahead of your rivals as you jostle for ultimate power and the title of Grand Vizier?

Personal Description:

Viziers is played over rounds, as players race to first gain 10 victory points, winning them the game. Each round, cards to be auctioned are passed out to players who secretly study them before they are called to commit to purchasing the different resources necessary to win (or avoid winning) the cards in their possession and everyone else's during the upcoming auctions. The cards are then returned and resolved in the order of their numbers. Both lucrative trade and strong enemies are acquired by the player with the highest of the relevant resource, along with global events and technologies. After the cards (and their rewards and punishments) are assigned to the players, a new round begins.

A MINI-EXPANSION FOR BANJOOLI XEET:

"The more experienced voices of the Noamomi tribe tell that years ago, after the nine-hundredth and first ostrich race (in which, almost everybody shoud know Xultu shaman became winner), the breeders realized that one of them hadn’t reached the goal. They searched every corner of the Village in vain as they didn’t hear from kanasu ostrich again …

… until the penultimate race, in which many attendees noticed that there were six ostriches running on the dry river bed, what came as a total surprise for the quiet Noamomi. However, old Xewwi instantly comprehended what was happening. Kanasu had returned to run after been missing for years, who knows which cruel spell of the shaman apprentices had been cast?....
And something in his eyes indicated that he would no longer be scared of anything or anyone."

This expansion consists of a single ostrich that sometimes joins the race. It cannot be scared, and if it reaches the finish, no player gains points for it.


“Cuentan las voces mas experimentadas de la tribu Noamomi que años atrás, tras la noningentesimoprimera carrera de avestruces (en la que, como todo el mundo debería saber, resultó ganador el brujo Xultu), los criadores descubrieron que una de ellas no había llegado a la meta. Buscaron por todos los rincones de la aldea en vano, ya que nunca mas se volvió a saber del avestruz Kanasu...

...Hasta la penúltima carrera, en la que muchos de los presentes advirtieron que llegó a haber seis avestruces corriendo en el cauce seco del río, lo que sorprendió a los tranquilos Noamomi. Sin embargo,el anciano Xewwi, comprendió lo que pasaba al instante. Kanasu, había vuelto a correr tras pasar años desaparecida, quién sabe por cuál de los crueles sortilegios de los aprendices de brujo... Y algo en su mirada señalaba que ya no se iba a asustar de nada, ni nadie.”
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Dantes Reise durch die Hölle is based on the first part of Dante Alighieri's Divine Comedy — Inferno. Players start in the dark forest and walk through the circles of hell in order to reach Purgatory. To pass from one circle to the next, players must collect a "coin of clemency" in every circle. To gain these coins, they answer questions and solve tasks. Questions are about the daily life in Dante's Florence, the Middle Ages, and the creatures and sinners that players encounter during their journey. All questions are multiple choice and require no previous knowledge of the Divine Comedy; they can be answered using deduction and working with the other players.

Tasks often require cooperation, e.g., a player has to select a prominent sinner from real life and send him to an appropriate circle while the other players have to guess the circle. Players interact a lot during play, both helping and misleading one another, stealing and donating coins. Players can also use "coins of indulgence" to get favors from other players or "coins of clemency".

The player who collects all "coins of clemency" and passes Lucifer in the last circle wins.

•••

Dantes Reise durch die Hölle basiert auf dem ersten Teil der Göttlichen Komödie von Dante Alighieri, dem Inferno. Die Spieler beginnen im dunklen Wald und durchqueren alle Höllenkreise, um das Fegefeuer zu erreichen.

Um in den nächsten Kreis zu gelangen, sammeln die Spieler in jedem Kreis eine so genannte "Gnadenmünze". Sie beantworten Fragen und lösen Aufgaben, um diese Münzen zu erhalten. Die Fragen drehen sich um das tägliche Leben im Florenz Dantes, das Mittelalter und die Kreaturen und Sünder, die den Spielern auf ihrer Reise begegnen. Alle Fragen sind multiple choice und können durch Kombinationsgabe und Zusammenarbeit mit den anderen Spielern gelöst werden - es sind keine Vorkenntnisse der Göttlichen Komödie nötig. Aufgaben sind oft kooperativer Natur, z. B. muss ein Spieler einen prominenten Sünder auswhählen und in den passenden Höllenkreis verbannen; die anderen Spieler müssen den Kreis erraten. Es gibt viel Interaktion zwischen den Spielern, die sich unterstützen und in die Irre führen, einander bestehlen und beschenken. Es gibt auch ein Handelselement: die Spieler können "Ablassmünzen" einsetzen, um sich die Gunst anderer Spieler oder "Gnadenmünzen" zu sichern.

Der Spieler, der als Erster alle "Gnadenmünzen" gesammelt und Luzifer im letzten Kreis passiert, gewinnt das Spiel.

This is an asymetric pure strategy game based on the events at Tiananmen Square in 1989.  One player (black) plays the Chinese Communist Party.  Their goal is to surround the Monument to the People's Heroes.  The students (white) must link from the Monument to the outside world.

The game is played on a standard go board with go stones.
Each player attempts to balance a piece of "trash" on the top of Oscar the Grouch's trash can.  The player who last placed a piece of trash when the trash can falls over loses the game.

Little ghosts like to play in Castle "Booh Booh Castle”. One of the ghosts will masquerades as a creepy monster and tries to scare the other ghosts with a terrifying cry: "Wuuuuaaaahh". The ghosts floating through the castle and hide in one of the rooms. Hopefully it is not the room where the creepy monster is lurking ....

Content:
• 1 game board
• 5 small ghosts
• 6 room cards
• 15 creepy tokens

Gameplay: One player is the creepy monster and selects the room of the castle where the creepy monster will try to scare the other little ghosts. After that, each little ghost may move to an other room (which is adjacent) or stay in his current room. Then the creepy monsters reveals himself and all little monsters who are in the same room as the creepy monster lose 1 token. Then the next player in clockwise order will be the creepy monster. Gameplay continues until at least 1 player has lost all his tokens. The player(s) with the most tokens left win(s).

Lohai is a trick-taking game for 3-5 (typically 4) players that combines the strategy of games games like Spades, Hearts, or Euchre, adding some additional mechanics and replacing Aces and 10s with special cards such as the Mover (moves a taken trick to a different player) or the Taker (automatically takes the current trick regardless of the other cards played). There are cards added and some removed to keep the size a standard 52-card deck. It is played with a special deck of cards, most of which are standard playing cards in the regular suits of hearts, spades, clubs and diamonds, but printed with some additional information such as point totals.

The available points vary between 50 and 450 for each round, randomly determined as printed on a card turned up before each round. The player with the most tricks and the player with the fewest tricks at the end of a round score the available points. If there is a tie for most or fewest, then no one scores the points for that position. The standard game has nine-card hands, so at least one person will always score the points. A standard game is played to 1,500 points, but players can agree to raise or lower this total as they see fit.

Luck of the Draw is a real time dice and card game that takes place in the Wild West. Two rival Posses are having a shootout at high noon and will settle the score once and for all! Roll your dice to take a shot while simultaneously turning over the top card of your Shootout deck. The Shootout deck can be a blessing or a curse! It’ll determine the direction of your shot, whether the backup comes in, or if one of your Posse members can get behind cover! The first player to eliminate the opposing Posse wins.

CONTENTS
• 6 dice cups
• 24 dice
• 120 game pieces or snake parts / nuggets in red, blue, yellow, green, black and white colours
• Score notepad
• Game rules
• Game board Rattlesnakes

NUMBER OF PLAYERS
All games can be played from 2 players up to 6 players at a time.

1. GOAL
The goal of the game is to be the first one to play all dice away from your cup.

2. GAME TERMS
Value: the value of the numbers on a die from low to high is 2-3-4-5-6-1.
Joker: number 1 on the die is the joker and can be considered to be any number from 1 to 6. A thrown number 1 can also be played as a 1, in which case it is valued higher than a 6. This applies to all game modes of THINKABOXIN.
Bid: a gamble or bluff on the total amount of dice of a certain value that are underneath all players’ dice cups (including one’s own cup).
Raise: raising a bid means that EITHER the amount of dice is raised (e.g. raising four twos to five twos, etc.) OR that the value of the aforementioned amount of dice is raised (raising three fives to three sixes or three ones) OR that both are raised (raising five fours to seven sixes, etc.).
Lift: the player who calls a bid into question, lifts his own dice cup. Afterwards, all players (including the player whose bid had been called into question) lift their cups in order to check who is right through counting the amount of dice with the value named in the last bid.
Won dice: after each round all players – except the player who was not right – can get one die out of their dice cups and put it away.  The amount of dice put away is called won dice. Only the dice that can be put away from one’s own cup count as won dice. 
Round: one played round (ends when the winning players have been able to put away one die).
Set: all played and counted rounds.

3. GENERAL GAME RULES THINKABOXIN
• Before the start of the game, each player always receives a personal dice cup containing 4 dice.
• All players simultaneously shake their cups with dice, and each player looks underneath his own cup to see what he has thrown himself.
• Players may not ever see one another’s dice (except for when cups are lifted).
• The youngest player starts the very first round. The rounds after that, the loser of the previous round or set begins bidding.
• Whoever starts a round always determines the playing direction. The player determines this through making a bid to either the player to the left or right of him.
• A bid that has been placed cannot be withdrawn or changed.
• Once a bid has been placed, the turn of whoever placed the bid is over, and the next player’s turn is at hand.
• The next player either raises this bid in the given playing direction or lifts his cup if he takes the current bid into question.
• Raise: raising a bid means that EITHER the amount of dice is raised (e.g. raising four twos to five twos, etc.) OR that the value of the aforementioned amount of dice is raised (raising three fives to three sixes or three ones) OR that both are raised (raising five fours to seven sixes, etc.).
• Raising a bid has to be done in the given playing direction.
• In case of a raise, the previous bid no longer needs to be kept in mind.
• When someone lifts the cup to indicate that he takes the previous player’s bid into question, this action is final and cannot be undone.
• When the bid has been taken into question and one cup has been lifted, all players should then lift their cups, and the dice with the values named in the last bid will be counted.
• When more than or precisely the amount of dice named in the bid are present, the bidder is right and the lifter is not right.
• When fewer than the amount of dice named in the bid are present, the bidder is not right and the lifter is right.
• Whoever is not right is not allowed to get any die out of his cup, all other players are allowed to get one die out of their cups.
• The won dice that have been put away from the cups have to be placed in front of the dice cups on the table in a way that is visible for all players.
• The player who was not right starts the next round and can determine once again the playing direction through placing a bid in the desired direction. 
• The player(s) who manages to be the first to place all the dice from his cup in front of him on the table wins the set.
• If dice roll out of one or more players’ cups or dice end up lying on top of one another, then these players are The Donkey. This means that all other players can get one die out of their cups. Afterwards, all players shake their cups and the bidding can be started anew by the one (or those) that is (or are) The Donkey.
GAME TERM: KNOCK-OUT RACE
Unlike stated under the GENERAL GAME RULES THINKABOXIN, a knock-out race does not stop when one or more players have played away all their dice from their cups. During a knock-out race, a round will continue until there is one loser left. This loser will be eliminated from the game and barred from further participation to this part. The next set will continue with the remaining players, and the last but one loser starts the round. This will go on until one winner remains.

4. GENERAL TIPS AND TRICKS THINKABOXIN
• At all times, keep an eye on how many dice are in the game at the start of each round. At the start of a 4-player game, 4 x 4 dice = 16 dice are lying underneath the cups. The lowest possible bid is in this case 1 die with value 2 (one two) and the highest possible bid 16 dice with value 1 (sixteen ones).
• It could be useful for the player whose turn is at hand to base his bid on his own dice. True bluffers do not do this. They do not care about which dice they have, and wildly undertake hit-or-miss bids. These unpredictable players give the game an extra dimension. 
• Determining the playing direction upon doing the first bid of a round can create a tactical advantage when placing a calculated and sharp bid towards the player who is ahead of the rest of the players.
• When there are 2 dice remaining in the game and these are both ones, the player who at that moment would bid ‘two ones’ wins, rather than the player who would bid ‘two sixes’. The joker is namely also the highest value on a die. In this specific case, both players should of course both have a 1 underneath their dice cups.

5. GAME MODES
Nuggets

• The GENERAL GAME RULES THINKABOXIN apply to NUGGETS.
• Each player receives 10 nuggets before the start of the game.
• Each player shakes his dice in the cup, and the first player of each set can, after checking the dice underneath his cup, determine what the stake for each player will be for this set.
• This stake is either 2 or 4 nuggets.
• All other players will then place the same amount of nuggets as their stake.
• If one or more players do not have any remaining nuggets and are out of the game, the stake for the other remaining players will always be the total amount of nuggets of the player with the lowest supply of nuggets. In that case, the stake could be more than 4 nuggets.
• When a player has no remaining nuggets to put in as stake, the game is over for this player and he can no longer play.
• The pool of nuggets will only be distributed when one or more players have played away all their dice from their cups.
• When there are multiple winners, the pool of nuggets is to be distributed fairly between them.
• Any possible remainder of nuggets will stay in the pool for the next set. When two players win with an uneven amount of nuggets in the pool, the remainder will stay in the pool for the next set.
• The player who has won all nuggets is the winner of NUGGETS.

Goldrush

• The GENERAL GAME RULES THINKABOXIN apply to GOLDRUSH.
• One player (the secretary) fills in the players’ names (in sitting order) and keeps the score for each round on the score notepad.
• 10 rounds are played in total.
1st set = 1 round, everyone starts with 1 die in their cups.
2nd and 3rd set = 2 sets, everyone starts with 2 dice in their cups.
4th until 6th set = 3 sets, everyone starts with 3 dice in their cups.
7th until 10th set = 4 sets, everyone starts with 4 dice in their cups.
• A set is won when one or more players have played away all of their dice from their cups. At that point, all won dice for each player are counted and noted under the players’ names for the relevant set.
• The running scores are continually counted by the secretary and announced to the other players.
• The final score is announced by the secretary, declaring who the (tied) player numbers 1 and 2 are.
• The (tied) player numbers 1 and 2 are pitted against one another in a final knock-out-race.
• The winner of this final is the winner of GOLDRUSH.

The Next Sheriff

• The GENERAL GAME RULES THINKABOXIN apply to THE NEXT SHERIFF.
• During the entire game one-on-one duels are played. Each player duels one another once, until one of the players has played away all of his dice.
• One player (the secretary) fills in the players’ names from left to right and, in the same order, from top to bottom. The secretary also keeps the score for each duel (the amount of won dice per player) on the score notepad.
• The secretary determines at what time which player plays against which player.
• The amount of dice that have been put away from the cup is the amount of scored points. This result is noted directly after each duel by the secretary.
• After all players have played against one another once, the total amount of won dice is counted and noted in the totals column on the right-hand side (Total amount of won dice).
• The score from the totals column is filled in at the lower section (in case two players have a tied score, the winner is defined as that player who beat the other player in a duel). The resulting tournament schedule works in the following way:
With 3 players: no. 1 directly granted place in finals. No. 2 plays against no. 3 for place in finals.
With 4 players: no. 1 plays against no. 4 for place in finals. No. 2 plays against no. 3 for place in finals.
With 5 players: no. 1 directly granted place in finals. Winner of no. 2 against no. 5 plays against winner of no. 3 against no. 4 for place in finals.
With 6 players: no. 1 plays against no. 6 for place in finals. Winner of no. 2 against no. 5 plays against winner of no. 3 against no. 4 for place in finals.
• The winner of the last finals is the winner of THE NEXT SHERIFF.

Last Man Standing

• The GENERAL GAME RULES THINKABOXIN apply to LAST MAN STANDING.
• A knock-out race is being played during the entire game (see explanation KNOCK-OUT RACE).
• One player (the secretary) fills in the players’ names and notes during each set which player has been eliminated and crosses that player’s name out on the score notepad.
• The winner of the knock-out race is the Last Man Standing.

Rattlesnakes

• The goal of this game is to be the first to place all your snake parts (20) on the game board.
• RATTLESNAKES is played on the RATTLESNAKES game board with 20 snake parts per player.
• The GENERAL GAME RULES THINKABOXIN apply to RATTLESNAKES.
• Each player receives 20 snake parts before the start of RATTLESNAKES.
• The amount of snake parts that can be placed on the board per set is determined by the amount of dice won by the player per set.
• After a played set, the player with the fewest won dice is the first to start placing his snake. In case of an equal amount of won dice, the player who was the last to lose can start placing. He also determines in which direction the other players are allowed to place their snake parts.
• If a player, for example, has won 3 dice in a set, he can then place 3 snake parts on the game board. Afterwards, it is the next player’s turn to place his snake parts.
• A snake always begins from one of six snake head squares on the game board, which can be freely chosen by the players.
• Only the first snake part placed by the player can be put on a ‘snake head’ square. Afterwards, no snake parts can be put on a ‘snake head’ square.
• Each square on the game board can be used only once.
• The snake parts that have not been placed yet must be placed in front of you in a way that is visible to all players, so that it is at all times clear which player is ahead or behind on the rest (this can influence the tactics of placing snake parts).
• Placing snake parts on the game board can be done in any desired direction via an adjacent square.
• The final snake part that is placed (the head) in a set is placed (temporarily) upright, so that it is clear from which square the snake slithers onwards into the next set.
• When crossing your own snake (through removing one snake part after winning a set with 4 won dice), the crossed part becomes the head (no extra snake part is therefore placed).
• When snake parts can be placed anew, the snake head is laid flat and it is from this square that new parts must be placed.
• The next round will be started by the player who was allowed to place his snake parts the first in the previous set. He once again determines the playing direction through placing his bid to either the left or right (note: a tactical advantage can be gained through this action).
• Once a player has chosen to go into a snake pit by having a snake part stand on an orange square, that player is then obliged to let his next snake part appear at another snake pit on an orange square.
• It is forbidden to immediately re-use an already used snake pit. When going out of a snake pit, the snake has to slither ahead on the regular squares. Players can, however, use multiple different snake pits within one game.
• NO snake parts are placed on a snake pit, as snake pits only serve as a passage to and from another snake pit.
• When winning a set (all dice are out of the dice cup) the player (or players) has the following options:
EITHER place 4 snake parts,
OR remove 1 snake part of your own,
OR remove 1 adjacent snake part of your opponent (this snake part is then given back to the player in question). However, this cannot be done to a snake belonging to a player who has also won this set with 4 dice.
• The player who is the first to put his entire snake on the board wins RATTLESNAKES!
• Should multiple players manage to put their entire snakes on the board within one set, a final round is organised for these players in the form of a knock-out race (see explanation KNOCK-OUT RACE).

Four surfers travel the world to see who can be the first to get $500 and 3 sponsors.

Players roll to move around the board, each space giving or taking cash or movement bonuses. Some spaces are surf contests which allow all surfs to surf against each other to win cash prizes and sponsors. Surfers flip cards which are their moves that provide points. A surfer can stop when he chooses, wipes out or the wave closes out. If he wiped out he gets no points, otherwise the points on the cards are summed.

description:
A complete Advanced Tobruk System (ATS) TT game depicting actions at the American landing zone on the eastern section of Omaha Beach, namely Fox Green, on June 6, 1944.
Included are 16 new scenarios ranging from one-map affairs to two-and-three-map dustups as well as one monster scenario, when combined with the companion game Omaha West, utilizes all six Omaha Beach maps.
Additionally, the game includes over 1000 counters with new art on most combat units, as well as all the necessary rules and ATS play aids.
Although this is a complete game ownership of Omaha West is required to make use of one scenario.

boardgame: ATS TT Kursk: Psel River Crossing
description:
An Advanced Tobruk System (ATS) TT expansion recreating the famous tank and infantry battles in the vicinity of the River Psel and the all important high ground of Hill 226.6 in the Soviet Union, July 1943.

Psel River Crossing utilizes the tried and true Advanced Tobruk System rules incorporating the new "TT" variant rules which feature new machine gun options, new collateral fire rules and the Advance to Contact sequence of play, among others.

This game includes new combat unit counters, scenarios, a comprehensive ATS Battlefield Walkaround and a remastered map based on the one found previously in Clash Along the Psel.  Ownership of standard ATS rules, playaids and informational counters is required.

boardgame: ATS TT El Alamein: The Battle of Ruweisat Ridge
description:
The Advanced Tobruk System (ATS) revisits the North African desert in this ATS TT edition.  The all new collection of squad level scenarios depicts the fierce fighting between Rommel's Afrika Korps and the British 8th Army in the vicinity of Ruweisat Ridge, firmly between the Mediterranean Sea and the Qattara Depression in Egypt, 1942.

The game includes all the color-die cut counters you need to play the scenarios, a brand new map, and a new set of scenarios that all play on the historical map. You will also find the usual Battlefield Walkaround and the latest ATS TT variant rules.
Ownership of the standard ATS rules, play aids and informational counters is required.

boardgame: ATS TT Cassino I: Gustav Graveyard
description:
The Advanced Tobruk System (ATS) returns to Snakeshead Ridge near Cassino, Italy for more intense squad level action on one of history's most rugged battlefields.  American and Commonwealth soldiers (including Free Poles, Free French and Gurkhas) battle the dug in Germans for the all important high ground of Points 569 and 593.

The game contains eight scenarios, a remastered, larger map of Snakeshead Ridge and a collection of ATS TT unit counters featuring brand new art that promises to be the most varied and colourful in any ATS game so far.  The special Battlefield Walkaround insert and ATS TT variant rules fill out this package.
Ownership of the standard ATS rules, play aids and informational counters is required.

This Advanced Tobruk System (ATS) release revisits Holland, 1944, and depicts squad level actions around the town of Oosterbeek, not far from Arnhem.  Players may pit British paratroopers against German SS (with new counter art) in a collection of scenarios played out on the two newly remastered maps.
Also included are the obligatory Battlefield Walkaround and ATS TT variant rules.
Ownership of standard ATS rules, play aids and informational counters are required.

boardgame: ATS TT: Battle of the Bracchi Hills
description:
An Advanced Tobruk System (ATS) game featuring squad level battles between American and German forces near Santa Maria Infante, Italia, 1944.  The rugged terrain, with several hills and a high ridge dominating the area was challenging enough for the US 88th Infantry Division, but the tenacious German 71st "Cloverleaf" Division made matters tougher still.

This game features a collection of scenarios depicting small unit action on a colourful map, along with all-new combat unit counters with new division-specific art.  Also included are the Battlefield Walkaround insert and ATS TT variant rules.
Ownership of standard ATS rules, play aids and informational counters is required.

This is the next module in the MMP "Panzerblitz Series" (PBS).

"Panzerblitz: East Front" will visit roughly the same ground as the original "PanzerBlitz", published by Avalon Hill in 1970.  Situations in East Front will span from 1939 to 1945, a broader swath than the original game, and will also include other combatant nations that fought in the titanic struggle for Eastern Europe during War World 2.

Unlike its predecessor, "Hill of Death", "East Front" will feature geomorphic mapboards, which will enable players to do Design-Your-Own (DYO) situations with the game, extending the usefulness of the components far beyond the 10-12 Situations in the box, and capturing the spirit of "PanzerBlitz" that affected a generation of wargamers and continues to do so to this day.

description:
Excerpt from the rulebook:

"Accusation is a card game in which players take on the roles of countries engaged in world events. Players use their country’s assets: military, intelligence, economy, diplomacy, and research to influence the outcome of world events and gain victory points to achieve world domination."

Players simultaneously and anonymously enact "world events" in the form of cards. They then bluff and use different abilities (each costing different sorts of resources) in order to further their agendas and gain victory points while trying to avoid bad reputation and resource loss, both of which make it more difficult to advance one's own agendas as well as accuse others of other actions.

The fast paced, primarily concurrent nature of Accusation makes it a high-intensity game. The only part of a turn in which any waiting occurs is during the turn-based "Accusation Phase" wherein players take turns attempting to call out others for playing cards.

Accusation is modern-themed and comes with 45 unique event cards, but it is designed to be highly extensible. New mechanics, new events, theme elements such as Cold War politics, and many other advances can (and will) be implemented in expansions.
