description:
The Red Queen is a card game pitting a team of 2 to 5 players against the infamous Queen of Hearts, a character as unpredictable as Lewis Caroll described in Alice's Adventures in Wonderland. Using deduction to work out how best to advance their plans, the team must assemble an army of soldiers to challenge the Red Queen.

In The Red Queen, the player cards are facing outwards in such a manner that all players except the one holding them will know what they are. Each round players will be able to give clues to one another to lift part of the mystery surrounding the pool of cards they collectively possess, build their army, or use special roles actions to help them in this endeavour.

... meanwhile the evil Red Queen will continually interfere with the team using a set of treacherous actions in the hope to make them lose 'heart', literraly, the currency representing the team's morale in the game. Her own army will also grow throughout the game, putting the team under pressure to raise their own as efficiently as possible.

The game features a strong thematic element and role cards based on characters directly inspired by Lewis Caroll's work. Alice herself takes on a key role too, unsurprisingly, but as an outsider will be of equal support to both sides and it is up to the players to make the most of her abilities.

In Fühlzwerge, players need to put fingers on their eyes in order to find the right items fast.

To set up the game, place all twenty cardboard tokens into the bag; these tokens are shaped to resemble the items depicted on them: an elephant, a ship, a wheel, a tree, and so on. Each player receives an equal number of cards from the deck, with each card showing one of these twenty items. The youngest player takes the dwarf die.

When the game starts, everyone but the youngest player shoves one hand into the bag at the same time and tries to grab items depicted on the cards in front of them. If a player draws a token matching such a card, great! Place the item on that card. If he instead draws a token matching a card in front of another player, well, he just helped that player get one step closer to victory as he must place the token of the card anyway. The player with the die rolls it repeatedly, possibly needing to jump up then sit back down, possibly needing to switch hands. As soon as she rolls her second dwarf, she passes the die to the closest player clockwise, who must then start rolling while she tries to fish tokens from the bag.

As soon as someone has tokens on all of her cards, she wins the game! (Players can also play without the die, with everyone fishing for tokens at the same time.)

boardgame: Conquest of Planet Earth: 'The Red Menace' Supplement
description:
The Red Menace are masters of disguise and manipulation!  Tentacled monsters that hide in the shadows and mislead with illusions and hallucinations, the Red Menace can be anywhere or anyone.  With panic and paranoia amongst the already frightened humans, they now swell the ranks of fanatical doomsday cults in an attempt to flush out this new hidden threat!  Because everyone knows, mobs with pitchforks and torches always get the job done.

This new expansion was initially only available at conventions where Flying Frog has a presence (it is now available through their web shop).  It includes new Events, Space Stuff, and Resistance as well as the new alien - The Red Menace.

Odysseo is a chance and strategy game. The goal is to move your checkers from a starting point to one of the available exits (the opponents' starting points).

During your turn, rolls the dice, then moves one of your checker by the corresponding number of cases. Whenever possible introduce a new checker on the board.
Checkers movement is limited by simple rules: 1) moving back and forth during the same turn is also prohibited; 2) moving through other checkers is not permitted; 3) finishing the movement on an opponent's checkers removes it from the game; 4) finishing the movement on a blue case removes your checker from the game.
Also, having one of your checkers being removed while standing on one of the white cases surrounding your starting point allows you to recover the removed checker.

The game ends when there is only one player with checkers left on the board and scoring is done as such: 1) saved checkers: 2pts each; 2) checkers on the board: 1 pt each; 3) removed checkers: 0 pt each.

The length of the game can be shortened by starting with fewer checkers.

From the box:

"Story Smash-Up is the laugh-out-loud game where movie worlds collide. You create sentences using word cards that feature your favorite scenes. The more you mix 'em, the funnier the sentence!

"Create sentences by playing cards ... 
Noun (an alien) + Verb (kissed the prince) + Fragment (on a turtle shell) + Fill in the Blank (and made Mom jealous) = 4 points.
Score 1 point for each card played! At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins!

"Contents: 112 Cards, 10 Tokens, Instructions. "

This game uses story elements from 4 Disney & Pixar films: Cars, Finding Nemo, Toy Story, and Snow White.  The basic idea could be used with many other stories of course.

Reaction game for large groups.

All players, except the dealer, are tapping on the table with their fingers. If the dealer calls a command, which can be a color or symbol, all players should lay their hand on the right card as soon as possible. But beware, a command is not valid if the leader does not say "command". If you make a mistake or are too late, you get a penalty point. Who will be the last man standing?

The simple rules ensure that everyone can participate directly.

Commando Pinkelen is based on a public domain game with the same name.

It is the year 2025 and the entire world has fallen under the reign of a group of super villains known only as "The Collective". People are oppressed and the world has fallen deep into darkness with little hope of even a glimpse of light. In this dark world, a group of superheroes has risen to combat the villains and retake the world from their grasp.

Each mission is deadly, as both villains and superheroes must enter combat, defend themselves, and collect the previous element "levinium" or face destruction. Levinium is the heroes's hope of taking down the villains.

Reign of Evil is a print-and-play game in the genre of superheroes and supervillians. The game uses a card mechanic, and no dice, in order to perform combat. It plays quickly with minimal down time for players. Players move figures around the board to collect levinium until all wound markers are exhausted, until the deck runs out, or all levinium pieces are gone. Alternatively, players can play timed games. On their turns, players draw a hand of cards that contain a mix of Move and Action cards. Using these cards, players move around the board and take various actions, such as combat or collecting levinium.

A fantasy expansion for chess, adding dice, cards, tokens, and treasure chests.

Players receive Mana tokens that may be traded to activate special abilities, cast spells, or upgrade units.

Treasure chests, scattered across the field, contain weapons, armor, potions, and accessories to boost individual pieces.

Each piece is assigned a die value (D4, D6, D8, etc.) for combat, as well as three special abilities.  Pawns dig up treasure.  Bishops cast healing and other protective spells.  Queens send lightning bolts across the playing field.  Kings inspire nearby units, and so forth.

Winning conditions are similar to traditional Chess, except that the king must be defeated in combat.  Check and Check Mate simply offer bonuses to defeating the King.

Epic Chess preserves the sound strategic elements of traditional Chess, while adding many new elements for Chess players of any level to enjoy!

Lost Legends: Demon Hunter, a mini-expansion for Lost Legends, adds a new hero to the base game that a player can choose to be. The Demon Hunter's special ability allows him to add 1 damage of Chaos to his attack each time that he fights a humanoid.

boardgame: Lost Legends: Forest of the Damned (Queenie 2)
description:
Lost Legends: Forest of the Damned, a mini-expansion for Lost Legends, consists of a new Level 1 monster deck that replaces the "Ruins" monster deck from the base game. It contains six animals, five humanoids, five undead and four dragons.

The rules are explained in German and English, but the cards contain only English text.
A Space Invaders-themed Jenga set with a new type of game play. As you remove blocks you're also trying to move your Invader down the tower, so you can kill the puny humans.

Caesar campaign's in Britannia: 55 & 54BC

THE GAME PLAY

To win, Caesar has to score points. He gains points when:
- He explores unknown regions
- He submits British tribes
- He returns in Gaul with his legions British hostages and booty

To win the Celtic has to prevent Caesar from marking points.

The first expedition lasts one month and Caesar has only two legions. It's very difficult for him to disembark.
The second expedition lasts two months. Caesar has 5 legions and the famous Gallic cavalry.

Actions in the game
A player takes an action with a chief, and then the other player takes an action too.
- Move
- Recruit (only for the Tribes, random units)
- Ravage (only for the Romans)
- Play a Stratagem (special real historical action)
- Reinforcement
- submit a tribe (for the roman player)
- Caesar special actions

Bounty
- it can boost recruitment and reinforcement
- you gain it with battle victory and ravage

Battle
- 4 tactical choices (units in first line can make critical hit but are the first to die)
- the best general has rerolls
- fire phase
- 2 melee phases

Specials
- Chariot actions: flee, dismount, special attack...
- bretons counters are hidden (not the number but the unity)

Available with French rules, but English rules can be downloaded from Vae Victis website.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Slaughterama, you are a vigilante hero who sets out to rid the land of evil, while also acting as clerk of your very own medieval store called a Bazaar. Traverse a unique board made up of tiles and fight a variety of demented foes. Once defeated, rob them of their treasures and use them to buy weapons and magic at opponent’s shops. There are also traps to avoid, the risk of becoming a zombie, snipers to watch out for, & magic portals that open up as the game progresses. This is a multi-player game, in fact, the more people playing...the more insane it becomes!

Slaughterama is a new, innovative table top game that is easy to learn yet quite challenging to win. There’s a good balance between luck and strategy, and the gameplay is fun NOT frustrating. With a variety of game mechanics at work, Slaughterama offers an engaging experience that is unlike anything else on the market today.

Amount of Players: 4 players (more by adding future deckpacks)

Object: Kill all 10 of the monsters in your deck before your opponents kill the ones in theirs.

Setting: A “medievalish” alternate universe where anarchy & chaos go for breakfast.

Duration: Customizable.  A normal 4 player game on average takes about 90 minutes.

Game Mechanics: Dice rolling, pawn moving, simple RPG fighting system, card playing, buying/selling goods.

Rawen's land has been a place of war for hundreds of years. Twelve empires, twelve powerful gods — all longing for the same thing: limitless might.

Everything started with a Cataclysm and the appearance of the New Ones, who ruthlessly squelched the Old Ones — except for those who escaped into other planes of existence, specifically four parts of the world that managed to survive the catastrophe: the Big Continent, also known as Askalar where most of the empires lay; to the North, Iceland, the Isle of the Mages; in the south, Fireland, the home of Demons; and in Oceania, Bale, the Life Tree's territory.

The fight has not ended after the New Ones victory. The balance of forces varies, and it takes only a little to break the fragile balance. Fellowships are made, then breached in moments, but the stakes are much bigger than the power over Rawen. The New Ones fight not just for victory; they are seeking themselves. A secret part of their essence, which they can hardly remember. They were born on another world, which they gave up in exchange for survival. In the depths of their minds, a name pulsates about which neither their new dependents nor The Old Ones might know: Earth. The War of the Gods started in the far past of Rawen, but it affects the future of the whole universe.

In the Rawen Trading Card Game, the Gods of Rawen wield their power through their Warlords. As they expand their territories, the armies of the opposing Gods frequently battle one another. Your goal is to defeat the enemy warlord with the help of fighting units, heroes, and summoned spells. Successful attacks by your army weaken your enemies, and thus weaken their God, too. If the enemy warlord ever has as many wounds as health points, the warlord dies and you win the battle.

In game terms, each player chooses a warlord and a class, then creates a deck of at least 45 cards, with the warlord card, class card and primeval energy cards not counting toward this total. The game is turn-based, and in the main phase of your turn, you may act in any order, placing cards from your hand onto the battlefield, attacking with your units, activating a special power, and so on.

The young magicians are back in Der geheimnisvolle Spiegel (The Mysterious Mirror), exploring a house and seeing what they can find when they look in the mirror. Even better, they want to get their fellow magicians to guess the shapes of what they draw in the mirror!

To set up, players each take a magician tile and slide it into the bottom of one of the tile ladders; they also shuffle the 46 "magic symbol" tiles, each of which shows a line drawing of a familiar item, e.g., candle, butterfly, arrow, spider's web, etc.

The starting player draws a tile, takes the magic wand, and "draws" the image seen on this tile onto the back of the large "mysterious mirror" with the light of the wand. The other players try to guess the image drawn; the first player to do so gets to draw with the wand next, while the previous drawer places that tile into the bottom of her tile ladder. Players keep taking turns this way until someone pops her magician tile out of the top of the ladder, winning the game.

With two players, Der geheimnisvolle Spiegel is a co-operative game, with players taking turns drawing an image that the other player must guess. If a player draws three times and her fellow magician still can't guess, then the tile is placed into a third tile ladder. If this ladder fills before one of the magician's ladders, then they both lose the game.
Gladiatori: The Dog is a new miniature for the Gladiatori board game. This miniature allows Animal variant in the game.
Gladiatori: The Bear is a new miniature for the Gladiatori board game. This miniature allows Animal variant in the game.

The game is a draughts or checkers game for a 3-D board of the type popularized by the TV and film franchise Star Trek. The rules for that game including how the board works can be found at the following link on Chess Variants:

http://www.chessvariants.org/3d.dir/startrek.html

Play begins with black pieces on all dark squares of the lowest fixed board and similarly on the two movable boards which start adjacent to it. To move a movable board, it must be empty at the time but with that proviso one can on a turn either move a movable exactly as in 3-D chess (explained below for clarity) or move a piece. The rules are exactly as in standard draughts (checkers) otherwise.

A big difference is that the movable board which start with white pieces begin BELOW, not above, the upper fixed board and movable boards start away from the player.

How the board works:
3 checkered 4x4 boards with a white square on the near right corner for either player are in fixed positions at levels 2, 4 and 6. The lateral edges are aligned but the three fixed boards are staggered so that the nearer two rows of the middle fixed board (at level 4) exactly overlap the farther two rows of the fixed board nearer to either player. The fixed board on which the black player has pieces starting is at level 2 and thus lower than the middle board. Similarly the fixed board on which white pieces start are at level 6 and thus above the middle fixed board.

4 movable checkered 2x2 boards with a white space in the nearer right corner are also included. Two of these boards start at level 3 with one corner each overlapping either of the two corners of the fixed board at level 2 farther away from the black player so that the other 3 spaces of the movable boards are not over that same fixed board; these two movable boards start filled with black pieces. Similarly 2 movable board start at level 5 filled with white pieces, and these board start with one corner overlapping either of the two corners of the fixed board at level 6 farther away from the white player. The other three squares are not under that same fixed board.

Levels 1 and 7 start empty. A variant of the game makes all the board fixed in their initial positions. When movement of movable board is allowed, an empty movable board can be moved up or down 2 levels (but not above level 7 or below level 1) in which case it switches between above or below the same corner of a given fixed board. Without changing level, the movable board can be moved to a position above or below the other corner along the same edge of a fixed board as it already occupies. So a movable board at level 7 above the back right corner of the fixed board at level 6 can if empty be moved to above the back left or front right corner of the board at level 6, assuming of course no other movable board is already there.

Spaces directly above or below each other will have the same color.

Pieces' moves general conventional draughts moves to 3-D as follows. One looks at the board from above and flattens it. If the piece could move onto a space in this flattened version of the board, it can move onto it so long as a piece does not skip a level. Namely, in a non-capturing move, one cannot move directly from level 4 to level 6 and one can only do so in a capturing move if jumping a piece on a board at level 5.

What playing is like:
It sounds harder than it is but does take some getting used to. The board geometry makes things interesting.

Sink the Titanic is a 1-2 player board game that pits you, the icebergs, against the evil Titanic. You must stop the Titanic from reaching its destination, but be careful, the captain isn't all there.

When playing a solo game, roll the two dice to determine the Titanic's speed and direction, then move each of your icebergs one space. In a two-player game, the player controlling the Titanic rolls the speed die, then determines which direction to move; the iceberg player then moves each of his icebergs one space.

The icebergs win if one of them moves across the Titanic's bow or if the Titanic runs through an iceberg.
family: History: The Sinking of the Titanic

description: A tin box combining two classic family games. Scrabble includes a split quad-fold full size board, 100 full size wooden tiles, 4 wooden tile racks, instructions & plastic tile bag. Yahtzee contains a small dice cup, 5 slightly smaller than standard 6 sided dice, 8 plastic chips, Yahtzee scoring pad and instructions.

The space fleet of the famous Ganuuk trader is for sale. 13 glorious spaceships that any space trader would be happy to put their hands on, and now you have a chance. Each ship is worth worth some victory points or will help you in acquiring other spaceships. The space trader that will earn the most victory points from the new Ganuuk spaceships will be the winner.

Micro Ships is a micro game with 13 spaceship cards, 1 resource value card and 20 resource markers (5 markers in 4 colors).

Each turn every player secretly selects his bidding resources. Simultaneously the players reveal their bid. The highest bid wins the spaceship.
Each space ship gives bidding bonus to bidding based on:

     Resource bid
     Spaceship color
     To a specific spaceship


Once a bidding is done, value of the resources is changed based on the winning bidding resources.

The game is played in 12 rounds and the winner is the player who's ships are worth the most victory points in end of the game.

Cherry Bomb is a single player zilch (or also known as “Dice 10,000”) game of probability with
health and a bonus points system added.
You will need:
. 6 dice.
. Dice cup.
. A paper (Or an empty excel sheet) pad for keeping score.
. “Zilch” scoring chart.

The goal is to get to a certain point amount without running out of health.

A new village tile for the game Keyflower. The Storyteller tile is available exclusively through Spielerei magazine edition number 101 (October 2013).

If this tile is not upgraded, a player places a worker on this tile and then takes either a worker or a skill tile, if available.  If this tile has been upgraded, a player places a worker on this tile then takes a worker and a skill tile, if available.

Lights, Camera, Action! is the hip and hilarious game that brings friends and family together for entertaining and comical fun! During each scene, players are challenged to read the short scripts while acting out their outrageous "TWIST Cards"! (ex: you are a sumo wrestler or you are a ballerina)

Try not to burst out laughing or miss your lines so you don't get "CUT" by the director! The player who gets the least amount of cuts wins the game! For ages 8 and up.

Includes:
    1 Authentic Movie Clapboard
    2 Script Books with 100 Original Skits
    100 Outrageous Twist Cards
    100 Script Pick Cards
    Board Game with 8 Pawns

Quest for Destiny is a card-laying game and characters in the game are based on medieval fantasy.

Players start with four Cards (either Spell or Area Cards) and control a Character Card. The Characters will start at the corner of a 7x7 imaginary grid. The aim of the game is to lay Area Cards to build a path and move towards the Objective Card in the middle. The first player who reaches the Objective Card wins the game.

During each player's turn, he has three actions. Firstly, he can play either an Area or Spell Card; or discard and draw a new card. Area Cards allow you to progress towards the middle. Spell Cards aid your progression or hinder your opponents' progression. Secondly, he can move his Character Card into an adjacent unoccupied Area if an Area card is played. Lastly, he draws cards back to a hand size of four.

Be the first to get released from the Infamous Dartmoor Prison. Shrouded in mist and mystery! A game of twists and barbed wire turns.

THE GUV'NOR! You play rival prison gangs; the Landing Mob, the Kitchen Gang, the Gym Boyz, the Garden Crew, the Laundry Syndicate and the Library Club

Win the game by earning 20 parole points and be the first to get released!!

The game is designed by people who know what it's like, so the game has an authenticity beyond the traditional escape scenario!. In this game it's a bad move when a gang member escapes, because it weakens the gang left inside!

Players compete to gain territory across the prison map.  Winners place their cards in one of the vacant cells in the block.  The block makes a decision by a majority vote about who is to be The GUV"NOR.  Unless one player has more than half the cells deals need to be done between players. Players then move along the track (called the perimeter wall) and where they land decides what actions take place.  Actions can change the number of cards in the block, place or remove trouble cards on the prison map, or require the block to make decisions.  Decisions will be determined by both the cards in the block at the time and the relationship  between the players.

This game enables all members of the family to play as it provides lots of times when "I win you lose" scenarios to arise.

Simple rules means they are easily mastered and that enables more sophisticated game play as players try to use the rules to get ahead.

Further rules including challenging the leadership of the GUV'NOR - making  regular decisions about who to back and what parole points to deal with  and lockdown - which gives the current GUV'NOR power to make the decisions the block did, including being ale to win a leadership challenge whatever the number of cards in the block raise tension as players try to find away to either stay in control or wrestle it away from others without the wrong rivals taking control.

If no one player dominates the Block then deals have to be done between players to form packs. But trust no one. Each turn players roll a dice and move around the board along the perimiter wall as they serve their sentence and have to make decisions about who they support. Decisions are determined by the number of gang member cards they have in The Block. Each turn can change the number of cards a player has and so alter their influence. Players have to manoeuvre themselves through their sentence until one player has gained 20 parole points. Points are lost or gained by getting caught by the staff, taking control of the Block, time served and from doing deals with other players to get their support.

This leads to a fast paced game, players are constantly changing sides, working out who best to support, and not getting caught by the staff. Watch as the  staff increase security, send you to solitary and threatens lockdown!   It is an exhausting game of wit, strategy, coercion and survival that brings out the best and the worst in participants! In the end you don't just win you probably get rehabilitated!

The rules are designed to be interdependent, hence the block makes decisions that effect the level of security.  At both extremes, ie too high or too low the scale has a trouble space.  If the scale is on trouble and all of the trouble cards are placed on the map then trouble starts.  this is a spontaneous battle for territory that lasts 4 turns and enables a potentially dramatic change in who runs the block.  This rule, like many others provides an exciting tension where players can predict what could happen next (within the dice roll) to change who is winning or what opportunities were lost to defeat who is currently in charge.

The game is very visual, so it matters where the players pieces are along the perimeter wall, how many cards are held in the block, what bonus cards are in solitary, whether the prison is in lockdown or not, and how many trouble cards there are on the map.

A game where your best strategy is paranoia!  Take brinkmanship to a nuclear level and control the World's most secret State!

The game is played in a fictional future North Korea. Play the leader of your own faction - THE ARMY, NAVY, AIRFORCE, BIG BUSINESS, THE PARTY AND THE FAMILY. Win by getting BRINKMANSHIP points, which reflect how good you are at manipulating your opponents and surviving as you raise the levels of international tension to nuclear levels. Win by getting to 20 points without blowing up the World

The Armed Forces, Big Business, The Party and the Family compete to fill the Central Committee with their supporters. The player who runs the Central Committee is The Leader!  There can only ever be one Leader, so it is importnat to understand the rules of the game and use them to your advantage! If no one player dominates the Committee then deals have to be done. Each turn players move around the board along the demilitarised zone which runs along the perimeter of the board and have to make decisions. Decisions are determined by the number of cards they have in The Central Committee. Each turn can change the number of cards a player has and so alter their influence. Players have to manoeuvre themselves through the politics until one player has gained 20 brinkmanship points. Points are gained by taking control of the Committee, time as Leader and from doing deals with other players to get their support.

At the same time the committee decides the level of international tension.  As public parades whip up popular fervour and brinkmanship is taken to a nuclear level so the opportunities for players to decide how to run the World's most secret State determine who ends up winning the game and who gets manipulated.

This leads to a fast paced game, players are constantly changing sides, working out who best to support, and not getting caught out in political exile. It is an exhausting game of wit, strategy, coercion and survival that brings out the best and the worst in participants! In the end you don't just win you have an great evening's entertainment!

Each space on the demilitarised zone offers the opportunity for the composition of central committee to change as players are beset with show trials, parades, rocket launches, 6 party peace talks and nuclear tests!

Past deals, double deals, double cross and you see where the strategy starts. Each player's strategy will be determined by their own strength of character, belief in their own destiny, how well they can manipulate others to support them and how they make best use of the opportunities as they arise.

As the game evolves so moments of growing tension occur as the population get ever more excited with increasing parades across the the Country, international tension nears extreme levels, infighting means there are no clear alliances and the date of the next central committee election approaches, only to be disrupted by a spontaneous war game! and rocket launch. Leadership crisis and mayhem. But then players only have themselves to blame as what happens is a result of the choices players make in the central committee. Ultimately players have the choice to create a peace agreement where the right strategies are promoted rather than a dash to the winning line! It is interesting to see how as a player nears the winning line that choice becomes more challenging!

As they say - Your best strategy is paranoia, take brinkmanship to a nuclear level and tale control of the Worlds most secret State!

Tonooo is an abstract game; the aim of the game is to be the first to have four small cylinders of different colours in front of you.  
Each player in his turn : 
 -Either draws a small cylinder from the bag and places it in one of the 4 big cylinders 
 -Or he picks up one of the four big cylinders and takes all the small cylinders placed in it.

Each time there are at least 2 identical small cylinders, the players keeps one of them in front of him.
      Each time there are not 2 identical small cylinders, the player is penalized  and replaces one of the small cylinders in front of him in the bag.
      Each time there are no small cylinders left, the player is eliminated.
There are also the uncoloured small cylinders (jokers)...
Expansion for The Agents. Adds 16 new agent cards to the base game.
Expansion for The Agents. Adds 12 mission cards to the base game.
Expansion for The Agents. Adds 16 new agent cards to the base game.
Expansion for The Agents. Adds 20 new agent cards to the base game.
Expansion for The Agents. Adds 18 new agent cards to the base game.

boardgame: The Martin Luther King Jr. Game
description:
The game is labeled as suitable for ages 6 and up, and has the King estate´s approval. The Martin Luther King Jr. Centre gets royalties.

It is an educational game. They can also switch places, take extra turns if they want to.The players have to choose if they want to marry Coretta Scott, meet President Kennedy or meet Ghandhi in India.

You spin the spinner, then you can move around the 63-space board, which includes historical milestones like the birth of King, his "I have a dream speech" , his assasination or his national holiday.
Some squares make the players to draw a quote card, then the quote from that card has to be read.

Roma Celer is a print-and-play express game of political strategy based on The Republic of Rome. The players must cooperate to keep the Roman Republic alive while struggling for dominance within the Roman Senate.

In Roma Celer, the players share a pool of dice which are used to resolve actions; each player gets to allocate some of the dice, but may be forced to divide them between actions beneficial to the player's faction and actions necessary for the survival of the Republic. The players also distribute among themselves the five high offices of the Republic, and use the corresponding powers to their advantage as well as to hinder the other factions.

In the end, the faction that rebels to the Senate and wins the resulting civil war, or gains the allegiance of the majority of the Roman senators, wins the game, but only if the Republic is not bankrupt and the Roman people are content.

--

Roma Celer uses 54 poker-sized cards and 29 half-sized cards; the players must supply 60-80 euro-cubes or similar objects to be used as markers, and 16-18 six-sided dice. The print-and-play components include counters that can be used instead of the cubes and to reduce the number of physical dice required.

Roma Celer was originally an entry in the 2013 "In-A-Tin" / "Express" Print-and-Play Design Contest.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Each year, to celebrate the union of the five Duchies and the election of the first Sovereign Queen, the royal family holds a grand reception to renew their oath of justice and unity to the hereditary Dukes. This event is called The Queen's Jubilee, and the highlight of the evening is The Fealty Ceremony: a re-enactment of that long-ago day when the Queen Elect gave a gift to each of the Dukes as a token of thanks for coming together to forge a kingdom based on peace and loyalty.

With the Queen's Jubilee only a few weeks away, King Sedgewickson and his lovely wife, Queen Felicity, have compiled a list of possible gifts for this year's Fealty Ceremony and posted it in the castle's plaza. Craftsmen and women from all over the kingdom are now hard at work, striving to be the first to craft five items from the list and win the honor of standing side-by-side with the royals as the gifts are presented to the five Dukes at The Queen's Jubilee.

King's Forge: Queen's Jubilee is an additional set of component's for the King's Forge board game.  It consists of a deck of 30 alternate craft cards that can be used in place of the deck of craft cards in the base game to enable a faster-playing game experience.  This expansion also includes 3 Gather Cards as well as an additional Conjurer Gather Card with 4 special clear dice that can act as a "limited wild" during the game.

Gather Cards:

     The bottom of the Festival card allows for you to reroll two dice.
     The Merchant card offers 4 choices, but you can only pick one
     Both options for the Monstery card allow you to skip the storage card and put the new dice into your crafting supply for the current round.
     The top of Gypsy Camp drives a hard bargain for two immediate Wood, but the bottom lets you peer into the future and pre-roll some of your dice.
     Conjurer - Since most Conjurers need resources to work on their spells, the top of CONJURER offers a relatively easy way to trade in some extra dice for a magic die.  The bottom half of CONJURER invokes 4 clear dice that will come with the card – sort of a free magical charm in honor of the Queen's Jubilee celebration. When chosen from the Gather line, all players get a clear die to use as a "limited wild". It has to match the color of one of the dice you roll during crafting and you have to return the clear die at the end of the round. The person who selects CONJURER also gets a +2 bonus that the other players do not get. CONJURER will be a great card to take when you know one or more of your opponents won't be crafting that round because they will not benefit from the Conjurer's die.



Note: The 2020 edition (containing King's Forge: Glassworks Plus Pack) can be found here: King's Forge: Queen's Jubilee

Breyer-Opoly is a version of Monopoly published by Late for the Sky, a company that specializes in Monopoly clones. The game features model horses made by Breyer. All the spaces have images of model horses and all the deeds and cards have the names of horse breeds on them.

Players roll dice to determine how far they move. Landing on a space gives the player the option of buying the property. When a player collects all the properties in one color group, the properties can be improved by building. When players land on a space owned by another player they must pay rent. Rent is determined by the location of the property and the number of improvements on the space. Some spaces instruct a player to draw a card which can be beneficial or harmful to the player. Players must avoid going bankrupt to stay in the game. The winner is the last player left in the game.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: Time: A Game About People, Places & Events Since 1923
description:
From the box: 
The Time Magazine Game "A Game About People, Places & Events Since 1923"

The object of the game is build the biggest and best issue of his own TIME Magazine.

Each player attempts to fill in a "Make-up Panel" with game cards that have different values.  Players also answer trivial style questions to earn advertising points.  
The winner at the end of the game is the player with the TIME magazine with the most points.

Outside These City Walls is a short story in puzzle form.

In this print-and-play card game, the player takes on the role of two jewel thieves, Amy-Bax and Zeke, and attempts to flee from one end of the city to the other to the safety beyond.

The city is laid at as three columns of eight cards. Each thief only moves onto cards of their own colour, which display the internal monologues from the character. As the thief leaves their card, the card is flipped, showing the opposite thief's colour and monologue. Some locations become locked and cannot flip, while other locations can be collected for their valuables. The game ends if both thieves can make it to the other end of the city, and an ending is determined by the number of cards collected throughout the thieves' escape.

Outside These City Walls is an experimentation in card game narrative, and the story told throughout play is of long-forgotten places and unrequited love. Even if the player resolves the puzzle and escapes with both thieves, their actions throughout the escape will determine the destiny of the two ill-fated nightrunners.

On a remote island, labeled "cursed" to ward off curious eyes, six exemplary people have been summoned against their will to fight against each other in the colosseum. None may leave until a Champion is chosen.

In Spellbound Champions you take the part of one of these six characters to compete as:

Vogak, the Steadfast Orc Warrior
Aurora, the Enchanting Human Magi
Cyrid, the Auspicious Goblin Gambler
Lavanna, the Agile Elf Ranger
Rosie, the Entertaining Halfing Bard
Baeddan, the Virtuous Dwarf Cleric

In a kill or be killed environment, you must push yourself to the limits, or hope you're equipped enough to survive a fight to the death.

"Spellbound Champions" is a player versus player role-playing game for 2-6 players that takes about an hour to play. Try Free-For-All, teams, or take on your nemesis 1 on 1.

Each character has a pre-constructed deck of 60 cards they must use to defeat their opponents. 3 types of action cards are in place: Whenever, Combat, and Ability. Abilities may only be used on your turn, while Whenever cards have no limit on them. Combat cards have a limit of 1 card on your turn.

Last player alive is the winner, unless on a team, in which the team is the winner.

From the Developer/Publisher:  
You want to rule the world. You have plans to do just that. Execute your plans, and maybe some pesky Rebels, before anyone else does. In the end, they will call you "Emperor".

Emperor of the World is a casual game of reaching your goals before anyone else can. The rules are simple. Roll 2 dice. Turn over a token of one of the numbers rolled. If, at the end of your turn, the tokens listed on your Imperial Plans cards are light-side up, you score that card. First person to score two Imperial Goals cards is the Emperor. Just be wary of those pesky Rebels that show up to ruin your plans anytime you roll a 12.

boardgame: Micro Machines Car Chase Board Game
description:
Catch the traffic lights and chase your opponents off the track and out of the game. Ages 6 and over.

Includes card playing pieces but use your own Micro Machines vehicles for added realism.

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: PAX Prime 2013 Promo Pack
description: A total of 44 additional cards for Cards Against Humanity were released as promotional material during PAX Prime 2013. The cards were distributed in packs of 10 random cards. The card themes focus on video games, board games, sci-fi, and technology.

Skimish Sangin is a new set of skirmish rules designed specifically for the Afghan conflict. Playing at an individual figure level, players get to feel the intenseness of adrenaline fuelled modern warfare, while still dealing with the responsibility of command.

From a game preview;

"Skirmish Sangin isn’t an IGO UGO game; characters are activated in combat phases based on their BODY attribute. The BODY attribute of the character dictates which of the ten phases of a combat round a character is active in – the higher their BODY (BDY) attribute, the earlier a character’s combat phases occur."

The game is designed as a realistic narrative miniature conflict game based on a modern theatre of war.

As such weapons are realistic, wounds have consequences, and players are left with a realistic story to tell.

Partnering with Irrational Games, WizKids brings you BioShock Infinite Heroclix!

From the WizKids site:

BioShock: Infinite comes alive on your tabletop with this all-new HeroClix offering based on the blockbuster game! Booker DeWitt, the Boys of Silence, and more characters from the BioShock: Infinite universe are available to enhance your existing HeroClix experience, or relive the excitement of the BioShock: Infinite video game and play with these figures in a standalone HeroClix match!

BioShock Infinite Heroclix are sold in both a  6-figure Starter Set and a 24-figure Gravity Feed.

Malefic Curses, the first expansion for Wiz-War, adds a fifth player to the battle, bringing a new sector board and new portals with him. New schools of magic enter the playing field as well, inviting players to tap the fearful powers of Hexcraft, Necromancy, and Chaos. Hexcraft allows a player to etch powerful runes in the paths of the maze, trapping those who dare to cross them. Necromancy offers a host of death-dealing tactics, and the powers of Chaos grow increasingly powerful, although they remain unpredictable at best.

A theft at the museum! Even though the museum is crowded with visitors admiring paintings and sculptures of great artists, nobody notices that the exhibition's most famous work of art has been stolen by an unknown thief. A voice sounds from the loudspeakers — "Nobody leaves the building!" — and the player inspectors are suddenly on hand to question visitors and find important clues. An exciting pursuit for the culprit begins in Millionenruab. Who will catch him first?

Vulpes is the classical logical game created by Marek Łukaszewicz in 2013. This strategic game for two players is played on the board 8x9 squares.

The game is played by two players.

At the start of the game each player has nine pawns (hunters)  and one king (fox). The white chessmen go first. The first move is made by the white king, then a white pawn (two moves), then the black chessmen can make two similar moves.
                                                               
The aim of the game is to eliminate the opponent's king (fox) from the chessboard, or to block it so that it cannot move.

General rules of the game

It is not possible to jump over the opponent's chessmen. 
                                                               
King (fox) moves vertically, horizontally and diagonally at least two squares at a time. The opponent's king is captured when two kings are on the same line without a single pawn between them. When two kings are on adjoining squares capture is not possible (the king moves at least two squares at a time) and the king is blocked as if by a pawn.

Pawns (hunters)
The pawns (hunters) can move one square at a time in all directions. It is not possible to eliminate the pawns, so the initial number of 9 pawns for each player remains the same for the duration of the game. Pawns may only move onto an empty square.

The winner is the player who eliminates the opponent's king or who blocks it so that it cannot move.

Game description from the publisher:

Take control of powerful Jaegers and defend the people of Earth from the devastation of the Kaiju!

Under the shadow of the Kaiju attacks, the Jaegers and their pilots are a source of inspiration for the people and a symbol of hope. Secretly take control of Jaeger pilots and lead them to gain fame in the eyes of the people as you defend the cities of the world from the destruction caused by the Kaiju. But even in the heat of battle, there is always room for some friendly competition.

In the Pacific Rim: Shuffling the Deck card game, each player secretly controls characters from the movie that are piloting Jaegers. Each turn, a Kaiju attack card will be executed — awarding fame as the Jaegers repel the attack, causing the Jaegers to take damage or causing the Jaegers to lose fame if the Kaiju manage to beat them back. The players use movement cards to get their pilots into the best position for the current Kaiju attack. The players who gain the most fame will win the game when all of the Kaiju attack cards run out.

Players can guess other player's secret characters which will award them half of the fame that pilot has gained throughout the game. The player with the most fame wins!

Players take turns launching a metal ball with a sprint loaded rod up a ramp and into one of the holes beyond.  Each hole is labeled with point values ranging from 10 to 50.  After 10 shots by each player, the highest score win.  A perfect game is 500 points.  Players can play on teams or cooperatively to try to beat the high score.

The skill of the game is primarily in how far back you pull the launcher, and thus how much force is applied.  A little looseness in the launching mechanism also permits a small amount of additional control.

Obelisk

A strategic two-player board game

Components
- 7x7 grid board
- 48 Reversi/Othello pieces
- 1 white piece (the Obelisk)

Objective
Be the last player to make a valid move.

Key Term
"Adjacent" includes orthogonal and diagonal spaces.

Setup
1. Place the Obelisk in the board's center.
2. Choose the starting player.
3. Turns alternate.

Gameplay
On your turn:
1. Place a Reversi piece white-side up on an empty space, then
2. Move the Obelisk.

Placement Options
- Adjacent to the Obelisk, or
- In the Obelisk's line of sight

Obelisk Movement
- Moves like a chess queen (any distance, any direction).
- Must end on an empty space.
- If placed adjacent to Obelisk: Move away from the placed piece.
- If placed in line of sight: Move adjacent to the placed piece.

Movement Rules
- Can move over white pieces, flipping them to black.
- Cannot move over black pieces.

Game End
The game ends when a player cannot complete both actions on their turn. Their opponent wins.

Teocalli Aztec for the God House.

A 4 in a row with 4 pieces and no board.
 All 4 pieces are one sided and are made up of right-angled pieces in these color patterns, 2 (white, black, white) and 2 (black, white, black) pieces.

Play

One player is white the other black.

Decide who plays first then turns alternate.

On a turn,

- Select a piece and place it orthogonally adjacent to another piece already on the table.

- The game ends when a line (orthogonally or diagonally) of 4 pieces in your color is made.

If after playing all the pieces there is no winner do the following,

- Move a piece and place it orthogonally adjacent to another piece, play continues like this until you have a winner.
 Restrictions
 - You may not move a piece that would by its removal create two groups.

- You may not move the last piece played by your opponent.

- If by chance you create a 4 in a row for your opponent and yourself on the same turn, your opponent wins the game.

From the publisher's website:

Architects of War is proud to present the American Uncivil War, straight out of the pages of History AND the silver screens of Hollywood! Inspired by the films of John Ford and classics like Gone with the Wind, The Horse Soldiers, The Great Locomotive Chase, and Shenandoah, American Uncivil War captures the spirit of these films with miniature games staged on the fringes of the American Civil War. Spies, raiders, foraging parties, missions behind enemy lines, bushwhackers, deserters and a host of other scenarios can be played out. Its a role-playing skirmish game with figures overflowing with character and it puts a whole new spin on gaming this period.

The rules utilize a card activation system for initiative, command and control. Unit size ranges from single character models to platoons and even larger companies. Characters have special attributes like Influential, Dead-eye shot, Heroic, and Despicable, all of which bring to life the untold story of the American Uncivil War of both history and Hollywood myth.

So choose your side, raise your flag, and prepare for action and adventure in the War Between the States!

The Rulebook is 128 pages and comes with a 68 card activation deck. Full color throughout with 76 model scene pictures, actual civil war photos from the National Archive,  12 table/scenario maps, 12 scenarios including seven that can be linked and four historical stand alone scenarios.

The Rulebook includes Basic rules, advanced rules, pages of attributes for your characters, a complete guide to narrated games, and more.
Duel fantastique is a set of additional rules to allow the use of heroes, non humans (elves, ratmen, werewolves, ...), magical equipement or spells in the groups of fighters the players use.

Life's tough for goblins.  As if the klastra birds and grignak spiders weren't enough, there's also the constant possibility of having your house blown up by your neighbors (not always on purpose, it's just that most goblins are too curious for their own good).  Mix that with a goblin's natural packrat tendency and what do you get?  A lot of junk left behind in storage (a booming industry for the last 79 years), usually without an owner.

In Goblin Garage Sale, players are goblin entrepreneurs hoping to get lucky.  Gobo Storing Places Inc. is having a large blind auction of many of its storage garages.  However, players are allowed to see only part of the items in the garage. Using their specific knowledge, players bid their hard-earned monies against the others for the contents.  To the winner: the garage!  Hopefully, it will be filled with glorious junk that the player can add to his personal hoard (for the goblin with the greatest hoard has the greatest bragging rights). If you're lucky, you may even find more monies for future bids!  Just hope that the unfortunate former owner didn't hoard useless things, like soap, and pray that when you open your garage it isn't full of horrors, like kittens (how embarrassing) or even a grignak spider (run!).

Goblin Garage Sale is a game of bluffing and luck.

Players are siblings whose parents have accused them of wacky wrongdoings. To appease them, players must create stories out of lies and embellishments, making them crazier and crazier by placing ever-encreasingly valued lies on top of each other, until there are sufficient lies on the story to consider it completed, at which point it comes out of play and is scored at the end of the game. Some lies allow players to draw or place more cards, allowing for powerful action chains. Players are only allowed to keep seven cards in hand at the end of their turn. Other players have some ability to damage opponents' stories, preventing the most outrageous ones from completing.

As soon as a player has completed enough stories, or the deck runs out, the game ends. The top lies from completed stories are scored for their lie value, and any remaining lies and embellishments add or subtract points from each player's score, with the highest score winning.

The Sino-Soviet Union has been the first of the three bloc to develop the technology of helicopters. Since then the other bloc, mostly the Allies have followed. But the SSU still has a nice advance in this cutting edge technology.
Although the Soviets mostly use their helicopter for combat missions or to deploy troops, they also have transformed some to carry back wounded personnel to the safety of the rear lines. Thanks to these, the SSU has greatly increased the life expectancy of its soldiers.

The object of the game is to be the first player to cross the opponent's forest (made of a 4*4 grid of trees), by removing enough of his trees to create a path connecting the border line between the players with the far edge of the forest (the side closer to the player).

This is achieved by rolling a die with 6 differently coloured sides, and trying to guess (and later, remember) which of the opponent's trees fit the colour rolled. Each forest is made of 6 pairs of differently coloured trees (the 6 colours are the same as the different sides of the die), one 'wild' tree that counts as every colour, and 3 bear trees that cannot be removed from the board.

The first player to cross his opponent's forest (diagonals don't count!) wins the game.

boardgame: Temptation: The New Sale of The Century
description:
From the box:

Now viewers can be players. It's fast, bright, educational, competitive and fun.

-with Electronic lights and buzzers.

Players are ask general knowledge questions as they race to come up with the right answer. correct answers earns cash, incorrect answers gets no cash. After several points throughout the game, players are offered the chance to buy discounted merchandise using the cash they have won from previous rounds, The catch is that no player knows just how valuable the item is, only that it is offered at less than full value. after 15 questions, the player with the most cash can either buy a much more expensive prize with their winnings or keep their money in the hopes of winning again in order to be able to buy something better. After each person had a chance to emcee the game, the player who has the most cash and merchandise is the winner.

boardgame: Super Smash Bros. Melee Battle Cards
description:
Super Smash Bros. Melee Battle Cards is a 52 card game that came free in an issue of Official Nintendo Magazine.

Each player selects five cards from the character select pile. Next, each player randomly chooses one of their cards and places it in the middle. Turn over one of the stage cards to see which stage you'll be playing on.

Up moves beat down moves, down moves beat left/right moves and left/right moves beat up moves.

The player with the most cards after five rounds is the winner.

Special printing for "Stadt-Land-Spielt" (German parlour games day) 2013. Also included with Russian Railroads: Mini-Expansion (2014).

This expansion for Stone Age adds two new huts: casinos!

When you build a casino, you may throw 4 or 5 dice (depending on the hut) and get the total sum of your dice as victory points.

English Rules : Expansion for 2-5 players only playable with Stone Age! The two casinos are mixed with the other huts. Two stacks are thus higher by one hut. In order to purchase a casino, the player pays the two raw materials depicted. They then roll with 4 or 5 dice. The sum of these dice is immediately awarded as victory points. Attention! No tools may be used.

Special printing for "Stadt-Land-Spielt" (German parlour games day) 2013.

This expansion to Elfenland adds magic spells and sea monsters.

The sea monster is an obstacle on a river or a ferry route. The magic spell "double transport" allows for a second means of transport. "Change" lets you exchange two means of transport.

Contained in the 1999 edition of Elfengold.

Special printing for "Stadt-Land-Spielt" (German parlour games day) 2013.

This expansion to Zooloretto adds a tapir.

Use the normal rules for Zooloretto, except for one rule: the first player to fill his 6-space enclosure, takes the tapir enclosure from the middle of the table.
Once per game the owner of the tapir enclosure [in addition to his normal action before or after it] may pay 1 coin to exchange all pieces of a species from his zoo with all pieces of a species from another player's zoo.

A gambling game played during Colonial times in the US.  It plays with 2 standard dice and is similar to Craps.

The roller first rolls to establish their target number, rerolling until they get 5/6/7/8/9.  Then there is a special roll, on which there are extra instant-win and instant-loss values.  Then there is a roll to determine the hazard number (rerolling if the target number comes up).  Then the roller keeps rolling until they hit their target (and win) or the hazard (and lose).

During the special second roll, there are extra rules:
- if you roll your target number, you win
- if you roll 12, you win if your target number is 8
- if you roll 11, you win if your target number is 7
- if you roll 2 or 3, you lose

The odds are stacked solidly in the roller's favor.

"it's more than a game"

"A TRIBUTE TO

Forrest C. Shaklee, Sr.

1894-1985"

A roll and move game, with the space landed upon requiring the player to answer a categorized trivia question, (based on the space landed upon), with the questions all referencing Shaklee Naturals products and employee rules and regulations.

Kilimanjaro is a game that teaches about African and African-American history and traditions.  It is a basic roll-and-move game with spaces that cause you to draw and read cards that are either informational and earn you points automatically, or players must answer the question to receive 5 points.

At the beginning of the game you roll to determine your 'name' based on the day of the week (you get the appropriate male or female name for that particular day) OR you can choose the 7th day (since you cannot roll it) if you prefer.

You then progress down different tracks and read about or answer questions about Ghana. Some spaces give you points automatically, some tell you to draw a card, some do nothing, and some will tell you to go to other spaces on the board (generally further back) - however you win by getting the most points, NOT by finishing first, so this isn't necessarily bad.

At the end of your trek around the board, you come to the 'Self Reliance' square where you 'wish' on a star (choose one of 3) and then attempt to roll one of the two numbers on it (they have 1-6, 2-5 and 3-4 on them) If you roll correctly you collect the points and you are done. Otherwise you draw a card. After 2 players have landed on their wished star, the game ends. The player obtaining the most points wins the game.

Qwirkle Erweiterung 1 consists of two expansions for the abstract strategy game Qwirkle: Qwirkle Select and Qwirkle Connect. These expansions can be used individually or together.


     Qwirkle Select adds an extra element of strategy to Qwirkle with powerful star tokens that allow you to take a tile off the board and add it to your hand.



     Qwirkle Connect adds bonus point opportunities to Qwirkle. You will need a keen eye – and the right tiles – to get the bonuses and maximize your score.

(Not to be confused with the UK game show just simply called Blankety Blank even though the two formats are quite identical to one another.) is based on the formerly popular Network 10 hit game show, hosted by the late Graham Kennedy from 1977-1979 followed by Daryl Somers from 1985-1986 and Shane Bourne from 1996-1997 later on Nine Network. (for which in turn was adapted from the hit US Game Show 'Match Game').

to set-up
1. elect a compere (for best results, pick a witty friend)
2. Place panel wallet so that the opening is at the top.
3. Put stiffener board. Place Key Cards in front of the game cards, place one on each side in such a way that no word can be seen in the windows under the panelists.

Order of play
1.Compere selects 2 contestants - A or B.
2.Compere asks contestant A the question which appears on the 'Contestant A' side at the top of the panel wallet.
For Example: in game 1 (Tom Thumb lost his "BLANK" while riding on a yo-yo).
3. Contestant A substitutes a word for 'BLANK' in doing so, Contestant A tries to guess the answers given by the panel.
4.Having announced his/her guess the compere slowly (revealing only one answer at a time) removes the key card.
Contestant A now knows how many of the panel agrees with his choice and scores that number accordingly.
5. The procedure is repeated for Contestant B.
6. Should a tied score result - the following game is then played.
7. The winner is entitled to play Super Match.
8.Compere picks up Super Match card and asks the contestant to respond a word (e.g. for Game 1 use the word 'FACE'. For Game 2 use 'BABY' etc.)
9.Contestant may ask all others at the table for help. Prize money is paid dependent upon which response the contestant selects corresponding to the three previous selected answers, (e.g. Word 1 'FACE', If Contestant says 'FACE MASK' he/she wins $50.
10.If the contestant gives one of the three answers (i.e. Pack. Mask or Life, he/she is entitled to play for the Jackpot Match.
11.If none of the three words is selected, the game finishes and another contestant is selected to play against the winner.
(NOTE: The two number '9's' are actually listed on the instruction card.)

Jackpot Match
1.Compere gives both the contestants and himself a pad and pencil.
2.Compere reads out the word (e.g. Game 1 - he reads Comic).
3.Compere and contestant each secretly respond on paper (e.g. Comic Book or Comic Strip). If the answers are identical, the contestant wins 10x his/her winnings from the immediate preceding Super Match round.
4.The game is thus completed and a new contestant is selected to compete against the current champion.
(NOTE: After completing Game 1, remove question card from wallet and lay face up on the table - then place Game 2 when completed on top, and so on. When the wallet is empty, turn the whole set of questions upside down and place them all back in the wallet - Remember to replace the Key Cards.)

In Der Grüffelo: Alles wird gut!, alles won't be gut unless you can reach the gruffalo before the owl, snake and fox fully emerge from the forest and eat you up, you being a tasty little mouse.

To set up play, arrange the nine tiles in a 3x3 field with the gruffalo tile at the end of the dotted path on these tiles. Place the four tiles each for the owl, snake and fox next to these tiles. Players take turns rolling the die. On a 1-3, the player moves the mouse figure this many spaces forward; if you advance off of a tile, then flip it face down. If you roll an owl, snake and fox, flip one of these respective animal tiles face up. If all four tiles of an animal are face up and you roll the animal again, you must move the mouse backwards on the path. If you don't have a tile on which to move (since you've flipped it), then everyone loses. Reach the gruffalo space before this happens, however, and you all win!

From the Box:

"Are you a general knowledge buff in need of a new challenge? A champion darts who wants to spice up the game? Or maybe you're looking to win some big prizes? This Double bill of TV Game Shows is just what you need for an entertaining evening of game show fun Split into teams, throw some darts and try to win Bully's mystery prize or work out the clues to try and bin the bin. with a little bit of general knowledge and practical playing. you and your teammate could soon be winning big in this double game show compendium."

Game description from the publisher:

In Shadowrun: Hostile Takeover, players assume the role of megacorporations attempting to assert dominance over the city of Seattle.

Hostile Takeover is a game of intrigue, shifting alliances, and secretive schemes in the most famous futuristic megaplex of Shadowrun’s Sixth World. The most wealthy and influential megacorps of the city contend for dominance of Seattle, and they use shady dealings and deniable assets to wage a war in the shadows for supremacy.

Additional description from the Publisher:

The Year of Shadowrun gets an extension into 2014 as Catalyst will publish Shadowrun: Hostile Takeover, a Euro-style board game with a strong, immersive theme running through every aspect.

“Games such as Lords of Waterdeep have shown you can take elegant Eurogame mechanics and wed them with an immersive world to create a wonderful tabletop experience,” said Randall N. Bills, Managing Developer for Catalyst Game Labs. “Shadowrun is a universe that screams for a host of board games to expand player’s enjoyment of visiting the Sixth World often. Pairing up award-winning designers with Shadowrun is going to be a fantastic combination.”
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Micro Force is a (very) light game to promote Hasbro's range of super deformed "chibi" GI Joe miniatures. Each miniature is 28mm high and comes with a round stand. Players should toss the stands at each others miniatures and tip them all over.
Hasbro's main priority obviously was selling the miniatures and designing a solid game behind it came second. They didn't even add the usual dice, it's just throwing small discs at the opponent's GI Joes or Cobras. But hey! Aren't those miniatures super awesome!!!

So, great components but maybe not much of a "game", even when Hasbro says it is :)

Power Grid: Australia/Indian Subcontinent is a pair of expansions for Power Grid!

• Australia does not have a single connected power network. Only in the populated regions, mainly in the south east, are several cities connected to each other. Because of this, players may connect to any city for a connection cost that's generally higher than normal. Australia does not use uranium for its power production, but it does mine huge amounts of the material to sell to other countries. This offers a completely new game element, even for experienced Power Grid players!

• The Indian Subcontinent is always in danger of suffering huge power outages if the players increase their networks too quickly. Additionally, the players must buy their resources on a limited resource market, which does not always guarantee enough resources for all players.

Este suplemento incluirá:

- Más trasfondo acerca de cada una de las cuatro Facciones de 1650 (Antiguo Régimen, Nuevo Orden, Usurpadores y Mercenarios). Desde detalladas descripciones de sus territorios, plazas fuertes y gobiernos, hasta un completo análisis de sus fuerzas.

- El trasfondo y las reglas de todas las Personalidades de 1650, presentes y futuras.

- Las listas de las 2 cuadrillas de cada Facción de 1650 (haciendo un total de 8 cuadrillas).

- Listas alternativas, como los Picas Rotas, los más veteranos de entre los veteranos Morados Viejos. O las cuadrillas propias de cada Personalidad de 1650, como la Leva de Prisioneros dirigida por Degoyito, o la Embajada de El Embajador.

- Compendio de todos los Enclaves, Antagonistas, Cartas de Panoplia y Destreza, habidas y por haber.

- Reglas para el uso de vehículos y caballería.

- Reglas para la creación de tus propios peones.

- Sistema de Campañas, con reglas para la mejora de peones mediante la obtención de experiencia.


This supplement will include:

- More background for each of the four Factions of 1650 (Old Regime, New Order, Usurpers and Mercenaries). Detailed descriptions of their territories and government strongholds, even a complete analysis of their forces.

- The background and the rules of all the Personalities of 1650, present and future.

- 2 lists of bands for each faction of 1650 (making a total of 8 bands).

- Alternative lists, such as the Broken Pikes, the most veteran among veterans of the Old Purple, and rules for the bands of each Personality of 1650, for example the Levy of Prisoners, led by Degoyito, or the Embassy led by El Embajador.

- Compendium of all Enclaves, Antagonists, Panoply and Dexterity cards, past and future.

- Rules for the use of vehicles and horsemen.

- Rules for creating your own pawns.

- Campaign system, with rules for the improvement of pawns by obtaining experience.

Stinky Donkey (Burro Apestoso) is a fun cards game for 2 – 6 players, age 6 and up in English and Spanish. Packaged in a convenient pocket size box that you can take to a party, family gathering, camping, or just have fun with your friends and family.

Objective 
Get rid of your cards as fast as possible by removing all pairs from your hand with the help of command cards. Avoid the Stinky Donkey card. Once all pairs have been removed from all the players, the player left with the Stinky Donkey card will be marked as a Stinky Donkey (sticker).

Components 
49 Stinky Donkey cards, 23 Command cards, 120 Stinky Donkey stickers, 1 Rules document. 
Setup 
Open your box, start by separating stinky donkey cards (animal drawings with stinky donkey logo on the back) and command cards. Shuffle both decks. Place command cards face down in the middle of the table. Pass out all stinky donkey cards evenly to every player.

Starting The Game 
Each player must make pairs, remove them from their hand, and set them aside. The player on the right of the dealer goes first by picking a command card from the top of the command card stack and follows the command. 
The Second Turn And Beyond 
Going counterclockwise, each player must pick a command card and follow its command.

Winning And Losing The Game 
Once a player gets rid of all the cards on his/her hand, him/her is now safe from being a stinky donkey. And once all the players get rid of their cards, the player left with the Stinky Donkey card will be marked as a Stinky Donkey with a Stinky Donkey sticker “HEE- HAWWH!, HEE- HAWWH!”.

Additional Instructions 
Take the command cards and the Stinky Donkey out and play as a memory game.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Defiant Expansion Pack
description: The USS Defiant is a state of the art Federation escort ship equipped with major weapon and tech upgrades including quantum torpedoes, ablative armor and a Romulan cloaking device. The ship's captain, Benjamin Sisko, commanded the vessel in defense of Deep Space 9, and later during the massive battles that took place against the Dominion forces during the Dominion War.

description:
This is a promotional crafting card for King's Forge. It was provided as an exclusive card for backers of the 2013 King's Forge Kickstarter campaign.

Details:

Ring of the Lords (#95)
Metal 6
Gem 5

description: Mega Bloks Race Car Builder Game utilizes board and building game play requiring both strategy and luck. Players move throughout the city stopping at different locations to build their racecars. Be the first player to complete your car and reach the winner's circle to win.

Turn based tactics meets collectible card gaming in a Japanese RPG-style online game by the creators of Star Ocean & Valkyrie Profile

Take a collectible card game, add turn-based strategy, and bring it to life with stunning Japanese artwork and story-driven role-playing. This is Alteil: Horizons, the all new sequel to one of Japan’s most popular online card games, soon to be ready for fantasy, tactical combat, card game, and anime enthusiasts worldwide.

Four Gods Sleep Under Lavato…

Harness Their Power!

You are an Iczer, a summoner and master strategist tasked with protecting the world of Lavato from the forces of Batora, the embodiment of evil accross all dimensions. An ancient struggle between Batora and the four Old Gods of Lavato drained the Gods of power, sinking them into a sleep that has lasted thousands of years. But the Gods still influence Lavato from the depths of slumber…

One night, a new Prince is born, and the sun suddenly rises. Night becomes day, and a miracle is proclaimed. The Church of the True God takes advantage of the miracle to orchestrate the installation of the Solar Prince to the throne - and the ensuing power struggle plunges Lavato into war.

With the violence, tragedy, and death of the war, Batora’s hunger for evil and chaos is stoked - and he’s set his sights on Lavato again! But with the Old Gods deep in slumber, who will protect Lavato from Batora’s endless rage?

Now is the time of the Iczers. They’ve studied the legends of Lavato’s Old Gods. They’ve learned to command the Gods’ powerful Four Spheres of Influence. They’ve mastered the art of using these Spheres to summon heroes, monsters, and dragons to battle the forces of chaos and evil. Will you be the brave Iczer who saves Lavato from destruction?

As in any collectible card game, a game of Alteil: Horizons begins with carefully crafting a deck. There’s a universe of over 1,000 cards to choose from, each representing the factions, heroes, villains, monsters and magic of Lavato’s past and present. Your collection begins with any of four free 30-card starter decks, themed around the four Spheres.

Once you’ve challenged another Iczer to a duel, use your resources wisely to play your cards. Spell cards represent your command of the Spheres; unit cards represent your ability to summon heroes and monsters. Each unit is a fully fleshed-out combatant, complete with hit points, attack and defense power, agility, and range. Combat takes place on a battle grid - maneuver your units so that their unique abilities fit your winning strategy. And their abilities really are unique! A fighter with two swords might be able to hit two enemies at once; a mage can tap into your resources to play spells that wreak havoc on the entire battlefield! You have complete control of each unit’s abilities during combat.

A number of features set Alteil apart from the other online card games out there. For example...

Range based grid: position archers behind your front-line fighters to launch a ranged attack. Position your mages in the rear to protect them as they cast powerful defensive and offensive spells.

Resource recycling: when a card dies, you get back the resources you spent on it. Two small soldiers die? Use the Spell Points they originally cost to bring out something big and scary.

Revival system: if a unit dies you can spend a copy of it from your deck to revive it on the battlefield. Keep your best soldiers alive to execute killer multi-unit combos!

Soul Cards: influence the pace of the game by choosing your starting Life Points and Soul Skills. Give yourself stronger Soul Skills and less Life for a more offense-based approach, or give yourself more Life Points to buy time for a crafty strategy.

Day/Night cycle: as duels rage on, the sun rises and sets. Units like the Undead and Werewolves are stronger at night - while a holy warrior gets bonuses under the first rays of morning - so summon them when they’ll be most effective!

Alteil has over two thousand individual pieces of art completed already. It’s got the visual style and story of a Japanese RPG. It’s got unique game mechanics. We hope you’ll become a huge fan. We already are. This is Alteil: Horizons.

Adapt3 is a variant of Adaptoid for exactly three players.

In Adaptoid, Black was both White's prey and White's hunter, but in Adapt3 these roles are separated! Each player now has two opponents, but only one of them is his prey: White hunts Red; Red hunts Black; and Black hunts White. This means that the next player in turn is your hunter.

Gameplay is the same as in Adaptoid with some minor clarifications.

description:
Tracy & Laura Hickman's storytelling board game that turns your e-reading device into a portal of adventure and imagination.

Use strategy and wits as you discover and collect the parts of your mysterious story, then be the first to complete the epic adventure. The tale is different every time you play … or you can download new Sojourner Tales to play from the website at sojournertales.com!

Beginning with the player holding the Lead Player token (Lead Player), players perform each turn in two rounds of play: a Journey round (movement) and a Story round.

Movement is by die roll down paths connecting ‘locations’ and ‘Chapter Disks’ situated on event spaces around the board. Players moves either allow them end movement on locations or ‘Chapter Disks’. If there is another player’s token on the end movement space, both players ‘dual’ for control of that space, the winner determining which of them will move 1-2 spaces forward off of that space. ‘Chapter Disks’ may be picked up off the board in the next round in which case that space is no longer counted for movement, thus offering diminishing options for movement as the game progresses.

Once all player tokens conclude movement, the Story round begins with the Lead Player. Players on ‘Chapter Disks’ of another player’s color end their round. Players who are on face-up ‘Chapter Disks’ of their own color take them off the board and put them on a display card in front of them. If they have as many ‘Chapter Disks’ as the current ‘chapter’ marker on the board, then they have an Epic Story’ event (referencing the ‘chapter’ and ‘scene’ markers on the board for the entry number) and become the Lead Player. Otherwise, they have a ‘Twisted Tales’ event, combining two cards from the ‘twisted’ deck and a third card from the ‘Tales’ deck into a short plot description.

Both Epic Story Events and Twisted Tales events generate a Tales reference number which another player at the table looks up in the Sojourner Story Book. This Story Book is either a paper book included in the game OR it is an ebook file (epub or mobi format) which has been downloaded from our website. This Story Book is the story engine of the game. Each entry may offer a particular challenge to the player, forward the ‘Chapter’ and ‘Scene’ markers on the board or grant ‘Destiny Cards’ which players may use to better their position or hinder other players during the game.

Optional rules permit creative players to generate their own ‘generic story’ extemporaneously during play.

Generally, the objective is to obtain five ‘Story Disks’ of your own color to win the game. You battle other players and the Tales Book entries as well to achieve this. The conclusion finishes the story and declares the winning storyteller.
