The goal of the game is to collect the most love points by the end of the game. In order to gain love points you will need to have your customers eat as much of your food as possible. And to do that you need to improve your food preparation skills and get good furniture for your restaurant.

Players start the game with one food stand (which lets you prepare one food card) and five food cards. Five food cards are placed face-up in a row in the middle of the table, and six character cards are also placed face-up.

As the first action in the game, each player chooses two of their food cards in secret. These are then revealed to show which food preparation skills they start with.

Taking turns round the table. players may take two actions of the four available. They may take the same action twice. The options are:


     Take a food card - take one of five face-up food cards on the table, or the face-down card from the top of the deck.
     Prepare food - Take one food card from your hand and place it on an empty food stand. You must have the skill required to play the food card.
     Buy furniture - discard any two food cards to buy the furniture you want. This could be more food stands, or wallpaper/floor tiles that may give you bonus points at the end of the game.
     Raise your skill level - Play a food card in front of you to raise your skill level.


When you prepare food, you need to check whether the hunger level of any of the face-up character cards has been satisfied by the food you have prepared. If it has, you take the character card and place it face-down in front of you. This is where most of your love points will come from.

At the end of your turn, replace any food or character cards that were taken from the table, shuffling the food discard pile if necessary. If you now hold more than three food cards, discard down to three.

The game ends when the deck of food cards has been depleted twice in a 3-player game or three times in a 4-player game. Play continues until each player has had the same number of turns. Love points are awarded for the character cards you have collected, from drink cards that you may have played during food preparation, and bonuses from the wallpaper/floor tile furniture cards. The player with the most points wins.

Beard! is a casual, silly, Beer & Pretzel style game for 2-6 players. This card game takes you on a  a ridiculous romp through reality as you compete with your friends to grow the first truly epic beard! Will you become a Lumberjack, face down Ninja! or find True Love? All is possible when trying to grow the greatest of all beards!

Gameplay is simple.

1. The player with the largest beard chooses who goes first.

2. The first player draws a Save Or Shave card (SOS Card), reads it out loud, and does what it says. Hopefully, it tells you to draw Beard! Cards, but it might tell you to lose Beard! Cards, Steal the Cards of others, roll a six sided die, or choose between multiple options.

3. Play continues clockwise.

4. Each Beard! Card has one letter: B, E, A, R, or D. The first person to have one of each Beard! Card, wins the game.

boardgame: Mice and Mystics: Lost Chapter – The Ghost of Castle Andon
description:
Did you catch that apparition in the corner of your eye? Blink and it's gone! Either I'm going crazy or the castle has a real life ghost! Between the presence of raiding pirates in the castle and this potential poltergeist, another adventure awaits for Collin and his companions!

The Ghost of Castle Andon is the second downloadable chapter for Mice and Mystics. This is a brand new chapter — a lost chapter — in the tale of Collin and his friends. This scenario uses six mice of your choice.

boardgame: Zicke & Zacke: Ran an die Federn
description:
It's eggactly high noon, and the chickens Zicke and Zacke have started a feather-trimming duel. Starting rump to rump, they flutter in opposite directions, but since they're running around the chicken yard in a circle, they will meet soon enough with loud clucking. Whoever manages to jump over the other adorns herself with a new tail feather — but the more the young chickens puff up their feathers, the slower they strut around. And the worms, who are skillful enough to flee time and again, also make for lots of stops during the race.

In the end, only the chicken that pays attention to closing her memory gaps will fill her rump with feathers and win by a beak.

In Zicke & Zacke: Ran an die Federn, thirteen face-up tiles lay in a circle around 13 face-down "yard tiles" that have the same pictures on them. Two players (or two teams) take turns moving the two chickens; each chicken needs to move to the space where a worm awaits, the worm being as many spaces ahead as the number of feathers not on the chicken. The player reveals a yard tile, and if it matches the space where his chicken's worm stands, he moves to that tile, then advances the worm the appropriate number of spaces, then tries to reveal that tile; if he reveals the wrong yard tile, his turn ends. If he manages to jump over the other chicken on his turn, his chicken gains a feather, but each feather slows a chicken down, making it harder to zoom around the chicken yard.

The player or team whose chicken gains four feathers first wins.
family: Series: The Chicken Family of Zoch

description:
The breakfast worms are coming! Twelve hungry baby birds stretch their wide-open beaks into the air. Will the busy parent birds manage to get enough food for all of them?

In Schmatzspatz, four wooden birds sit on the edges of the circular rotating "board in the box", each with a worm in its beak. In the middle area are twelve hungry little birds with open mouths. A symbol die determines the sector of the board where the most hungry baby birds are. The player now decides which one of these birds to feed and tries to rotate the board to move one of the adult birds to the right position.

The turning board occasionally blocks the feeding, however,so the players must remember these "blocked" positions. When the worm can successfully be fed to the bird, the baby closes its beak. If the players manage to feed all the baby birds before running out of worms, they win the game together.

Schmatzspatz includes a competitive version in which each player tries to get rid of his worm supply first.

Time to tune your Slicks racing car and make it unique with specific tires, flights and boosters.

In Slicks: Tuning, you can choose between six different types of tires, from hard to extremely smooth. Some show a high performance over the first rounds, but suddenly explode; others start with less-than-perfect performance, but maintain that level for a long distance.

This expansion comes with a full set of tuning tools for all six players, allowing each player to establish a personal set-up that matches his strategy and favorite driving style.

Material:
6 sets of tuning tools, each consisting of
- 3 stripes "tire" (double sided)
- 1 Marker "Flight" (double sided, +1 for green and yellow)
- 1 Marker "Booster"/"save tire"

Intrigue City: The Bank Conspiracy is the first expansion of Intrigue City.
It consists of five new Advanced Influence Action Cards and one new type of card: the Bank Vault.

This is an era of great change in the economy of Aar. The balance of power among the Guilds is changing continuously. The members of the Spider Lodge have to be very careful and take new actions fast in order to keep the control of the six Guilds and remain the ultimate rulers of Aar. This era is known in the history of Core as the Bank Conspiracy Era.

Do you have what it takes to be a knight of the round table?

King Arthur has an opening in his legendary roster of heroes! With just the right combination of strength, fortitude, and originality, the job is yours!
Accept His Majesty's quests, make the realm a better place, and secure your name in the history books... before your rivals get a chance!

Each turn players roll dice and commit them to match different quests from the king. Collect sets of matching colored quests or one of each to win the game.

boardgame: CRMA: The Battle for Colony ZXC123
description:
The futuristic colony ZXC123 is under attack by an enemy group known as the Boss Squad. Four members of the colony have been given CRMA (Combat-Ready Mechanized Armor), highly upgradeable equipment that they'll use to protect their colony and fight off the Boss Squad.

In this solo roguelike game, players work through the card deck, fighting enemies and dealing with events around the colony. The order of these is randomized through shuffling, and each encounter is removed from the deck after resolution. Cards also act as items that the player can attach to their CRMA to increase their effectiveness, trade at caravans for other items, or break down during combat to influence dice rolls. Card backs are used as vital stats indicators, and two of these are removed from each game for the player character, further randomizing each game experience.

The goal of the game is to build up your CRMA's strength to the point where you can defeat three Boss characters in combat. If you run from three Bosses, you lose. If you only defeat two bosses and the deck runs out, you still win.

boardgame: In the Name of Roma!: Actions of the 80 Roma Infantry Regiment on the Eastern Front – August to December 1941
description:
IN THE NAME OF ROMA is a wargame supplement that contains 30 company and platoon level scenarios covering the actions of the 80a Roma Regiment from August to December of 1941. You’ll follow the 80th from the open steppe to the Donetz industrial basin, including the amphibious attacks across the Dnieper, and the fateful decision of Column Chiarimonti to attempt to seize Nikitovka.

IN THE NAME OF ROMA includes:

• 30 scenarios divided in six distinct campaigns
• Historical background information and period area maps
• Italian and Soviet briefing for each scenario
• Formation information and stats for Early War Italians and Soviets
• Forward by Richard Clarke

Though primarily written for the Too Fat Lardies rules systems (I Ain’t Been Shot Mum; Troops, Weapons, and Tactics; and the newly released Chain of Command), the scenarios in IN THE NAME OF ROMA work equally well with Arc of Fire, Bolt Action, Flames of War or your favorite rules system.

GenDu "The Gentleman's Duel" is a trading card game where you play as a gentleman in The world of the Gents. You can challenge other gentleman to a duel to test their might and prove your own. The gentlemen set up dungeons and battle to the death.In the game players build their respective dungeons while the objective is to get one of their nouns (also referred to as creatures) through the opposing player's dungeon whilst destroying anything in its path. Both players build dungeons and then try to conquer each other, which makes for an intense experience.

Gentlemen duel for many reasons; honor, respect, recognition, fancy shoes, the love of a fair maiden, crisp bacon, a good shawarma, or to maybe one day be a
Master Gentleman.


    Gentlemen can build their own deck of 50 cards and duel against other Gentleman.
    You can also purchase Starter Decks, which are a built deck of 50 cards ready to play. All Starter Decks are random.
    Or you can purchase Booster Packs and build your own deck from scratch.
    Trade with your friends to get cards that will help your deck and increase your odds of winning
    Or try and collect all 136 different cards in the series.


The cards in a Gentleman's deck are the powers and defense they have at their disposal to defeat any challenger. Build wisely.

For more information on how to play GenDu The Gentleman's Duel. Check out our Game Play Video on our Rules page.

You can also create your own GenDu cards!!!

GenDu The Gentleman's Duel is the first and only trading card game ( TCG ) where all cards released in every series are created by the players.


    Every Booster pack and Starter deck comes with blank GenDu cards.
    Starter decks have 3 blank cards and Booster packs have 1. 
    Players draw their card ideas on the cards provided and Send Them In.
    Players can also use our Card Maker online to submit cards.
    The cards are  posted on our Facebook page for votes.
    The cards with the highest votes are printed into the next series.
    Author of the card has their name printed on the card
    Author also gets first copy of the printed card sent to them for FREE!


This is what sets GenDu apart from any other TCG. Now for the first time players have an opportunity to be a part of the game creation. Furthermore with player input and collaboration, GenDu can will always remain fresh and interesting. We welcome all players to send in as many ideas as they want so that they have a better chance of one of them being picked to be in the next release of GenDu.

The Gentleman's Duel awaits....
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Trailer Park Wars!: Terror in the Trailer Park
description:
Unleash mayhem with this 2013 expansion to the base game Trailer Park Wars! 
Why?
Because it’s fun. That’s why.

Add to your Trailer Park Wars Game with this frighteningly fun expansion.
Terror in the Trailer Park includes:
24 new trailers
75 Trailer Park Wars! Cards
50 Purple Yard Flamingo Miniatures (NEW Color!!)
12 Trailer Park Name Tiles

Shuffle the cards into the base game cards, name your trailer park (how about Bloody Bones Diseased Swamp?) and get ready to release a reign of terror!

New characters include: Steve the Serial Killing Carnie, the Mega-Church Pastor, Blammo the Birthday Clown as tenants along with Zombie Chihuahuas, Bunny-Crab Spider, and Flying Spider Monkeys.

New Amenities, too! Get a Bacon Vending Machine or a Kommando Kitten.

Add this expansion to your Trailer Park Wars Game for a Gut Bustin' Great Time!

The player is the CEO of a movie studio during the golden age of Hollywood (1927-1955). Competition for talented people is fierce, and the public's tastes in movies change all the time. The player has to manage and hire new people, and approve the right films, so that the have as much Fame (victory points) as possible, at the end of the game.

Created for the 2013 Solitaire Print and Play contest, Cine-vilzation uses six small containers to manage the production and payout of film genres.  Players have to do their best to predict when films will become popular, as timing a film release correctly will earn the player fame and clout in the film industry, which can help produce even more films.

Twelve film types are grouped into three genres centered around the resources that make Hollywood run, capital, acclaim and power.  Players keep track of these resources, using tokens, dice and cards. The tokens can only be used once, and may need to be held to pay upkeep costs at the end of each round.  Cards can be played to help pay for a film, but holding on to them will generate tokens. Dice can be used multiple times, but cost tokens each round.  Dice also control an invisible opponent, who tries to get cards before you do, and speeds up the end of the game.

Players will have to evaluate their supply of resources, the popularity of films, and how much time remains to try to make the best films they can.   When resources are dedicated to a film, they get placed into a container, and will be unavailable for several rounds.  The containers shift during upkeep, with any dice inside the first container getting rolled for the AI.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
SETUP:

-In a 4 player game, use all 3 artifacts.
-In a 2-3 player game randomly remove 1 artifact.
-In the solitaire game randomly select 1 artifact to use.

When building the map, when you place the 1st town marker, select an artifact and place it on that town.  Place the remaining artifacts as you place the final town marker(s).

3. CAMPAIGN PHASE

If you take control of a town with an artifact in it, place the artifact next to the gender tile of the hero that attacked the town.  You decide which side of the artifact is placed face up (artifacts have opposing terrain on each side).  That hero is now considered to be wearing the artifact.

Your hero wears the artifact until that hero is Vanquished.  You may not switch an artifact to it opposite side while your hero wears it.  If you Vanquish another hero who is wearing an artifact, then remove the artifact from that hero.  Your attacking hero may choose to wear it, if you wish.  A hero may never wear more than 1 artifact.  If you do not take an artifact, it remains on the space until any hero without an artifact wears it.

In a 2 player game no player may have more than 1 artifact.

An artifact allows the hero wearing it to use the heraldry symbols as a power bonus in battle.  If you play a creature in battle with a heraldry symbol that matches your hero's artifact, that hero gains +1 power when attacking or defending.  If you play 2 symbols that match your artifact in a single battle, then you may return 1 of your played creatures back to your hand (return 2 if 3 match, 3 if 4 match, etc).

boardgame: The Downfall of Pompeii: The Dancing Flames
description:
PREPARATION -
Mix these 3 tiles with the other Lava Tiles in the cloth sack.

SECOND PHASE: RUN FOR YOUR LIVES!
No Lava - if this is drawn, remove it from the game, no Lava is placed this turn.
Draw 2 - if this is drawn, remove it from the game, draw 2 more tiles and place them.
Wild - You may only place this adjacent to another lava tile. If this is the first tile of the game, draw another and return this to the bag.  It is considered the same symbol of any adjacent tiles for the sake if future tile laying.

boardgame: Lords of Vegas: Mob Rules & Union of the Shakes
description:
INITIAL SETUP

These 2 mini expansions can be added individually or can be used in combination with each other depending on the type of game you would like to play.  Put the desired markers face up on Frank Sinatra Drive.

TAKE OVER

If you fail to take over a Casino, you may choose to take one of the markers, use it powers and then place the marker in front of you face down to show that you have used it.  You must be able to use the power to take the marker.  You may not take any markers if you have a marker in front of you.  Markers are intentionally limited.

MOB RULES

Mob Support - If you have fewer dice than the boss of the casino you just failed to take, you may immediately reroll 1 of your dice in that casino for free.

UNION OF THE SHAKES

Union Support - If you have more dice than the boss of the casino you just failed to take, you may immediately reroll 1 of your dice in that casino for free.

Large multi-map, operational-level game covering the entire American Civil War that is easy to play. Division and Brigade-level. Naval units and Confederate trade aspects. Political rules. Weekly turns. Production and historical reinforcements.

Focuses heavily on hidden units, recon and fog-of-war. Several card decks that add random events and historical flavor without driving the game.

Several scenarios: 1861, 1862 and 1863. Plus two smaller single map intro Campaign scenarios serve as a way to learn the game system.

From The Game Crafter, LLC:

Cool Factors
-Short setup time. Ready to play in minutes
-High variety between games. Great replay value
-Awesome RPG style pixel art

Retro RPG-styled deck building card game

Welcome to CardCraft! The deck building game set in the world of classic RPG’s! Delve through dungeons, gather party members, find new equipment, slay monsters and eventually claim the Magic Crystal that sits guarded by its vicious keeper!

This is the Base Game, needed for all additional expansion content and ready to play in minutes for up to 2 players.

New expansions will be released regularly, with the first scheduled shortly after launch!

description:
A game set in the Battlestar Galactica Universe using a variation of Dice of Arkham by Mads L. Brynnum.

The Setting: After the Cylon surprise attack that wiped out the 12 colonies of man, the Battlestar Galactica leads a rag-tag fleet of the last remaining humans on a long and perilous journey to Kobol, the home of the lost 13th Tribe. You control the fleet as you make you way to your new home, deciding when to run, when to fight, and how to survive.

Game Overview: This game is more than a simple re-skinning of "Dice Of Arkham", the mechanic has been changed and modified to lend itself to this unique Sci-Fi Universe, yet it remains true to its heritage. Those familiar with "Dice Of Arkham" will have no problem picking this game up. For those who aren't, first of all I recommend you grab DoA RIGHT NOW (the link to it is above). It's an excellent game! There is also a very complete set of rules that will explain this game fully included in the pdf.

Components:
Game Sheet & Rules (which you print)
8 tokens (which you provide)
8 Dice, 6 of one color and 2 of a second color (which you also provide)

Approximate Game Length: 10-20 minutes

Gameplay: Each turn you roll your Jump Prep Dice (6 dice, however you only roll 5 of them until you complete the first "Humans" event) to perform your actions such as repairing your ships or advancing along the Jump Preparation Track. You will also roll the Draidis Dice (2 dice) once per turn which shows you what Cylon threats will have to be resolved in this area of space before you can continue. The Cylons are also attempting to move down their track and perform Traitorous Acts to prevent the Humans from reaching the home of the lost 13th Tribe, Kobol. As each side completes their Track, an event is triggered. If the Cylons complete 3 events before the humans do, you lose. If you complete 3 before the cylons do, you win!

This game will ALWAYS be free to Print and Play!

--Lord Malachi

Submitted for the 2013 Solitaire Print and Play Contest

Dribling is a basketball simulation game which is situated halfway between classic board games, strategy or letters. Each participating player takes the role of coach and can design a complete basketball team and fully tailored from the creation of the various players. Source: Publisher's website

Wanted: Igor! is a monster-building game in which players take turns playing cards in order to create as many creatures as possible under Dr. Frankenstein's orders.

At the beginning of the game, a number of plan cards one less than the number of players are revealed. Players must connect body parts to the five different slots on the plans in order to complete the creatures, receiving a certain number of Marks for each body part placed. Once a creature is complete, the player with the most Marks on that creature gets credit for its creation and scores a victory point (VP). The completed creature is then discarded and replaced with a new plan card. Whoever first scores 4 VPs wins.


     IndieCade 2014 Official Selection, Los Angeles, California
     Seal of Approval from The Dice Tower
     Best Sellable Board Game - Nordic Game Jam 2013, Copenhagen, Denmark
     Nominated for the People's Choice Award - Nordic Game Jam 2013, Copenhagen, Denmark

description:
Advances in nautical technology made longer travel by sea possible. Players take over the role to build a trade empire for their nation. They are chartered to explore the vast seas outside the known world and found colonies to harvest rich goods on a modular world map.

Nations which can control a monopoly of particular goods will be able to make the most profit. But weakly protected colonies will be easy targets for the other nations and fierce battles are fought between fleets for those key colonies on a tactical battle map.

Players can invest their money in developing new ship types (carracks, galleons, frigates, etc.) or harbor defense. Or, they can spend their money by buying new ships, trying to find the perfect balance between merchant and battle ships.

The game is won by the player who has the most victory points after a fixed number of rounds. As points are awarded for earning money, not keeping it, make sure to spend it wisely!

Road To Rabaul is a scenario and campaign book for the popular CHECK YOUR 6! Air Combat and Campaign Rules. The book provides four campaigns and 24 scenarios under one cover. The first campaign (5 scenarios) follows the beginning of the Japanese air campaign against Darwin, Australia from February to June 1942. The second campaign, recreates the desperate defense of Port Moresby, New Guinea from March to August 1942 with 5 scenarios. The third campaign (7 scenarios) focuses on the 5th Air Force assault on Japanese bases along the north coast of New Guinea in 1943. The last 7 scenarios concentrate on the US Navy and Marine Corps attacks against Rabual. The book also features some of WWII’s legendary aces including Thomas McGuire, Greg Boyington, George Welsh, Hiroyoshi Nishizawa, Shogo Takeuchi, Peter Turnbull and Les Jackson.

HISTORICAL RESEARCH: This book includes several introduction sections containing campaign historical background, plus a bibliography for further reading. A campaign map is included that displays campaign targets and airfield locations.

AIRCRAFT STATISTICS and CAMPAIGN INFORMATION: This book contains aircraft statistics for 18 Allied and Japanese aircraft in CHECK YOUR 6!  format for use in the scenarios. The book also contains detailed notes and rare pictures of the various aircraft featured in the campaign.

Players embark on a journey into the jungle in the footsteps of the ancient civilization Maya. Players will earn points for their treasure trove. In each round, players have four cards with available maps. In his turn, a player puts on the table his personal map (the card) held in the hand. Then he flips the selected card treasure lying on the table. A player gets points when the colour and the shape of treasures. After four rounds who has the most points is the winner.

description:
A team game, where each team tries to determine the mashed up movie title.
Scoring is one point for each title guessed, and a bonus point for both titles. If there are points left over after the 1 minute round, the other team gets a chance to steal the remaining points. Whomever gets the most points gets the card. At the end of the game, you take the cards and try to make combos (most movies starring same actor, most in the same series, most in same genre).

As it's a team game, you will see one person knowing a title, announcing it, then the other team members using that title to guess the rest of the movies. Also, sometimes a player knows the movie but cannot remember its title, so will describe the movie to their teammates. Those are fun moments for me to see.

You will see an opponent anxiously watching the clock for opportunity to steal.

The numbers at the bottom of the card are used to create themed decks.  This lets you use a sub-set of the cards you own to come up with a themed game.

September 1, 1939 – For the second time in the 20th century, the world is at war. With the invasion of Poland by Germany, Europe plunges into war a mere 20 years after the end of The Great War, now to be known as World War I. Just over two years later, in December of 1941, the other half of the globe joins the war as Japan declares war on the United States and the British Commonwealth and tears through Western Pacific territories with astonishing ease. Will you save the free world as the Allies, or conquer it as the Axis?

WWII: The Card Game is a 2 player card game that pits the Axis against the Allied Powers in the Second World War. WWII: The Card Game is a constructed deck game in which both players construct their own deck before the game begins and then use their deck to wage war on their opponent. During the game, players build their resources and deploy military units to take control of locations by defeating their opponent’s units in battle. By winning battles and controlling key strategic locations around the world, players cause their opponent to lose cards from their deck. The first player to empty their opponent’s deck is the winner!

Just start and get into the party game. Re-experience Tabu. This little edition of cards (22 cards of double-sided cards with terms and one rule card) is a stand-alone playable game of Tabu. It can also be used as very small expansion to the base game.

It has quick and easy rules like just set up two teams and explain the card given from the other team to your team members in 30 seconds. If you did it successfully your team scores 1 point; if not, or if you use one of the "Tabuwords", you get nothing.

Promotional set for 7th player to Gringo - Polish edition of Skull & Roses.

In What?!? Oh... Couples Banter, players turn quibbles into jokes, bickering into banter, and all around ridiculousness into a winning distraction. It's a game of easy improv where the sport of talking meets the challenge of listening.

Each card has either a simple phrase, a fill-in-the-blank phrase, or an action. As players play their cards back and forth, a conversation is created on the fly. Listen carefully for Questions and Demands or you will lose the round!

It's a game for anyone that likes to talk.

description:
In Circus Divas, a two-player dice-buying game, you play the owner of a Depression-era circus going from town to town parting fools and their money. But in order to do that, you need to hire some help and, ultimately, earn more than the rival circus that keeps hitting the same towns you do and draining your profits.

Each player can hire performers for his or her circus from a common pool. Those performers each allow the player to roll one additional die for profit, from the lowly d4 to the mighty - but unpredictable - d20. But these performers can prove to be real divas. When a performer rolls a 1, he or she throws a diva's tantrum and has to be consoled - or he or she will quit. And if you're consoling one diva, you can't console another if they should have a tantrum, so choose carefully.

The first player to reach $100 in savings wins. Play time takes about 15 minutes and requires at least one d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, and d20 to play. Counters for money are also helpful, but pencil and paper could work just fine. Enjoy it, and please let me know what you think!

Draw Blood is a rummy-style card game for 2-3 players, with a deck that consists of 54 cards: three types of monsters (Vampires, Werewolves and Water Monsters), three "Kill" cards (for killing your opponent's Monsters), and 36 numbered blood cards.

Like most rummy games, the object of Draw Blood is to be the first player to get rid of all the cards in your hand. To do this, players can lay down melds of sets and runs; unlike other rummy games, however, you can't just lay down melds whenever you want. You must play a monster card first. Knowing when to play monsters is a key strategy, but you also have to figure out when to kill your opponent's monsters to prevent them from laying down their own melds.

The numbered blood cards come in two colors: odd numbers are black and have a skull in the right corner, while even numbers are red and have a heart in the right corner. You can kill your opponent's monsters with a Kill card or a three-of-a-kind of skulls. With a three-of-a-kind of hearts, you can bring one of your monsters back to life. Blood cards can also be played as melds for points, and deciding whether to play for points or to kill your opponents becomes a key strategy in the game.

The first player to play out his cards ends the hand, then everyone tallies his score. Whoever first reaches a preset point total wins!

This is a free promo available at Spiel 2013.

It includes a Double Buy card that counts as two Buy cards, and a Rules card. After being used, the card must be passed to the player on your left.

Yōkaï no Mori contains two simpler versions of Shogi to introduce the game to new players and youngsters.

In the classic version, each player has only four pieces – kodama, tanuki, kitsune, and koropokkuru – and they're trying to either catch the opponent's koropokkuru or move their own koropokkuru to the opposite side of the game board. The board is just 3×4 squares, so the game is rather short. As for how to play:


     The tanuki moves one space orthogonally.
     The kitsune moves one space diagonally.
     The kodama is directly analogous to the pawn/soldier of Shōgi and can be promoted to a kodama samurai, equivalent to the gold general in Shogi (moving one space orthogonally or one space diagonally forward).
     The koropokkuru is analogous to a king in Chess, except that he can win through promotion as long as it doesn't leave him in check.
     Even on this 3×4 board, you are still able to drop in pieces that you captured from your opponent!
     Unlike in Shōgi, you can drop a kodama for mate or have two kodama in a column.


The expert version has a larger 5×6 game board with each player having eight pieces (3 kodama, 2 oni, 2 kirin and 1 koropokkuru).


     The kirin moves equivalent to the gold general in Shogi.
     The oni moves equivalent to the silver general in Shogi (moving one space diagonally or one space straight forward) and can be promoted to a super oni, equivalent to the gold general in Shogi.


The game also adds more rules from the full Shōgi game, in particular:


     You win only by capturing your opponent's koropokkuru, not by reaching the opposite side of the game board.
     You can't have two of your own kodama in the same column/file.
     You can't drop a kodama for mate.


The Japan Shogi Association created the rules for this game in order for players to learn important concepts in Shōgi, such as how to handle the generals effectively, why pawn promotion is so important, and how to mate the opponent's king with successive piece drops.

This game from the 1960's from the German Demokratic Republic or from Poland is a soccer game for the table.
The game is for two gamers.
On the plastic playfield every gamer has 10 outfield players and 1 goal keeper in his team. The outfield players and the goal keeper have little springs under the feet and the gamers try to shut the little metal ball in the goal.
The winner is the gamer who had shot more goals in the playtime.

A board game that uses components of a checkers set. 22 pieces, 11 per player, and an 8x8 board. Layout: two diagonally opposite corners are labeled "Camps;" a player's counters are arranged in a diagonal pattern guarding his or her camp. (Please note: opening set-up of pieces has been revised.)

Movement: pieces move as a rook in chess. If a piece is making a non-capturing move, it can only move forward, toward the enemy Camp.

Capturing: this is done by moving away from an adjacent enemy counter. So, if at the start of your turn, an enemy piece is next to one of yours, you move away from it in a straight line to capture it.

Winning: the person who gets a counter onto the enemy's Camp wins. (In the extremely rare case in which a player has no legal move but does not occupy the enemy's Camp, then the immobile player loses.)

The object of Ideas al Minuto is to say as many ideas as possible in one minute.

In teams of 2-5, players receive challenges based on the cards that they draw. Within one minute, each team gives as many answers as possible on the same challenge, with the conditions changing each time thanks to drawn cards showing animals, characters, places and things. The first team, for example, might tell a story of a teacher who starts on an island and ends in a circus, while the next tells the story of a baker who begins in a hospital and ends in a submarine.

Each challenge card has a difficulty of 1-5, and team's score points based on this difficulty level. The team with most points at the end of the game wins.

"Fobal is a board game of questions and answers for all football lovers.

In Fobal you'll find these football questions you always wanted to find ... in other words, Fobal is the game that you will be able to demonstrate the amount and quality of your footballing knowledge."

Tournament Mode: who add the most points from correct answer, after 19 questions wins.

boardgame: Templar Queenie 1: The Messages & The Coins
description:
Templar Queenie 1: The Messages & The Coins, a mini-expansion for Templar: The Secret Treasures, consists of two modules that can be used individually, together, or in combination with any other modules for the game.


     The Messages: Shuffle the seven message tiles with the harbor tiles at the start of the game. Whenever at least one message tile is revealed when adding tiles to the harbor, each player can either score 2 VP or return a played character card to hand.



     The Coins: If no coins are on the board when someone plays "The Bells" card, place one coin in each room. When a player ends his movement in a room with a coin, he collects that coin. Players score 1-12 VP at the end of the game depending on how many coins they have relative to other players.

boardgame: Templar Queenie 2: The Secret Letters, The Tapestries & The Lamps
description:
Templar Queenie 2: The Secret Letters, The Tapestries & The Lamps, a mini-expansion for Templar: The Secret Treasures, consists of three modules that can be used individually, together, or in combination with any other modules for the game.


     The Secret Letters: Each player has one secret letter tile that he can discard to replay a (valid) character that's already in his discard stack. If unused, the secret letter tile is worth 3 VP at game's end.



     The Tapestries: Each player has one tapestry tile that he can place in a room. When Remigius awards points in a room, anyone with a tapestry in the room scores twice as many points as normal, then all tapestries in the room are discarded. If unused, the tapestry tile is worth 3 VP at game's end.



     The Lamps: Each player has one lamp tile that he can place in a room. When Remigius or Severus ends movement in a room with a lamp, the lamp's owner scores 2 VP, then reclaims his lamp, which can be placed again on a future turn.

boardgame: Skirmish! The Scrambles plus expansions pack
description:
User summary:

A special release of Skirmish! The Scrambles containing both expansion packs Antimagic and Breakthrough for Essen Spiel 2013.

Space Station: Core Modules consists of two double-sided cards that add new core modules — Luxury, Military, Scientific and Support — to the game that are revealed when you flip your starting core module. Each module has a unique function.

The mad sorcerer Grislyk has stolen the Book of Lore from the elves and hidden it in his dungeon lair. As the elven warrior maiden Kora, you must brave three increasingly dangerous levels to recover the Book, destroy Grislyk’s magical Black Crucible, and slay the wizard himself. Magical artifacts will help you evade Grislyk’s traps and slay his army of goblins and skeletons, but you will also need your wits and a bit of luck, for knowing when to search, when to fight, and when to run is the only way to survive the DUNGEON OF DEADLIEST EVIL.

Dungeon of Deadliest Evil is a solitaire adventure game that takes all the essential elements of a classic dungeon crawl, like slaying monsters, finding powerful artifacts, and exploring a multi-level dungeon, and packs them into an easy-to-learn game that sets up and plays in 20 minutes.

Gameplay is based on a simple dice allocation mechanic. You have three abilities, Speed, Combat, and Defense, and at the start of each turn you must assign one of three dice (a d4, a d6, and a d8) to each of them. While the game involves a good deal of luck and dice-rolling, judicious dice allocation is the key to success. Victory is achieved by destroying the Black Crucible, retrieving the Book of Lore, slaying the evil wizard Grislyk, and finally leaving the dungeon alive.

Titanium Wars: Confrontation, an expansion for Titanium Wars, adds the following to the base game:


    41 new Arsenal cards: 25 units, 8 improvements, 8 buildings
    6 new Planets / Events
    4 new Leaders
    1 mercenary card (for 1 vs. 1 mode)
    New (replacement) Tactic cards (50)
    New (replacement) summary cards (4)
    The Versus Mode: 1 vs. 1 or team vs. team

You were extremely excited when the Dungeon Construction Kit you had ordered finally landed on your doormat. That excitement lasted until the moment you opened the box. Unfortunately it seems that some evil blackguard had put a curse on it and you immediately found yourself inside your own kit. Now there's only one thing for it: construct your way out. And while you're at it, kill as many monsters and take as much treasure back to the real world as possible...

A print & play card game where each card can either be a room on the map or an action. The best rooms are generally also the best actions, so you have to choose which is the best way to use each card. The card pool allows you to see up to 12 cards at a time, of which up to 4 will be immediately available for play, so there is a limited opportunity to plan ahead.

Dungeon Construction Kit - Cursed! was designed for the 2013 Solitaire Print and Play Contest.

Afterlife is a narrative driven tabletop war gaming experience. The story of a near-future Earth torn apart by civil war after the collapse of the social order imposed by the Unity Council will be told over a number of separate volumes.

Earth burns as sprawling Mega Cities built for a world of prosperity become brutal war zones and the Unity Council’s authority crumbles away. Dozens of corporations, emergent governments and resistance movements form loose factions and alliances in battles for control of Earth’s resources and technology.

Each new Volume of Afterlife will expand on previously released Volumes, adding new miniatures and rules options, and continuing the narrative to its eventual dramatic conclusion.

The Afterlife Volumes will be player driven and interactive. Players will be able to report back the outcomes of battles and the tactical choices made during campaigns, and the information will be used to drive the next stage of the campaign or impact events in future Volumes.

The game will be skirmish level. The game play will be primarily statistics and D10 mechanics.

A dexterity game for 2 or more players.                                      
Plays in 10 minutes with 6 players.      
For ages 6 and up .

In Snapps there are 6 blocks and a 8cm×8cm square board and all you have to do is place the blocks onto the board… simple. 
Well, yes and no: You see, the blocks have magnets in them, and you must place the blocks without making any of them snap together. The first few blocks will be easy to place but as more blocks get placed, the available space on the board dwindles, and it becomes rather tricky to place the last blocks.

If all the blocks get placed on the board, the game enters a second phase, in which players must take turns to push a block towards the centre of the board. At some point the blocks will suddenly snap together, and the player taking their turn at the time is eliminated. Eliminate all your opponents to win.

Knowledge about the physics of magnetism is not essential; what you need are a steady hand and nerves of steel, because those snapping blocks will make you jump.

Schnapps is the adult variant, in which the player who makes the blocks snap together must drink a shot.

Hole of DOOM!!! is akin to Battling Tops in that players spin tops on a wooden board, trying to push the opponent into the dreaded hole of DOOM! For 2-4 players.

From the Cubiko website....

In late 2012, whilst playtesting 'Clockwise'  eight year old Samuel continued his annoying habit of spinning the games delicately designed cog mechanism.

"Samuel stop it! Turn the cogs gently, don't spin them." I pleaded.

Now Samuel is a great playtester and since he seemed obsessed with his spinning discovery, and wasn't about to stop any time soon, I decided it was best to follow his lead.

So there we were spinning the cog mechanism harder and faster when suddenly the smallest cog jumped from its hole and spun by itself.  Well that was it, Samuel completely dismantled the wooded mechanism and began spinning each cog individually. Biting my lip, I reluctantly joined in and a game which resembled the classic 'Battling Tops' ensued.

I gradually warmed to the abuse of my cogs, however, our makeshift board was littered with 5mm holes and the spinning cogs would fall as they cross them. Samuel thought this was great and he called them holes of DOOM!

A dice game based upon the card game Cribbage. A player uses 13 dice to make pairs, straights or find dice that add up to 7.  The game was designed for the 2013 Solitaire Contest, and can be played by 1 or 2 people.

Aim of the game
Players have to make others guess words by giving positive and negative statements about the term in question and without mentioning that word. By doing so, they practice argumentation skills and looking at something from different angles.
 Game play
The game can either be played in teams (4 or more players) or everyone against everyone else (up to 5 players). The stack of cards is placed face-down next to the board. Each of the cards has a word on it (e.g. "talk shows", "mobile phone apps", "EU enlargement"). The starting player starts by giving a positive statement about the word on his/her card. If a player guesses correctly, both the player who explained and the one who guessed move 6 spaces on the board. If the word isn't guessed, the same player has to use a negative statement next round, and if successful both players get to move 5 spaces (one space less per round that it takes to explain the word). The player whose token reaches the finish line first wins the game.

A "Maya the bee" themed memory and dexterity game for children.

Theme
A thunderstorm is coming! Maya the bee and her friends are trying to bring home as much honey as possible before it gets too wet. Who'll manage to collect the most honey?

Aim of the game
Collect the most honey by remembering where the honey is and by skillfully throwing your token in the right places.

Setup
The 32 square meadow tiles (4 water lilies, 6 honey flowers, 22 dandelions) are shuffled and placed face down in the middle of the table. They form the meadow.
The player tokens (Maya, Willie, Flip, Beatrix) are placed at the edge of the meadow.

Game play
Players take turns throwing their player token on the meadow tiles. If your token lands on one or more meadow tiles, you place the tile face-up next to the meadow (if it is a water lily), place it face-up in front of you (dandelion) or place it face-up in front of you and get to look at two of the face-down meadow tiles and place them in a spot of your choice in the meadow (honey flower). If your token lands on another player's token, you have to give him/her one of the tiles you've collected.
When a player turns over the fourth water lily, he/she finishes the turn, and then the game ends. The player with the most flower tiles wins the game.

Collectible Card Game about Anatolian History and Mythology. New game mechanics are adding new excitement to your game experience.

Sancak Beyi is a card game for two players.

Both players begin the game with the title Sancak Beyi. The General purpose is to complete the two Tuğ of the Flags before the opponent and to win the game with the title "Beyler Beyi". During this process, to avoid the opponent to complete

The game is played with specially prepared 60 cards for each player on a special prepared game area. When starting the game both player has to shuffle their card decks and pick up 9 cards from the top of the deck. The players have to agree who begins the game.

There must be 2 Tuğ cards, minimum 4, maximum 5 Cengaver cards, minimum 6, maximum 20 Asker cards. The rest of the deck may include depending on the players choice, any numbers of Ganimet, Olgu and Bilgelik cards.

In Cope each player controls four members of a family who each start the game at a different stage of life (child, teen, adult, senior citizen), or "growth level" as the game has it.

Each growth level is represented by a track which is moved around by die roll. Various spaces on the tracks will require players to draw Good News or Bad News cards. Others will allow the player to try the "Ladder of Life" by coping with a crisis or opportunity. Coping is performed by rolling a large dodecahedron; the result may let the family member cope with the problem and advance, or it may require help from a family member at another growth level, in which case that member has to be moved.

Family members may get "promoted" to later growth levels. The levels are designated by plastic rings of different colors placed on the pieces.

The object of the game is to get all of your family members home first, but the real objective of the game is educational. As the  introduction to the rules say, "the purpose of the game is to teach individual and family participation on how to COPE with daily situations in life."

In Shotzee each player has a score sheet with four 3x3 grids. On a turn, a player rolls from 1-4 dice (their choice) and then all players must enter the number rolled in an empty space in one of their grids.

After all the grids are full they are scored. Players score for columns in which the first two numbers add up to the third, rows in which the second number subtracted from the first equals the third, and long diagonals in which the first two numbers multiplied together equal the third.

Bonus points are given for combinations where the result (third number) is particularly high, for having multiple additions or subtractions score in one grid, and for "Shotzees," which are multiplications where the result is 21.

As a variant, on each die roll each player must write the number in one box in each of their grids.

boardgame: Thunder Gods: Kamikazes Over Okinawa (Second Edition)
description:
Thunder Gods: Kamikazes at Okinawa portrays the massive Kikusui offensive by waves of hundreds of suicide attacks against the Allied invasion fleet off of Okinawa. The game is composed of three mini-games, one on the US destroyer “picket line”, another on the carrier fleet, and a third on the invasion support fleet, that can be played separately or combined. Players use a standard deck of playing cards to activate their aircraft and ships’ Anti-aircraft batteries as well as resolve combats.

Rules include Operation Ten Go, the Japanese fleet’s final mission of the battleship Yamato and her escorts in a one-way sortie to attack the American invasion fleet, Kaityn suicide submarine attacks, as well as variable deployments of ships and CAP (combat air patrol). Each copy of Thunder Gods has three maps, 51 double-sided, un-mounted counters, 5 pages of rules, and 4 pages of historical commentary and bibliography. The game is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh with graphics by Bruce Yearian.

Part 1 of the game was originally published in issue #5 of Panzer Digest. This edition features the other two unpublished modules/games, new graphics by Bruce Yearian, linking "campaign game" rules and 4 pages of historical commentary/designer notes.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A great game to get the party started. Riotous fun designed to bring out your competitive side. Try to guess correctly, the word or common phrase of your team mate in this manic quick fire game. Game contents include word cards and an electronic timer. Don't get caught in possession!
Preperation for the game:

     Split into two teams.
     Decide how many points a team needs to score to win the game. We recommend 8.
     Choose which coloured side of the cards you will play for your game.
     Shuffle the deck of cards and place down in the centre of the table.


Playing the game:

     One player is nominated from each team to be the describing player for the first round, the remaining players on the team are the guessing players.
     Once you have selected your describing and guessing players a member of either team can activate the timer.
     Once the timer is activated the timer ticks for an undisclosed amount of time.During this time the describing player of Team One collects a card from the top of the card pile. Each card features 10 items of phrases. The describing player selects any one of the ten and describes their selection to the guessing members on their team. The description can only be made by voice , no gestures are allowed. The describing player must not use any part of the word or phrase to help their description. The guessing players do their best to answer as quickly as possible.
     On correctly guessing the item or phrase the card is passed to Team Two's describing player who then selects a different item or phrase from the same list of ten. They then proceed to describe the selected item to their guessing players. Again, on a correct guess the card is passed back to Team One's Describing player, who picks a new item or phrase. This process continues with both teams attempting to describe and guess correctly as quickly as possible so they may return the card to the opposing team.
     During this time the timer continues to tick until eventually a buzzer sounds marking the end of the round. Whichever team is left holding the card is the loser!


Tips and points to remember:

     You cannot predict when the buzzer will sound because this changes each time you play a round.
     Once a team have guessed an item or phrase correctly the card is deemed to be in play with the other team. Unnecessary delays in handing over the card or taking the card are not allowed. Persistent offenders should be penalised by losing a point.
     Teams must pay attention so as not to repeat the description of an item or phrase already featured in that round. Repeating words only wastes time for your team!


Scoring:

     The losing team in a round fails to score, however, the winning team scores 1 point.
     Should the teams manage to guess all ten items and phrases on the card correctly before the buzzer sounds both teams score 1 point.
     For round two change your describing and guessing players.


Winning the game:
The first team to the agreed number of points is the winner!

Good Luck!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Game description from the publisher:

A new animal has come to Farmer Ben's farm — Sparky, the little dog. He immediately starts investigating his new surroundings. Which animals still live on the farm? In Trubel auf dem Bauernhof, players accompany Paulchen as he goes to see the different farmyard animals and listen as they welcome him loudly!

This collection of games offers plenty of variety in the form of a cooperative dice game, fun sound memory game, lightning-fast reaction game, and two story games.

boardgame: Oh Schreck, der Speck fliegt weg!
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Emil's huge elephant stomach is grumbling, so he's going to the supermarket to get some food. At the store, he piles up his shopping cart with one delicious treat after another — but cheeky mice are trying to scare Emil to make his tower of treats fall. In Oh Schreck, der Speck fliegt weg!, the players can help Emil and win only if they have courage, skill and a steady hand.

Game description from the publisher:

Who will be thrilled to enter this big wonderland of games? In Großer Spielezauber, both very small ones and their parents will find ten different games with easy rules and a short playing time, such as a picture bingo, color die game, memory game, domino, stacking game, look-closely game, and many more. Their gradually increasing degree of difficulty and the caringly designed game material will provide long lasting play fun.

Game description from the publisher:

Wow, there are so many things going on here! Today Mimi Mouse is playing with cheese, corn and nuts in the larder. She always has so many fabulous ideas. Be it a memory or die game, the game collection Mimis Kullerspaß — with construction elements of My First Ball Track Sets — offers a lot of diversity. Hence the very smallest can already have ball-rolling fun and become acquainted with first game rules.

Game description from the publisher:

The two cheeky friends Zack Zebra and Luisa Lion are thinking up many funny game ideas for their zoo friends. Who wants to join in?

Be it a memory game, a competition or a look-closely game, the game collection Wo sind Zebra, Löwe & Co? offers to all small animal fans a variety of fun games with simple rules and a short play duration. With this collection, children enter the world of zoo animals and, as they do so, learn fine motor skills, concentration, close observation and a basic understanding of rules. Plus, they can play independently with the wooden figures until their heart's content.

Game description from the publisher:

What do Lilli and her friends want to wear today? Be it a skirt or short trousers, blouse or shirt, sneakers or ballet flats, inside Lilli's wardrobe are the most amazing clothes. But where are the stripy socks again? In the drawer or in the wardrobe? Whoever memorizes well the location of the tiles and has some luck in Lilli's Favorite Clothes will be able to dress her girl first.

The Green Game is an entertaining, enlightening and sometimes alarming way to examine the ecological facts and myths that face us today.

The person who bought the game always goes first. Players roll the die and move their tokens around the circular track. Landing on certain spaces lets the player answer a question. Correct answers reduce the player's carbon footprint, while incorrect answers increase the carbon footprint. Other spaces have players draw "Eco-Warrior" cards that reduce the carbon footprints, or "Eco-Pig" cards that increase the carbon footprint. The first player to reach the smallest carbon footprint on the scoring board (8 points) wins the game.

The Green Game is made from recycled material, soy inks and wooden game pieces.

boardgame: Dogfight D6's: An Aerial Combat, Dice Rolling Game
description:
Take to the skies in a tabletop dogfight! Players take turns rolling dice to soar across the board, attempting to shoot down their opponent's planes while using evasive maneuvers or returning fire. With 12 2-sided, shiftable sky panels that make up the game board, an Event deck that alters battlefield conditions, and the option of choosing to play as 1 of 8 unique pilots instead of a basic ship, no 2 Dogfight games will be the same!

About Dogfight D6's: Whatever version, Dogfight D6's is a dice rolling, aerial combat themed game which was designed to allow for competitive players while still providing appeal to gamers who prefer less wordy, "quick pick up and play" kinds of games. The objective remains the same for either audience (roll dice to move and shoot down planes), however, the rules are written to allow players of both audiences to participate in the same game and enjoy it without being imbalanced towards either one.

Features in this Version:


     2 sided, interchangable playing field pieces- each has 1 "all blue spaces" side and 1 "cloud patterns" side, allowing for a different playing field each game!



    Plane tokens for up to 8 players, although the rules are structured to allow for more players.



     Pilot Cards and Custom Pilot Setup Rules- Now choose either the Basic Pilot Setup (all Stats are set at 2 with no abilities) or use these rules/cards to play a game with unique abilities and rolled Stats.



    Event Cards-Cards which affect gameplay and battlefield conditions as long as they are active.

The star troopers are the regular White Stars troops… Naturally anything regular in the armed forced of the U.N.A. would be extraordinary anywhere else. The star troopers training cycle is designed for the mass production of professional soldiers. The star troopers are gathered in large units whose efficiency is due to both the quality and the number of its soldiers.
The star troopers are the ideal complement to steel trooper units. They drown the enemy is a hailstorm of bullets with their assault rifle. And when the MG starts playing, the hailstorm becomes a hurricane.
For further tactical potential, one of the star troopers can be promoted Sergeant.

Contents:
• Box of 8 miniatures.
• 2 cards : Steel TacArm Sgt, Steel TacArm

“Imagine yourself overlooking a battlefield from thirty five thousand kilometers above. Death from on high.”
– Wing troopers’ advertisement campaign, AT-42.

The Attachment Box: Wing Troopers contains all the miniatures necessary to strengthen a standard wing trooper unit: officers (ranking from sergeant to colonel), a medic and special weapon bearers (a laser gun, a missile launcher and two sniper guns).

The wing troopers form extremely fast and deadly units. They are the spearhead of the offensive. Their favorite strategy is to strike behind enemy lines, catching the enemy in a pincer movement, and fall back on a defensive position once it is done.

With an extended choice of special weapons and the addition of officers to their ranks, your wing troopers can now face any kind of threat. There are literally going fall upon the enemy!

Contents: 
• Box of 6 miniatures + 6 alternative heads, 1 officer with assault rifle, combat knife, grenades. 1 missile launcher bearer. 1 Volcano MG bearer. 1 flamer bearer. 1 medic with assault rifle, combat knife & grenades. 1 field engineer with assault rifle, combat knife & grenades. 
• 8 cards : Wing trooper officer x5, Wing trooper x3

A long time ago, a little before the Middle Ages, flourished the kingdom of Abraxas. Queen Gourmandine IV ruled the kingdom of gastronomes with an iron fist in a velvet potholder. Each year, the greatest magicians of the known world competed to create new recipes as delectable as they could. It's now Midsummer, and the Abraxians are looking forward to that special tournament day when the most powerful magicians of the kingdom face off before everyone else gets to chown down!

In Magic Cooking, you take the role of one of these magicians and use the available ingredients the best way that you can. Whose cooking will reign supreme?

boardgame: AT-43 Hero Box: Lieutenant Dragomira O-3
description:
"Right away, Sir!" "But...I haven't given you any orders yet?!?" "No need. I know." - Lieutenant Dragomira O-3, before her first engagement.

Dragomira O-3 is an officer and a hero for the Red Blok army. She can become the officer of any Dragomirov kolossus unit. Lieutenant Dragomira O-3 is a monster of endurance that not only allows to increase the maximum number of fighters in her unit, but she also provides it with an excellent officer. Her "Gestalt" ability increases the leadership point pool of her company. The special rules for Dragomira O-3 are detailed in AT-43 Campaign: Frostbite. T

Contains:
    1 miniature
    1 card

“In the absence of orders, seek something worth shooting and destroy it.”
- Popular RPG soldaty instruction.

Here come the officers available to lead the RPG soldaty to victory. They lead their men with an iron hand and obtain such obedience from them that these officers are respected for their leadership skills throughout the universe.

To make sure nothing will ever stop your RPG soldat units, here are some extra special weapons and specialists. There will never be too many soldiers to defend the Revolution!

The box contains 6 miniatures plus the appropriate cards.

Players acquire various objects at public auction. Each object is present three times: in good condition, in poor condition and a fake object. The goal is to capture as much works of art of great value, avoiding works in poor condition or even fake art.

In each round, the first player turns over a action card. If it shows an auction, the player takes the hammer of auctioneer and take the object card with the starting price 2000 francs.  The best bid wins the card, then the purchaser discovers the condition of the object he just acquired!

Some action cards allows player to look at the condition of the object.

The goal is to acquire objects forming series - a series of three objects is double the amount of objects that compose it. 
When all the object cards are sold, the player with the most valuable collection is the winner.

The board represents a grid map of 8 columns x 9 rows with a central zone comprising six central squares and a totem pole in the center!
Each player has 12 pawns and 3 riders. Each pawn moves one step forward or sideways, only the rider can go back. Riders are always moved after the pawns.
All pawns can take a shot at a time, unless the rider who can play two consecutively. 
Only pawns can enter the central squares.
The first who eliminates all opposing checkers with at least one of his pawns in the sacred precincts wins a round and scores points. The first player to reach 300 points wins the game.

Tongbrekers (a.k.a. "Tongue Twisters") is a Dutch game in which players draw cards (from a deck of 51 cards), then attempt to correctly utter the tongue twister on that card three times in a row at an ever faster speed. If they succeed, they get to keep the card, which can be used to complete sets of cards for extra points. In addition to the 48 regular cards — which come in eight colors, with two cards in each of three degrees of difficulty — the game includes three special cards that allow a player to attempt to steal a card from someone else. The game ends once all cards have been drawn, and the player with the most points wins.

Return to the 18th century when Łazienki Królewskie (Royal Baths Park) was the summer residence of King Stanisław August Poniatowski. Deliver to the king the most gorgeous artwork and win the game!

Łazienki Królewskie, which literally means "Royal Baths", is the largest park in Warsaw, Poland. The park-and-palace complex lies on the "Royal Route" linking the Royal Castle with Wilanów palace to the south.

In the game Łazienki Królewskie, players use movement cards to try to meet the king walking around the park. When a meeting occurs, the artist delivers artwork and the player receives victory points. Each turn, the player can move up to three different artists and perform the corresponding actions or draw cards. The player who ends up with the biggest artwork collection — and thus the most victory points — wins.

The game has an educational value in that the artwork collected by players is real and can be seen in Łazienki Królewskie. Three real historical artists — Marcello Bacciarelli, André Le Brun and Jean Martin — are included in the game.

Game description from the publisher:

The notorious pirate Captain Sabertooth is looking for a new crew member — but not just any pirate wannabe can join him. If the players want to be hired by Cap'n Sabertooth in Hetzen nach Schätzen, they'll have to prove to him how clever they are and join him on a treasure hunt! They need to run as fast as they can from one treasure chest to another to get rid of their pirate cards. Whoever first discards all of his pirate cards at the treasure chests correctly, then picks up Pirate Fex from the compass card wins this fast-paced treasure hunt!

Game description from the publisher:

As speedy as a snail and as heavy as a bear — hmm, which is slower and which weighs more? As clever animal detectives who are always tracking down different animals, players in Schneckenschnell & bärenschwer have to answer questions like these and many others! At the three check stops, they receive valuable hints as to the animals' speed, size and weight. Whoever draws the right conclusions from the hints given can win the game. With this game, all players can broaden their animal knowledge in a fun way.

Show me as I can't hear anything!
Draw without saying anything!
Tell me, but without saying too much!

In the party game Babelo, players compete individually or in teams to race across the board from start to finish. On a turn, the active player or team rolls a die, moves his pawn the indicated number of spaces, then secretly reads the word or phrase on the card associated with the number rolled. Depending on the color of the space where the pawn stands, the player now presents clues to the other players in a particular manner:


    On a blue space, the player can use only gestures, as in charades. The player cannot write, draw, or make any sounds at all.



    On a red space, the player must describe the phrase using only synonyms, descriptions and other words that suggest the phrase without repeating any part of it.



    On a purple space, the player must draw the phrase without talking, gesturing, making sounds or writing any words.
