boardgame: Cthulhu Wars: Great Old One Pack Three
description:
Gobogeg, Sandy Petersen's Original Great Old One Miniature Figure for the Cthulhu Wars game.

Gobogeg, the Twice-Invoked. This is our interpretation of the awful vision which Danforth received in the final scenes of Lovecraft’s “At the Mountains of Madness”. Gobogeg is the embodiment of the Moon-Ladder – the final destruction of all Earth existence, as a new moon bursts forth from our surface, and the continents and seas dissolve into seething magma. Naturally enough, his presence changes the rules for ALL the players in Cthulhu Wars in a huge way.

The first player to Awaken Gobogeg takes the loyalty card, and places the figure on the map, under his control.

Gobogeg comes with its own unique power, plus if you can fulfill the requirement for its spellbook, you can enhance it even more.

Comes as the Cthulhu Wars Great Old One Pack Three.

Night Shift is a Solitaire board game with Survival Horror themes. It describes the efforts of three different characters (an FBI agent, a security guard, and a mercenary) as they seek to survive a night of Lovecraftian horror as monsters and madness descend upon a storage facility nestled between the sea and a spooky wood.

The game is played out on an 8x8 hex grid and simulates the escape of a character through a simple set of movement and action rules. Enemies actions are controlled by a simple set of A.I., unique to each enemy, while the scenario to be played can be determined randomly and can be modified slightly according to preference or dice roll. The main driving force of the game is a narrative table, aptly called the Horror Table, which produces results that apply mechanical changes to game play as well as describe the nature of the dark events befalling your character. Couple with a search table, Night Shift creates an environment rife with spooky stuff.

This game was originally created for the 2013 Solitaire Print and Play contest.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A traditional Dutch game from the Zeeland region, very similar to the version of the Danish-Norwegian game Gnav or the Swedish Kille, played with wooden pawns rather than cards, of which it is a derivative; all of these games are actually reimplements of the 16th century French 'hazard' card game Coucou, later popularized in 17th century Italy as Cuccú (Cuckoo).

A set consists of 42 wooden pawns with a number or picture hidden under the base. Each of the 21 subject occurs twice: the numbers from 0 to 12, the trumps "Rider", "Bird", "Cat" and "Inn" above the numbers, and "Whitebread", "Mug", "Pot" and "Jester" below.

At the beginning of the game, each player recieves a number of point tokens. In each round, each player draws one pawn from a bag and secretly looks at its value. Then the players in order decides whether to keep their pawn or trade it with the following player. If that player holds a trump, special rules for each trump applies, otherwise the pawns are traded. At the end of the round, the player with the lowest valued pawn loses one of their point tokens.

A player who runs out of point tokens is out of the game. The last remaining player is the winner.

It is not known exactly when this little known variant first appeared. It must be after the the wooden pawn variant of Gnav – originally a card game – gained popularity as an effect of a ban on playing cards by the pietist king Christian VI of Denmark-Norway (1730–1746). On the other hand it probably did not happen very much later, as it lacks a couple of the developments otherwise separating Gnav from other games in the Cuccu family.

From the introduction to the rules:

Straight Jacket is a fast, fun card game for 3 or more players — 4 or 5 is best.

In Straight Jacket, the players take on the roles of inmates wearing straight jackets and trapped in a padded cell. All of you want to escape, but you’re not exactly pragmatic about it. The person with the most points at the end of the games, wriggles from their jacket and escapes.

User summary:

The deck consists of 75 poker-sized cards.

Players draw up to three cards to form a hand of three, play the lowest value card from their hand of three to their play area, then pass one card each to the left and right of them.  Cards may have instructions which are to be followed when they are played.  Play continues until the draw pile is exhausted.

Splush is a group-gathering game for Shibumi that uses entries and slide drops. The board starts as shown below:

● ○ ● ○
○ ● ○ ●
● ○ ● ○
○ ● ○ ●

Players then take turns either:


     Entering a ball of their color from the side onto a 2x2 platform, which may push other balls, or



     Removing a ball of their color and entering it back into play as per move 1; this removal may cause a drop.


Entering a ball may cause a drop but may not push any balls off the board. You cannot enter a ball onto any ball that has just
dropped as part of the move. A player wins by connecting all of their visible balls (when viewed from above) into a single connected group.

"The Game of Promises and Ultimate Power" is mostly a roll & move design with some negotiation and voting in the long version of the game.

Themed around British politics with the iconic black door of 10 Downing Street as the box art, the object of this game is to become Prime Minister. In the short version of the game, the winner is the first person to do so whereas in the long version, the role of Prime Minister can change hands and the game is said to be won by "the last person to remain in the game after all the other players have tired of the Prime Minister's leadership".

Players roll and move their way around the board, trying to pick up Vote cards and debating other players if they land on the same space. In the short version, such debates are decided by rolling dice but in the long version the players must act out their side of the debate and the winner is chosen by a vote of the other players.  Players must also face "chance"-like Affairs cards when they roll doubles and may end up in Jail or Hospital where they must roll dice to be set free.

Players consecutively work their way through the Town Hall, Parliament and Ministry spaces to reach Number Ten. They are paid a higher salary for reaching each of those levels but then require a greater number of votes to proceed to the next.  In the long game, the current Prime Minister can influence this process by exercising special powers such as awarding a government grant to a player or sending someone to Jail.

Dice-game for 2-4 players.

A roll and move game where you should be first to reach number 39. If you land on a number with a 2 or 6 then you have to move backwards 2 or 6 steps. If you land on number 10 you have to start all over again.

Auuuuuuuugh! Your publisher is breathing down your neck, so now it's a late-night, caffeine-fueled, rattling-keyboard-pounding race to get the first chapter of your thriller novel Invasive Species finished before he decides to tear up your lucrative contract. Through blind inspiration (drawing cards) and expert cut-and-paste editing (arranging cards on your board), you strive to complete your chapter before it's too late.

In the card game Invasive Species, players create a story by using 36 cards that have numbered text showing where each card should go in the story. Each turn, a player draws a card and decides whether he wants to keep it or give it to his opponent. He places any kept cards on his personal board, with the goal of creating an ascending numeral sequence. When the deck runs out, players take turns sliding misplaced cards into their correct positions. The first player to correctly "write" his story wins.

Invasive Species is based on the novel of the same name by Mike Mayer.

From the Box:
In this "push your luck" dice game, players attempt to acquire stages by matching their dice rolls to the icons on the card.  These cards make you more powerful against the bosses.  Each boss that falls before you unlocks a more powerful die.  Once a player has defeated a set number of bosses, the game is over and the person with the highest core is the winner.

Featuring all pixel artwork, 5 unique scrolling stages, 20 enemies, 5 bosses, and even Basic and Advanced variants.

boardgame: Run, Fight, or Die!: Guy Promo Character Board
description:
This promotional character board was handed out at GenCon 2013 if you participated in a demo of "Run, Fight, or Die!"  It illustrates the character "Guy."

It was also shipped as a reward to the Kickstarter backers in 2014

Description:
Castle Defenders combines the strategy elements of chess with a spell system like Magic: the Gathering™. It was inspired by the brilliant video game Hero Academy™ for iOS and contains similar gameplay mechanics. The combat system has been simplified to better fit the physical board game format.

Goal:
Players send units to attack enemy castles and defend their own. You win by destroying all enemy castles OR units.

Gameplay: 
-The game board resembles a rectangular chess board. Players agree on a layout and arrange their castles and bonus tiles (extra attack, shields, or bonus castle damage).
-Players maintain a 6-card hand from their personal deck containing units (mage, healer, knight, etc.), spells, and equipment.
-On their turn, players choose 5 actions to perform (cast a spell, move a unit, attack, heal, etc...).
-Part of the game is trying out different moves to find the best plan, so you decide when your move is final.
-Players receive bonus spell cards when two of their units are destroyed (this balances gameplay and allows you to counter an opponent's momentum).

Strategy elements:
-Units have strengths/weaknesses and must work as a team.
-Formations help reinforce attack/defense effectiveness.
-Board location and unit placement is key (e.g. "Is my Knight far enough away to avoid being knocked out?" "Is my Archer close enough to knock out the enemy Healer?").
-Unit trading: killing enemy units can leave yours exposed...is it worth the risk?

Trade gems in Rabat: a fast-paced, frantic and loud card game. You need to react fast and try not to loose track.

5 Gem cards and 3 mission cards are displayed in the middle of the table.

All players play simultaneously trade single gem cards with the displayed cards on the table or other players as fast as they can. The first to have the exact match for a mission card shouts "rabat," discards his cards and gets the mission card, then draws 5 new gem cards and reveals a new mission card to trade gems for.

Each mission card is worth points. When there are no more mission cards left the game ends and the player with the most points wins.

Coup: Reformation, is an expansion for the original version of Coup: City State from La Mame Games. It adds new cards to the game and rules for factions and team play. This increases tension in the early stages for four or more players, and 15 additional character cards allows Coup to be better played with up to ten players.

With Coup: Reformation, each player must declare themself either Loyalist or Reformist (Catholic and Reformist in the first edition) and can target only members of the other faction. Conversion is possible, however, for yourself or for another player by paying a charitable donation to the Treasury (Almshouse). Like all factions, once you have eliminated or converted the other group, you just descend into in-fighting, so there's still only one winner and no second place.

Coup: Reformation adds a new fluid team dynamic to Coup as players jostle with their allegiance to take advantage or seek protection in the early stages of the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A dice game for simulating a game of basketball.  There are 4 six-sided dice included, 2 for the Home Team and 2 for the Visiting Team (1 Offense die and 1 Defense die for each).  Players take turns rolling their Offensive die against their opponent's Defense die.  Points are scored by comparing the rolls against a chart.

Comes with a miniature duffel bag to store the dice.

Game description from the publisher:

You are at a masked ball in 18th century France. No one knows who you really are, but in this frivolous world of manners and mockery, your friends are out to expose you. Can you work out the clues and mock them first?

In Pied de Nez (a.k.a. "thumbing your nose"), you have a hidden identity and want to figure out which of the other players are your two closest friends. Clues are revealed slowly; every time you reveal some information about yourself, you earn a drink and you need to use those drinks to be able to do a "pied de nez" at the people for whom you are searching.

Pied de Nez is an analytical game of deduction and hidden identities for 6-10 players, with the game being playable (but not ideal) with 4-5 players.

La Puta i la Ramoneta (in Catalan) is a card game of political fiction to undo the tie between Catalonia and Spain. Catalan independentist, Spanish unionist, Autonomists and other characters will pursue their goals and play the cards the best they can to add up points and subtract the adversary ones. Each character will be able to negotiate and play the roles of the Puta and Ramoneta, in other words, to deceive or to prevent deception.

The game is a satire to the actual system of politics in Catalonia. It wants to dedramatize the process of independence and rivalry between the two nations.

Each player takes a position in the political representation and tries to win by accumulating points of Culture, Economy and Laws to eventually carry out his political project. Whether it is Catalan independence, more autonomy or Spanish centralization.

description:
Lucie is a little mouse whose stomach is making gurgling noises.

For Lucie, it is time to stock up. So, she has to get out of her hole and explore the house to find cheese. To feed her whole family, she really needs to find lots and lots of cheese and… the more the better!

So she is now ready to get these delicious small golden cubes. But be careful as mousetraps are scattered all over the house. And above all, Minou, the big cat of the house, is ready to jump over her and to munch her.

Like Lucie, you are a small mouse ready to do anything for delicious cheese. 
Now go hunting but do not forget to get back into your hole in time. Otherwise, you can lose everything.

When you get out of your hole, you draw the first card from the pile and you place the face-up card in front of you.  Now it is time for you to make a decision with what you have on your card or just because you like taking some risks.
Either you go back into your hole with your precious cheese or you draw a new card to get more.
If your decision is to draw a new card, you will be facing the same options: either you go back into your hole or you keep on drawing.
You can then keep on drawing, but be careful! If you draw at least 3 cats or 3 mousetraps successively, you will be caught and all the cards you have drawn during that round will be lost. They will then be the discard pile.
If you decide to go back into your hole, you will get back the cards you have drawn during that round. Pile these cards, face down, in front of you. From now on, these cards will always be yours. Once your turn is over, the player on your left can start playing.
The game is over as soon as all the cards are drawn. Each mouse counts the bits of cheese she has on her own cards. To win, you must have more cheese than the other players.

Two variants are included in the game :
-cards Dog and cards Discreet mouse 
-six role cards

"The Pirate Deck" is a card game for 2-4 players. Players take turns strategically using pirate cards to board a ship and steal treasure by matching card icons.

Games take only 10-25 minutes to play. The games are quick and lead you to want to play a second round. While this is a light game, there is strategy involved in order to make the best use of the cards in your hand and complete as many coin icons as possible.

Gameplay begins with setting up 5 "Starting Cards" in front of the players in the shape of a shared pirate ship, along with a pile of 25 treasure coins. Players start their turn with three cards in their hand, and take turns playing cards to try to match up coin icons on the ship. Pirate cards can be added to the ship in specific locations, replace other pirates, or be removed from the ship entirely (with adjacent cards being pushed together to close the gap). A variety of other cards add another layer of interest and player interaction to the game. For each silver coin icon matched on the ship, one actual coin is taken from the treasure pile. Two coins are taken for matching a gold coin icon. When the pile runs out, the player with the most treasure coins wins.

description:
X-3 is played on a hexagonal board of 61 spaces. The spaces are designed so that dice can fit into them with three faces showing, one along each axis of the board. The playing pieces are dice in two base colors (black and white) with six differently-colored faces.

There are rules for two games in the box:

Morpion/Triplets/Ensembles

        A placement game; players alternate placing dice in an effort to form patterns. If using the "Morpion" rule players try to get four faces of the same color in the same direction in a row (or five for a more challenging game). If using the "Triplets" rule players try to create groups of three dice with matching-colored faces touching the same point, and if using the "Ensembles" rule players try to create groups of four dice where the center die matches a face to each adjacent die. (It may be that players are intended to use all three scoring methods together; the rules are rather short and not clear in all respects.)


Dames de Couleur (Color Checkers)

        In this game players start with 18 dice in the three rows closest to them (facing determined randomly) and on each turn move one diagonally forward. Opposing pieces can be jumped, but only if the adjacent dice faces match. The players can turn dice to different faces instead of moving them. The goal is to capture the most dice.

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Bol'Goreth Lieutenant Pack
description:
A hulking figure, tainted with evil, is emerging from the murky depths of the Valdari Swamp, spreading pestilence and plague wherever he steps. Bol'Goreth, who originally appeared as a new lieutenant for the Overlord to control in the Trollfens expansion, now appears in this standalone expansion — Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) - Bol'Goreth Lieutenant Pack — to allow him to be used without having to play the Trollfens campaign.

In addition to including a sculpted plastic figure that can replace the existing lieutenant token, this Lieutenant Pack introduces the "Raging Infection" Plot deck of specialized cards, which can be triggered during encounters by using deadly new threat tokens.

The Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) - Raythen Lieutenant Pack reflects the dwarf rogue's duality by offering you everything you need to play Raythen as a hero or as the overlord's lieutenant. In any game outside of the Labyrinth of Ruin campaign, you can use the included Hero sheet to play as Raythen in a quest. You'll be able to steal search tokens with your monsters and keep the best treasures out of the heroes' hands with cards like "Foiled Again". The overlord can disguise his dark schemes with the mirages and phantoms contained in this Plot deck, using them to fool the heroes and keep them off guard en route to their ultimate demise. Meanwhile, your monsters with the Cursed trait will reap the benefits of Ariad's influence, and with Phantasm, you can lead the heroes on a merry chase.

description:
As per box:

Can learning be fun too? You bet! Play and find out for yourself! The Junior Oxford Game comes with more than 1500 challenging questions divided into 23 exciting word games, and well over 100 Wild Word Fights. Improve your skills as you learn to understand the explosive power of language!. It's exciting, it's fast, and it's fun! The Junior Oxford Game has been written in close association with School Teachers and English language experts from Oxford University Press.

Keep your word a secret!. Your heart pounds as you race across the board to collect the letters of your secret word. Spin the disc to try your luck! A fast game about spaceships movement.

Each turn the players move their spaceships, each one with different speed and try to reach the orbital station. In each turn every player can rotate some parts of the board for trying to delay the movement of the other player, causing him to waste useful time, trying to regain the path that leads to victory.

description:
Luna is a Pachisi based game, produced by Spika in East Germany in 1966. There are three each of six player pieces, which are small spaceships or satellites. They are precision cast in red, black, green, yellow, white and blue plastic.

The goal of the game is to "land" all of your spacecraft on the moon first. The board pips help you advance or retard a player's movement toward the moon. The movements are determined by the roll of a single die.

The game seems to be inspired by the space race, and the game tokens bear some resemblance to the Sputnik satellite. Greg Stachowski added this information, "The rocket looks to be pretty generic, perhaps based on a Kosmos. The spacecraft above each of the rockets is clearly a Vostok. Actually the artwork is damn good, those are pretty accurate renderings of all the spacecraft, even down to little details like the curved antennae on the Vostok or the hexagonal base frame on the Ranger (in fact given the accuracy of the others I might hazard a guess that it is a 1964 or later Ranger (6 through 9) due to the rectangular rather than trapezoidal panels)." Greg's identification of the satellites is in a labeled image he prepared.

Publisher's Description: 
How do you think a compass is related to fried eggs? What does a roller skate have to do with a ballerina? It’s up to you! This game (for both adults and kids) inspires you to come up with creative connections between seemingly unrelated things. Pluck objects from the bag and imagine an association—poetic, practical, serious, or silly—to reveal hidden webs of meaning and surprising details about how you perceive the world. There are no wrong answers—only intriguing ideas. Be pensive. Be spontaneous. The setup and rules are simple. The possibilities are infinite.

Contents:

     1 Two Sided Game Board
     1 Drawstring Bag
     85 Object Chips


Setup:
Choose one side of the board to play on, the blue (easier) or green (harder).  Provide slips of paper and writing instruments to the players.  Each player draws 3 object chips, face up, from the draw bag.  The youngest player chooses one object chip blindly from the bag and places it in the center of the board.

Game Play:
Each round, one player (the curator) places an object chip on the board, connected to a previously played object chip.  Other players take three minutes to privately choose an association between the object and the others to which it is connected on the board, writing it on a slip of paper.  When three minutes are up, the curator shuffles and reads the examples aloud.  Each player, except the curator, votes on the best connection, drawing one object chip blindly for each vote received.  The role of curator then moves to the next player counterclockwise.  When all players have served as curator, the player with the most objects is the winner.

An alternate "cooperative" ruleset is provided for a puzzle activity experience, placing pieces on the board and attempting to make compelling associations.

Medieval Japan has so much to offer in the way of scenery! Vast mountains, quaint villages, hidden shrines and much more. The real treasures however are those that jump and fly - the rare brown squirrels, cranes, egrets and eagles who make their homes in remote locations. These hard to find creatures are the ultimate destination of any tour of the countryside.

In Gaido, players take on the role of guides and travelers journeying through the countryside of medieval Japan. Guide duties are shared, with each player taking the responsibility for one day of their journey. The guide directs the group of players through the Japanese landscape, to their journey’s end. Only the current guide can decide when a journey is over.

There are four animals the group is trying to spot, squirrels, cranes, egrets and eagles. These animals are found in four different locations and represent the end of a journey. On the play table, players will create four stacks of cards, called Journeys. These stacks represent the different paths to the four animals.

Every Journey card has a Kanji symbol on it which indicates how many points each player will receive when that stack is scored. Cards can be played facing any direction. There are sixteen Journey cards and four Journey’s End cards which, when played on one of the four journeys will immediately end that journey and score that stack of cards.

Gaido offers interesting play choices, with a strategy that’s deeper than it first appears and a simple play style. Only 20 cards and 24 counters in size, it’s played in an average of 15-20 minutes.

Common root vegetables? Simplicity in a burlap sack? NOOOOO – Spud is a load of hot-hot-HOT potatoes and sizzling swift thinking and rapid reacting! Wild mayhem spills out and reckless hot potato passing begins!

THE OBJECT:
Move around the Potato Cards by following the directions on the Action Cards. Avoid being stuck with Potato Cards. The person with 5 Potato Cards is out. (Toss potatoes to other players quickly and repeatedly. Remember, the potatoes are smoking hot. Move fast my friends, or you will get burned.)

THE CARDS:
Spud consists of different cards: Action Cards & Potato Cards.

The Potato Cards – have potatoes. 
Laughable characters like “Wedge”, “Tots”, “Yukon”, and “Couch.” (They are a hoot.)

The Action Cards – have actions to explain where potatoes should be passed. 
“Right” “Left” “Anyone”… you get the picture. Draw a “Spud!” card and you get to draw a Potato Card to give to another player.

THE SNAFUS:
The action cards have actions, and they also have colors. Green, orange, blue. A Potato Card can only be moved if the color on the Action Card is the same of that on the Potato Card. If you do not have an action card of the right color to move a Potato Card – you’re stuck with it.

Among the Potato Cards are “Rotten Potato,” he’s one tough character. Draw him and you’ve got to keep him. And some deceptively sweet-looking potato cards that count as two potatoes.

Some of the cards create general chaos – “Everybody immediately draw 3 Action Cards”, “Everyone pass your hand to the left.”

ENDING THE GAME: 
When you are piled up with 5 potatoes, you are out. Though the fun still continues until only one player is left. The last player standing (or sitting) without 5 potatoes is the winner!

To win this race anything is justified!  
Change the race track surface, change the weather condition, throw obstacles in the path of your opponents.  Even change car parts or the driver!

Board game for all ages, created during SPJam 2013

Game Play: 
"'ReviraVolta' {'Turnaround'} is a racing game using cards.  These cards are used to represent your car and driver during the race; the condition of the track and the weather, obstacles, and special events.  Initially your car and driver are represented by 3 cards drawn at random, composing the driver, the car body, and the car's method of locomotion.  You have a hand of five cards at your disposal.  The first player to complete a lap of the race track is the winner.

Each turn you have a chance to take an action and then move your car, or you can move your car and then act. The distance your car moves depends on the interaction between the parts of your car, and the prevailing condition of the track and the weather.

As an action you can play a card from your hand to replace parts of your car or driver to improve the distance your car moves.  You can also play a card to replace the prevailing conditions, possibly increasing your car movement, or adversly effecting the movement of the other players.  There are also obstacle cards to slow down another player.  'Revira volta' cards swap the driver or parts of the car amongst all the players.  At the end of your turn you draw cards to return to a hand of 5.

Use your action wisely because in Revira Volta the winner is the player who can overcome obstacles, and use the weather variations and changes to the race track to their best advantage!"


Pra vencer essa corrida vale tudo: trocar o terreno da pista, as condições do clima, colocar obstáculos na frente dos oponentes, e até mesmo trocar o seu carro ou o piloto !!!

Jogo de tabuleiro para todas as idades, criado durante o SPJam 2013.

O Jogo: 
"Em Reviravolta, todos começam com um carro e cinco cartas na mão. Durante seu turno o jogador deve usar uma carta e comprar uma para repor a mão.

Com esta carta ele pode mudar o clima, o terreno, uma parte de seu carro ou até atrapalhar o adversário.
Use com inteligência, para ganhar vantagens ou atrapalhar os outros competidores.

Em Reviravolta vence aquele que for mais rápido e perspicaz, ultrapassar obstáculos, variações climáticas e mudanças de terreno.
"

Ever thought of becoming president? Now you can! Give interviews, bribe, foment protests, play good and evil to become the most popular politician ever.

Plebiscitum is a card game, based on a contemporary international politics theme. It is played in teams called parties, fighting to win the elections.

In order to win the elections the player must fulfill both of the following two requirements:
- The player’s party must be the most popular.
- The player must be the most popular within his/her party.

The backbone of Plebiscitum is the idea that players should try to make their own party win, and at the same time beat their own party members in order to get elected. To do so, players can play cards both against other parties’ members or against their own party members.

Batty is basically a competitive version of the classic Tower of Hanoi puzzle. Players each get three cards (A, B, and C) to place stacks on and then try to get a given set of numbered cards arranged in one stack in descending numerical order; the cards are moved from stack to stack with the players forbidden to place a card on a card with a lower number.

Players start with three cards, then proceed to four the next round, etc., up to a maximum of fourteen (the number of cards provided per player). All except the highest-numbered card in each round are shuffled, which is the only difference from the usual Tower of Hanoi puzzle.

This Promo for Rokoko includes 4 new Dresses which are more expensive to make but are also more rewarding.
Furthermore, it includes 5 additional Resource tiles with new combinations of resources.

Yedo: Sakoku, a mini-expansion for the Yedo base game, includes a thematic event card that's shuffled together with the other event cards. When the "Sakoku" card is revealed, the church burns down and is covered with the included "burned-down church" tile. From this moment on, players cannot gain Mon from the church.

Daredevils & Co. is a steampunk-fantasy tactical card game for 2-8 players. Lead your army of brightness or forces of darkness to conquer mighty fortresses. Massive rivalry and adrenaline from beginning to end of the game. Beautiful, diversified cards offer great variety of tactics that lead to the victory.
The goal of the game is to take enemies' Fortresses. Players choose their units and special cards and each turn attack other players or defend their own fortresses. There are the following game variants: 
1. The game ends after an assigned number of sieges. After each siege score is counted up for every Fortress taken. Player who scores the highest after the last siege is the winner. 
2. The game ends when a player takes an assigned number of Fortresses.
3. Both scenarios stated above can be played in two variants:
- Deathmatch - each faction competes against the others.
- Team play - bright factions team up against dark factions.

From Publisher Website

Upgrade Your Classic Fairground!

Do you already own Car Wars Expansion Set #9, Muskogee Fairground & Family Emporium, and just need the expanded information from Muskogee Mayhem, a Car Wars adventure pack to add more autoduelling antics to this ever-popular destination? boardgame: The Blockade Runner: An Exciting Game
description: The Blockade Runner: An Exciting Game demonstrates how board game often reflect current events in American History. The game refers to "the late war with Spain," more commonly known as The Philippine War, from 1899 to 1902. Naval battles, most importantly the battle of Manila Bay, characterized this war and The Blockade Runner game demonstrates this. In the game play, one player takes the part of the runner and the other forms the blockade.

Small World: 6 Player Board, an expansion for both Small World and Small World Underground, is designed for use with exactly six players, with players competing in three teams of two players. Players can discuss strategy (openly) with their teammate, but each player competes and takes all actions on his own: choosing new races, deciding where to attack and (most importantly) scoring. At the end of eight rounds, each team's score is equal to the team member with a lower score — and whichever team has the highest score wins.

Small World: 6 Player Board features a double-sided game board with a surface landscape for use with Small World and an underground world for use with Small World Underground. The expansion includes one relic and four places — concepts introduced in Small World Underground — specially designed for team play, such as a tavern that lets you collect two coins and share them with anyone you want, a mirror that allows you to change powers with your teammate, and a boneyard that lets you protect all of one player's "in decline" troops from racial and special powers owned by opponents. And then there's the Golden Soapbar...

Centerpiece is a roll-and-move game primarily for 2 players, or 4 players in teams. It's described by the designer as "super-charged Monopoly," but it's most similar to Mario Party without the mini-games. The game board is printed on map paper, which allows it to fit inside the small box. After unfolding the board, the colorful box top becomes the "centerpiece" of the game board. Players take turns rolling two dice off of the box top; at least one die must end up on the game board itself, and neither can roll farther than the board's border, or the roll is invalid. They then move the sum of their roll. Every space has special effect that triggers either when landed on or when leaving the space. The goal of the game is to collect feathers, represented by paper money, in order to buy puzzle pieces. Puzzle pieces can be purchased when the player lands on two of the corner spaces, and passing the Start Together space generates an income of 25 feathers. Feathers can also be gained and lost in various ways.

Aside from rolling off the box top, the main distinguishing feature of the game is that each player's pawn is represented by a pair of stacked poker chips. When landing on the Banana Split garden space, the poker chips are split up, and are subsequently treated as separate pawns. If they ever occupy the same space, other than the Banana Split space, they are recombined. Split pieces give more options when moving, and some spaces reward the player for having their chips either combined or separated.

As in Monopoly, rolling doubles allows the player to move again, and rolling snake eyes sends the other player's chips to the Bird Cage (identical to Monopoly's Jail space).

Description from Kickstarter:

Super-charged Monopoly. Dice fall off box, the game travels in. White and black poker-chips get pulled apart and put together.

To play simply learn to drop the dice off the box, and keep them on the game-board.   The very first corner space states, Divide Your Game Parts.  What made that work, are five No/Rest circles added to select spaces.  They take away the choice, of which game-piece to move.  Before I adding those circles, catch-22's would happen.   A different space states, Exchange Your Bank of Feathers.  It allows a player to come from behind at anytime.

The inside of the box works great as a game-bank.   Each time around the board Eagle, Duck and Seagull feathers are rewarded.   The goal of CENTERPIECE, becomes a RACE to purchase nine-puzzle-spaces one at a time.  Have a bank of two-hundred feathers, have game-parts together.  Then land on the Start Sun space, or Bidgecage space to WIN.  Players keep score, with black and white pawns, under the box, at the games center.   Who's best at finding the best price for a numbered puzzle-space, while staying out of the Birdcage corner space.  For example to Rent A Horse, to get to the Half-Price space, or not.

All together there are twenty-eight game spaces.  One last example of a game-space states.  Roll Doubles While Here, and spend three-turns at the Birdcage corner. Your game pieces, if divided, would be joined again.  Rolling doubles while in the Birdcage, lets a player leave early.

Because the game-board paper was designed for new maps, it folds forever.  ( It was the most expensive part of the game.)

CENTERPIECE = fast-paced FUN!!!  Tested on family and friends.   People will ask what's the fun all about?   Copy-righted in 1992, a ten-year renewal was also completed.  Old-school face-to-face battle with new IMPROVED format.

Operational-level wargame simulating the Smolensk battle from July 2nd to August 15th. The main German attack carried out by Two Panzergruppes, the Infantry and support units of Army Group Center as they advance against Russian forces on their way to striking at Moscow.

Command and variable movement system that includes cards that add random events and historical flavor, but do not drive the game. German units are Divisions, Regiments and specialized Battalions. Russian units are untried Divisions and specialized Regiments. Both sides have their own unique manor of play to show their historical capabilities and tendencies.

boardgame: Der kleine König und seine Freunde
description:
The little king and his friends want to paint a pretty picture for his aunt on her birthday, but they've misplaced all the pails of paint. Help them find the paint so that they can paint their colorful picture. Who will collect the most colors?

Der Kleine König includes simplified rules so that children as young as 3 can play, while the standard rules are for ages 7 and up.

Take "two" and "one", and make it "twone"!

In Zweins, cards adorned with pictures are flying onto the table and everybody tries to find one compound word made out of two cards. You might see a leg and a sphere, for example, and combine them to create "football"; a mobile phone and a mouth could combine to "phone call". Who can come up with the most double words?

Zweins includes rules that allow children as young as six to play, while the basic rules are for players aged 10 and up.

The horizon looms. Beyond it lies an unspeakable evil, a malevolent force seeking the destruction of everything. He bends wills. He corrupts. He is the director. All will succumb to his darkness. Already, he has bewitched the minds of a powerful few, and they have fallen under his powerful spell. He will be unstoppable!

You and you alone are the one force that can stand against him. You are the master strategist. Your battle skills and master planning have taken years to perfect. Allies have rushed to your side, willing to fight and die for your cause! You have only a few resources, however, which doesn't bode well. To prevail against this monstrosity would take an army, a legion — everything!

Everything vs. Everything plays like a simplified wargame with units on a field that maneuver and attack one another. The units are powered by special abilities, and you prioritize how and when to use those abilities.

Everything vs. Everything can be played solitaire or as a two-player head-to-head game.

(from Paul Koenig Games website:)

"Gettysburg: Bloody July, 1863" is a two-player brigade level wargame simulation of the Battle of Gettysburg, July 1-3, 1863. As the Confederate player, you must try to crush Meade's army before it can concentrate. As the Union player, you must hold on from the initial shock of the blow, regroup as your army slowly concentrates, and then either weather the storm or launch your own counter-attack against Lee’s outnumbered forces.

GBJ is an old-fashioned beer and pretzels wargame that is not burdened with cumbersome command & control or supply rules.

Paul Koenig's Bloody Civil War Series is based upon the intermediate system of Avalon Hill's Gettysburg 1977.

description:
Game description from the publisher:

"You are a monarch, like your parents before you, a ruler of a small pleasant kingdom of rivers and evergreens. Unlike your parents, however, you have hopes and dreams! You want a bigger and more pleasant kingdom, with more rivers and a wider variety of trees. You want a Dominion! In all directions lie fiefs, freeholds, and feodums. All are small bits of land, controlled by petty lords and verging on anarchy. You will bring civilization to these people, uniting them under your banner.

But wait! It must be something in the air; several other monarchs have had the exact same idea. You must race to get as much of the unclaimed land as possible, fending them off along the way. To do this you will hire minions, construct buildings, spruce up your castle, and fill the coffers of your treasury. Your parents wouldn't be proud, but your grandparents, on your mother's side, would be delighted."

In Dominion, each player starts with an identical, very small deck of cards. In the center of the table is a selection of other cards the players can "buy" as they can afford them. Through their selection of cards to buy, and how they play their hands as they draw them, the players construct their deck on the fly, striving for the most efficient path to the precious victory points by game end.

Dominion is not a CCG, but the play of the game is similar to the construction and play of a CCG deck. The game comes with 500 cards. You select 10 of the 25 Kingdom card types to include in any given play — leading to immense variety.

Dominion: Special Edition includes the Dominion base game — with the coin cards bearing new artwork first seen in the Dominion Base Cards accessory — as well as the "Black Market" card originally released as a promotional item.

Part of the Dominion series.

description:
“Don’t try to anticipate every move. Just anticipate the moves that matter.”
    –Reina Roja

Double Time is the sixth and final Data Pack in the "Spin Cycle" for Android: Netrunner, and it introduces a handful of powerful Double events and operations. Doubles all require their owner to spend a click to play them, in addition to their normal costs, meaning they’re high-risk gambles in a game that’s as full of bluffs, counter-bluffs, risks, and gambles as Android: Netrunner.

Still, if you play them right, these Doubles can offer tremendous rewards. In Double Time, you’ll find a significant focus on these events. Twenty-one of them are mixed into the Data Pack’s sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty individual cards), along with new hardware, programs, ice, upgrades, and more. Will you sacrifice versatility for raw power? It’s time to Double down!

boardgame: Bloody Buron: The First Step to Caen
description:
BLOODY BURON - The First Step to Caen

Bloody Buron is a Scenario / Campaign Game pack which offers players the unique challenge of attacking a prepared defensive position, set up guarding the Norman village of Buron, Northwest of Caen, France in the summer of 1944:

- One colour 24 x 30 inch historical map of  the Normandy countryside northwest of Caen,

- Four Scenarios played on various parts of the map,

- An Four-Scenario Date, Campaign Game; and

- Chapter BB that includes the rules to play the Campaign Game.

The four included scenarios focus on the following situations:

Buron, France, 8 July 1944: Operation Charnwood opened at 0730-hrs, but the 59th British Division was still struggling to win its initial objectives of Galmanche, La Bijude, and St. Contest. Once more Buron would be attacked by the Canadians with St. Contest still in the hands of the fanatical 12th SS-Hitler Youth Division under the leadership of Kurt Panzer Meyer. For the Highland Light Infantry, raised in Waterloo County, Ontario, this fight was to be their first major battle... 
 Buron, France, 8 July 1944: On the right flank, D Company, came under heavy mortar, artillery, and machine-gun fire as it assaulted the anti-tank ditch and the positions immediately behind it. The fighting was heavy and close-in, numerous times D Company was pinned to the ground by the intensive fire, only to get up and continue its advance went it let up, or was quelled by the Sherbrooke’s tanks, called up to aid them... 
 Buron, France, 8 July 1944: On the left flank, B Company, had orders to clear the eastern half of the village. Closing up with the anti-tank ditch, it began to encounter heavy opposition from not only the ditch, but positions directly behind it. In the face of heavy fire, B Company again and again charged the entrenched Hitler Youth, who were equipped with large numbers of automatic weapons. When unable to advance further, due to intense fire, the Sherbrooke’s tanks were called up, but communications started to go haywire... 
 Buron, France, 8 July 1944: The Highland’s B Company fought to secure the western half of the town, while D Company struggle to secure the eastern half; and by late morning, both lead companies had suffered heavy casualties, but had managed to gain a foothold in the village. C Company then moved forward between the two-lead assault companies, assaulting the village and pressing the Canadians' attack...

A Hero Will Rise to Face the Agents of Darkness.

The Spriggan model is unique to KS, but the deck used is not.

The Spriggan uses spells. It has two types of spells: attacking spells that awaken nature to attack the darkness like Bee Swarm or Gaia's embrace, and spells that equip the Spriggan with temporary items: like Oaken Armor and Willow's Kiss.

The equip spells provide the Spriggan with items it can equip and use with it's more standard attack cards. These items have limited hit points or do limited Blood. When they are spent, the item goes away.

There will be only one miniature for the Spriggan. We consider it genderless.

description:
Have you ever wanted to be a warrior-poet?

A new Skald Hero for Myth.  An amazing new character with a very cool play style.  Two miniatures, one male and one female.

The Skald is a heavy AP user and carries high Threat as long as you are belting out boisterous ballads to lay low fated foes.

From the company website:

The game is played out between forces composed of several vehicles and infantry squads per side. Each vehicle has a number of design options regarding which weapons are selected, engine size and various offensive and defensive accoutrements such as extra armour, targeting devises, smoke dischargers etc. This means that players can create very different vehicles from the same chassis. 
With the infantry we have divided them into a number of categories such as Light Infantry, Assault Infantry, Warriors and Heavy Weapons. Each category has a defined profile, a set of stats, weapons and armour that help it conform to their definition. This means that each type of squad has a particular function on the battlefield.

Light troops for instance move fast across the battlefield are good at scouting out the enemies dispositions and also getting out of trouble fast. Assault troops are designed to get in close with the enemy - they have the armour and firepower to take the fight to their opponents. The Warriors role is obviously to make contact with the enemy and destroy them in deadly hand-to-hand fighting. And Heavy Weapons teams are designed to hang back a bit and support forward units with heavy long-range fire.

Players will therefore need to learn how to use their troops to their best advantage as well as how to combine them with other squads in their force for maximum efficiency and effect. For instance, using light troops to identify targets, using heavy weapons to weaken and suppress the target and then sending in the warriors to finish them off at close quarters. Sounds easy? Not so in practise! What this simply means that it is not just a case of the dude with the biggest gun winning the day (although the game does have plenty of big guns!). The right troops have to be used at the right time in the right way.

The game also includes a number of leader types. These are heroes with certain special skills that will boost the performance of the force. Some of these figures might be just great individual fighters, others may have leadership skills that can enhance the power of the rest of the force.

What is very apparent to players is the amount of choice that they have in creating their force, from vehicle design to infantry squad selection and leader choice. A different army can be assembled for every game. 
Most battles will be fought over very challenging terrain. All manner of risks and rewards can be found - from hidden obstacles, explosive and flammable materials but also extra ammunition and weapons. The terrain always plays its part in games of Sulphur.

Another vital component to the game is the use of Asset Cards. These are played and collected during the game to further enhance the performance of a force. Each card is specific to a certain troop type (for example light squad, leader, vehicle etc). Each card will award the player a special skill or piece of equipment that will benefit that squad (and hopefully encourage the player to use the squad to its maximum advantage). These Asset Cards can have great significance in the game and add to the players burden of command and decision making - but they are a lot of fun too, especially when they thwart the plans of your opponent.

And from time to time there are the deadly and unpredictable incursions of Sulphur. The deadly field of  battle is made even more challenging by this unholy threat. Will the combatants form a temporary truce and turn their guns on this new and devastating threat or will they abandon their opponents, withdraw from the field and let them face the terror alone? The menace of Sulphur hangs over every battlefield like a cloud. Most times it does not appear - but the fear and unease that it generates is ever present…

boardgame: Crabs Adjust Humidity: Volume Two (fan expansion for Cards Against Humanity)
description:
Love Cards Against Humanity, but even with the official expansions do you find yourself playing the same cards over and over again?

Enter Crabs Adjust Humidity: Volume Two, a crappy little third-party, unofficial, unauthorized expansion card set — featuring 80 white cards and 32 black cards — that blends seamlessly with the original game.

There’s a strange smell in the air, smoke is drifting out of the vent down the corridor…oh no, the ship is on fire!  As you reach for a fire extinguisher and a few crystals to fuel it, ZAP!  A crazed robot hits you with its stun baton and you fall to the floor.  How did things get so out of control on the ship?!

Damage Report: On Fire introduces two brand new tools – the stun baton and the fire extinguisher.  Additionally, there is a new fire game mechanic that spreads deadly flames around the ship.  Can you survive with these new challenges while still keeping your ship’s systems online?

boardgame: Damage Report: Problems with Puffballs Promo
description:
A careless crewman on board the Endeavor has brought aboard an unapproved pet, and now the crew must deal with an infestation of adorable, purring balls of puff, before their rapidly multiplying numbers flood the ship.

In Problems with Puffballs, players must contain the threat of multiplying cute aliens called Puffballs.  Buy yourself time by releasing a few out of the Airlock, or permanently solve the problem by Teleporting them away onto a nearby alien ship.  And do it all quickly…your Chief Science Officer’s calculations put the Puffballs’ multiplication rates to uncontrollably dangerous levels in only 21 minutes!

This promo includes 24 puffballs and a sheet of rules.

boardgame: 30 Days of Night: A Game of Survival
description: 30 Days of Night: A Game of Survival is based on the 30 Days of Night comic book series from Steve Niles and Ben Templesmith. No details have been announced about the gameplay.

Good Fortune is a fast-paced, light tabletop game themed around life's lucky breaks. It is suitable for families, festive gatherings, newcomers to board games, and casual gamers.
The game uses a modular board made up of tiles, which are arranged in a random combination. Players draw fortune cards with different combination of symbols, which they can choose to keep or swap for new ones. Based on the fortune cards in their hand, players take turns to place game tokens on the board and claim the fortunes on the corresponding cards. Most often, players have multiple board locations available and can choose their tactic based on the fortunes they most wish to claim and on the combinations that yield the highest number of points. The goal of the game is to collect the highest number of points by claiming as many fortunes with the player's lucky symbol as possible.

Frozen Rampage is the first game release from Rusty Nickel Miniatures.

Frozen Rampage is a two player, fast-paced, turn-based, fantasy miniature ice hockey game. The starter box includes two teams, human and orc, each of which is composed of ten miniatures.

The goal of Frozen Rampage is to use strategy and an arsenal of special abilities to outsmart and outscore your opponent. A game of Frozen Rampage will take approximately 40 minutes to an hour, and the learning curve is typically less than one game.

A team sets includes 4 defensemen, 4 forwards, a goalie, and a star player and will all be cast in plastic.

Unique miniatures such as the Referee, Kevlan Gor, and star players not available in the starter sets will be cast in white metal.

Get to the Quarantine Zone (QZ) is a part card game / part board game RPG set in the mid 1800's in a place called Cherry Creek, Colorado. The mining town it is set in was quite wealthy and bustling with business until one day people started getting sick. After the sickness, many died. After they died, many got back up and started eating the living! It wasn't long before word spread that this was happening all over the country. Quickly, everyone left town as fast as they could heading toward a "Quarantine Zone" they heard of in Boulder, Colorado. You and a small group of other survivors got the last wagon in town and headed to Boulder. It wasn't long before the wagon broke catastrophically and you were all stranded. You set up a basic camp and decided to make missions into town to get supplies and parts for the wagon. Once fixed, you will be ready to Get to the Quarantine Zone.

Bonus station card (#23). Base game has three 'placeholder' station cards that can be cashed in for a promo of choice 'from the box'.

It was given in Essen 2011 for the buyers of the base game.

Tilt is played on a hexagonal board divided into six horizontal and six vertical channels by raised squares. Each player has a set of square pieces numbered 1-5, with more of the lower numbers (plus a "flag" piece) which move along the channels.

Pieces can be moved individually or as columns of adjacent friendly pieces. A piece or a column can push an opposing piece or column if it stronger (i.e., if the sum of the pieces' numbers is greater). Flags cannot move on their own, but must instead be included in a column.

Pieces which are blocked from moving (even temporarily) can be removed by the owner if it is to their advantage. It is also legal to push your own pieces if you like (even off the board).

The object of the game is to touch or immobilize the opposing flag.

boardgame: Connect With Pieces: The Hobbit an Unexpected Journey
description:
The game components are the (700) pieces of a jigsaw puzzle (27" x 20"). Each piece has one of five symbols embossed on it. Solid-color pieces may be able to fit in more than one position in the puzzle, but other pieces will only have one valid position.

The players are all working to assemble the puzzle; using the main set of rules, one player plays for a minute at a time, with the others using this time to select pieces from the box. There are also "scramble" rules in which all players can select pieces and play them as they wish.

Regardless of the rules used, players score points for playing pieces, with more points being awarded for playing more pieces and for playing rows of three pieces with the same symbol or squares of pieces with four different symbols. Points are also awarded for correcting misplaced pieces.

Players can play for a given amount of time or until someone reaches a given score (or until the puzzle is finished).

The Sanitarium: The Foyer expansion includes a revised rulebook for Sanitarium--which completely replaces the original rulebook, a Foyer starting location, four double-sided Character cards, and revised scenarios (which also completely replace the originals).

The Solo Scenario was unintentionally omitted from the revised scenario cards, but a printable PDF may be found at the Asmadi website at http://asmadigames.com/files/sanitarium_solo.pdf

boardgame: Flames of War: Fate of a Nation – Arab-Israeli Wars, Six-Day War, 1967
description:
Fate of a Nation:
Arab-Israeli Wars, Six-Day War, 1967

On 5 June, 1967, Israel surprised the Arab forces massing on its borders with a pre-emptive strike. By the end of the day a hundred thousand soldiers and several thousand tanks were locked in battle — a struggle that would decide the fate of Israel.

Both sides had spent a decade preparing for this titanic struggle. Both sides acquired every modern tank they could, and upgraded those they retained from earlier wars. The opening battles saw more tanks in combat than the famous Battle of Prokhorovka between the Germans and Soviets near Kursk in 1943. The clash was swift and brutal. Within days a thousand wrecked tanks littered the battlefields and Israel was victorious.
Fate Of A Nation

INSIDE YOU WILL FIND:

Armoured forces for Israel, the United Arab Republic (Egypt), and Jordan in the Six-Day War.

The Israeli P’lugah Tan’kim (Tank Company) fields Sho’t,
Centurion, Magach (M48), M51 Isherman, M50 Sherman, M1 Super Sherman medium tanks, and AMX light tanks.

The UAR Kuteybh Debabh (Tank Battalion) fields the latest T-54 and T-55 tanks backed by older T-34/85 medium tanks, IS-3 heavy tanks, and SU-100M tank destroyers.

The Jordanian Saraya Mudr’e (Armoured Squadron) fields Centurion and M48 tanks.

These forces are supported by mechanised infantry, artillery, and air power.

This expansion module presents army lists and additional rules for the Six Day War for use with the Flames of War miniatures wargame system. This is not a stand-alone game, use of the Flames of War core rulebook is required.

Published in Wargames Illustrated #312.

Neo Realms Entertainment is proud to announce the release of P.O.N.E.

Protectors of Neo-Earth or P.O.N.E. is a tactical board game with deck building properties. Players battle for control over castles that afford them points toward more powerful warrior cards. When battle is joined at a castle, the players choose their battle cards and fight for control over the lands of Neo Earth.
    
P.O.N.E. has 3 phases of the game & they occur in the following order each round:  Payment, Movement & Combat.

- Payment Phase:  Players are paid based on the number and types of lands controlled.

- Movement Phase:  Each player may move any or all of his pawns one space to an adjacent castle.

- Combat Phase: Combat occurs when one or more pawns challenges another pawn for control of a castle.

The object of the game is to collect the most opposing pawns. The game ends when only 1 color pawn remains.

boardgame: NFL Rush Zone Trading Card Game
description:
You have been chosen to be an NFL Rush Zone Guardian. Join a team of brave Guardians, defending the Core and battling on the gridiron. Suit up Guardian, and gather your team of Rusherz and prepare to battle. Make sure to power up your team of Rusherz with the 32 team Megacores and your collection of PowerStickerz – all in your quest to score the most Touchdowns!

Each game consists of 4 quarters, with 2 battles in each quarter. In every quarter, each player starts one battle on offense, and one battle on defense. The player with the most TD tokens at the end of the last battle wins.

The Nameless are a 3rd Season alien expansion team for DreadBall, the futuristic sports game.  This team features slimy aquatic aliens from several apparently different but symbiotic species cooperating to play DreadBall.

The team expansion introduces 8 player models including:

4 x Nameless Strikers 
2 x Nameless Guards (Hard) 
2 x Nameless Guards (Sticky)

"The Nameless Confederation is home to many related species living in harmony. The Ancients reflect this in their playing style, each player working closely with his team-mates to achieve victory."

The Koeputki Kolossals Zees are a 3rd Season expansion team for DreadBall, the Futuristic Sports Game.  They team comprises several identical chimpanzee (hence the name 'Zee') clones.

Each basic 'Zee' team has 10 Jacks, fielding no strikers or guards.  The Zees rely on mischief and mayhem on the field in order to stay competitive with stronger, faster and more skilled teams.

"Don’t be fooled by the fact that they’re genetically modified monkey clones – the Kolossals’ underhanded antics make for great entertainment, to the delight of their fans..."

The year is 2025, and the war on crime is all but lost. The streets are filled with fear as abandoned buildings and darkened alleyways haunt the imaginations of the few innocent citizens brave enough to remain. Gunfire echoes across the cities as gang violence brings vast urban decay. The global war between the Mafia and Triad syndicates has brought civil society to its knees. The fate of the criminal world is to be decided by the force you can muster and economies you can develop in the ultimate crime war that is: INFRACTION.

Game play consists of hiring mercenaries and building developments. Each player is trying to kill the other's boss. Using mercenaries to attack, and developments to gain riches, as well as the odd trick 'o' trade, they try to dominate the board in the hope of wounding the enemy boss. The game starts with each player drawing cards and using a family bank balance as a kickstart to their criminal empire. From here you build your syndicate over the course of the game.

Heroes of Ahlmeran (HoA) is a cooperative card game where 2 - 5 players come together and build an adventuring party from unique Hero Cards and Feat Cards and then complete Quests to help the land of Ahlmeran. The game requires both team strategy and problem solving abilities.

As the players, you will build your party from a selection of 12 Heroes spanning across 6 Races. The Humans, lost among the land now, with no real home. The Dwarves, at home among the rock with their advanced technology. Elves, Cast out by their past mistake, they are no longer rulers of the land but rather 2nd class citizens. The Canin, a proud race resembling a blue wolf. The Puar, the cat people of Guuman. And then the Gnomes. No one knows magic more intimately.

From those races you will select of the 6 Classes that best suits your play style. Artificer, master of artifacts and arcane knowledge. Barbarian, nomad tribes bestowing their nature knowledge and sheer might to the people. Elemental Mage, master of the 4 elements. Priest, Master of the holy arts, both offensive and defensive. Ranger, commander of nature and all that resides within her. And Templar, defender of the people, master of arcane. Each of the classes come with bonuses and a unique 40 card deck that your character will use.

Then, you select a Location Deck to play with, a deck of 15 cards to represent where your quest takes place.

Finally, you pick a Quest Deck, the enemy or circumstance your heroes are struggling against. It could be anything from breaking a curse, thwarting a thief to facing a brutal goblin ambush!

Over the course of the game, you will play cards from your Class Deck, working together with your friends to complete whatever the Quest Deck requires for your victory.

With each Quest Deck completed and Location Deck explored, you gain the chance to accumulate more rare items, allowing you to play the game countless times with more combinations than we can count

A mythico-historical card game based on 1000 cards that weave an edutaining spell through the looking glass of time.

This is a game of history, drama and mystery made from 1000 unique Cards, each inspired by special parts of history. It may be played by 2 people, although the game is ideally played with 4-6 participants. The title evokes the oldest known Japanese poetry anthology, Ten Thousand Leaves, which transferred many oral poetry traditions to paper for the first time in the 8th Century. A Thousand Leaves is also a translation of “Chiba”, a region in Japan.

Every player is a Leader of one of the 8 Realms. The game takes place in Turns, each of which consists of 4 PHASES: Diplomatic, Military, Society and Spirit. In each Phase, each player may take one free Action (“Phase Action”).  Armies may traverse the globe or Priests build Temples, units turn against their masters or debates alter the fate of nations.

The game also features a representation of ancient warfare, complete with a Center, Left and Right and real battlefield strategies.  Each player takes an action through pieces (Face Cards) on an imaginary board, alternating until all pieces have taken at least one action before the next turn begins.

Sticks and Stones is a fun, strategic card game, that gives you the power to choose your path to victory.

In S&S you will:

-Battle your opponent through the use of characters, enhancements, vehicles, heroes, and creatures!

-Beef up your characters with potions, special abilities, magic, and more!

-Crush your enemy using brute force, or a more tactical approach!

S&S has:

-Unique, and crazy cool artwork!

-Custom die for tracking progress!

-High quality components made to last through years of fun!

-An easy learning curve, while also giving you the option for more in depth gameplay!

How is it played?

1. Build your deck from a large selection of cool characters, crazy enhancements, and awesome creatures. Then choose your hero for the battle!

2. Obliterate your opponent! Using your deck you will Deploy>Enhance>Attack>Discard. Use ranged and melee cards to attack. Juice them up with some spells. Place in some defensive vehicles to block incoming attacks. Use counter-attack cards to send the enemies attacks right back at them!

3. After capturing the opposing hero, regroup and rebuild your deck for the next battle. Don't forget to adjust your strategy, because now your opponent will gain an advantage through the use of terrain cards! So stay alert and think outside of the box or you might be caught blindsided by a brutal attack

boardgame: Shadows over the Empire: Fallen Allies
description: Shadows over the Empire: Fallen Allies, a mini-expansion for Shadows over the Empire, consists of four C-level Leader cards, one Infatuation card (which is used by Dorian the Libertine), and rules for their use.

In Path of Dice the goal is to create a connected path of dice from your starting area to the opposite end of the board by placing the just-rolled dice according to a few simple rules. Your opponent is of course doing the same from the other end thus creating a lot of conflict.

It's a fun, fast, simple and surprisingly strategic game for two or four players.

A 2-player game is played in about ten minutes with strategic decisions being made on most throws.

With so many dice being rolled luck does play a big part in who wins the game. But the strategic depth of the game could still surprise its creator even after 50+ played games.

Juego de tablero para varios jugadores inspirado en el wrestling, en concreto en la WWE, editado y creado en España.
Cada jugador representa un luchador, y debe lograr ser el único que quede con puntos de vida sobre el ring.
Para ello usaran cartas que podrán usar para atacar o defenderse.


Inspired by wrestling, specifically WWE, edited and created in Spain.

Each player takes a fighter, an energy marker, and 50 random cards. In turn a player will take a card and roll a die to advance. When all players are in the ring, a player may start a fight against an opponent. Cards are used to attack or defend. The game ends when there is only one player with energy.

family: Series: action engine (Crooked Dice Game Design Studio)

description:
DreadBall Ultimate is an expansion for DreadBall: The Futuristic Sports Game.  This boxed expansion includes a Season 3 rulebook (available separately); Four Giant MVPs; a durable non-slip rubber gaming mat to represent the game field; a DreadBall Ultimate card deck; and a DreadBall Ultimate counter sheet.

DreadBall Ultimate allows up to 6 players to face off on an expanded game pitch.  DreadBall is hectic enough facing just one opponent.  Face the mayhem of competing against up to 5 other players!

This is a talking children's boardgame.  This is a roll-and-move game with cards.  The theme of the game is eating.

The object is to be the first one to get rid of all of your food cards.

Player uses a cardboard "bombsite" to drop marble bombs on "flip" targets in the box for up to 1000 points. Perfect score is 3150 points.

Unit is self contained.  Lid of box is lifted off containing the flip target display.  Player stands over the box dropping the black marble "bombs" using the provided "bombsite" marble tube.

Method and "bombsite" actually work very well!!  "Bombsite is made like a pair of binoculars with a tube attached.  When adjusted they actually display an X to site over the "target" and drop the marble from the attached tube. All made of heavy cardboard!!

boardgame: Dark Darker Darkest: Light in the Dark Expansion
description:
This expansion to Dark Darker Darkest adds Glow in the Dark zombies to the base game. A total of 40 glow-in-the-dark zombies is included:


     10 Guts zombies
     10 Chunk zombies
     10 Disco Doll zombies
     10 Tuff Guy zombies


This expansion was advertised as introducing a new scenario and new zombie abilities to the base game and having additional tokens and rules to use the glow in the dark zombies. However, the game ended up shipping with nothing but glow in the dark figures.

The grey version of these zombie figures are included in the Dark Darker Darkest: Zombie Expansion (2013).

This expansion to Dark Darker Darkest adds RadioactiveRed zombies and introduces a new scenario and new zombie abilities to the base game.  Note that Dark Darker Darkest: Kickstarter Special already included a playable version on this expansion but did not include any of the "RadioactiveRed" creature or zombie miniatures.

It contains:

    66 RadioactiveRed Miniatures (7 survivors [with colored bases], 4 nemeses, 5 creatures, and 50 zombies [10 of each of Standards, Guts, Disco Dolls, Chunk, and Tuff Guy])
    10 Expansion Specific Equipment Cards
    1 Reactor Room Tile
    27 Radiation Markers
    Rulebooks (1 English, 1 German, and 1 Spanish)

Long ago, in a decade not so far away, there existed video games so legendary their legacies carry on to this very day.

16-Bit RPG: The Board Game is exactly that: a board game that pays homage to a time period in video game history gone by. A period that holds a very special place in the hearts of many!

Collect loot, gear your character, use skills, fight monsters and progress through the world until you’re ready to take on one of the mighty Final Bosses!

- Classic RPG styled pixel art
- Easy to learn loot collecting board game
- Skills and Events offer variance in each game

Gameplay

During Your Turn you roll the dice and move the spaces to see what happend; like finding loot, entering on a market or fighting a monster! You use your cards to deal with the adventures by equiping wargear or using items like potions. 
You play until one player gets to defeat the final boss.

boardgame: Qin: Toad and Dragon Turtle Game Boards
description: The expansion Qin: Toad and Dragon Turtle Game Boards consists of a double-sided game board that you can use with the Qin base game. On the "Dragon Turtle" side of the game board, players must build around a massive mountain range, while the "Toad" board offers more starting positions as well as paddy fields that make the villages less accessible.

From the box back:

ELEPHONKEY The game where the truth has a major deficit

Everybody’s full of Elephonkey in this not-really-political party game!

Play your hand and lay down your cards, announcing their ranks… are you gonna tell the truth? Maybe… and maybe not! Donkeys, Elephants, Tree-Huggers and Tea Partiers are the “suits” and there are a couple special interest porkers mixed in.

If you think somebody’s lying about the cards they play, shout, “ELEPHONKEY!” Be first to get rid of your cards and you’ll have a landslide victory!

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Krayton Expansion Pack
description:
The Krayton was a Ferengi D'Kora-class Marauder in service in the mid-24th century.  Under the command of DaiMon Tog, the Krayton abducted Betazoid ambassador Lwaxana Troi, her daughter Deanna, and Commander William T. Riker.

They were tracked down by the USS Enterprise-D and Captain Picard was able to secure the release of the prisoners.

This ship is the competitive prize for Month 1 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, The Dominion War.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Players start with three stacks of five discs, each stacked by decreasing size 5-4-3-2-1. Each move involves splitting a stack to deploy each piece along a (possibly different) line of the mover's choice, to stop on the closest N+1 disc, if any, else the furthest empty cell. The game ends when a multicoloured stack of height 5 is formed, and is won by the player with the majority of discs in this stack.

The Heat is an expansion for the co-op board-game Police Precinct.  It consists of two  decks of cards.  One deck is called The Gear Deck, and the other is called The Specialist Deck.  There are 8 cards in each deck.

The nature of the cards makes the base-game easier (the cards help for the Police to do their job better).  The cards are used in conjunction with heightened challenges for the police created by difficult to handle set-up scenarios.

This expansion really adds to the replayability and strategic team-work aspects of Police Precinct.

Huey Leader puts you in the cockpits of the Vietnam War’s best Close Air Support aircraft. In Huey Leader, fly search and destroy missions to attack enemy troops, fly missions to deploy US ground troops into hostile Landing Zones and then provide Close Air Support to help the troops complete their mission, and even fly resupply missions to keep US Forward Bases supplied.  Huey Leader is based on our popular Thunderbolt-Apache Leader game system. If you've played TAL, you'll be up and flying missions against the Vietcong in minutes.

Huey Leader features an operation combat sheet that tracks the progress of enemy formations attempting to overrun your sector. You get to select the formation you want to engage each day and assign the air assets you think are best suited to the task.

A major part of the game is mission planning. You decide how to arm your aircraft and which pilots are best for the mission. Pilots are rated for skill level. Deciding which pilots to fly each mission is a major consideration. Your pilots will become fatigued with every mission. If you fly a pilot too often, his skills will degrade. Your pilots also gain experience with every mission flown. Should you keep flying the top pilots and further fatigue them to get the job done, or fly the new guys to prep them for the tough missions ahead?

Missions are quickly resolved using an area movement system to display tactical combat over the target. Speed and realism take top priority in this game. Terrain cards randomly generate each battlefield. The cards include mountain ranges that block lines of sight from both you and the enemy forces. If you plan it right, you get to fly nap of the earth through the canyons and come screaming over the ridgeline to rain fire down on the enemy before they can react. Beware! During each battle enemy air defenses will pop-up on the map to ruin your day, so watch were you’re flying!
