description:
Colorinto is a deck of abstract paintings that challenges players with different games of visual skill, ranging from solitaire to speed contest or to strategy.

When put together, those paintings form a museum, in which every room has one picture on it. It can be only visited following certain rules that are hidden inside the paintings.

The goal of the game is to discover and create paths that cross the maze, sometimes discovering the longest route in less time than the opponents, other times by creating the museum itself so it follows certain conditions.

A game recommended for people with a liking to challenging games with simple rules, abstract art aesthetics or unconventional mazes.

boardgame: Might & Magic Heroes: Crag Hack the Barbarian
description: This mini-expansion introduces into M&M Heroes: The Boardgame a new Hero and 2 new Hero Starting Abilities. Crag Hack is a neutral Hero – it does not belong to any faction and may be hired by any player.

Clique Mystique is a tile-laying card game for two players, using the Mystique Deck. The object is to own the most cards at the end of the game. Play starts from a randomly dealt 3x3 array of cards and gradually expands into a 7x7 virtual board. Players form groups of closely related cards (Cliques) or completely unrelated cards (Motley Crews), capturing opposing stacks in the process.

An exclusive double-sided promo sheet available at Spiel 2013 in Essen.

Playing The Outcast Heroes, you will become a commander of a Polish underground military unit and fight against Communism. The USSR wants to include Poland in its influence zone, but there are still people who resist and stand up for independence. Will you manage to stop the red plague?

The Outcast Heroes: Alternative Missions is a double-sided promotional item that can replace the "Free the prisoners" special mission card with two new missions, with alternative rules and different difficulty levels. Both sides of the item show real prison buildings from the communist times in Poland.

boardgame: Sigismundus Augustus: Dei gratia rex Poloniae – Bona Sforza d'Aragona
description:
During Sigismundus Augustus: Dei gratia rex Poloniae, a board game for 3 to 5 players, each player rules one of the 16th-century noble families. The aim is to establish the position of the House in Rzeczpospolita and in the international arena. To gain more prestige than other players and win the game, each House has to fight for the influence in Sejm or for authority among neighbors, while also maintaining a strong and effective army.

Sigismundus Augustus: Dei gratia rex Poloniae - Bona Sforza d'Aragona is a double-sided promo sheet that represents the ambiguous opinions on Queen Bona circulating among historians. One side shows her as generous and helpful, while the other as treacherous and poisonous; each side has an ability that can be used once per round.

The wind is from the north today, carrying a foul stench.

I used to think of necromancy as a black fog, thickening and spreading, covering us in oppressive gloom. I see, now, that this was wrong: A fog is made all of the same stuff, but our doom has many distinct facets, each one we penetrate revealing a new evil behind. Some have become terrified of the darkness; others made numb by despair. Many have had their bodies rent, while others are starting to lose their minds. A scout has just sent word of another new variety of monstrosity that has appeared near the village...but also the discovery of a new artifact that may help us fight them.
 Fate, it seems, never runs out of ideas. We can only handle each new challenge as best we can as it arrives on shifting winds...

Darkest Night: On Shifting Winds from Jeremy Lennert expands the game with a variety of new content: It adds the Ranger, Scout, Wayfarer, and Wind Dancer as new playable heroes, introduces eight new blights and new treasures to search for, and also includes new Event and Artifact cards.

On Shifting Winds can be used either with or without the first expansion, With an Inner Light.

Note: This game requires that you (or someone in your group of players) own a copy of Darkest Night.

Complexity: 4.5 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 9 on a 9 scale

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Rise of the Runelords – Adventure Deck 4: Fortress of the Stone Giants
description:
Fend off a stone giant invasion, raid magical dungeons, and unlock the mystery of an ancient empire with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Fortress of the Stone Giants Adventure Deck (Deck #4).

This 110-card expansion to the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Rise of the Runelords Base Set includes new locations, monsters, villains, loot, and more, as well as 5 new scenarios that constitute the complete Fortress of the Stone Giants adventure. Continue your character’s legend with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Fortress of the Stone Giants Adventure Deck.

description:
In Dungeon Duelers, players will take the rolls of Duelers beginning their journey to become Duel Champions, hoping to one day become members of one of the four Guilds of Glory. The game represents a vast journey across the entire world of Thexia. During the game players will capture monsters of their own which they will train. There is no limit to how powerful your monster can become! They will face several encounters like Duelers, Bandits, wild monsters, traps, and other challenges. As they travel between the regions they will discover ancient dungeons filled with rare monsters and treasure. Players will also complete quests that earn them fame which increases their chances of being chosen to be a Guild member. The game can be played competitively or semi-cooperatively if players want to team up against each other to speed the journey along.

The game is divided into basic and advanced versions. The basic version is much faster to play and simply requires removing a few components and not using some of the content such as the monsters unique abilities. The advanced version is played using all components and takes about 2 hours to play. There are also components that allow for custom character creation which allow a player to save their information to continue playing again possibly using expansions.

boardgame: Legacy: The Testament of Duke de Crecy – Extra Mansion Cards
description:
These 7 cards were available in pre-order of Legacy directly from Portal Games website.

The extra Mansion cards can be used to replace the standard Mansion cards from the game. Besides increasing a player's Prestige, each extra Mansion has an additional unique effect.

Unless explicitly stated otherwise, the Mansion special effect is resolved immediately and one-time only after the Buy a Mansion action is completed.



PL:Tych 7 kart dodawanych było do egzemplarzy Dziedzictwa zamawianych w przedsprzedaży bezpośrednio z Portal Games.

Dodatkowych kart Pałaców można użyć w zastępstwie standardowych kart dostępnych w grze. W odróżnieniu od podstawowych Pałaców – oprócz podnoszenia Prestiżu – każdy z nich posiada dodatkowy, unikatowy efekt. 
Jeśli wyraźnie nie zaznaczono inaczej, to efekt działania Pałacu należy rozpatrzeć jednorazowo po jego zakupieniu, natychmiast po zakończeniu akcji Pałac.

Amerigo Queenie 1: Volcanic Island, a mini-expansion for Amerigo, consists of one module that can be used individually or in combination with any other modules for the game. When setting up the game, place the volcanic island tile in the central playing area, then place one gold tile face down on each marked space on this tile. Whenever someone builds over one of these tiles, they reveal the number on the tile and immediately receives that much gold.

Confirmed by Stefan Feld: "The volcano island tile is a tile with 4 islands separated by a volcano (instead of water).
You can´t build on the volcano space."

boardgame: Amerigo Queenie 3: Special Production Buildings and Tokens
description: Amerigo Queenie 3: Special Production Buildings and Tokens, a mini-expansion for Amerigo, consists of one module that can be used individually or in combination with any other modules for the game. With this expansion in the game, when a player chooses the red Plan action, he can take one of the four production building tiles instead of a neutral landscape tile; when he uses the green Build action to place this building on an island, he takes one of the five special production tokens available and places it on his player sheet. These production tokens are treated like any other during scoring.

boardgame: Amerigo Queenie 2: Cove Island, Bay Island & New Progress Tokens
description: Amerigo Queenie 2: Cove Island, Bay Island & New Progress Tokens, a mini-expansion for Amerigo, consists of additional components that can be mixed with those in the base game. The cove island tile has many small island pieces with a large central waterway, while the bay island tile has a massive block of land that will almost certainly create a large island in the playing area. The six progress tokens add new abilities that players can gain during the game, such as the ability to ignore the pirates once or to take a second action at the end of a round.

Ironcrux is an abstract strategy game in which 2-4 players take turns moving their colored piece, which starts from one corner, across a square grid of pieces. Each move shrinks the grid by one piece, making maneuvering increasingly more difficult. In certain situations, a few removed pieces can be returned to the grid to open up new movement options.

When a player cannot make a legal move, he is eliminated. The last player standing wins.

Ironburst is a tactical combat board game using detailed metal miniatures and modular hex-based maps. It plays quick and has simple rules with good tactical scalability. Set-up is fast and so are battles.

The goal is to complete objectives: gathering resources, building, or eliminating specific enemy troops, etc. Players gather resources located on the board to build and repair units and to buy special Event Cards.

The miniature line and the game were developed together, so the miniatures are designed to fit the game mechanisms and vice versa. More specifically, each miniature has multiple moving parts that can be removed (if they are destroyed) and rotated (to aim, to indicate that a weapon has already fired, etc.). Thus, these modular miniatures allow players to do bookkeeping and track game events directly on the battlefield instead of using paper. The moving parts are magnetic and can be swapped in and out to allow players to customize their forces.

To allow the moving parts to take the stress and friction of repeated movement, Ironburst miniatures are cast in a special zinc alloy called ZAMAK, which is not as soft as tin (and much lighter), nor as fragile as resin, while keeping a good amount of detail.

description:
In ConSTARlations, players take turns using cards to place, move, replace and remove cards in order to make specific patterns on the board (as dictated by their secret pattern card). When a player's pattern appears on the board — no matter whose stars are responsible — they score the card and pick another.

Pattern cards are worth 1, 2 or 3 points depending on whether the pattern consists of 4, 5 or 6 stars. Players can score their pattern card during their own turn, but also during everyone else's! So they must keep their eyes on the board at all times.

If either of the two Black hole cards are played, all stars on the board are removed and returned to their players.

When one of the piles of pattern cards runs out, everyone gets one last turn before players count up their scored cards to see who has won.

Simon says "roll the dice!"

One player takes the role of the master, rolls the two dice, and reads aloud the results.  The other players try to obey those instructions.  If anyone can't obey the instruction, doesn't obey it, or falls over while trying then they are eliminated.  The last person standing becomes the master for the next game.

The two dice are a "question" die and an "action" die.  The question die stipulates a condition, and the action tells you what to do if you meet that condition.  So, you might roll the two dice and read out "If you are a boy, stand on one foot" or "If your first name contains an A or an I, hold your left arm out straight" or "if you are smiling, stare at either your feet or the ceiling".  You have to keep obeying all prior instructions as you execute new instructions.

Some actions give players a choice, such as "put your right hand on your head or on your stomach".  If such an instruction comes up again later, the players have to pick the option they aren't already doing, and do it instead.

All you need are 2 dice to play.  You can either create custom dice, or use two differently colored dice and look up the results on a simple table.

"The spread of civilization may be likened to a fire; First, a feeble spark, next a flickering flame, then a mighty blaze, ever increasing in speed and power." - Nikola Tesla

Components:
63 Civilian Cards
12 Starter Civilian Cards
20 Worker Tokens
18 Map Cards
4 Starter Map Cards
20 Player Populace Tokens
5 Neutral Populace Tokens
8 Six Sided Dice
1 Rulebook

Nation: A Mighty Spark is a worker placement strategy card game that takes place on a planet where bugs have evolved into the dominant species.

Players place worker tokens onto a modular map, made of cards, that begin hidden but are revealed as they explore. For each worker placed one of five actions is chosen: The Battle action allows players to modify populaces and civilian cards on the table. The Build action allows placement of populaces onto map cards. The Develop action allows placement of civilian cards from your hands on to the table: providing food for more workers, building materials for populaces and military might. The Draw actions allow players to draw more cards into their hand. The Explore action allows for the revealing of face down map cards.

Players continue taking turns until the civilian draw deck depletes. Victory points are counted based upon the number of civilians in play, populace control and military might.

description: Mezmo is a collaborative game for 3 to 11 players, ages 5 and up. In the game players take turns adding a single tile to a slowly growing game board where the other players judge whether or not the played tile fits the "Theme" of the pieces it connects to. The game focuses on the personal judgement of each of the judges, as was as the current player's ability to convince others of their artistic vision. Failure to appease the judges forces the player to take back their piece, as well as receive another random tile from the bag. The game ends when one of the players has no more pieces to play. It's a laid back, casual game that inspires alot of conversation and thought, and is best played with a creative crowd.

Publisher Blurb:

The Game of Inauspicious Incidents and Grave Consequences! In the Gloom card game, you make your eccentric family of misfits suffer the greatest tragedies possible before helping them pass on to the well-deserved respite of death. Just mix the 55 transparent cards in this set together with your copy of Gloom to add morbid new Modifiers, Events, and Untimely Deaths.

Unquiet Dead also introduces Stories, Undead, and Timing Symbols. The families of Gloom have many skeletons in their closets. In Unquiet Dead, the spooks come out to play. Mad scientists can Reanimate Relatives or Invent Invisibility. Vampires and shape shifters can Terrorize the Townsfolk and Go Mad in the Moonlight. Will you Give Up the Ghost, or will you hold onto it?

STORIES: These cards give your families even more to fight over. Whoever claims a story gains a special benefit ... but how long can you keep it?

UNDEAD: There are seven special Modifiers that allow a Character to become a supernatural creature -- a vampire, mummy, ghost, ghoul, wereduck, invisible person, or haunted portrait. These Undead Characters are both living and dead; you can still play Modifiers and Events on them, but they also count toward your Family Value and toward ending the game.

TIMING SYMBOLS: Card effects in Unquiet Dead have symbols to let you know whether a card has an Instant effect that occurs when the card is played from your hand; an Ongoing effect that lasts until it is covered by another card; or a Persistent effect that can last as long as the Character is still alive ... or Undead.

boardgame: Fortune and Glory: Sands of Cairo
description:
The Sands of Cairo Supplement is a Web-exclusive for Fortune and Glory and features 14 new game cards. The cards are exclusive to this supplement and will not be available anywhere else. They feature all new artwork.

The new cards in the Sands of Cairo Supplement are designed to be shuffled right into the existing Fortune and Glory card decks and will give your Heroes new Dangers to face and new Cliffhangers to overcome as well as a host of new desert–themed cards set in or near the city of Cairo! Find back alley street markets where you can purchase camels and scimitars to help in your adventures, but beware, the local thugs are always watching and may not take kindly to outsiders!

This is a expansion to the game 7ombie TV which is a spin off to 7TV, but has a fraction of Ghouls and school girls as every system needs one. Players make their force spending points and following rules on the number of hero's and sheep they can have as there are three levels of characters. The game is won when one side completes the objective, points can be scored for other task completed during the game such as killing of zombies!

For Ghouls and Colleges brings a UK twist and some minor misdemeanors to 7ombieTV. Play cannibal ghouls such as the twisted Number 11 or two new survivor casts – the Girls of St Searle’s or the vicious Borstal Boys.

Each player forms a fraction using the type cast up to a point cost. Cards are given at random to add a little flavor as weapons or one off effects. The games will have different objectives to complete and bonus points for completing mini task.
family: Series: action engine (Crooked Dice Game Design Studio)

boardgame: The Great Adventure Kids Bible Card Game Set
description:
The Great Adventure Kids Bible Card Game Set is a fun-filled and beautifully illustrated system designed to teach children as young as three the "big picture" of salvation history. Kids young and old and adults, too will have fun while learning the story of the Bible.

Instructions for six games are included, from the easier "Memory and "Go Fish" concepts, to the more complex "Rummy," "Crazy Eights," "Doubt It," and "Spoons." These cards are designed to help players learn the progression of the periods of Bible history by color as well as recognize key characters, events and Bible references. Perfect for home, classroom, and religious education settings.

A dice game for simulating a game of baseball. There are 4 six-sided dice included, 2 Pitcher dice (one for each team), and 2 Batter dice, both used by the player who is at bat. The Pitching player rolls their Pitcher die to determine the type of pitch, then the Batting player rolls their Batter dice to determine the outcome of the at bat.  A run is scored when the Batter dice add up to 4 total bases, or when the Pitcher die comes up "Home Run."  Like real baseball, a player stays at bat until they accumlate 3 outs by rolling "Strike Out" on either the Pitcher die or Batter dice.

Comes with a miniature duffel bag to store the dice.

boardgame: Palacio de Viana: Las llaves de palacio
description:
With the keys, the closed door at the Palacio could be not a problem...

Contains 5 key tiles (one per player) and rules.

With a key, you can pass through the door of the basic game, and one of the character (Ama de llaves) gives you one additional VP.

Free promo item at the Festival Internacional de Juegos Córdoba 2012.
publishers: Festival Internacional de Juegos de Córdoba

boardgame: Theseus: The Dark Orbit – Space Station Cards
description:
These 10 cards were available in pre-order of Theseus directly from Portal Games website.

This mini expansion contains 10 Space Station cards. These cards are Common cards.

Common cards do not belong to any faction. Common Action cards are activated by any player, Common Special cards and Module cards operate in favor of each player. Any player can use Common Shortcut cards, while Common Traps damage each player.

Common cards are meant to diversify the gameplay, and their use significantly changes the situation in the sector from the very beginning of the game, forcing players to change their usual strategies. These cards are designed for experienced players who already know the game well and want to try something different and more challenging.

When players want to use the Common cards, they should shuffle them, draw one or two and place them in any of the 3 Common sectors (not the Faction sectors) in the game. It's up to the players to decide how many cards they use and where to place them.



PL:Tych 10 kart dodawanych było do egzemplarzy Tezeusza zamawianych w przedsprzedaży bezpośrednio z Portal Games.

Karty Stacji Kosmicznej są kartami Wspólnymi.

Karty Wspólne nie przynależą do żadnej frakcji. Wspólne karty Akcji może uruchamiać każdy z graczy, Wspólne karty Specjalne i Modułów działają na każdego gracza, każdy gracz może korzystać ze Wspólnych kart Skrótów, a Wspólne karty Pułapek szkodzą każdemu graczowi.

Karty Wspólne służą do urozmaicenia rozgrywki, a ich użycie znacząco zmienia sytuację w sektorach już od samego początku rozgrywki i wymusza zmianę dotychczasowych strategii. Karty te zostały stworzone z myślą o graczach, którzy poznali już grę na wylot i chcą ją urozmaicić i dodatkowo odświeżyć.

Jeśli gracze chcą użyć kart Wspólnych, tasują talię kart Wspólnych, losują jedną lub dwie z nich i kładą w dowolnym (dowolnych) z trzech sektorów uniwersalnych (nie należących do nikogo).

description:
THE GREATEST RACING GAME SYSTEM NEVER DONE.

Racing Simulator is a racing car game for 1 to 9 players.

The system of game is simple but sophisticated, cars follow each other in single tail (1 car by space), simultaneously all the players reveal 1 speed card.
From the last car we are going to look by comparing speeds and place on the track if a car can overtake, decrease or increase the gap with the car which precedes it, and so on up to the lead car.

Every player possesses potentially the same Speed cards as his opponents, but does not know what are going to make for every turn, cars forward and behind him.

Add Bonus/malus dependings on the tyres type/condition, and the Action cards played.

The game is excellent because the player finds himsef with exactly the same management problems of his tyres and his pitstops that the driver in the reality. According to the quality of tyres supplied by the manufacturer, and the course of the race, what is the best ? 1, 2, 3 or 4 pitstops (Hard or soft tyres) !!!

Cars not attributed to a player are managed by the system.

The races are governed by the real F1 rules.
Qualifications, start in grid, launched start, change of tyres (1 to 4 pitstop opportunities dependings the tyres delivered by the manufacturer), quality and wear of tyres, rain, track humidity level, incidents, accidents, penalties, safety car, everything is simulated with simple rules easy to implement.

1 party lasts approximately 1 hours 30 (40 minutes for the Family game).

boardgame: Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island – Additional Beast Cards
description:
7 new Beast cards available in pre-order of Voyage of the Beagle expansion directly from Portal Games website.



PL:
Tych 7 kart dodawanych było do egzemplarzy dodatku Wyprawa HMS Beagle zamawianych w przedsprzedaży bezpośrednio z Portal Games.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Game: Robinson Crusoe – Adventures on the Cursed Island

description: Manage and research the fish populations of the Blackjack River while supporting fishermen in their efforts. When a population stabilizes at exactly 21 fish, remove it from the river to complete a Study. Each completed Study contributes to your body of research, while notable patterns and observations grant additional recognition. Continue until the Field Station runs out of resources, then total your Studies and Breakthroughs to measure your contribution to the scientific community.

The second in a series of dice games played on an 8x8 board. It combines elements of Poker, Pente, and Yahtzee. The dice are rolled, placed, moved and rerolled. The first player to form 3 separate rows of modified poker hands on the board wins.

On the northern border of Elsemore, where the High Road becomes but a path and disappears into the nightly thicket of the Blackwood Forest stands an inn the walls of which remember a time of Heroes now long gone. And as the days grow short short and grim, as the cold shadows of the North lengthen once again, songs of great deeds are sang in the golden glow of an age-old hearth. Tales of woe and glory, of hope and despair await those who turn their ear to the Songs of Artha.

Songs of Artha is a solitaire/cooperative fantasy adventure card game that pits legendary Heroes against monsters and perils of the dark fantasy world of Artha. Introducing a deckbuilding-inspired deck management mechanism, diverse enemies and randomized adventures, Songs of Artha is a game for fans of exciting fantasy adventures, as well as for gamers who enjoy involved card play and in-game deck manipulation.

Live the story of every Hero alone – or combine Hero Core Sets to play with your friends - and write a saga the world of Artha will never forget.

Wiss is a new alien race for Among the Stars. It includes:

1x Wiss race tile
3x Wiss Main Reactors(3 cards - 6 colors)
4x Wiss Ambassador cards

Wiss will be available exclusively through the BGG store.

To Je Fotbal (This Is Football) is a true simulation of a football match, a strategic board game that brings football action to your table by combining football rules and game strategy.

To Je Fotbal is a game for two football managers. Each team consists of the basic eleven players and five substitutes. The players are marked with a colored base corresponding to their role on the pitch. The forwards, midfielders, defenders, goalkeeper and captain all perform different functions. The basic skills can be enhanced by using special cards according to the manager's chosen strategy. During one turn, the manager can move three players in any direction according to their attributes and kick the ball if it is in possession of the team. Playing time is 2 x 45 turns (and maybe extra time).

The basic rules make it easy to get into the game for those aged 10 and up, and they cover situations such as passing, shooting, goal kick, saves, offside, posts, tacking for ball and position, extra time, substitutions and (of course) goals. The extended rules contain special situations such as corner kick, throw-in, free kicks and penalty kicks, fouls, yellow and red cards and even diving.

The game pieces are miniatures of football players in team jerseys with names, numbers and even sponsors' logos. The atmosphere of a football stadium is completed not only by a full-colored 82x55 cm game board with goals, flags and advertising panels, but also by an imitation of a scoreboard. All components (special cards, scoreboard) are in English, with the printed rules in the box coming in six languages.

The first edition, "10th Czech vs Slovakia derby", is supported by the Czech and Slovak national teams. You will find such football greats as Petr Cech, Tomas Rosicky, Marek Hamsik and Martin Skrtel in the teams.

This is a free print and play game.

Life on Earth rides on the back of huge continents, and in the seas around them. Different species are interconnected with one another and influence the habitat for all life. In this game you introduce species (represented by playing cards) to your individual continent.  Each species influences the global temperature, as well as having a preferred temperature range of its own. The individual continents have a unique special ability, playable once per game. The aim of the game is to collect the most victory points by having species on your continent survive Extinctions. This game lets you play out an alternative history of Earth, past, present and future!

C.W. Faulkner published numerous sectional card based board games around the turn of the century.  Zooloo has uniquely shaped cards that form an animal themed globe.

Play starts by shuffling the smaller card deck and taking turns playing cards around the circular disc.  All players start with a set number of counters.  Players try to play the best card they have in hand, then take a card at random from the hand of the next player.  When the smaller deck is played out the dealer shuffles and deals the larger card deck.  Areas on the circular disc carry penalties or bonuses like paying a counter into the pot or taking one out.  The game ends with the circle is complere and the player with the most counters left is the winner.

Flyto is another sectional card based board game published by C.W. Faulker after the success of Upidee.  This time the theme is a balloon race instead of horses.

The board is created by laying out cards featuring landscape artwork.  Players start with a number of counters then roll and move die cut balloons around the board.  The winner is the player with the most counters when the race ends.

Honor is a board game of adventures in a fantasy world. Each participant must fight and solve the problems of this world to be the most honorable of all, with honor being the symbol of victory.

The game consists of a modular map that gives variety to each game, making it shorter or longer, easier or more difficult. With six unique characters from which to choose, each participant should try to accomplish missions or commissions that apply different regions, and each hero in turn should try to keep his home province happy, while winning the love of neighboring provinces, including those of his opponents. All players fight against beasts, gain experience, complete all kinds of missions (including combat), engage followers, and use potions to achieve the goal of the game.

Funny Farmyard is a card laying game from the turn of the century to create funny combination images of English farm animals.

The 52 card deck is shuffled and dealt equally to the players.  Every card is numbered 1 to 4, the first player plays a #1 card from their hand, the second player a #2 and so on.  If the next player does not have the correct number in sequence, they are skipped over until the 1-4 sequence has been played.

"The four cards are placed close together on the table as each person plays, and the result will be that some extremely funny and curiously-shaped animals will be made."

The player who plays the fourth card to complete the animal keeps the set.  When all the cards have been played the player with the most sets wins.

Description from the 1892 catalog:

"A beautiful novelty. The game represents a tower with a princess appearing and disappearing at the upper casement. Also a dungeon cell, and a vine, by climbing upon which the knights endeavor to reach and rescue the princess. When the board is ready for play it is placed in an upright position, thereby giving a more realistic effect."

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – I.K.S. Ch'Tang Expansion Pack
description:
The I.K.S. Ch'Tang was a B'Rel class Klingon Bird-of-Prey in service with the Klingon Defense Force in the late 24th century.

General Martok commanded the Ch'Tang during his raid on Trelka V in 2375, assisted by his first officer Worf and third officer Kor.

This ship is the competitive prize for Month 2 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, The Dominion War.

A simple and quick game of dice rolling set in a fictional Japanese role-playing game called Bravura Burst. The game is played with two sets of unique six-sided dice. The first set of five dice have six different role-playing attributes on them such as strength or charisma. The other set of dice represent the six different types of jobs the heroes in your party can have based on the attributes they rolled. There are also Fool dice for characters that could not get a job.

On the first four turns, players will roll the five attribute dice up to 3 times in an attempt to give one of their four heroes a job (such as rolling at least three strength to create a Barbarian, or two intelligence and two charisma to create a Summoner). Among the six available job dice there are 24 unique jobs (and one more for the unique Fool Die) to play with.

Once everyone has rolled up their individual parties of four, they have a competition against a deck of cards that represents monsters and traps in a dungeon. They do this by drawing the top card of the dungeon deck and rolling the dice representing their party up to three times against the numbers and tricks on the card. The player is trying to roll enough damage to defeat the monster and enough blocking or healing to stop as much damage as possible. Traps works slightly differently with the party trying to disarm them with either job dice that can do so, or by trying to roll various specific combinations of attribute Dice to manually disarm them. Once they make their final roll for the turn they add up the numbers and see what they scored.

The competition is score based with a very simple system. Players gain 1 point for killing monsters and disarming traps, and another point if they can end the turn having blocked or healed all the damage that monster or trap just did. If a player can both defeat a card and block or heal all the damage in the same turn they gain 3 points instead.

An adventurous flight over Germany.

Above the Clouds is an entertaining search-and-strategy game for learning geography. Each player must reach his plane some destinations which are assigned to game start. But since everything is under a blanket of clouds, you have to push the right cloud aside to find the right place. By his flight on the board, he may, under the clouds that obscure the destinations, the geographical position of towns, rivers, lakes discover and mountains of his country.

But watch the lightning!

BrainBox is a series of thematic games where players study a card for 10 seconds and then answer a question based on the roll of a die. Answer correctly and you keep the card. Collect the most cards after 5 or 10 minutes and you win!

Each box includes more than (50) 8.5 x 8.5 cm cardboard cards with beautiful pictures on one side and questions on the other, a 10-second sand timer and a 6- or 8-sided die. Also fabulously fun for single-players or large groups!

55 cards show all 10 provinces and 3 territories, their capitals, attractions, fauna and flora, cooking, famous citizens, nicknames, and more.

A dice game for simulating a game of football (American football or gridiron). There are 4 six-sided dice included, 2 for the Home Team and 2 for the Visiting Team (1 Offense die and 1 Defense die for each). Players take turns rolling their Offensive die against their opponent's Defense die. Points are scored by comparing the rolls against a chart.

Comes with a miniature duffel bag to store the dice.

Um enigma é lançado e você precisa prestar muita atenção nas letras que estão misturadas no tabuleiro para decifrá-lo no tempo da ampulheta. Se achar necessário, peça ajuda a Robert Langdon, que tem dicas essenciais para você. Quanto menos dicas você usar, mais pontos conseguirá marcar e, assim, montar este enigmático quebra-cabeça. São 396 palavras!

O jogo consiste em tentar descobrir, com o menor número possível de dicas, a palavra que será formada pelas letras que foram espalhadas no tabuleiro. Para cada acerto, são concedidos pontos na forma de peças de um quebra cabeça. Quem terminar o quebra cabeça primeiro será o vencedor!


A puzzle is thrown and you need to pay close attention to the letters that are mixed on the board to solve it in the time of the hourglass. If you find it necessary, get help from Robert Langdon, who has essential tips for you. The less tips you use, the more points you will score and, thus, assemble this enigmatic puzzle. There are 396 words!

The game consists in trying to find out, with the least number of hints, the word to be formed with the letters that were thrown on the board. For each right guess, points are granted as pieces of a puzzle. The first to finish the puzzle is the winner!

Superheroes are everywhere, from the comic book page to blockbuster movies and prime-time television. Now, superheroes step into the spotlight at your own table! Could one of these heroes secretly be you?

Superhero Stories is a card game where ordinary citizens become superheroes, fighting dangerous villains and embarking on adventures inspired by the greatest comic books of all time!

Each player is dealt a hero origin and super power. Different origins allow players access to sidekicks, weapons and other useful items that help the hero complete a plot line.

Successful completions of subplots assigned to each character award victory points. Once the final plot is complete, the Superhero with the most victory points wins!

"The invasion has begun and now stronger fighters have landed...
Only the brave GD agents face this menace to save our planet!"

The Elite Alien Army expansion enhances the game difficulty and the overall game experience by combining the Galaxy Defenders core set elements with these new aliens.

This expansion may be integrated into any Galaxy Defenders mission to enhance the challenge, by either replacing an entire alien color rank or simply by following the campaign enhancements detailed in the Rules Booklet.

This is not a complete game, a copy of the Galaxy Defenders core set is required to play.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Deep Space 9 Expansion Pack
description:
Deep Space 9, originally known as Terok Nor, was an orbital space station constructed by the Cardassians in orbit of Bajor during their occupation of the planet. Following the Cardassian withdrawal, the station came under Federation administration at the request of the Bajoran provisional government.

After Commander Benjamin Sisko and Lieutenant Jadzia Dax discovered a stable wormhole to the Gamma Quadrant in the system's Denorios belt, the station was relocated to the mouth of the wormhole and became a vital commercial port and defensive outpost.  It later became a key strategic location during the Dominion War, for both the Dominion and the Federation Alliance.

Deep Space 9, in the form of an oversized cardboard token with associated cards, is the participation prize for Month 1 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, The Dominion War.  The Grand Prize of the Dominion War tournament will be a pre-painted plastic miniature of Deep Space 9.

A fun matching pairs based board game for 2-6 players aged 6 & up...As you travel around the board you have to match pairs of naughty animals to get points..The person with the most points at the end is the person who kidnapped Father Christmas & gets all the toys ! (ie wins)..But watch out as other players can steal your cards which means you have less points than you thought.

Tattletale is a fast-paced microgame about the shifting allegiances of elementary school. Each player secretly represents a different faction and plots or opposes plots to get the most playground glory before the teacher has enough and shuts everything down.

During the game, successful plotting will increase the amount of Homework or Horseplay in the school. In addition to earning points for plotting or stopping a plot, each player's secret role gives them bonus points at the end of the game. They might be any of five roles, including the Teacher's Pet, who earn points for Homework, the Jock, who scores for Horseplay, or someone a little unusual like the BFF, who scores with another player.

Twice a game, the teacher comes in and players literally point fingers at each other to cast blame and reduce glory. The basic turn is structured as an iterative Prisoner's Dilemma with the extra twist that a third player opposes you and can cancel both actions if they guess cleverly.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Firefly: The Game – Big Damn Heroes Promo Cards
description:
Firefly: The Game - Big Damn Heroes consists of five cards that feature some of the main characters of the Firefly television series created by Joss Whedon. The promo includes:


     Malcolm - a You Can't Take the Sky from Me card
     Zoe - Silverhold Munitions card
     Wash - Space Bazaar card
     Kaylee - Regina card
     Jayne - Silverhold Munitions card


Firefly: The Game - Big Damn Heroes was released by ACD Distribution in 2013 as a preorder bonus for Firefly: The Game, then distributed by normal retail outlets in 2014.

Later, the two promo cards "Wash's Lucky Dinosaurs", and "Mal's Pretty Floral Bonnet" were added in to this card set, and it was released as the "Firefly Card Pack" by Gale Force Nine.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Campaign To Victory: 1914 In The West
description:
Multi-map conflict simulation covering the German initial attack on Liege and their drive into Belgium and France in WW I until the end of November 1914.

Intermediate complexity yet very playable with unique no-dice system.

Divisional-Brigade level with step-reduction. Limited intelligence and FOW, unit cohesion effects, approx. 5 miles per hex, 2 day turns, variable weather, alternative orders of battles, Special multi-player rules.

Cards for random events; but do not drive the game.

Campaign game with small and medium scenarios.

boardgame: Star Wars: The Card Game – Lure of the Dark Side
description:
“Obi-Wan was wise to hide her from me. Now his failure is complete. If you will not turn to the dark side… then perhaps she will…”
   -Darth Vader, Star Wars: Return of the Jedi

Lure of the Dark Side is the second Force Pack in the "Echoes of the Force" cycle for Star Wars: The Card Game! Echoes of the Force leads you on an epic exploration of the power of the Force, crossing both the light and the dark sides. Are you disciplined enough to survive the temptations of the dark side? In Lure of the Dark Side, you will uncover both the raw power of the dark side, and the methods the light side can use to resist the seduction of power.

In the second Force Pack of the cycle, you must choose for yourself whether the power offered by the dark side is worth the cost. The ten objective sets (two copies of five different sets) introduced in Lure of the Dark Side are vital in leading you to victory, whether you seek to rise above the enticements of the dark side, or to draw others to the power offered by hate.

Contains objective sets 102-106.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Star Wars – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Word'em is a card game of betting, bluffing, and spelling-showdowns for ages 10 and up.

Word'em melds the “make-your-own luck” aspect of poker and the cerebral challenge of word games.

Every card features a letter and a point value. Your goal in each game is to form the highest-scoring word with the cards available to you, and win the hand.

Word'em cards can be used to play nearly every kind of poker variant with little modification.

The Immortal Saga is a collectible competitive card game set in the distant past on ancient Earth. The gods have discovered an unbound power that they receive through human worship, and they thirst for it more than anything else in the universe. We were once used to collect raw materials for the divine sky-chariots to haul away into the heavens, we were nothing more than creatures created by the gods for slave labor… until the divining occurred.

We rebelled against the creators. Fed up with our defiance and costly ignorance, the golden ones ascended into the heavens, much to our surprise. Then, slowly at first but with increasing frequency, our children were born with gifts. We learned that the gods wanted to apologize and through freeing us from their whips and even granting our children the power of the ascended, the beings we once dreaded, were loved as fathers and mothers. We came to accept them as divine beings, for does not a father or mother disciple their children before they are allowed to make decisions that affect their lives? The gods are benevolent and we even have come to depend upon their charity.

All they ask is for our worship and the rains shall fall, the crops shall grow and we shall be at peace… or so we thought. Without worship, the gods cannot function and if one loves their god and their way of life, they will fight for it. So, our leaders, mortals and Immortals alike, have created vast armies to spread the word of our benefactors, the supreme beings that rule over us, like omniscient parents.

The characters are played by Immortals that have been gifted by their god’s, powers of immense potential and limitless possibility. For their gifts, the gods require the Immortal peoples to pontificate the benevolence of the heavenly-ones through words, actions and deeds. Some Immortals find that politics of leadership and the courts are their strong-suit but more often than not, they take to the fields of battle to vanquish the antithesis of their deities. Killing mortals and Immortals, the demi-gods of war shall know no bounds and shall be bound only by their master’s will.

Players create characters through an easy but in-depth creation process. Each character has a school where he learned to use his powers, skills that he picked up to help him during his adventures, techniques which are the moves he uses to engage his foes, equipment to deal damage and blunt attacks, and of course his deck of power. Characters move upon a semi-randomized board as they try to knock their opponents out cold.

The Starhawks are Asterians, the mysterious humanoid aliens that have the suspicion of many within the Corporate worlds.

The team rules stat them as fast movers on the pitch while  simultaneously making them fragile; their armor checks are harder for them to make if they are possibly injured.  They also have special rules that allow them to attempt to trick the referees into punishing the other team by faking fouls.

The base team includes:
3 Jacks
1 Guard
4 Strikers

"The Starhawks see themselves as clearly superior to the average DreadBall team, but that doesn’t mean they aren’t above bending the rules – or just cheating! – to gain any advantage possible"

This game recreates the journey of the Book of Exodus, wherein Moses was called upon by the Lord to lead his people from slavery under the Pharaoh of Egypt and guide them to Canaan, the Promised Land.

This game is for two players, one as Moses, the other as the Pharaoh.

Moses must safely lead his people across the Red Sea, while the Pharaoh must stop him.

This is a strategy game, the winner determined by placing road tiles strategically and effectively.

Shootout in Silver Creek is an entertaining and fast-paced dice and card game.

Take a trip back to the old days of The Wild West and try your hand at running the town of SILVER CREEK. The fight for Silver Creek ain't fer yer average cowpoke. You and your gang are not alone in eyeing the possibilities of the town. So now, the down and the dirty fight has begun in earnest, where all bets are off!

The game offers many opportunities to be a real outlaw: fighting, brawling, shooting, robbery and hanging are a small part of what your gang has to go through to claim the prize that is Silver Creek.

You are divided into two bands of outlaws, fighting each other using cards played each turn. On your turn, you can play both a single Instant Card and a single Shooting Card. The Instant Cards offer a variety of special effects, while the Shooting Cards allows you to roll the dice and see if you wound your target! Every outlaw begins the game at 10 health, and if you ever run out you're out of the game.

When there is only one gang left standing, they gain control of Silver Creek and win the game. Good luck cowboy.

Dictator of the Sea is about a battle between navy and pirate.

Perform one of the three actions below. As soon as the action is done, your opponent begins the next turn.
1. Select one face down Board tile on the board and flip it face up.
2. If a tile from your side (Navy or Pirate), the Whale tile, or the Shark tile is face up on the board, activate one of these units.
3. Spend Treasure to bribe an opponent's unit. Replace one of your opponent's units with your own unit.

Game ends when
1. If one side no longer has any units on the board
-> The player who still has units on the board wins. This player wins even if all his or her units are face down.
2. If 10 turns pass after all tiles are revealed
-> Add all the ranks of captured enemy tiles and the ranks of any of your remaining units on the board to determine your score. The player with the highest score wins. Equal scores means a tie.

A free promotional demonstration version of ZnajZnak. This mini-version is distributed free at Polish boardgame conventions.

In standard ZnajZnak there are 132 cards. In this free demo version, there are only 10 cards. Each of them presents 12 different symbols concerning the contemporary history of Poland. If you take a closer look at any two cards, you'll find out that they contain only one common character. The aim of a player is to notice the character on two other cards on the table and to tell its name as fast as possible. If the player succeeds to do that, he/she passes his/her card to another player. If he/she does not know the name of the character, he/she turns the card, reads the definition of the symbol, puts a new card on the table, and the game goes on. The winner is the person who first gets rid of all the cards in hand.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Gemstone Companion is a supplement to the Gemstone Fantasy Miniature System that expands the original game rules to include a point-buy system, new terrain rules, two new hero types (assassins and priests), six new scenarios, 47 new spells and prayers, 17 sample non-player characters, and 32 new creature entries. Three new optional rules are also included, along with errata to the core rulebook.

Gemstone Adventures expands the Gemstone Fantasy Miniature System to include rules for guiding adventuring parties into monster-haunted dungeons in search of fame and fortune.

The supplement describes 10 new hero types, 10 sample villains, 17 sample adventuring parties, 23 new spells and prayers, five new scenarios, new terrain rules for doors and treasure chests, advice on setting up and using a dungeon environment in Gemstone, and rules for building campaigns and advancing heroes.

The book comes with a cut-apart insert containing 96 encounter cards and 140 treasure and other counters.

Karaokards is a new, fun, social card game, inspired by karaoke and based on the songs in your head. It offers two completely different ways to play, both based around playing cards that describe elements of a song.

One ruleset enables a traditional card game, gathering friends around a table (and can involve drunken singing, but doesn't have to). Players are dealt five cards. Each card contains two "clues" to a song -- for example "Artist begins with B" or "Genre: Rock". In each turn players play a card and build on a central tower of these clues, defining a song (of the player's choice, which may be switched turn by turn). As the tower gets bigger so it becomes harder and harder to find a song that matches all the clues. If a player cannot play a card they pass; the winner of the round is the last player able to play a card, and they score the number of cards in the tower as points.

The other game is something completely new: a game for teams or individuals to play during karaoke. Players are dealt five cards. Again, each card contains two "clues" to a song, but this time the players must combine as many cards as they can within their own hand to come up with a song to sing. Once they've chosen a song they add it to the playlist. When it's their turn to sing they announce the clues they chose and score points for each card they managed to use. During the song, other players may "duet" and score points for their own cards that match their song. When the song is finished players who have sung refresh their hands from the draw pile.

The winner is the player who has scored the most cards when the draw pile has been exhausted and the playlist completed.

Sound the alarm! A huge Tornado! is wreaking havoc on the outskirts of Tornadoville, USA! Rescuers from the elite Tornado! Rescue Unit have been sent to help. You are one of these Rescuers. Get ready to clear debris and free family members from the rubble, but beware the Tornado! Its particular brand of cyclonic evil can block you from finding people in need. Who will be the best Rescuer? Only time will tell!

In Tornado!, two players take turns as the Rescuer and as the Tornado! As the Rescuer, players will flip over cards and remove debris while hunting for and saving trapped family members to earn points. But the Tornado! player will attempt to block the Rescuer’s every move by throwing cubes onto the playing field. Not only can the Tornado! keep the Rescuer from scoring points, it can force the Rescuer to score points for the Tornado!

boardgame: Speculation Queenie 1: New Action and Movement Tiles
description:
Speculation Queenie 1: New Action and Movement Tiles, a mini-expansion for Speculation, can be used individually or in combination with any other expansions for the game.

This expansion consists of twelve action tiles and twelve movement tiles that can replace or be added to the tiles in the base game. Although which action tiles to add or replace is left up to the players, the expansion provides three recommended scenarios. The new actions include the forced passing of one card to another player, ignoring a bank holiday card, accrual of interest, payment of tax, forced purchases, forced sales, and dividends.

子育てドラゴン, which translates to "child-rearing dragon" was published in the doujinshi magazine つぎはぎたより in 2007, which was available in a PDF version for international download shopping from after 2008 until 2013.

In the game, the players take on the roles of dragon mothers who are stuck in human form until their respective child has reached adulthood or died (the latter does not occur in the game). Therefore, challenges have to be overcome by the child or the mother-child combination. Being published in an RPG doujinshi, the game has a lot of flavor text/descriptions.

The players decide when to rest or when to move to a different location (from a set of 6 locations), but otherwise is dependent on the luck of the roll. Each turn (defined as 10 years), unless the mother is resting, a die is rolled to determine the needs of the child and the mother then has to match or come within 1 of that die result. Success leads to a roll on a positive event table (modified by the care attribute/skill) while failure leads to a roll on the risky event table for the current location (which are often but not always negative events). In addition, after handling the events, the player may move the family to an adjacent location.
At the end of each turn, tiredness is checked to see whether the next turn is spent resting. When playing with 2+ players, players in the same location may attack each other to steal loot from each other.

The first player to lead the child to adulthood (which requires 20 units of loot) wins the game. Solo play is mentioned as an option, but no special rules are provided and some of the possible player powers are incompatible with solo play.

Speculation Mini Expansion 2: 7th and 8th Players, a mini-expansion for Speculation, can be used individually or in combination with any other expansions for the game. This expansion includes components to allow for up to eight players in the game, namely additional shares, money, and company cards.

In addition, each player now has a minus card in hand along with the company cards. Instead of laying down a company card at the start of the turn, a player can lay down a minus card. Before revealing which companies move during the turn, everyone who played a minus card reveals it, then secretly plays a company card or "Bank Holiday" card; this company moves backward on the movement track instead of forward, or one "Bank Holiday" card played by someone else is ignored.

boardgame: Firefly: The Game – Mal's Pretty Floral Bonnet Promo Card
description:
The Firefly: The Game - Mal's Pretty Floral Bonnet promo consists of 1 card featuring Malcom from the Firefly television series created by Joss Whedon. It allows a player to ignore die rolls for attempts to capture Wanted Crew. Also it does not count towards a Crew's gear carrying limit (normally 1).

This promo was released by Think Geek as a bonus for ordering Firefly: The Game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Metallum:Psi is a mini-expansion for Metallum that consists of one promotional planet tile. Mix this tile along with the ones from the base game during step 1 of the game set-up. When using Psi, the active player switches the places of his Factory Ship with the opponent's Factory Ship.

Marching to Richmond is a simple strategic game covering the  American Civil War in the region containing the two capitals; the map depicts most of Virginia, most of Maryland, and Pennsylvania as far north as Gettysburg.

The system is very similar to that in Watt's The Peninsular War 1808-14; on each turn a player can spend 12 movement points among their forces, with a maximum of 6 per unit. Units can be moved by ship to a limited extent, and in this game also by railroad. Only one attack can be made each turn, with the outcome determined by die roll; the die results don't depend on the opposing forces at all, but the number of die rolls allowed depends on the number of attacking units and the defensive terrain.

The game is played to an agreed-upon time limit (2 hours is suggested) at which point a small fixed number of final turns is played. The Union player wins if they control enough Confederate cities (depending on whether they still control all of theirs or not), otherwise the Confederates win.

The game was originally published as a do-it-yourself kit (Watts' description) in 1974. A revised version titled "prototype trial edition" was published in 1989. It is unknown if a further edition was released.

boardgame: Speculation Queenie 3: Action and Movement Dice
description:
Speculation Queenie 3: Action and Movement Dice, a mini-expansion for Speculation, can be used individually or in combination with other expansions for the game.

This expansion consists of two dice: one a movement die showing the values 1-6 and the other an action die showing "Trade" on three faces, "+1" on one face, "-1" on one face, and a sand timer on the final face. Instead of using the action tiles or movement tiles from the base game, you can use one or both dice. This change replicates the original game self-published by designer Dirk Henn and adds more randomness to the game as the same movement value or action can appear repeatedly instead of the action and movement tiles being "used up" until the tile bags are reset during the game.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 4: The Fountain
description:
Escape: The Curse of the Temple - Fountain, the fourth mini-expansion for the Escape base game, can be used individually or combined with any other expansions for the game. The fountain tile is shuffled with the other tiles at the start of the game. If a player rolls two golden masks while on the fountain tile, she recovers one of her lost dice; a player can recover only one lost die during the game.

This expansion also includes a new pair of curse tiles, with two copies of each curse. "Poison" forces you to lose one die when the first or second countdown ends, even if you're back in the starting chamber; if you're not in the starting chamber, then you lose two dice instead of one. "Strangle vines" forces you to reroll dice showing the adventurer instead of allowing you to keep them while you reroll other dice.

Funny Physiognomies is a very early sectional card game published in England in the nineteenth century.  Images and sets are created by laying three sectional cards next to each other to earn points.

There are three decks, one for heads, a slim deck for midsections, and another deck for legs and feet.  Players earn points by playing all the matching parts of a figure.

Ionia is a historical board game depicting the first colonization of the ancient Greeks between 12th and 9th century B.C. at the west coast of Minor Asia.
The players take the role of the Ionians, Aeolians and Dorians, cross the Aegean Sea, and establish to Minor Asia new cities, build temples, barracks etc. 
The game ends when the last city on the map colonized by the players and the winner is the player with the most victory points.

Components:
1  Game Board 40x30cm
3  Wooden Meeples
4  Wooden Octagons
30 Tiles 3x3cm
24 Tiles 6x4cm
1 Dice 8sided
Rulebook 4 pages

Chemundo is a teachware cardgame in the world of chemistry.

General features of the basic game:
• Choose the 40 inorganic chemistry cards (IC) or
organic chemistry cards (OC) deck and all the action cards; thus, having a deck of 61 cards

The game:
• Shuffle the cards.
• The dealer then deals seven cards to each player.
• The players choose a referee.
• In each round each player may discard one matching card.
• When dropping the card, the player has to name the
compound on the card correctly.
• If the player is unable to discard, he has to draw a card from
the drawing pile.
• The game is immediately over when the first player goes out of cards.

There are rule variants for Chemundo Rummy and Chemundo Poker

Andaman Fleet is a contemporary game of naval warfare taking place in the Eastern Indian Ocean. It can be played alone or with Asean Fleet.

Time Scale : 
- 8 hours per turn
- 80 km per hex.

The players maneuver war ships, submarines, aircrafts.

The game reuse rules from Victory Games Fleet series, but improved with new weapons as AWACS aircrafts, Ballistic missiles, and SAM.

There are also new rules regarding submarines, Aegis ships, AWACS, and long range air-to-air missiles.

The game includes 7 scenarios, from simple to more complex:

1/ A few Acre of Island
2/ Assault on Andaman Islands
3/ Sword of ASEAN
4/ Wolf Pack
5/ Invasion Bengal
6/ The Enemy Below
7/ Last days of Yangon

An early and unuusual game by De La Rue c. 1900, copyright by Ernest Legh.  The goal of the game is to build a pagoda with specially designed cards without causing the structure to collapse.  The pasteboard box the game comes in serves as a base for the building with gauges that ensure that the first story is correctly formed. It seems complex with rules for attempting to replace partial collapse and foul placement of cards.

War of the Austrian Succession is a wargame of Frederick the Great’s first military campaign, the First Silesian War of 1740-42. This was the opening War of the Austrian Succession which eventually involved the major powers of Europe. The intent of the game is to show operational level warfare in the 18th century Age of Kings. There are two sides in the game, Prussia and its allies (the Coalition), and Austria and its allies (Habsburgs).
 The map portrays the area in Central Europe where the original campaign took place; each hex on the map represents about 7.5 miles across. Each unit repre­sents a brigade or wing of an army (approximately 4,000 - 12,000 men and their equipment), and each turn represents one or two months of operations, depending on the season.

Components: One 22" x 34" map & 280 counters.
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession

description:
The Ukomo Avalanchers are an alien race of sentient dinosaurs (the Teratons) introduced in Season 3 expansion for DreadBall.

Armored hides make Teratons tough customers to begin with.  Couple that with their in-borne defensive ability to teleport when attacked from behind or to move through obstacles as necessary and you can see why they're a popular team.  Playing them takes finesse, though, as they field no strikers and are comparably slow against many other teams---even if they can teleport a limited distance.

The basic team includes 8 models:
4 Teraton Jacks 
4 Teraton Guards

From the DreadBall Sports Network:  "The Avalanchers haven’t been around for long, but their hard-hitting play style has already won them plenty of fans. The fact that they’re giant teleporting reptiles doesn’t hurt, either!"

In Regnbågsspelet (The Rainbow-game) the four ponies Twilight Sparkle, Pinkie Pie, Rarity and Lyra Heartstrings compete as to who reaches the castle first. If you follow the rainbow you advance on the board. If you end up in the waterfalls you risk sliding backwards, but if you are lucky one of your pony-friends might help you escape the slide. The pony that reaches the castle first wins.

A My Little Pony-version of the classic snakes and ladders-game with a few modifications.

In the game box you will find a large green wooden bed, a red blanket, a small blue pillows, a game pad with carpet and the cheeky cat Monty. In the photo cards you can see where Monty is hiding - on the bed, under the bed, next to the pillow.

In turn kid takes the photo card and must reconstruct the scene like seen on the card to score a point.

Player with the most points scored if the deck is exhausted is the winner.

A game in a travel box that attempts to simulate the life of a star. The goal is to collect the most fame and fortune.

Each player has a career (like pop star or director) and spins the wheel to see how many spaces one has to move forward or backward. On each space something happens: Pay or gain a fortune, gain fame,  choose a new career. The player who is first on the last space, “superstar!”, gets three additional fame.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
It's time to leave your parents/cousin/ex and find your own home. Your paltry years in the housing queue don't count for much in the absurd rental market of this city, so you have to navigate the second-hand market and jump from one shaky solution to the next. Work hard in order to afford the exorbitant sublet-rents, study hard in order to be eligible for subsidized student housing, or play hard in order to discover sweet opportunities in your network.

Stockholm is an angst-packed game about living someplace. The game covers six years. Each year you secretly divide your action points between work, study and social activities. Players who work more get more income, players who study more get study grants and student accommodation, and players who engage more in social activities get to pick first from the offers on the informal housing market.

The aim of the game is to maximize any two out of three scores: Urbanity (the supply of services in your area), Living Standard (the indoor size and quality of your housing solution, incl. the absence of annoying people) and Sea&Forest (the scenery, swimming and skating opportunities, ability to pick blueberries and mushrooms).

And if you find a flat with more room than you can afford to rent on your own, then you can snuggle up with other players. There's no place like inside when in Stockholm.

boardgame: Ascension: Chronicle of the Godslayer – Constricting Horror Promo Card
description: Reward: Gain 4 Honor. Reveal cards from the center deck until you reveal a Hero. Acquire that Hero without paying its cost and banish each other card revealed this way.

New souls have arrived in the Inferno, and it's up to you to guide them to their proper Circle of Hell so that they receive a just punishment.

In Nine circles of Hell, the first player to pick up and deliver three souls and return to Limbo wins the game. However, Hell is not an easy place to navigate. Portals leading to the nine Circles can be approached from either of two directions, and each approach teleports you to a different place on the board. To complicate things, when a player uses a portal, the portal spins around so that its approaches are now switched. To deliver your souls, you must puzzle out how to use the portals in your favor to get to where you want to go.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

The game comes with 12 can cards (numbered 1-12) and 2 dice. Each player receives 6 can cards, which they display forming a pyramid.  On their turn a player must throw both dice and verify if the other player has that can. If they do they obtain the card. The game will end when one of the pyramids is empty.

boardgame: Ravensburger Spiele Minis: Ferkel Mau Mau
description:
"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

This game is very similar to the popular UNO, but smaller. It comes with 24 numbered cards (1-6 in 4 colors), 4 special +2 cards (draw 2), 2 stop cards (skip), 3 color option cards and 3 oink oink cards (each player must stand up and say oink oink, the slower player must draw another card).
The first player to get rid of all their cards will be the winner.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

The game comes with 14 blocks, 16 round cards and a die.
On their turn players must throw the die. The number in the die indicates how many pieces they must place in the tower that will be created in the game. If the player makes the tower fall then he will lose the round.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

In this game there are 10 big tiles with 1-4 stars each. There are also 9 small tiles (3 clouds, 3 sun, 3 drops). At the beginning of the game the small tiles are placed over the big tiles. The amount of stars in the big tile remaining will determine which small tiles can be moved. Players try to move the small tiles o that 3 small tiles of a kind end up together.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

There are 14 cards with different objects and 12 chips in the middle of the table. All players look at them and try to memorize them. On a player turn all other players must close their eyes and the player will hide one of the cards. Then players open their eyes and try to guess which card is missing. If they do they get a chip. If they say the wrong object they lose a chip. The first player to get 3 chips will win the game!

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

The game comes with 28 double-sided cards. 
On their turn, players must choose a design from the cards in the middle of the table. Each card indicates how many copies of that design are in the game.  Then they must try to find all copies of that design, one by one. If they do (without revealing any wrong card), they get those cards and then they must place them by the other side before them. So, when a player chooses a design from the middle of the table that has a copy in front of another player then he steals that card. The game will end when there are no more cards in the middle of the table. The winner is the player with the most cards.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

The game comes with 24 playing cards (with 4 animals each on a 2x2 grid), 4 animal cards and 4 double action cards.
Each player receives an animal card. Their objective is to try to get that kind of animal surrounded by other animals. On their turn a player draw one card and then place it next to another already placed card in a 5x5 card grid. The requisite is that at least one animal on the new card is touching the same animal on the previous card. When all cards have been placed the game ends. Then players receive one victory point for every one of their animal surrounded by other animals. 
The double action cards are a variant in which once in each game a player can place 2 cards on their turn instead of only one.

Wiraqocha: Scrolls of Quezacoatl is a mini-expansion for Wiraqocha consisting of four scrolls. During set-up, place the scrolls with the artifacts on the four temples. (Each scroll bears the color of a temple.) When an explorer conquers a temple, he can take the scroll with the artifact.

Later in the game, a player can use a scroll as a die with the value indicated on the scroll — but only for conquest; a scroll cannot be placed in defense on a tile. When a player uses a scroll, it returns to its matching temple.

If during phase 1, a player has an explorer on a temple where a scroll appears, he can take it. If the explorer leaves the temple for any reason, the player keeps the scroll until he uses it. A scroll can be stolen in a base camp as an artifact or a somnium crystal.

Alien Attack! is a strategy board game for 2-4 players that pits them against an impending alien invasion - and each other. A ground war with the aliens seems inevitable as the first waves of enemies approach. Players take the role of weapons development companies seeking a contract with the Earth Defense Force.

Defeating aliens gets you brownie points, but sloppy work will let the next player sweep up the wounded remains. Balance subterfuge and self-promotion while blowing up aliens to claim ultimate victory!

				
				
					=========The game is comprised of multiple player rounds over the course of three waves of invading aliens, with the starting player rotating between each. At the start of each round, cards can be played to give an advantage or disadvantage to yourself or other players for the duration of the round. Most player-to-player sabotage happens at this time, so players go into their turns aware of what odds might be stacked against them.

Once a round has begun, players each do the following as a complete turn:

- Receive energy tokens and event cards;
- Commit energy tokens to their weapons;
- Use event cards for their action, or as currency for upgrading weapons or hiring employees;
- Fire charged weapons into a 3x3 grid of aliens.

Each weapon has its own targeting system and damage types, with stronger weapons needing more energy to fire. Each alien has their own Rock-Paper-Scissors style strengths and weaknesses against damage types.

Players keep alien corpses for end-game points, but can trade them for additional event cards on the Black Market. Weapon upgrades and employees also provide points at the end of the game, but on a handicap system - more points are awarded for weaker weapons or poor employees, awarding clever strategy over pure luck.

The game typically ends when the Wave III deck empties and the 3x3 grid can no longer be stocked, but there are a few conditions where all players lose if they can't coordinate their efforts well enough!

In Alien Overlords, a sci-fi strategy game, players control fleets of starships and attempt to conquer or destroy the 24 planets on the game map. Players start with only one or two planets. Gobble up as many Neutral Planets as you can before enemy players' ships threaten your planets and Home World.

Before play begins, each player picks technologies and bonuses that influence their strategy for the game. The game features three classes of starships: Frigates, Cruisers and Battleships. Frigates are cheap to build, Cruisers move the fastest, and Battleships dish out the most damage. Planets are also of three sizes according to the resource value of that planet. Custom average dice are used for battles, allowing an element of chance while making it difficult to win or lose by luck alone.

Focusing on combat and strategic movement rather than resource management, Alien Overlords plays quickly. Technologies are chosen at the start of the game rather than researched during the game. If a ship is damaged it either explodes or repairs itself so that players do not have to keep track of damage. Over 50 unique, interesting, and sometimes funny Situation Cards give temporary bonuses or allow players to steal from, sabotage or destroy enemy forces.

Alien Overlords allows freedom of ship movement across the map rather than using "space lanes." There is only one type of Resource to manage, an income based on the planets under a player's control. Number and locations of planets on the game map can be adjusted to suit the number of players, the desired game length, and for variety.

Host is a card game, with an alien invasion, zombie infection motif. You have a hand of cards and a secret objective to match a particular set of cards. You draw a card, then pass a card to anyone else at the table, until you reach 10 points. The trick is to see which set cards negatively affect you and pass them off on to the other players. You also must deduce what they need and what they don't need and try to pile on the negative points. We've also add a chaotic event deck, that if you pull an event you will likely reset the game, scramble everyone's cards, or end up with a whole new objective.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Elite Attack Die Resource
description:
The Elite Attack Die is a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 1, "Operation Return," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, The Dominion War.  It is considered a Resource.

Resource:

        Beginning with Month 2 of the Dominion War Organized Play Event, "The Battle of Chin'toka," each player may equip their fleet with exactly 1 Resource.
