The Squadron Point Costs for Resources are located on the Resource Reference Cards. Players may choose from among any available Resources for the Dominion War Storyline OP, but cannot change Resources between Battle Rounds.



Once each round, the Elite Attack Die may be exchanged for 1 of its player's normal attack dice.  Each face of the Elite Attack Die has a result on it, as opposed to the blanks on normal attack dice, and thus gives a greater chance of a positive outcome for the attacking player.  The Elite Attack Die may be added to a squadron for 5 Squadron Points.

A children's game from the 70's.
Each player controls 1-4 cars. Skidding and collisions are covered.
Every player has the same number of cars, but each one may decide for himself, which of the cars he wants to move after the throw of the die.
The first player to get all of his cars across the goal line has won.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Command Tokens Resource
description:
The Command Tokens set is a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 2, "The Battle of Chin'toka," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, The Dominion War. It is considered a Resource.

Resource:

        Beginning with Month 2 of the Dominion War Organized Play Event, "The Battle of Chin'toka," each player may equip their fleet with exactly 1 Resource. The Squadron Point Costs for Resources are located on the Resource Reference Cards. Players may choose from among any available Resources for the Dominion War Storyline OP, but cannot change Resources between Battle Rounds.



The Command Tokens set allows a player to choose 5 tokens from a set of 10 and place them on the token bar.  Once each round, that player may use the ability of 1 Command Token and flip it over, not to be used again during that battle.  Each token allows the player to either take one of several free actions (Evade, Battlestations, Scan, or Target Lock), re-roll attack or defense dice, add one attack or defense die to their own dice pool, or predetermine the outcome of one attack or defense die in a roll.  The Command Tokens set may be added to a squadron for 5 Squadron Points.

boardgame: Ascension: David, Prophetic Guide Promo Card
description:
David, Prophetic Guide is an Ascension promo card featuring July 2013 Ascension Immortals tournament winner, David McNamara.

Cost: 5 Rune
Value: 3 Honor
Effect: Draw three cards, then put two cards from your hand on top of your deck in any order.

Hurricane: The Game of Preparedness is an update to the original Hurricane Game created in 1988.  The new version features a more compact and redesigned box, additional resorts, and new Hurricane Trivia cards. In the original game a player would read the hurricane update on their turn, but in this version the hurricane updates are determined based on rolling the dice.

2-4 players brave the storm as they buy and sell exotic resorts in hurricane prone areas.  They must buy insurance and take advantage of opportunities to prepare their resorts, or face the imminent threat of the hurricane!

As in the original, there is also an advanced play option, where players cruise their yachts through the inner game board from one resort to the next, while trying to avoid the inevitable hurricanes.  But players must also beware of another threat... The Bermuda Triangle!  The player with the most money at the end of the game is the winner, and therefore the best prepared player.

boardgame: Dead@17: The Battle for Darlington Hills
description:
Dead @ 17: The Battle for Darlington Hills is a deck-building game in which players fight for the side of good or evil, building powerful decks. This game, based on Josh Howard's Image comic book series, has players taking on the role of the main character Nara and her friends. They will have to collect and use an arsenal of weapons that can range from knives to powerful magic. These weapons are used to push back the seemingly endless army from Hell. One player will use an evil avatar to summon these minions. Using characters right out of comic they will attempt to open portals to Hell at locations around the town of Darlington Hill.

The struggle between the factions plays out in the supply deck of cards as well as at the locations. Each card that the players acquire has a use for either the good or the evil player. The more powerful cards for one is a weaker card for the other. Players may take on aggressive or defensive strategies while building their decks. The game concludes as one player opens the gates to Hell or the other banishes them to the darkness.

Infamy: Corruption is a 6-card mini expansion for Mercury Games' Infamy.

It contains 3 new Mission Cards and 3 New Scheme Cards, one for each faction in the game.

The Triggerman Mini-Expansion is the second small expansion for Mercury Games' Infamy.

It contains 6 new Mission Cards, one for each sector in Infamy.

The Zombie Apocalypse has begun!

Fortunately, YOU have your priorities straight. What could be more important? It’s time to grab your shotgun and go out Geocaching!
The Zombie Apocalypse Geocaching expansion pack adds an incredible amount of fun, twists, and strategy to Cache Me If You! Nothing puts a thrill into geocaching like running down a few Zombies along the way. A Zombie or two won’t be much of a problem, but watch out when they start to gather and overwhelm. And if they get to you, you will play out the game as a geocaching Player Zombie!
The expansion pack includes all of your favorite items… shotguns, chainsaws, crowbars and more!
     A New Zombie Hunting Character
     Specialized Equipment Cards
     Additional Event Cards
     Even More Caching Area Cards
The Zombies will be moving and gathering their numbers… Geocaching will never be the same!

In DeZpair: 24 horas de deZespero, a band of mercenaries tries to rescue the survivors of a zombie-infested city before the military "cleans up" the place with a nuclear bomb. You have 24 hours to swipe the city clean of people before the bomb explodes!
   
The game uses a modular board made of tiles to model the city search and zombie attacks, with custom dice used for character skills. Players use the dice to fight the zombies, search for survivors, and loot anything that the previous citizens left behind before joining the undead horde. The tiles show how many zombies are in a location, and the game unfolds through the exploration of these tiles.

This awesome set contains everything you need to recreate the daring raid that paved the way for the Allied D-Day invasion.

The Mission

The mission, in the opening minutes of the Normandy landings, was to capture the Bénouville Bridge and the nearby Ranville Bridge in order to prevent German reinforcements from reaching the beaches, where Allied forces were pouring ashore. 180 men, led by Major John Howard, landed close to their targets in gliders. Using the element of surprise, they were tasked with seizing the bridges, and more importantly, hold them against the inevitable German counter attack. Bénouville Bridge was later renamed Pegasus Bridge, in reference to the shoulder emblem worn by the British Airborne forces.

The Battlefield

Using the contents of the battle set, you can really set the scene for this pivotal battle. The centrepiece is a laser-cut model of the bridge itself, accompanied by models of the Cafe Gondre, as well as a pill box, a gun pit and even two telegraph poles!

Next come the forces themselves - for the defenders, 20 German infantry, backed up with a 5cm KwK 39 anti-tank gun and MG42 machine gun team. The attackers are a 10-man British Airborne squad plus a 2-man PIAT team, and an exclusive model of Major John Howard, which is only available in this set. You'll also get decal sheets for both the sides to help you prepare your forces for battle.

Last but not least the box includes a scenario booklet that describes how to set up and fight the battle for Pegasus Bridge. (Remember, this is a supplement for the Bolt Action game, so you'll need a copy of the core rules too!)

Box Contents:

Laser-cut Bridge
    Laser-cut Cafe Gondres
    Resin Gun Pit
    Metal 5cm KwK 39 anti-tank gun and 3 crew
    Laser-cut Pillbox
    Metal German MG42 MMG team
    2 laser-cut telegraph poles (on the Cafe sheets)
    20 plastic German Infantry and plastic bases
    Major John Howard and plastic base (exclusive new model)
    10 man British Airborne squad and plastic bases
    2-man PIAT team and plastic bases
    Scenario booklet
    Decal sheets for the Paras and Heer

High Frontier Interstellar is a solitaire expansion to High Frontier Colonization that allows players to travel out of our solar system to the stars beyond.  It can be played independently or as an extension of a standard (Colonization) game.

To play you need all of the following:

     High Frontier
     High Frontier Colonization
     The free Interstellar rules available from Sierra Madre Games
     The Interstellar Map available on Zazzle.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A Checkers variant for 2, 3, or 4 players.  The game is played on a 14 x 14 grid of red and black squares, but with four squares in each corner taken out.  The central 10 x 10 grid is the field of play and is marked by a thin white "Haven line."  Player pieces are in four colors and begin in the four sections outside of the Haven line, called "King's Havens," on the black squares printed with checkers pieces.  (Each player has 10 pieces.)

Rules are essentially the same as English draughts, but for the complication of having four players.  Once players move their pieces into the field of play, they may not move back over the Haven line at any point on the board.  Promotion is achieved at the furthest row in the field of play from each player.  Rules are also provided for partnership play.

The central 8 x 8 square section of the board is highlighted so that it may be used for a standard game of Checkers.

boardgame: ΔV: The Game of Spatial Combat
description:
Based on the elegance of chess, checkers, and go, and inspired by the beauty of deep space, DeltaV is the game of spatial combat.

Someday, humanity will harness enough energy to become a starfaring species. But what then? Will we explore the stars, united? Or will humanity fragment into the bubble of space around the Solar System? We're apparently alone in the galaxy, and if we turn inward, against each other, the war will be more frightening than any fought so far.

In the game of DeltaV, you can find out if you have what it takes to survive spatial combat. Fight against one to five other fleet commanders over a region of space. Deceivingly simple, the DeltaV board contains only as many positions as a standard checkerboard. But in three dimensions, those positions can have much greater significance.

One board supports up to six players, and multiple boards may be combined for even larger battles.

Rioting Gangs is a Booster-Pack expansion for the game Police Precinct.

It includes an assortment of new Event Cards in new locations:
3 Emergencies (3 different colors, all "Rioting Gangs")
1 Street Collapse
1 Caught In The Act
1 Budget Cuts
1 Homicidal Maniac
1 fixed Car Theft

and

1 Compass fix (sticker)

Features:
Accommodations made to help the colorblind.
Block Numbers (to help find locations)

An adaptation of Checkers for 3 or 4 players, Fourplay uses a 10 x 10 grid of squares for its board, with four corners of four squares each left off.  A border surrounds the central 6 x 6 "Field."  The outer portions of the board are called "Bases."  There are four colors of pieces; each player begins with 6 each and places them on the dark squares of their base.

The game plays essentially the same as English Draughts, but with a few differences.  Once players move into the Field, they may not move back into their own or any other player's Base, unless it is as part of a multiple jump that ends back in the Field.  Once a piece is in the Field, it moves as a promoted piece, in any direction.  Captures are compulsory, and a player's own pieces may be captured.

Variations are provided, either for last-man-standing victory conditions, or for scoring points.  Also a more challenging variation called "DUBL-CHEKRS" is described.

The board itself is reversible, with a standard 8 x 8 checkerboard on the reverse.

The board and instructions feature small diagrams of variations of the Fourplay board, with larger Base areas; it is unknown if the game was ever published with these alternate boards.

Flip over the cards to collect sets of non-matching gear to win. Sets of cards are created or added to by all players. To start a set, put down two cards from your hand or to add to a set, you may play one card. Cards played must be different in all of the three elements on the card, gear, number and color. You keep the set if you are the one to complete it. The player with the most sets wins.

On July 16, 1861, the federal Army of the Republic was ordered to Richmond to capture the Confederate capitol.  The two armies met at a railroad junction town called Manassas near a stream called Bull Run.
  
Now the commanders of the opposing forces can re-live and re-fight that famous battle.  The board is stylized representation based on actual maps.  The map is printed on vinyl coated paper with one inch hexes.  There are 54 card stock printed pieces enclosed in clear hard plastic cases that stand upright.  This is in essence plastic-cased minatures. The game has a hidden strength factor the effectively simulates the raw, unseasoned troops in the actual battle.  There are also provisions for artillery fire, and terrain modifiers.  Victory is obtained by eliminating the opposing player's 6 strength General, two 3 strength generals or all the enemy troops.  Victory can also be obtained if the opposing player or team surrenders.

Only 500 copies of this special wooden box edition were produced, with 100 available for preorder 2013 in October.

It also contains Glück Auf: Phasenkarten, an exclusive expansion to Coal Baron that adds 5 "phasecards" to the game during setup. The cards change the shift phase of the game. Every turn begins with choosing one of the cards (this allows the player to choose in which part of the game the players can use their workers). The cards give a special bonus to the player who uses them. These cards were only available at Essen 2013 with the limited wooden box version.


ESSEN, Ende des 19. Jahrhunderts: Solarenergie und Ökostrom sind noch reine Zukunftsmusik. Stattdessen gibt es „unendliche“ Kohlevorkommen. Ihr seid Besitzer von Kohleminen und müsst zusehen, dass ihr Loren produziert, in eure Bergwerke schafft und euren Stollen so viel Kohle wie nur möglich entreißt. Mit der geförderten Kohle bestückt ihr Aufträge, und sobald ihr diese ausliefert, erhaltet ihr Siegpunkte. Allerdings benötigen die Aufträge immer ganz bestimmte Kohlesorten. Da jede Sorte in einer eigenen Stollenetage schlummert, müsst ihr manchmal tief runter ... und das ist teuer.
GLÜCK AUF ist ein „tiefschürfendes“ und dennoch leicht zugängliches Brettspiel, das für Familien ebenso geeignet ist wie für erfahrene Spieler. Insbesondere die eigenen Bergwerke, mit ihren beweglichen Förderkörben, garantieren Spielspaß auf jedem „Niveau“. Glück Auf!

—from eggertspiele.de

Content: 15 Orange God tiles (expansion can only be played with original Bora Bora game from Ravensburger)

Place the Orange God tiles in the general supply next to the game board.

At the beginning of phase A, if a player rolls two of the same number he receives one Orange God tile from the general supply. If he rolls three of the same number (i.e. the same number on all three dice), he receives two Orange God tiles. If a player rolls two or more of the same number but there are no Orange God tiles left in the general supply, he doesn't receive anything.

Whenever a player places a die on an action card, he can use the Orange God tiles to turn his die 1 number up or down. The tiles then go back into the general supply. Note: the die number must be changed before the die is placed onto an action card. One die number can be changed per Orange God tile used. Also note, a die showing the number 1 cannot be turned down to a 6, and a die showing the number 6 cannot be turned up to a 1.

boardgame: ASL Comp: Bandenkrieg – Hell Behind the Eastern Front
description:
North of BORISOV, RUSSIA, 4 April 1943: Operation Lenz Sud had commenced in hopes of finally reducing or eliminating the growing Partisan force near Borisov. As the German forces began the push the partisans were ready and awaiting for an ambush. The Partisans were not trained soldiers and the tactics of the partisans were simple and the challenges put on them by their leaders were less than would be expected from a military unit. Surprise and ambush were their best tactic. Their spot chosen lay on both sides of the Narotch stream. Having capturing a German field gun earlier and using their long hidden anti tank gun—a leftover from the retreating Russians in 1941—the ambush was prepared. Mines were placed, demolition charges were planted by explosive teams smuggled behind German lines and now the wait began. One Partisan survivor remembered: “We would ambush the Germans on the Narotch stream bank. We knew the Germans planned to come to the aid of the guard from the other side of the stream. Our commander, Sagalchik, was also a guide, as he had lived in Miadel for many years and was familiar with the area. When we arrived everything was quiet. Suddenly shots were heard. We lay down and awaited the Germans. When we received the sign to attack we ran to the houses where the Germans and Lithuanian sharpshooters were. The battle lasted a few hours and the Germans dispersed.” Within an hour the fight was over leaving the battlefield in partisans hands. The Germans suffered 40 dead and over 50 wounded. “Lenz-Sud’ was off to a bloody start.

Bandenkrieg: Hell Behind the Eastern Front is a historical module depicting combat that occurred during an anti-partisan operation, Operation Lenz Nord, near Borisov in Byelorussia in April 1943.

The three majors wars of Finland are represented by scenarios in this product: Winter war, Continuation war and Lapland war. There is also a bonus scenario that depicts action from "Vapaussota" (Finland's war of independence in 1918).

Fire & Ice is essentially a combined and revised reprint of two previous scenario packs: 1996's Jatkosota and 2004's Finland at War.

 Components:

     special rules (8 pages)
     2 identical countersheets
     4 winterized maps (W1, W2, W3, W4)
     1 8x11" overlay map (no identification on it)
     25 scenarios (1 in 1919, 8 in 1939-40, 7 in 1941, 1 in 1942, 1 in 1943, 7 in 1944)


(from the introduction and user's description)

boardgame: Jungle Speed Safari: Hyena Promo Card
description:
Hyena's Feast (2 additional cards)

When this card appears, all players become scavengers and race to be the first to touch the "lost cards" that lie between the totems. The first player to do so wins the cards and places them face up into his score pile. If there are no cards between the totems, nothing happens, and play moves onto the next player.

It's an "every man for himself" bombfest on a battlefield of hexagon spaces. Have a blast running around the battlefield, collecting power-ups, and trying to trap your buddies with explosives before they can do the same to you!


    Notes and features*



    2 sided, interchangeable floor map pieces for a different battlefield each game.



    Up to 4 players compete on either a Small Map using 7 of the above mentioned map pieces, or a Large Map using all 19 pieces.



    Blow up Bricks to reveal Power-Ups and gain an edge. Just beware Misfortune and its random negative effects!



    All of these features, packaged to fit in your pocket.

A deluxe edition in a wooden box containing the base game, the expansion (L'Extension) and some previously unreleased cards (extension "Expert", also included in the "la Totale / deluxe" edition). Only 3000 boxes of this limited edition were released, each contains a certificate of authenticity signed by both authors. It seems that this version was only released in France in december 2005. According to the French wikipedia page it was sold out in a month (no source cited). However some boxes were given as tournament prizes during the parisian GenCon in May 2007.

Each player is dealt a hand of cards. In order to win you must be the first player to get rid of all of your cards. Each turn, all of the players reveal one of their cards. If two cards are identical, those players must make a grab for the Totem. The faster player then gives their cards to their unfortunate adversary.

To add to the difficulty, certain cards are almost identical, which can trick a hapless player into grabbing the Totem by mistake -- a grave error. Other cards force all players to make a grab at once, change the method of play, or otherwise add to the difficulty.

The wooden Totem sits in the middle of the table, waiting for the player with the fastest reflexes to snatch it up and win the game.

boardgame: Happy Feet Two: Hold on Erik
description:
In Hold On Erik!, Young Erik from Happy Feet Two sits atop a towering wall of ice, and it's your job to delicately poke out frozen blocks one by one—using a fish, of course. On each turn, players use the little fish pushers to prod a block loose from the icy wall. The tension builds with every successful move...until someone sends poor Erik toppling into the cold. This chilly challenge is quick to learn, fun to play, and adorable in execution—until things collapse.

Knockout, Hold On Scooby-Doo!, Happy Feet Two: Hold on Erik, and Spider-Man: Spider Sense are all about removing bricks without toppling the structure—but each adds its own flavor. While the “Hold On” trio features perched characters and plastic frameworks, Knockout trades in cartoon mascots for pure demolition thrills with its unique battery-powered jackhammer and iconic “Knockout” brick that could spell your doom. Same tension. More vibration.

A "chesimal" is a chess animal, an autonomous multi-unit piece with its own "brain" unit, an activator that allows same-colored pieces that are directly connected to the activator to move. A chesimal that has lost its brain can no longer move, although the remaining units of the chesimal stay on the board, blocking friendly pieces and subject to capture by enemy pieces.

The game, an introduction to the idea of chesimals and activators, is played on a 16x24 board, with 32 units per side forming 4 chesimals. Each chesimal is a mix of 3 different short range chesslike units, each of which moves differently. Two of the chesimals are "royal" units, consisting of a royal brain, 2 knights, and 4 "men", pieces that move like a king but are not royal. The other 2 pieces contain a brain, 4 men, and 4 rooklike or 4 bishoplike pieces, which only move 1 or 2 squares/turn.

Players alternate turns, moving 1 chesimal/turn. The object of the game is to capture your opponent's 2 royal brain units before losing both of yours.

Tombs is a quick and easy game of tomb-raiding in both colour and (printer kind) lineart versions. The Tombs board is a brickwork grid of tiles the player must navigate to reach their goal. However the Tomb is always geared against the player, who must keep their valiant band of explorers alive by selling artifacts and managing inventory to argument the trials of the Tomb. To win the expedition must recover the Sword of Valhalla before their inventory runs out or they all meet an untimely end.

description:
Mayhex! possibly the greatest and most addictive fantasy card game ever created - coming soon from Thornway Games.

Taken from the Celtic portmanteau of mayhe​m (a situation where there is little or no order or control) and hex (a wicked spell or curse which brings bad luck), Mayhex! basically means trouble!



Mayhex! is a fantasy based zombie survival card game (for 2 - 8 players (the more the better), aged 13+) which features a new game mechanic - you are able (amongst other actions) to Dice Duel or Coin Duel via the cards, so no real dice or coins are required to play the game.




The Objective:

The object of the game is to be the first to gain 100 points; only then will you be able to cure the zombie virus and save yourself.

Things are never quite that easy though, are they? Especially when there are Goblins stealing your equipment, Undead hunting in the catacombs and Mayhex! magic getting in the way.



Contents:

Each pack consists of a deck of 108 unique cards.

Every card has two dice (scores ranging from 2 to 12) and a coin (either a Head or Tails) printed on it (to enable you to Dice Duel or Coin Duel). You don't require anything else to play.

Mayhex!'s 108 unique playing cards consist of Positive Cards, Negative Cards, Action Cards, Mayhex! Cards, Infection Cards, and Uninfected Cards.



Like any good card game, Mayhex! requires both skill and a touch of luck to win.

At the start of your turn you must select a card from the Well. You must play one card from your Hand on either yourself, into the Pit or more enjoyably into another player's Lab.

How your friends will laugh when you make them Dice Duel to see if their entire Hand must be placed directly into the Pit or maybe you’ll give them minus 25 points or better yet, an Infected Card.



We'll be uploading how to play Mayhex! tutorial videos to the Thornway Games website in late February 2015. (Pro tip number 1 is: learn the cards!)

From the back of the box:

The rag monster loves fabric patches to bits! And he can't get enough of them! Sometimes he even eats so many patches at one time that this belly hurts. So he'd better be fed, but with the right patches. Do you want to help? Who has a good sense of touch and pulls the right patches from the little bag?

Pick a Patch is a collection of three nimble-fingered educational games. Each game uses set of cards with different kind of fabric that have to found in a bag by touch. The goal is to find sets with the same texture.

Dungeon Fighter: The Big Wave is the second of four expansions for use with Dungeon Fighter, each themed to one of the four elements: air, earth, wind and fire.

This expansion includes new monsters and equipment, a new pirate hero, new maps with special rooms, and Cthulhu as the Boss. As in a fantasy role-playing game, players can accumulate experience points, then use them for power cards that allow for devastating water powers, such as the "Major Healing" spell — but beware the new monster choking ability! You'll have to fight them off by using the three small elemental dice to make new "impossible" die rolls...

boardgame: Yamato 2199 Game: Chapter 5 – Raise the Siege
description:
Tactical level wargame based on Japanese animation series Space Battleship Yamato 2199.

Both players are fleet commanders of the Gamilas Empire, and they alternately battle against Terran's space battleship Yamato. When a player engages against the Yamato, their opponent controls the Yamato.

Each turn, players determine initiative. The players take turn managing movement and combat in any order. The movement is affected by facing. The combat is affected by fire power, range, armor and dice.

Each Gamilas fleet has 27 fleet units. Yamato is loneliness, however she is very strong and tough.

The player who sinks the Yamato wins the game.

The game includes a rulebook, a game map, and 50 die-cut counters.

Eriku is a bonus character for Tokaido / Tokaido Crossroads

The card was given out at Essen Spiel fair 2013, with the release for Crossroads.

Small backstory on the card: Eriku is a friendly wink to Eric Burgess (host of the podcast Boardgame Babylon). When I came to the Strategicon convention in Los Angeles, in 2012, I taught Eric Tokaido. The game pleased him, so he decided to explain the rules to other players during the event. I caught him at one of his explanations, committing a small rule mistake: he indicated that travelers took a meal at the beginning of the game, the relay station of departure. I then politely interrupted him, saying, "No, Eric, travelers do not eat before starting their journey, this first meal must be earned!" To which he replied, "Of course we eat before starting the trip, we are Americans ain’t we!" The character Eriku was born shortly after.

Included in Tokaido: Crossroads & Matsuri (2025).

Rally racing with Gears! This expandable board game features fast paced, full contact racing set on the desert world of Terra Nova. Race around the track, burn rubber to pull ahead, jump over terrain, shoot your opponent if he gets a bit to far ahead, take a swipe at him with your vibro blade or push him in the wall. All this along with spin-outs, skids and rattled pilots bring the exciting world of Gear Rally racing to your table top. Includes a new fast and easy to learn ruleset with lots of rule and game play examples.

Badlands Rally uses D6 dice to resolve both opposed and unopposed actions. Each model, called a Gear, has a number of Attributes that distinguish its own special characteristics and advantages in the game. Players must choose what combination of attack, special movement or other actions will lead them to victory. Dice mechanics allow for quick resolution of these actions through simple rolls of varying numbers of dice depending on the modifiers that apply. All modifiers either add dice to a roll or subtract them. There are two kinds of roll: A Roll and a Check.  A Roll is an unopposed test which uses a simple comparison of dice to an Attribute for a number of successes.  A Check is an opposed test between players that uses the high dice in a roll and adds a bonus based on the other dice compared to an Attribute. In each lap of a game a Gear may resolve between four and six actions depending on player choices. A standard match is 2-3 laps.  Variable Gear upgrades, Gear choices, and Scenarios allow for an almost infinite variety of game conditions. Additional Gear data cards are provided that allow player to use almost the entire Heavy Gear line of miniatures from Dream Pod 9 to add even more variety to their games. Up to Eight Gears can race at once with two to four players being the norm.
 The box set includes rules booklet, full color hex map, 4 easy to assemble racing gear miniatures of the Rally Viper, Rally Pitbull, Rally Jerboa and Rally Ferret cast in pewter with plastic hex bases, 8 double-sided datacards (Rally Viper/Viper II, Rally Pitbull/Pitbull II, Rally Jerboa/Jerboa II, Rally Ferret/Ferret II, Rally Grizzly/Mamba, Rally Cobra/Chasseur MkII, Rally Sapeur/Iguana, and Rally Hunter/Dart Jager), assorted counters (rattled tracker gears, rattled, rally, damage, and upgrades) , 2 double-sided quick reference cards (rules and weapons/upgrades), and 6 dice. The double-sided Rally Grizzly/Mamba, Rally Cobra/Chasseur MkII, Rally Sapeur/Iguana, and Rally Hunter/Dart Jager datacards can also be used to proxy in other similar size gears from the Heavy Gear Blitz and Arena miniature lines.

Invictus – Latin, meaning “unconquered”.

2 player abstract game played on the spaces of a 9x9 board.
Each player has 18 dice pieces, one set black the other white.

Setup
Place the 9x9 board between the players.

Next, players turn 3 of their 18 dice pieces so that the top faces show all ones then 3-twos, 3-threes, 3-fours, 3-fives and 3-sixes.

Now Black sets his dice pieces onto any space of the second and third rows of the board that are closest to him, then White does the same.

Object
Player with the highest score at games end, wins.

Play
The fifth row of the board is known as the Divide and divides the board into two territories.

One player plays black the other white.

Black moves first, then play alternates.

On your turn you must take one of two actions:

1.Move a die one space either forward, left, right or diagonally forward, never backwards onto an empty space.
2.Capture a die one space diagonally forward, like a pawn in chess performing a capture.

When any friendly dice reach an opponent’s first row, they are removed from play and used in scoring at endgame.

Captures
A friendly die may only capture an opponent’s die of equal face value or less, the captured die is returned to your opponent if its face value is greater than one. On receiving the captured die from your opponent you must turn that die so that the face value of the die now reads one less, then place this die onto an empty space on your side of the Divide.

Any captured dice that have a face value of (1) are removed from play and kept for scoring at endgame.

The game ends when a player moves all of his remaining dice onto his opponent’s first row.

If any of your opponents dice are past the Divide and in your territory at endgame they will be added to your opponents score.

Score 
Add up all of the top face values of your dice that reached you’re opponents first row, any captured dice and any of your dice that were within enemy territory.

Highest score wins!

The Myth: Slaughterfield Supplement Pack contains three separate Slaughterfield Decks. These decks can only be used with the Slaughterfield game mode.

The first deck is a 10 card Random Spawn Generator. This allows you to have a completely random game every time. Each session of Slaughterfield consists of 10 waves of terror. The Heroes task is only to survive as long as they can and take as many of them with you as possible. Traps can be turned against the Darkness, but to separate from the rest of the group is certain death.

The second deck is a 15 card Slaughterfield Items deck. These items can only be used in Slaughterfield, but every treasure pick-up is guaranteed to be a good one. This deck greatly increases the survivability past Wave 5.

The third deck is a 15 card Slaughterfield Achievement Deck. This deck contains tasks for the Heroes. These tasks are dealt randomly to each player and are kept secret until turned in for a reward or at the end to supplement victory points awarded for kills and waves survived.

A simultaneous strategy game for 2-4 players.

You are a spider competing with other spiders to build webs and catch flies around a house, but watch out for the humans!

The bigger your web the more likely you are to catch a fly, and the more likely you are to be spotted by the terrible humans.

Each player plays cards that causes humans, flies and spiders to act at the same time. Plot your own plans while attempting to interrupt others.

The winner is the first to catch three flies, or the last spider standing, depending on the difficulty setting.

The Firefly: The Game -  Wash's Lucky Dinosaurs Promo Card features Wash from the Firefly television series created by Joss Whedon. When Flying, a player can re-roll any Test result of 1.  Does not count towards Gear Limit.

description:
Uni-Spec (also called Spec in the rules) is played on a 7x7 grid. The grid spaces are purchased one at a time by players; on each turn the active player chooses an unowned space to be sold. If it is one of the spaces on the main diagonals, it has a printed price (from 1-7 units) and the player simply buys it. Otherwise an auction is conducted.

Each space on the board has an intrinsic value of 1, but a group of orthogonally-connected spaces is worth the square of its size.

If players need more money, they can take loans equal to half the value of their owned spaces. The rules for this are a bit unclear (no mention of repayment, for example). Players can also sell spaces back to the bank to acquire cash; such spaces are not resold  to players (a fifth color of marker is provided for them).

The game ends when all spaces have been sold. The player with the greatest net worth is the winner.

A few simple variants are provided (e.g., ignore the prices on the main diagonal spaces, don't allow loans, etc.).

The empty space between planets reveals the dark mysteries of the cosmos. Quantum anomalies are abundant in the VOID, and study there is rewarded with more frequent breakthroughs.

At the start of your turn, for each of your ship dice on the void map tile, you gain +1 research.

Gaining research from the void does not use up any of your actions or special ability uses for your turn. Ships that earn you +1 research from the void can move and use special abilities normally that turn.

boardgame: Tzolk'in: The Mayan Calendar – Tribes & Prophecies: Ek Chuah
description:
A new tribe and a new prophecy for Tzolk'in: The Mayan Calendar - Tribes & Prophecies.

It was given out by Czech Games Edition at Essen Spiel 2013.

Later it was a promotional tribe tile given away as part of the 2020 Secret Cabal Kickstarter Promo Pack.

Ek Chuah allows the player to borrow resources from the bank.

description:
Join Klaas Vaak (the Sandman, who brings on dreams and sleep by sprinkling sand into the eyes of the children) and travel the world of the Efteling, the stars, and the moon, on the back of the owl Oehoe Houdoe. But beware! Klaas Vaak has to be back in his sandcastle before sunrise.

You get 20 minutes of playtime. Grab a die and for every player a pawn. Every player throws the die and moves his/her pawn the number of pips shown on the die. Will you make sure that every child is in Dreamland on time?

The first player to reach the sandcastle wins the game, but if no-one has one within 20 minutes, everybody loses.

My proposed successor to a Moscow-Campaign-scale/-type game, Barbarossa's Climax: Moscow 1941, available as a free print (off, assemble,) and play game at www.CoatneyHistory.com/BarbClim.htm .

As relatively simple as it is, it is still a 4-player afternoon-and-evening - weekend - game.  There *is* a system-introducing Battle for Tula subgame with it (like 1st Alamein is the subgame for my larger Battles for Alamein, and I have also just completed a smaller scaled Battle for Moscow game, Moscow vs. Barbarossa 1941 - at www.CoatneyHistory.com/MvB41.htm - which incorporates and demonstrates (in its smaller and more simple format) many of BC's basic design concepts).

6-day turns, 10mis/16kms per hex, brigade-division level, Blitzkrieg breakthrough phase (in addition to higher odds BReakthrough combat results enabling a unit to attack twice), Combat Odds Round Off (including UP) and *Expansion* Rule, Russian Katyusha Rocket Regiments (8, eventually), the obligatory Siberian Reserve Gamble, and more.

Over 300 units, most of them infantry having Entrenched/(Artillery-)Emplaced backside.

description:
A variant of Checkers for up to four players, American Checkers was published in time for the USA's bicentennial year of 1976 and features the colors red, white, and blue prominently.

It is played on a 13x13 grid of checkered squares, with six squares in a triangular pattern removed from each corner.  Occupying the center of the board are four large square safe zones where pieces may move and not be captured.  Nine pieces per player are used, in red, white, blue and gold, with their starting positions marked on two rows of squares close to each side of the board.  The game may be played individually or in partnership, and is essentially the same as Checkers but for the extra players and safe zones.  Capture is compulsory when not in the safe zones.  Promotion occurs by reaching the opposite end of the board.  Last player with pieces remaining wins the game.

Up and Down is a dice game played with one dice with the letters U P D O W N on the faces.

The players roll the dice during a turn until either "UP" or "DOWN" has been spelled out in full. "UP" counts one point, "DOWN" nothing, after 10 turns the player with the most points wins.

Copyright from the back of the dice blister 1967 by F.F.G.

A quiz game.

Each round starts with a question and the setting of a 30 second timer.

Each player has two opportunities to answer the question (the questions are open, for example "Name a car brand that begins and ends with a consonant", so there are always lots of possible answers). When a player answers, she puts her hand on the board. Next player puts her hand on top of the first hand and so on.

When the timer goes off, round is over. The topmost hand scores five points, the next hand scores four points and so on. Scores are recorded and the next round begins. The player with the most points after 20 rounds wins.

The ultimate evolving party game, inspired by games like We Didn't Playtest This, Risk Legacy, and 1000 Blank Cards.

On your turn, you simply play a card from your hand of five in front of another player and they do what the card says.  If this is your first game, however, no cards have any text on them.  It's up to the players to provide text on the cards, based on one of eight possible symbols printed onto the cards.  For instance, the Judge symbol requires players write something that everyone must do and then be judged by the player playing the card.  Whoever is judged best is given the card, and the corresponding points value also printed on the card.

The game ends when any one player has 5 cards in front of them, or the draw deck (comprised of 25 cards drawn randomly from all of the cards included in the game) has run out.  The player with the most points wins!

MathMind® is a game of simple math and fast hands. Each player has a set of cards with the numbers from 0 to 9. First player places a card (by choice or randomly) on the left side of the game board, next player (clockwise) places a card (also by choice or randomly) on the right side of the game board. Now the first player rolls the MathDice.

As soon as the dice cup is lifted an equation is revealed. All players calculate the result and then try to find it among all of the answer cards spread over the table. He or she who finds the answer first grabs the card and places it next to him/her in such a way that all players may reach it. The answer card now constitutes one point.

Once the answer card has been found the dice cup is passed on to the next player (still clockwise), the played cards are removed from the game board and a new turn begins.

Should an answer that has already been taken by a player come out again, everyone may try to steal that answer. Again fastest hand wins. If a player grabs an answer card that he or she has already has by his/her side, a special card is given to remember that extra point.

The game is over when all cards have been played. The winner is the one with the most answer cards. In case of equality the digits on the answering cards are counted, higher number wins (i.e. "2" is one digit and "1.25" is three digits).

To sum up the idea of the game:
Finding the answer was easy - but where is it?

Ski the Mountain is a competitive skiing game for up to six players in which the board is the piste map. The game recreates a real day on the piste with challenges, restaurant stops, and unexpected events.

Players begin at the bottom of the mountain with targets to achieve during the day. Each player plans his route, then sets off to ski as many runs as he can before the sun sets at the day's end.

Each turn players can collect piste cards to add to their hand, travel up ski lifts, or play piste cards to ski runs. Event cards can be played throughout the game to hinder your opponent or give you an advantage. Remember not to get stuck on the mountain when the sun sets!

Players score points for each run completed with additional points for some named runs. Each completed target scores additional points.  Whoever has the highest point tally at the end of the day wins.

The 21 Mutinies: Treasures of the Empire mini-expansion consists of five treasure counters that are shuffled with those from the base game to offer more variety during play. These counters give you:


    Gold, either 1 or 3 coins depending on which you draw
    One victory point
    A jewel accompanied by two bottles of rum
    A wooden leg, which is worth nothing but a fun prop at parties
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

boardgame: The Rivals for Catan: Gavin the Polyglot
description:
This 2013 promo card for the Rivals for Catan was given out by Klaus Teuber at SPIEL 2013 in Essen (and can also be bought in the Catan Shop). A limited English-language version was released during a Cyber Monday promotional at the Catan Shop on Dec. 2, 2013. Only 50 copies were listed as available to the public and a $50+ online purchase was required.

It is a unit/hero. 
It costs 1 lumber and has 1 skill point.
It has a red "Base Game" back.

German text of the card:
"1 x in Ihrem Zug: Sie dürfen 1 Rohstoff einer Landschaft, in der Sie 3 Rohstoffe besitzen, gegen 1 beliebigen anderen Rohstoff tauschen."

English text of the card:
"1x during your turn: You may exchange 1 resource from a region where you have 3 resources for any 1 different resource of your choice."

The Channel Islands Game is based on the real routes and itineries in the Channel Isles. In playing the game hazards and dangers are encountered similar to those experienced in real life. It is only by chance and skill that a player can win.

Object of the Game:

To complete a tour of all five islands marked on the board, using the travel tickets to St Peter Port having successfully visited and placed a counter on a cross on each of the five islands.

Each round in Befuddled, you roll 14 word dice and try to find words before a timer runs out. A special die rolled at the same time determines the minimum word length for that round; at the same time, special "shake-it-up" cards add restrictions, e.g., forbidding the use of an "s" to make words plural, removing two letter cubes from the current roll, or playing a round with your opposite hand. Each word that you alone create out of the rolled letters is worth one point if it's exactly as long as the minimum word length; longer words are worth more points.

The player who first passes a point threshold — or alternatively, the player with the highest score after a predetermined number of rounds — wins.

In this game the players are stock market share holders, trying to put their opponents out of the game by bankrupting them. The game is based on the Greek Stock market of the 90s and the press of the era.

It has many similarities to Monopoly, but in place of real estate now there are company shares. Houses and hotels are replaced by percentage of the company in stocks, owned by the player. Paying money based on stocks they own to other players as well as various taxes and public service bills may cost money to the players similar to monopoly.

An interesting change is the fact that two players can merge their shares in order to get a bigger percentage payment from other players.

The goal of course is to take out of business your opponents by causing them to go bankrupt.

Your father, the king, united four small fiefdoms into a mighty kingdom. Now that he is dead, the former royal families of those fiefdoms are plotting to take over the throne that is rightfully yours. You are outnumbered, but in your struggle to defeat them you have a powerful ally: your court jester, the Joker, who as your father's friend and advisor was always much more than he appeared to be. He is highly skilled at court intrigue, and can help set them all against one another so that you can retain your throne. He is your secret weapon - your Royal Assassin!

This solitaire card game is played with a standard 52 card deck with 1 (or optionally 2) Joker cards.  The goal is to eliminate all royalty cards before the stockpile runs out.

From the back of the box:

Floor It! is very easy to play. Players choose the number of laps to race and then move around the track by choosing one of three card types - Floor It!, Cruise, or Take It Easy.

The game is a trade-off between speed and safety. Each player must decide how they will drive in order to safely navigate the course. It is safest when driving in a straight section of the course. The greatest danger is when driving in a corner. A driver who decides to go fast or "floor it" is more likely to crash than a driver who chooses to "take it easy".

From the publisher:
Take turns growing a tree by adding leaves and branches onto a wobbly, not always cooperative trunk. Whoever causes any part of the tree to tumble must pick up the pieces. Trunk can be adjusted to increase difficulty, making this fun for the whole family. It is made up of natural wood. An outrageously fun eco-balancing game with lead free paint.

User note:
This might be an uncredited and/or unauthorized version of the SdJ  Kinderspiel winner, Arbos.

Jolly Roger is a simple dice game containing six dice, each of them has the "1" replaced with the image of a Jolly Roger.

The goal is to capture the most cargo and score the most points. On his turn, a player rolls all dice and for each Jolly Roger rolled, he can "capture" one other die and score its points. The player may re-roll any dice two times. Should he come up with 5 Jolly Roger symbols, he scores no points this round.

After a given number of turns, points are tallied and the player with the highest score is the winner.

A special map for playing Catan on the island of Corsica.

The game follows the usual Settlers of Catan rules with two major differences:

- Settlements and roads may only be built on marked spaces
- Victory point chits are put on some crossing points marked on the map; they are claimed by the first player who builds a road there

The booklet Quest: Zeit der Helden - Abenteuerband 6 contains two solitaire adventures for Quest, both of which won an adventure design contest held by publisher Pegasus Spiele:


    "Der Sammler der Lieder", a solo-scenario for the hero Talorien.
    "Karomins Berufung", a solo-scenario for the hero Karomin.


Both adventures feature the start of the adventuring career of the hero mentioned and should be played with one player and one Questmaster only.

Genius Loci is a two-player combinatorial game played on a hexagonal board. According to the Romans, a genius loci is the spirit of a place; in this game, the spirits allow movement between places.

Playing pieces consist of men, in two colours, belonging to the players; and gods - neutral pieces which can neither capture nor be captured, but enable the movement of the other pieces.

Each turn consists of the following steps:
(a) optional - move a god
(b) required - make a sacrifice; move a man of your colour according to the rules of a god, so as to capture another piece (a sacrifice)

The game ends when a player may not make a sacrifice on their turn.  They lose.

This game is inspired by the "Activator Piece" discussions in the BGG abstract games forums.

In this tactical game, each player takes on the role of a Spanish wine merchant and try to achieve the highest income with excellent wine.

But the competition and many a storm complicate the business. Since it is important to see how the market develops and when the growing areas receive the "mark of the season." In addition, any player can get through clever recommendations to the commission of wine tourists.

The player can trade with 3 different varieties: Tempranillo (purple), Garnacha (blue) and Mencia (light blue). The player who has achieved the highest earnings after 4 votes, winning this card game.

This game was created for a design contest as documented here:

http://boardgamegeek.com/article/13809424#13809424

The game is played on an uncheckered board of 8 rows and 12 files; the game does scale though to boards of smaller size. Each player starts the game (sitting across the board from one another facing a long side of the board) with 12 stones placed in the nearest row, 10 identical pieces starting one each on all spaces except the two corners each of which is occupied by an activator. A supply of neutral colored stones and of additional pieces in the player's own color (designated white and black) are also needed.

All stones move like rooks in chess, i.e., vertically or horizontally in an unobstructed line.

The object is to create a situation where one has at least twice as many pieces on the board as one's opponent or where the opponent has no legal move. Draws are not possible.

What's notable here is the use of two activator pieces based on an a notion created by designer Joe Joyce, as per the contest mentioned above. This allows captured pieces to be removed from play, converted to immobile neutral pieces or replaced by a piece of one's own color, depending on how they are captured.

boardgame: Archon: Glory & Machination – Kickstarter Stretch Rewards
description:
A promo package for all Kickstarter backers consisting of 15 new tiles for the game as well as four cards with rules.

The new tiles are:
2x Chapel
2x Tavern
2x House of Trade
2x Weaponsmith
2x Craftsman
2x Inn
1x Library
1x Armory
1x Gallery

A game of Chess for 3 players, Noris Schach (Noris Chess) is played on a hexagonal board divided into triangular cells, with a bight of 5 cells taken out of every other side, leaving a total of 106 triangles and three corner areas of the board where pieces will begin from.  Each player has the normal complement of Chess pieces, but for an extra Pawn and another piece called the Cardinal, which moves like a Queen but cannot capture and can only be captured under narrowly defined circumstances involving both other players.

The game is then played according to the usual rules of Chess, but for the complications of an extra player and additional directions of movement resulting from the triangular cells.

Learn to count in a fun way!

Choose a card with sums (for smaller children it is possible to choose a card with only numbers) and lay it in front of you.

Each player grabs a number-card from the Lucky Dip Tree on his turn. Is this number the result of one of your sums? Bingo! Be the first to complete your card and you'll win the game!

But be carefull for those tricky cards. When turning Bobo you'll be able to choose a number card of your opponents, but with some cards you have to give one of your cards to one of your opponents!

Mountain Mayhem is the ski-based cross sport of racing and bludgeoning set in a fantasy universe.

Players choose skilled skiers to tackle the particular mountain course (and the competition), grab some equipment and muster their courage to face the obstacles laid before them...including the other skiers. Choose your skier and your equipment wisely, avoid the pit traps and skier attacks, deal some damage yourself, and plan the smartest course. Do this, and you may just win the game.

Players score points for speed and points for pain. Do one. Do the other. DO BOTH! Players score 1 point for each point of damage dealt and 1 point for each space they are ahead of the last place skier at the end of the game when a skier crosses the finish line.

A small free expansion, handed out to fans at Spiel 2013.

This mini-expansion includes four new Town tiles, increasing the total number of Towns that may be formed in a game to 14.


    Tile 1: When taking this Town tile, get 2 Victory points and move 2 spaces forward on each of the 4 Cult tracks. This tile provides 2 Town Keys.



    Tiles 2 and 3 (double-sided): When taking this Town tile, get 4 Victory points. You may move the Marker on your Shipping track forward one space for free and get a number of Victory points as indicated on the Shipping space you move to. If the Fakirs take this tile, they instead can skip one more Terrain or River space when they use Carpet Flight.



    Tile 4: When taking this Town tile, get 11 Victory points. 


This mini-expansion was rereleased in Terra Mystica: Erweiterungsbogen, a promotional mini-expansion released at Spiel 2015.

Bobo and his friends are fond of rhyme. Bobo says 'book' and Tjerk immediately replied 'cook'! So they can go on for hours!

This upgrade kit for the Bobo Lucky Dip Tree teaches young children about playful rhymes and reading. Choose a word map and bulletin words from the Bobo Lucky Dip Tree. Look for words that rhyme with the words in your word map. Be the first player to fill the whole map to win!

In Perspective of Mirrors, players take on the roles of intelligence directors utilizing various assets to complete espionage operations and score Information Points. Players use the Espionage cards to alter the landscape and achieve goals. Assets are utilized in the field to seek clues and information vital to success. Operations are the missions handed to you from above that need to be completed.

Being in the right place at the right time can make the difference between success and failure. When states spy on states, not only are the proper assets required to be in the right place at the right time, but also with the right support. It takes a skillful mastermind to direct your nation’s assets in a manner that is beneficial to your missions but also confounding to others.

PairPlay is "a memory game with an educational twist. How well do you know your European countries and their capitals?" (quoted from the publisher blurb)

It is a variant of Memory, containing 108 cards (54 pairs) of European flags. When a pair is found, the player has to name the country and its capital to claim the cards.
Small 6 tile expansion.

Mango Republic is a tabletop game for two to four aspiring dictators. Players use their cards to expand their power and attempt to take complete control of the government of a nascent democracy.

There is a board to keep track of the activists, parliamentarians and generals in Mango Republic. Tokens placed by the player on the districts count as activists, tokens on parliament count as members of parliament, and tokens on Fort Peace count as generals.

The more activists in a district the player has, the more parliamentarians he gets. The more parliamentarians he has, the more cards he can hold. Cards give you options to gain activists, generals and hurt your opponents.

Whoever establishes complete control over the government, wins.

If Section Seal separates your Station in more than 2 parts, it still applies. You only count the locations in the smallest part though.

This card was a promo given out at Spiel 2013 (Essen).

description:
Earl Von Venture’s Guide!

In Seekers: Treasure Hunt, you are an archeologist, and your goal is to find the buried treasure.

Objective:

There are 4 clues in every game.  Land on all 4 eye stones to collect clues to find the exact location of the treasure and dig for the treasure.

Gameplay:

In order to play the game, each player starts by selecting two character cards and drawing 3 playing cards. Each character will be represented on the board game map by a colored gem, which will be used to search for the treasure. Each player's character starts at the designated start boxes. Now the game is ready to be played!

Choose a player to start the game.The game is played in turns, in a clockwise direction.The following are the actions each player takes during his/her turn:

1. Draw one card from the top of the playing cards pile.

2. Activate Character Abilities and Move each of your characters.

3. Use any number of cards (playing cards and treasure chest cards) in your hand.

4. Discard extra cards.

During the game, players can go to the various treasure chest tiles to collect treasure chest cards, each of which has a special ability.Some of these cards help you get to the eye stones faster and help you win faster.

The game ends when one player lands on the treasure location and digs for the treasure.

boardgame: Capt'n Sharky: Abenteuer auf der kleinen Schatzinsel
description:
Capt'n Sharky and his mates are looking for the treasure of Pirate Bill. But not every hiding place actually contains loot so the players help Capt'n Sharky to find out where the gems are.

Players move their pirate pawns along the row of island tiles by playing one of six movement cards. The card hand is repleneished only after all cards have been played. Whenever a pirate lands on an empty tile he gets to place the treasure chest on the tile and opens it to checks whether he's found something. When all gems have been found, the player with the most gems is the winner.

A variant requires players to catapult the gems into the ship's hold in order to score them.

The island tiles and the treasure chest use magnets to make the chest display different symbols when it is placed on a tile.

This game is basically a smaller carry-along version of Capt'n Sharky: Abenteuer auf der Schatzinsel with a few differences in implementation.

ReMatch is a tactical strategy game for two or three players. Players fight a David and Goliath like battle with different armies. Combinatory skills, and anticipating the opponents’ movement will make the difference in this game.

The game is played on a small hex board. One side fights with 4 pieces, the other side with 8 pieces (4 each of two colors). The player with two colors must alternate between them. The pieces move and capture others as in checkers. Capturing is mandatory. The larger army must capture themselves and the smaller army has a special move.

The game ends when all pieces of one side have been captured or a player cannot move.

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Shadow of Nerekhall
description:
Game description from the publisher:

An ancient evil rises in Nerekhall, and each night heralds a series of sinister events. In Shadow of Nerekhall, the newest expansion for Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition), players try to determine whether outside forces are corrupting the city — or whether the city itself is rotten to the core. Shadow of Nerekhall introduces a riveting campaign, divided into nineteen quests filled with deadly monsters and traps. This expansion introduces four new heroes, four new classes, new quests, monsters, and more, as the heroes travel through the city, attempting to save it from the demonic creatures entering Nerekhall from the Black Realm.

Also integrates with Road to Legend app (Android, Apple iOS, and Steam).

description:
In Euro-Express, players can create or expand nine speed rail connections with a total of 28 sections that are primarily links between the capitals of the European Community. Important regional sub-centers of some countries are also connected to the network. Existing train connections are shown on the schedule as rails.

The goal is to create a train through Europe, and only through team work can players hope to achieve this challenge. Each player takes the role of the Minister of Transport of the EC countries. In the EC, the ministers meet regularly in the Council of Ministers of Transport. The Common Community Commission proposes the construction of a particular route. The European Parliament may accept this proposal or reject it. This also applies to the Transport Ministers Council, which will decide after discussing the planning and financing of the track.

Any Minister of Transport can try to fund the project through national budgetary resources, which he can invest in the construction of the Euro-train. In addition, the Transport Ministers Council may fall back in the game on loans from the European Investment Bank (EIB) and funds from the EC transport budget.

Until all the capitals of the community are linked by the Euro-Express, the Council has to overcome many hurdles and crack many hard nuts.

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Paint the Town Red
description:
Spider-Man and his allies make their long-awaited return to the Legendary Universe! Legendary: Paint The Town Red is a Small Box Expansion to the Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game.

5 new Heroes!  2 new Villain groups!  2 new Masterminds!  4 new Schemes!

The game will feature the long awaited appearance of Black Suit Spiderman as well as the evil Mastermind, Carnage. Each Small Box Expansion Set comes with 100 playable cards featuring all original art.

description:
Quoted from the rulebook: Policemen have to catch a disguised thief. Whoever wins the largest number of medals becomes the best policeman. Policemen can jointly investigate in catching a thief and they can win and share more medals,

In each round one player is the thief and the other players are the policemen. The thief draws one thief card (from 32) and the policemen have to find out that card.

In turn, the policeman turn over 4 cards from the suspect deck, which consists of the same cards as in the thief deck. Then they ask a closed question (yes/no) about a feature of the thief, like baldness, wearing a beard, wearing glasses, having a red jacket etc., which the thief has to answer. After that, the thief is allowed to turn over one face-up card.

After six questions the remaining cards are turned face up. Now the policemen have to point to the suspect, they think suspect being the thief. If all policemen guess correctly, they get a number of medals that is printed on the thief card. If they point to different suspects, only the correct guessing policemen gain 1 medal. If all policemen fail with their guesses, they all lose one medal.
 The game ends when each player was the thief once. The player with the most medals wins. Ties are possible...

Adds 5 additional Action cards to be mixed with the ones from the base game.

- Second Wave: triggers an additional zombie attack in one area.
- Team Spirit: allows movement of two characters to one area.
- Noisy Delivery: adds a zombie to each area and gives every player an additional action card (drawn and distributed by the player of the card).
- Tripped: changes the movement of a character to the crossroad.
- XX:30: draws an additional Hourly Event card and resolves it for two areas of the player's choice.

In this simple abstract game, you will attempt to gain more points than your opponents by placing stones onto the board. Castle contains 3 different boards, each with slightly different rules and a different component requirement.

Came with the Level 99 Games Minigame Library.

This expansion was first published in Ystari magazine, issue 2 (October 2012, p. 23), along with solo rules (p. 11).

4 colony tiles as alternative when advancing to a new colony level

I
- worker action: 1 food --1x--> 2 victory points
- mushrooms are food when placing pheromone tile

I
- worker action: place / replace one tunnel (without getting earth)
- 1 virtual warrior per turn to step on enemy pheromones only

II
- worker action: no discarding of excess cubes this turn
- 1 virtual warrior per turn to hunt prey only

III
- worker action: get 2 larvae
- possibility to place 2 nurses on the objective atelier space
This promo tile for RRR allows a player to add a neutral tile from the neutral area to their personal area.
This promo tile for RRR allows a player to draw a neutral tile at random from the neutral tiles not chosen for this game to their area.
This promo tile for RRR allows a player to discard this tile on their turn if it is currently their ally.

Yspahan: The Souks consists of a deck of 18 larger cards that replaces those in the Yspahan base game with mostly new abilities. The expansion cards bear a purple frame to allow for easy recognition compared to the original ones.

Yspahan: The Souks is included in the Ystari Box of expansions for six Ystari games.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Equinox Expansion Pack
description:
The Equinox was constructed at the Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards and launched in early 2370. By 2371, Captain Rudolph Ransom assumed command of the vessel. The Equinox was a limited role starship, designed for short-term planetary research and boasted a top speed of only warp 8; however, it was one of the first vessels to be equipped with an Emergency Medical Hologram.

In early 2371, the Equinox was pulled into the Delta Quadrant by the entity known as the Caretaker. During the vessel's first week in the region, 39 crew members – roughly half the crew – were killed in a conflict with the Krowtonan Guard. The Krowtonan Guard claimed that the Equinox had violated their borders, but Captain Ransom gave the order to continue through their space. The decision would haunt Ransom for the rest of his life.

In 2151, the Somraw attacked and raided a Xarantine outpost. Among the goods the Somraw crew stole from the outpost was some Xarantine ale. description:
Issue game of Wargame Japanese History #19.

Strategic level wargame for the civil war in ancient Japan.
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jinshin_War

"Shingeki no Ohama" means Advance of Prince Ohama. Prince Ohama was the winner of the Jinshin War, and then he became Emperor Tenmu.

Players control a faction of either Prince Ohama or Prince Ohtomo. On each turn, players secretly select three of five action chits. Each chit allows movement, mobilization, or an event.

description:
The USS Sutherland (NCC-72015) was a Federation Nebula-class explorer starship in service to Starfleet in the late 24th century.
In 2368, the Sutherland was temporarily commanded by Lieutenant Commander Data while it was part of a blockade along the Romulan-Klingon border.

During the Dominion War it was assigned to the Ninth Fleet at Bajor and commanded by Captain Shelby. Manuele Atoa also served aboard the Sutherland.

This ship is the competitive prize for Month 4 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, The Dominion War.

Las Vegas Boulevard, labeled the first expansion for Las Vegas, includes several modules that players can use individually or in combination, specifically:


    Eight dice in two new colors (yellow & grey) to allow for up to seven players.
    Purple dice (10 in total) that allow you to remove an opponent's or one's own die when placed (with enough purple dice to have up to eight players).
    A large die in each color, except purple (7 in total), that counts as two dice when determining who scores at a casino.
    Two white dice to play the basic variant with up to 7 players.
    Rules for playing a solitaire version of the game.
    Rules for playing a variant of the game.
    Five bills worth $100,000.
    Ten one-shot cards (Action cards / blue), with each player receiving one such card at the start of each round.
    Ten special cards (Bonus cards / green).
    Six special cards (Rainbow cards) of variable random value.
    A new, seventh casino (Slot Machine); on a turn, a player can place all the dice he rolled of a single number in this casino, but then no more dice of this number can be placed in this casino by any player.
    One summary card.


(Note that Las Vegas Boulevard was initially announced as Las Vegas Lights and later renamed Las Vegas Plus.)

description:
Bolster your armies with Savage Guardians, the first expansion for Hordes: High Command!

Savage Guardians contains 5 copies each of 16 different cards, plus 4 additional warlock cards, to bring new strategies to your Trollblood, Circle Orboros, Skorne, and Legion of Everblight decks.

Requires Hordes: High Command core set to play.

This simple roll-and-move game takes a sarcastic look at the career of a firefighter. Players move from the Academy to Retirement. Cards played along the way represent Fire, Rescue, Station and Administration.

The goal is to reach Retirement first.

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Die Wunschbrunnen
description:
Die Legenden von Andor: Die Wunschbrunnen (the wishing wells) – first released at Spiel 2013 in Essen, Germany – is a single card expansion for Legends of Andor that can be used with Legend "Ära des Sternenschilds"

Rules:
When a hero being on a field with a well instead of emptying it, disposes it on one of the open placement areas on the back of this card, the according wish is instantly fulfilled once. The well cannot be used for the rest of the game anymore.
Wishes:
1. When a hero places a well here, the wolves only have double the strength value of the hero. This also applies for the alpha wolf.
2. When a hero places a well here, Varkur only has 6 strength instead of 10.
3. When a hero places a well here, all gifts of the Andori are instantly refreshed once.
4. When a hero places a well here, he may remove one creature or a boulders token from a golden shield at the castle.

A print and play card game with dinosaurs.

Draw cards and put your dinosaurs in play: herbivorous, omnivorous, or carnivorous. Make them change with mutation.

The game ends when the volcano and the meteor cards are in play, it is the begining of the mass extinction, the K. T.

Score points for the bigger dinosaurs in play, but there's no place for every one, you count only: 3 herbivores, 2 omnivores and 1 carnivore.

The cards are language independent, rules are in French and English.

When Godd created the Wurld, she saw that it was beautiful, full of green mountains, white shores, and blue seas. Godd wanted to display the Wurld to all Creation, and so she hung the Wurld in the Heavens, suspended by nine gold chains gripped by nine dragons. A great Torch was placed in the mouth of a tenth dragon, who circled the Wurl to give it warmth and light.

But now, something is wrong with the tenth dragon. The great torch appears to be receding from the Wurld. It is growing colder and darker. Snow is falling in the harvest month. The crops are withering and dying. Furred beasts are descending from the north, wild and ravenous.

You are the Dragon Priestess of your village, a healer of great skill. The people under your care look to you for hope. Knowing that time is precious, you gather supplies and prepare for a journey north, to the Shrine of the Tenth Dragon. Only there do you have any hope of conversing with Godd and the Tenth Dragon. Only there can you learn what must be done to return warmth to the Wurld and save your village.

The Tenth Dragon is a solitaire card-based push-your-luck game in which you, the player, must overcome harsh winds, blinding snow, and hungry beasts in order to reach the Shrine of the Tenth Dragon.  Each card has multiple purposes, with an event, a twist, and an item or boon to help you through your quest.  Progress through the deck three times, each time with slightly different rules.  If you survive, you win!

Pocket Golf simulates an 18 hole round of golf using custom made dice, Caddy tips and an exclusively designed course.

Play solo or in a group. Stroke play, tournament or skins formats. The winner will be the player with the lowest score for the round. Try to beat Par for the course in solo play and then try to beat that score next time your play.

The game contains 8 custom engraved dice for your clubs, rules and a uniquely designed 'Green Moose Valley' golf course that shows the course layout with hazards, a quick reference distance chart and Caddy tips. All designed to fit in your pocket!

Ideal for quick game at the 19th hole!

Pocket Football is a fast paced dice simulation of the beautiful game, football.

8 custom made dice represent your players on the field and rolling one die at a time results in an action. Work the ball up to your Forward and shoot on goal to try and find the back of the net.

Everything from headers, volleys, corner kicks and penalties have been distilled into 8 dice that come in their own carry bag with rules, to fit nicely in your pocket!

Challenge your friends and play as your favourite teams - make sure you bring your best commentary voice! Winning is decided by the player that scores the most goals.

Set up your own tournaments and try to take your team to the finals!


        2015 New Edition is a re-release of the game with some changes to dice and gameplay. Still 8 dice, but two new changes (TACKLE die & GOALIE die) include the opponent a little more in the action. 
    


Penalties have been improved and more scoring chances!
