boardgame: Mayfair Games Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #5
description:
The Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #5 is composed of 3 expansions:

Five Points: Gangs of New York – Flophouse
Includes 1 extra building tile. If you control this building's space at the end of the round, then you can remove up to 3 of your rabble from any neighborhood tile and return them to your headquarters.

Steam: City Growth Special Contracts
Includes 5 city growth markers on 5 colors. They can be used instead of the normal gray city growth markers. You place 1 of these markers on to the city when you are using the City Growth action. Now this city has a one-time demand for that color cube. When 1 cube of the marker's color has been shipped to the city, then the marker is turned to the other side, which is exactly the same as a normal city growth marker.

Alchemist: All for One, Two for All
Includes a new variant cauldron and a 3/7 potion value tile which can only be played on the new cauldron.

boardgame: Defenders of the Realm: Battlefields – Elf Lord
description:
The Elf Lord is a Kickstarter bonus tile, which replaces the Druid in the Hero Deck and and adds some different strategic choices for the Hero player.

Setup: Players shuffle and play their deck as normal. If the Hero player wants to include their KS Elf Lord tiles, they take these 4 tiles and shuffle them, placing them face down next to their draw deck.

Game Play: When the Hero player is playing one of their Wild cards, they may discard the Wild card rather than using the ability and draw one of the face down Elf Lord Wild tiles playing it instead. This is an optional action enabling the player to play the original Wild card or choose to play the Elf Lord. They make this decision prior to seeing the Elf Lord card.

Optional Rule: If the Dark Lord player is more experienced and wished to handicap themselves, they may allow the Hero player to have the KS ELf Lord Wild tiles face up and choose which one to play each time rather than the cards being random.

The Aloof ELf Lord: After the Battlefield that the Elf Lord Tile was played on, the Tile is returned to the box and not shuffled back into the other tiles. Therefore, the Elf Lord will only only appear on the Battlefields 4 times, being discarded to the box when the respective battlefields are resolved.

After the Great nuclear war... People are gone, Ants are Smart and the Backyard is at war!

Ant Attack is an exciting new 2 player card game of strategy requiring the correct amount of boldness, cunning and deception.  As overall commander, you deploy your units and leaders onto the battlefield into well-tuned squads in attempt to destroy your enemy.  Deploy snipers at the correct moment to wreak havoc on the less defended support units.  Send your scouts out to discover the enemy’s true makeup.  Know when to rally the troops and when to call for a truce.

Each game comes complete with 2 identical decks to begin your 6-legged struggle for control of the back yard.

This expansion for Giftzwerge contains 4 new symbols (toadstool, golden sun, silver moon, grey glove) on 30 transparent stickers.
They are put on empty lawn tiles of each player and the compost heap.

Toadstool: Remove one purple toadstool chip in the compost heap from the game. If there are none left, you are eliminated from the game.

Golden sun: Protects you from all actions of other players, as long as your lawnmower rests on the golden sun.

Silver moon: Secretly look under as many lawn tiles of your garden or the compost heap as your dice throw shows. You may tell one or more neighbours.

Grey glove: Swap the positions of any two tiles in one garden (including start and poison tiles).

Contents:
30 transparent stickers
7 purple toadstool chips
1 streamlined rules sheet (including new rules)

boardgame: It's a Dice Game, After All!
description:
It's a Dice Game, After All! is a free (not for profit) print-and-play game inspired by the Days of Wonder game Small World. In this game, you play as one of twelve fantasy-themed tribes attempting to colonize the world by any means necessary.

The object of the game is to be the player with the most victory points after 10 turns. Points are earned by maintaining the size of your tribe, and by rolling dice to extend your civilization into new regions. Special powers and abilities may also offer the potential for bonus points.

This game has no official association with Small World, its publisher Days of Wonder, its designer Philippe Keyaerts, or its artist Miguel Coimbra. This is strictly a product of fandom for the wonderful game that these people have produced.

Digimon Fusion is a CCG based on the newest Digimon animated series, which launched on Nickelodeon in September, 2013.

The Digimon Fusion CCG will be available in two SKUs: Theme Decks (Red and Blue), and Booster Packs. The Theme Decks contain 40 cards (a 30 card deck and 10 card booster), and are packaged eight to a display. The Boosters contain 10 cards (of a 60 card set), and are packaged 15 to a display.

The cards follow a similar rarity system as the Power Rangers Action Card Game: C (Common), R (Rare), SR (Super Rare), and U (Ultra Rare). Each Booster Pack comes with 10 cards: 6 Commons, 3 Rares, and 1 Super/Ultra Rare.

Be The Dungeon Hero or The Dungeon Boss! Devious Deceptions Galore!

THE DUNGEON HERO


     You're a dangerous fool armed with a random Deathball!
     Bash down doors with hate!
     Imbibe mysterious potions!
     Sleep in an inn--FTW!
     Control giant Green Slimes with fire!
     Destroy three Beholders and win the game!


THE DUNGEON BOSS


     Shoot down your enemy's fireballs with the latest in Beholder Eye Beam technology!
     Watch your pet Phoenix *EXPLODE* on its own!
     Deploy your minions behind Red Doors of Doom!
     Throw the Dungeon Zero's fireballs back at him!
     Mentally push around your enemy through Secret Doors of your choice!
     Your enemy only has 5HP? ...Cake!


Contents:

1 - Cuttable card of '1HP' tokens
2 - Rules cards
1 - Cuttable card of three 'Fireballs', two 'Lovely Fireballs', and one 'Deathball
3 - Secret Door cards
1 - Monster Manual card
10 - Red Door cards

    5 - Pink Life crystals



    Now included at no additional cost! Track your Dungeon Hero's life with 1HP tokens or easy to pick up Pink Life crystals!

A dexterity game in which a domed center containing 4 colors of marbles is then automated with 2 D cell batteries to begin to "pop" and move. On each of 4 sides of the dome is a row of slots for each player and colored to match each of the balls or popcorn; blue, green, white and red. In front of each slot is a button and if pressed will push out the popcorn that falls into the slot if it is not a matching color. Each slot can hold up to two balls and the goal is to get as many of your matching color as possible.

2 "D" cell batteries are required for popcorn effect to work.

2 to 4 players

36 Fetter cards in 3 suits

1 Pawn

1 Movement track

Objective

Players are Norse gods trying to stop Fenrir from reaching and devouring Odin.

Setup

Place the pawn on the first wolf spot of the track board.

3 or 4 player game

The Deal

The dealer thoroughly shuffles the deck and deals out all 36 cards one at a time to each player.

The player to the left of the dealer goes first by asking one of the other players for a card by rank and suit. The player asking for a card must already hold at least one card of the same suit.

If the player that was asked has the requested card he plays the card to the table face up starting a discard pile (the cards should be fanned so that the rank and suit are showing). Then move the pawn along the track backwards the number of spaces indicated on that discarded card. Now the asking player continues his turn and may ask the same player or another player for a card.

If the player that was asked does not have the requested card then the asking player must discard face up onto the discard pile a card of the same suit asked and moves the pawn along the track forwards the number of spaces indicated on that discarded card and now the turn to ask passes to the next player to the left.

The gods win if they discard all their fetter cards before Fenrir reaches Odin, otherwise they lose.

A 2 player game

Each player is dealt a hand of 10 cards and the remaining 16 cards are stacked face down on the table. A player who does not have the requested card draws one card from the stock before asking the opponent for another card. The remaining rules are the same as in a 3 or 4 player game.
Twelve extra mission cards for The Agents. This expansion was a stretch goal for Kickstarter supporters.

Pathfinder Dice Arena allows players to battle as their favorite Pathfinder heroes in tournaments for gold and glory. Players build up moves to create high scoring combos while using counter moves to interfere with their opponents’ actions.

Points are scored based on patterns of icons and colors formed within their grid. Players can also trigger more abilities from their character sheets and talent cards or by purchasing gear and Fate cards with gold. At the end of two rounds, the player with the most points wins the tournament.

Sakura is a 2-4 player game taking place during the Feudal Japan Era. Sakura is a strategy and a resource collection game.

The game begins by players assembling the game map with individual map tiles. Players then build a city and rice paddies, and collect resources to build an army.

Special Cards can be used to bolster your attack or weaken your enemy.

The game is over when any one player has conquered an enemy city.

Discover a new way to play a trivia game!

You don't need to have great knowledge to score points; just be smart.
Each player has question cards in his hand.

Your turn:
- Choose one of your questions.
- Choose who will respond and how you ask your question.
- Predict what the result will be (success or failure, for example).

If the chosen players or respond as you expected: you earn points.

400 questions, 6 ways to score points: no need to be a sage to win!

In this first chapter, Fin Finaud takes you to the world of cooking and food in Quebec and elsewhere.

Philip is the best acrobat from far and wide. He is very ﬂexible in order to be able to perform the most unbelievable movements. Little monkey Bo, his friend, is simply amazed at his feats. Are you as ﬂexible as Philip?

Each turn two mobility cards are turned face up. Each mobility card shows Philip with a marked body part. They show which acrobatics you should perform. Example: When Philip's shoulder and knee are marked on the two face up cards, all players are supposed to touch their shoulder with their knee (the players can freely pick between left or right). If none of the players can correctly execute the acrobatics, monkey Bo will move closer to the banana. The players win the game ends when all mobility cards are used before Bo reaches the banana.

Middle East '48 (originally known by Sinai '48) is a brigade level simulation of the Israeli War of Independence in May through June of 1948.

Map is 11x17 glossy paper of the Sinai through Lebanon, 64 uncut, one-sided counters on card stock.

One player commands the Jewish (Israeli) forces, while the other commands the various Arab forces. Game play is I-go, You-go with 15 game turns.

Victory is based on the number of major cities held by the Arabs at the end of the game.

Published in Wargaming: The Magazine of Fantasy & Historical Simulation, Issue 04.

description:
Word jungle - there are 25 two-sided boards with letters.

Each player chooses one board and starts looking for the words which they can mark with the erasable marker until the sand timer stops the round. The player who has marked the most words after several rounds is the winner.

A traditional Japanese trick-taking card game based on the European game Ombre (originally "l'Hombre"), played with a special 75-card five-suited deck. This deck developed from Tenshō karuta, itself a close copy of the 48-card traditional Portuguese cards called "dragon cards" due to the dragons holding the suit symbols in the aces. Its creation was prompted by a ban on the older form of playing cards after Japan closed off the contact with the Western world in 1633, and became critical towards everything of European origin. Sunkun karuta took this game one step further.

The deck contains all 48 cards from Tenshō karuta; these are supplemented by introducing a fifth suit and increasing the number of cards in each suit from twelve to fifteen. Most additions refer to Japanese and Chinese culture, presumably a conscious choice intended to downplay the European origins of the game.

The suits are はう (hau), "flower" based on Portuguese 'paus' meaning "clubs", いす (isu) a shortened form of 'espadas' or "swords", こっぷ (koppu) from 'copas' or  "cups", おうる (ōru) from 'ouros' or "coins" (literally "golds") and finally the added ぐる (guru) with the traditional Japanese 三つ巴 (mitsudomoe) sign as suit symbol. As in the European predecessor games, higher-numbered pip cards beat lower ones only in the former two suits, while the latter ones are "reversed".

The "dragon aces" are split into two distinct ranks by reintroducing a normal "one" in each suit and separate from the ろばい (robai) or "dragon" which normally ranks right above the ones. Above the number cards (1–9 plus robai) there are five court cards. From low to high, these are: そうた (sōta) "queen", うま (uma) "horse", きり (kiri) "soldier" (originally a king), うん (un) various Japanese mythological figures and すん (sun) various Chinese officials. The latter two ranks give the game its name. In the trump suit, the robai is promoted to above the kiri and the sōta above this again, except that the robai of hau is a permanent trump which is the fourth highest regardless of which suit is trump.

The little ghosts want the midnight party in the old castle, but the common castle cats do not let them inside. Only as a pair of ghosts can float through one of the tower windows. Luckily the fireflies cheer on their friends: Whenever it has managed a pair of ghosts in the castle, a firefly lights are seen! Whoever finds the most ghosts pairs and be the first to get all your own fireflies glow is the winner.
A spooky memory game for 2 - 4 children 3-8 years.

Isabella, the little witch, is brewing a magic potion. She is throwing all kinds of ingredients in her cauldron. Sometimes she forgets whether an ingredient is already in her cauldron or not. Help Isabella to remember and score wizard hats in return.

Each turn ingredients are placed in Isabella's cauldron. You can score a wizard hat if you can remember which ingredients are in her cauldron. The player with the most wizard hats at the end of the game, wins the game!

From the publisher's website:

Alarm in the hen house! Everybody tries to snatch the most egg cards and not let them drop. That is not so easy: whoever receives an egg card has to clasp it under his chin, between his knees or in other very unlikely positions. When it comes to the big egg dance, everybody has to run up to the matching hen ? but keep in mind that scoring is done as soon as one player loses one of his egg cards.

The Sorcerer's Apprentices want to go to the castle of the famous wizard, but no one knows the way through the sinister magic forest. That's why they need to find the enchanted mythical creatures that live hidden in the woods. Only they can help the sorcerer's apprentices to reach to the wizard. The aim of the game is to assemble mythical creatures from two cards and to get to the castle of the magician first.

The Missions Expansion is a deck of 51 mission cards plus 5 blank mission cards), meaning players no longer all have the same objective of just collecting four matching relics. Instead, players draw cards that provide objectives which are more challenging to obtain, and are kept secret.

Contains 56 Mission Cards, 16 new stack tiles (marked with an orange flower), 9 triangular orange curse tokens, and 13 new relic tiles (comprising a new Mask relic for each color and a full set of black relics).

boardgame: Relic Expedition: Villages and Markets Expansion
description:
This expansion contains two new types of jungle tiles: villages and markets. Villages contain relics for which players must trade supplies; they make some relics more costly to obtain, and they give players a reason to carry duplicate supplies. Markets contain supplies for which players must trade relics; they give players another way to obtain the supplies they need, and they give players the opportunity to remove from the game a relic that their opponents need.

Contains 4 Market Tiles and 4 Village Tiles, one additional standard supply token of each type (marked with red text), a second additional First Aid supply token and a second additional Machete supply token (both also marked with red text), and 9 green triangular curse tokens.

A mini-expansion that provides three new small features that require the same equipment as their larger counterparts to traverse and 2 new types of relic table tiles based on terrains featured in the core game (quicksand and poison ivy).

This mini-expansion contains 8 map tiles that are marked with a violet flower, and also contains 4 copies of a new supply item: The Whip.

An eight-sided animal die that replaces the six-sided one found in the core game, and includes a new symbol: the question mark. This allows a roller to select any animal type, and move all counters of that type.

Dragons like Knights, and Knights like Dragons... With ketchup!

2 players face off, one as the Dragon, and one as the Knight.
Winner of 2 out of 3 battles between 3 (or 4) unique match-ups of a Dragon and a Knight.  Is it the Plate armored Knight vs the Hatchling Dragon, or the peasant wearing Quilted armor vs the Ancient Dragon?  You won't know until you flip your Armor Cards!

Each player has 4 levels of protection (Armor) as well as Effort cards and Weapon Cards.  
The Knight can attack with a [Mighty] and [Good] Effort with his [Razor Sharp] [Halberd] for 11 points, or if his hand is full of weak cards, he'll be forced to make a [Pitiful] Effort to attack with his [Crossbow] for 2 points.
What to play, what to hang on to?  Deceptively simple, with a deeper strategy.

Whoever defeats their opponent by reducing their Health to zero in combat will have dinner when they're done.

Snow Ball Fight!! pits 2 teams of kids (5 kids per team) in a snowball fight to the last man standing!  Each team member has a number, and a color, so your little one can practice matching and identifying their colors and numbers.

Draw cards from the draw pile and see if you hit someone, miss, or miss by elimination (each kid can only be hit once), and be the first to decimate your opponent!

You and your friends have sneaked onto the Johnson's property, and now it's time to TP their trees!

Each player has a picture mat with a Tree on it, and a complete deck of TP/Hazard cards.  Your goal is to TP as much of the tree as possible before the cops show up, while dealing with Hazards which will remove your TP.

A Deck contains 2 complete games; 2 Play mats and 2 complete decks of cards with different colored backs to help identify which deck they belong to.

The game can be played solitaire (using 1 deck and 1 Mat), or versus; each player using a mat, combining the decks into 1 big deck, and playing the Hazards on each other!

Three different color sets are available, allowing up to 6 miscreants to be TP'ing the Johnson's trees at once!

boardgame: Dungeon Petz: Bob the Blob Promo Pet
description:
A promotional pet initially given with copies of the expansion Dark Alleys upon its launch at Essen Spiel 2013. There is an extra set of fasteners for this pet in the Dark Alleys expansion.

Bob the Blob: Originally, this pet had no name. And no shape. And no personality. No, wait, that’s not true. Actually, Bob’s personality is very well defined: He is eager to please. That’s why he lets us call him Bob, and that’s why he will be whatever you want him to be.

Monopoly with VfL Wolfsburg, a soccer team from Germany.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Schwabing, a district of Munich, a city in Germany.

When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

Monopoly set to Hanau, a city in Germany.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Witten, a city in Germany.

When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

Verrück doch mal
German text on backside of the box: In diesem spannenden Spiel wird versucht, durch Bildung möglichst vieler und langer Wörter die meisten Punkte zu erzielen. Durch das Verrücken von Buchstabenchips muss ein Wort gebildet werden, wobei jeder verwendete Buchstabenchip Punkte bringt. Auch Sonderpunkte sind möglich. Die Spieler sind abwechselnd an der Reihe, wodurch sich immer wieder neue Möglichkeiten ergeben. Optimales Wort gefunden? Oder sind etwa noch mehr Punkte möglich? Flexibles Denken ist hierbei gefragt. Doch Eile ist geboten, denn die Zeit läuft ab.

English: Try to get the most points by creating the most and the longest words. Build it by shifting letter chips, every chip counts. Players alternate, so every time new chances occur. Found the optimal word? Or are more points possible? But do not dawdle, time runs short.

boardgame: Journey: Wrath of Demons – White Bone Demons Expansion
description:
As our Pilgrims cross a desolate and craggy mountain pass, they unknowingly stray into the land of the White Bone Demons. At first, the band meets a few skeletal warriors, possessed bones of a long dead army, risen to serve their Queen. After battling against the more corrupted White Bone Demon forms, the Pilgrims find that their Cleanse Ritual is not so effective against these Demon-possessed skeletons. Finally, the narrow path takes them to the Forbidden White Cave, where the Pilgrims confront Yara, Queen of the White Bone Demon Clan, who realises that this is no ordinary group of mortals. If Yara can eat the flesh of Tripitaka, she can gain immortality, regenerate her flesh and regain her beauty. As she launches her minions into a pitched battle, the fast moving White Bone Demons scuttle across the battlefield and Yara herself joins the fray. Will Yara’s poisoned attack will be the end of our four travelers? There is more at stake than just the Pilgrims lives. If they can defeat Yara and the White Bone Demon Clan, Tripitaka will recover the second piece of the lost Sutras and will be one step closer to restoring the seal on the Underworld.

As normal for expansions, this is not a standalone product. There will be a rules supplement, but it will be based on the Journey rulebook. You will play with some Map Tiles and Bridge Tiles from Journey and the base rules are the same. But the expansion is more than different shaped playing pieces. The White Bone Demons play very differently to the Bull Demons, being faster and harder to cleanse. Faster means it is easier for the Pilgrims to get overrun. With the Cleanse Ritual being less effective, Pilgrims will have to risk more kills and Bad Karma. You will need new strategies to defeat the White Bone Demons.

Finally, there is a new game mechanic. The White Bone Demons are the reanimated bones of long dead warriors. Demon Spirits possess the bones and over time, warp them in to horrific shapes. This separation between the Demon’s spirit and the warrior’s body may explain why the Cleanse Ritual is harder, but it is certainly the explanation of the biggest surprise the Pilgrims will face. Dead White Bone Demons are left on the board and if Yara can attract another Demon Spirit, these piles of bones may spring back to life!

boardgame: Journey: Wrath of Demons – Spider Demons Expansion
description:
The Spider Demon Expansion gives the Pilgrims a chance to retrieve the third piece of the sundered Sutras. But before Tripitaka can assemble more of the sacred scroll, his band will have to defeat the Spider Demons. The book describes them as “seductive spider spirits, half beautiful women, half spider”.

In the Silk Cave, high in the Silk Mountains, Zige the Spider Demon Queen spins her webs of the toughest silk. Her minions weave traps of sticky cobwebs and wait for unsuspecting prey to become ensnared. Will they feast on our brave travellers?

Once again, we will introduce new game play elements with the Spider Demons. These Demons fight at longer range, spitting poison and throwing spider web nets. Pilgrims will be more likely to suffer on-going conditions, like Poisoned and a new condition Entangled. Webs act as traps that hold the Pilgrims and attract the Demons when the Pilgrims are entangled. Each Demon moves towards the closest entangled Pilgrim, and may get to attack while the Pilgrim is unable to defend: an entangled target cannot use the Defense Dice.

Two players are prospective sheep farmers aiming to herd the most Sheep from a Flock.

Every turn, players will choose one card from their hand and play it face-down in a Flock, while saying "bah" for each Sheep on the card. Players can choose to tell the truth or lie about how many Sheep there actually are. Once there are seven or more "bah"s said between all players, a player can summon the Dog and say "bark, bark, bark!" If there's no Wolf, the player can pick up the Flock and check for the Wolf. If there's no Wolf, the player can harvest some Sheep, getting them closer to victory, or if there's a Wolf, it has eaten some Sheep! Players can also accuse one another for playing the Wolf, with game-altering consequences. Accumulate 9 sheep during the game and you win!

Being a pirate is dangerous, filled with adventure and wonders to be seen. However all of that is not why we do what we do. We do it for the loot, the gold gems that sparkle and drive us insane. All the bloodthirsty pillaging, all the cannon fire, it's all to get the loot. Once the voyage is over, it's what we wait for, the Divvying of the loot!

In Divvy, you take the role of pirates on a voyage. Each round is a voyage to obtain loot for the crew. On your turn, you pick a mission for the crew to go on, and attempt to complete the mission. Completed missions give you infamy among the crew and places money through the hold of the ship. At the end of the voyage pirates take their share of the loot, and voyage once more.

The first pirate to have enough gold to retire from the violent lifestyle wins!

Described by the Publisher/Designer:

There are One Bra-zillion™ ways to score with KickShot and Rio-lize™ your soccer goals. KickShot® is a board game for 5 year old kids through adults that closely mimics an actual soccer game. Play KickShot with dice, action and referee cards to strategically move the ball across a board, intercept, and score goals. The game supports three levels of play depending on age and desired challenge.

Learn proper ways to execute soccer action with endearing characters
- Move the ball down the pitch with action cards
- Earn referee cards for penalty, corner kicks, offside and direct free kicks
- Advance through three levels of play: kids "grow" from Junior, Warm Up and League Games

KickShot Board Game and all the associated products make ideal birthday and holiday gifts, and fundraisers for soccer clubs and non-profits. The products will surely get the entire family into the spirit of World Cup 2014.

The players are miners, whose objective is to transport bags of silver from their mine to the harbor of Vera Cruz (end space).

It's a simple roll and move game. When the meeples land on a marked space, the players have to follow the instructions in the rules. While passing through the "Indian Gorge" the meeples are in danger from an Indian, who is moved, whenever an odd number is rolled. The player who is the first to reach the end space is the winner of the game.

The year is 2013. It's been 150 years since RoBooth was defeated at the great Battle of Gettysburger, and things are finally back to normal. Pixel Lincoln is running for president once again, but has a new thorn in his side…. multiple versions of his 16-bit self. It's weird, confusing and retro! Which Lincoln will become president? Which Lincoln is the real deal? Who has the better Beardarang?

Oh, and Lincoln's hat is missing again and the world is doomed again with all of that time travel stuff….

Pixel Lincoln: Re-Election is a 4-part expansion to Pixel Lincoln: The Deckbuilding Game, consisting of new abilities, card types and game modes, but with a whole bunch of what we already know and love: Items, Enemies, Bosses, Mini-Bosses, Characters, Secret Items, and more!

This expansion was designed for the people.

Chikken is a game of dice, risk, strategy, and luck.  It's designed for two to six players of any age, as long as they can understand how to make matches with dice.

The goal is to get as high a score as possible without losing your turn. To do this, you roll five special dice (four white, one red), each with one side blank. The red die is called the "Danger Die."  If at any time you roll all white blanks, or you roll a red blank, your turn ends without scoring.

You score points by making three/four/five-of-a-kind matches or a straight (1-2-3-4-5). After each roll, you can stop and bank your points (gaining bonus points for matches, plus the total of all spots on the dice), or you can set aside dice of your choice and reroll what's left. However, you MUST set aside any blank white dice after each roll.

An added element of risk management comes when you have a bonus score available, such as from four-of-a-kind. When you do, you have the extra choice of hanging onto those bonus points and rerolling all five dice, with the bonus score still in jeopardy if you should roll blanks and lose your turn. This lets you "start over" with a higher starting value, but all the same risks of losing your turn.

Educational Value

Chikken teaches kids to understand and analyze risks. For younger kids, it's a matching game, and for slightly older ones, they can count up spots, or add up the numbers as they go. And it's still fun for everyone!

Onmitsu is a Print-and-Play "small card" game. Besides the rules, the whole game is contained in one 54-card deck of cards.

In Onmitsu, you are a spy – an Onmitsu – a sort of spy slash messenger slash ninja slash espionage secret kind of sneak. Your job is to deliver a scroll with a message to your Lord’s boss, the Daimyo. You have a sealed scroll – but so does everyone else. Nobody knows how has which one, either.

Only one message is the “real” message. Others are decoys (which need to be delivered, but aren’t worth as much) and at least “Poison” scroll – a scroll with a hidden message to kill the bearer. Someone doesn’t trust you very much…

Your job is to not only deliver the scroll, but figure out which one you and the other players have. You can always crack yours open to find out, but if the Daimyo shows up before you repair it (or pass it off on some other spy) you are in trouble.

Game play is simple – you draw and discard to form a hand of cards, each of which can be Locations or People. All of them give you the ability to perform actions like steal cards, repair broken scrolls, gain intelligence, or other forms of intrigue.

You win by having the most points, earned by delivering the most important scroll, accumulating cards, and not being the messenger who gets killed.

The King of Kingdom Land has perished and left his subjects with no heir to rule their fair land. Enter the Barons who all vie for the crown despite their colorful quirks. Using their followers and keen sense of tactics, players will use their baron to jockey for position and finally take rule of the land.

In Kingdom Land players will take on the role of Baron's vying for the vacant throne. Players will roll custom dice in an effort to build up specific types of followers all while trying to gain majority over the regions that make up Kingdom Land. Players will also have the opportunity to learn specific tactics and build monuments which with further increase their chance of dominance. After six rounds Kingdom Land will be evaluated and the Baron with the most points will fill the empty throne of Kingdom Land and rule forever!

boardgame: Rugrats Water Fun Park Adventure Game
description:
Race your favorite Rugrats character along the colored path and up the Super Slide steps.  The first character to reach the top and slide down into the middle space is the winner!

On your turn, spin the color wheel and move your character forward to the first space that matches the color on the spinner.  If you land on a ticket space, spin the wheel again except this time you will move along the path that matches the symbol that the wheel stops on.  Once you reach the Super Slide and spin the red or blue color, drop your character down the chute.  If you land in the middle window, you are the winner!

boardgame: Toboggans of Doom: Promo Tile Set
description:
The feel of the wind on your face, the sight of the finish line just over the next snow bank… and the elation of rocket-jumping over evil trees while being chased by a phalanx of chainsaw-wielding snowmen! It’s the wild challenge of extreme downhill sledding against a hill of crazy, zany, fun obstacles in Toboggans of Doom!

Includes 3 Promo Tiles:

    Presidential Co-Pilot Upgrade (Barack Obama)
    Distracting Assistant Obstacle (also from Bucephalus’ Suicide Bomber game)
    Psychic Rat Obstacle (also from Mad Scientists:  Lab Rats).

Feudalizm is a strategy game set in a medieval atmosphere. The player takes on the role of a local prince and expanding the power of subordinate lands. The game offers a wide range of possible actions: construction of castles, towns, cities and rural location, construction of business facilities, trade, sieges and assaults of castles or towns. The action is varied random events in the form of cards. The modular system allows the board to change its appearance at each game.

Each player starts the game with the disposal of city, a sum of money, a certain amount of raw materials and the deployment of troops and several farm buildings. The player's task is to manage the development of his principality by building new business facilities, colonize new areas, increasing the strength of the army, the establishment of villages and towns, castles extension locks. The main objective pursued by each of the players is dominance over its rivals, which can be obtained on how the economic or military conquering lands belonging to the opponents.

The game is divided into rounds, each round corresponds to one seasonal: spring, summer, autumn, winter. During each turn a player can perform up to four specific actions. The game ends when one player has to fulfill the conditions of economic or military dominance over their opponents.

The basic raw materials present in the game are : wood , stone , ore, iron , food , horses, consumer goods , and weapons and armor .

The game includes a deck of 30 cards random discovered alternately by the players. There are also so-called card . advisors drawn or selected before the start of the game . Features counselors have a significant impact on the course of the game.

The currency of the game are known. kordyty denominations of 1 , 2, 5 and 10 Outbuildings, workers, settlers , towns and villages are in the form of cardboard chips. Castles in the discretion of the buyer may be in the form of chips or resin models.

In choosing the path of military conquest available to the player are various units : light and heavy cavalry , infantry peasant, municipal police, swordsmen, axemen, halberdiers, archers, crossbow and machine battle . Battles can be resolved in a very fast manner (for the impatient players) , or on the board battle (for players with a strategic verve ).


Jest to gra osadzona w klimacie średniowiecza. Gracz wciela się w rolę lokalnego księcia i rozbudowuje potęgę podległych sobie ziem. Gra oferuje szerokie spektrum możliwych akcji: budowa zamków, grodów, lokacja miast i wsi, budowa obiektów gospodarczych, handel, oblężenia i szturmy zamków lub grodów. Akcję urozmaicają wydarzenia losowe w postaci kart. Modułowy układ planszy pozwala zmieniać jej wygląd przy każdej rozgrywce.

Każdy z graczy rozpoczyna grę mając do dyspozycji gród, pewną sumę pieniędzy, określoną ilość surowców i oddziałów wojska oraz kilka obiektów gospodarczych. Zadaniem gracza jest  kierowanie rozwojem swego księstwa poprzez budowanie nowych obiektów gospodarczych, kolonizowanie nowych terenów, zwiększanie siły armii, zakładanie wsi i miast, rozbudowa grodów w zamki. Głównym celem do którego dąży każdy z graczy jest dominacja nad rywalami, którą można uzyskać na sposób gospodarczy lub militarny podbijając tereny należące do przeciwników.

Gra podzielona jest na tury, każda tura odpowiada jednemu sezonowi: wiosna, lato, jesień, zima. Podczas każdej z tur gracz może wykonać do czterech określonych czynności. Gra kończy się w chwili gdy jeden z graczy spełni warunki dominacji gospodarczej lub militarnej nad swymi przeciwnikami.

Podstawowe surowce występujące w grze są : drewno , kamień, żelazo , rudy , jedzenie , konie, dobra konsumpcyjne , a broń i zbroje .

Gra zawiera talię 30 kart losowych odkrywanych na przemian przez graczy. Występują również karty tzw. doradców losowane lub wybierane przed rozpoczęciem rozgrywki. Cechy doradców mają istotny wpływ na przebieg gry.

Walutą w grze są tzw. kordyty o nominałach 1, 2, 5 i 10. Budynki gospodarcze, robotnicy, osadnicy, miasta oraz wsie występują w postaci kartonowych żetonów. Grody i zamki w zależności od uznania kupującego mogą być w formie żetonów lub żywicznych modeli.

Obierając drogę militarnego podboju do dyspozycji gracza są różnorakie jednostki: lekka i ciężka jazda, piechota chłopska, straż miejska, miecznicy, topornicy, halabardnicy, łucznicy, kusznicy, oraz machiny bojowe. Bitwy rozstrzygane mogą być w sposób bardzo szybki (dla niecierpliwych graczy), lub na planszy bitewnej (dla graczy z zacięciem strategicznym).

Simple card game for 2-4 people.

Players are trying to form their own province in a kingdom using 5 different characters.

Players must always look after single provinces to make sure that each of them makes the maximum income to the royal treasury. Once shall you overlook any bad thing about any province and it will pull your growing kingdom back. And also neighbors will never leave you in peace trying to advance their kingdoms and take a piece of other lands.

Two or four player soccer game using metal miniatures; players each have 1 field player and 1 goalie, using a ball that position determines the possession/active player. On a board with checkerboard patterns different areas (dark & light) determine who's next to kick (dark or light team).

The player puts the field kicker next to the ball and releases its leg to kick the ball. The other player should try to block the kick with his/her goal keeper. After the kick the possession is once more decided and the same procedure starts all over again.

Goalies can be managed by two other players, game lasts 2 x 10 minutes.

Remarks: Game was produced during 1930's; 'Wunderteam' was a specific name for the Austrian national soccer team during the years 1931 up to 1933.

Objective: get familiar with additon/subtraction of small numbers
 - collection of a few different math games for preschoolers -

1. Be the first to reach 10 exactly: put on appropriate math sheet, turn the spinner and add or subtract shown number.

2. Be the first to get rid of your 11 wooden slides with different numbers: put on appropriate math sheet, turn the spinner and solve the math formula by filling in the correct slide.

Collision is a super-hero deck building game, created by Magenic Technologies. In Collision, players play as software superheroes (The Automator, The Architect, The Oracle, Big Data, and The Mechanic) to build a deck utilizing additional heroes, super-powers, gear, and additional equipment to fight villains big and small, from small bugs all the way up to the evil Retrograde and Rain Cloud.

On each turn, the active player plays cards from their hands. Most cards in the game yield from 1 to 5 power, which is the main currency of the game. Other cards allow manipulation of the player's discard pile, drawing additional cards, ridding the deck of unwanted cards or additional bonuses. Having accumulated power during their turn, players finally use their power to purchase additional cards to increase their deck's strength. Each card purchased is worth victory points, and the person who has the most victory points at the end of the game is the winner!

Robin Hood and his gifted team of young vigilantes must help the downtrodden people of their land and deal with evil plots of the Sheriff – before finally meeting up with the villain himself in a final showdown to decide the fate of the Nottingham Sector.

This is a fully cooperative game which takes place over a series of rounds. Players must work together, pooling their time and resources, to contend with sinister plots affecting the Nottingham Sector.

By rolling specific types of dice (Combat, Stealth, or Diplomacy), players may choose how best to resolve the Plots. At the end of each round, the Threat Level may rise, making subsequent rounds much harder.

In the end, the players will have to push forward through the Sheriff's fortress-like Factory to confront the villain himself!

Only through careful management of the dice, tactical decisions about how to resolve the Plots, and a little luck of the roll will Robin Hood and his heroic team win the day!
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
A trivia game about the popular TV series, Duck Dynasty.  Answer questions and complete famous quotes to see who knows this show better among your family or friends! Fool other players with your believable answers and spot the genuine quotes from among the decoys to score.

Players race to answer the most questions correctly for the first player/team that makes it once around the board wins!

Contents:
1 Answer Pad
1 Die
1 Game Board
4 Movers and 4 Mover Bases
130 Quote Cards
70 Trivia Cards
10 Rules Cards
Game Instructions

Each player starts with four dice of each of six types (d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, and d20), and the first to lose all twenty-four dice loses the game.  Each battle is different according to the terrain dice thrown, which determine how many and which dice may battle, if any are modified/doubled, and how many actions each player may take per turn.  Each type of die has basic abilities (resurrection/banishment, mimicry, split attacks, join-forces, re-roll opponent die/dice, re-roll own die/dice), and all but the d4 have special abilities activated by rolling pairs (or more) of the same number.

The player with initiative (usually lower total roll) takes one, two, or three actions (based on the throw of the terrain dice) before the opponent has a chance to respond. Actions include one die attacking another or activating abilities, or activating abilities and then attacking an opponent's die (most common).

There is a balance between thinking through the possibilities and over-thinking what your opponent may do, particularly in the selection of which dice to roll at the beginning of each battle.  Each battle will consist of different combinations of dice, resulting in different abilities interacting, keeping the game fresh and interesting throughout.

The First World War is over and it is time to choose your side. Underground secret societies, or rather cabals, are battling for world domination. Containing spies, witches, faeries, magicians and other creatures from all over the world you must select your cabal wisely.

Cabals: The Board Game brings together key deck building mechanics with board control in a new battle that can either be won through board domination or conquering the opponents stronghold. Once the battleground has been constructed the luck of the draw and your deck building skills dictate what creatures you have at your disposal. Units battle one another backed up by spells and lead by a single hero for your deck from one of the four cabals.

The turn sequence comprises of gaining domination points, gaining resources, drawing cards, the main strategic phase (containing combat) and ending your turn. Resources dictate what cards you can use and this combined with loyalty points (depending on the cabals and their allegiances) the player has a deep, strategic game to conquer on many levels. The game is based on the digital Cabals: Magic & Battle Cards and these same key mechanics prevail but this version has more streamlined gameplay suitable to a tabletop game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: This is a game about the Alamo with a military theme. Designed to be enjoyed by 2, 3, or 4 players ages 10 or older with different skill levels for each age group. One player represents the Alamo defenders. The other player or players represent the attacking Mexican army. The outer pathway is a square depicting the walls of the fort. The inner pathway is a circle depicting the interior of the fort. Players move along the outer pathway by throwing dice and drawing event cards. Once a breach has been created the action moves to the interior for a final showdown. Includes tournament rules for a five game series to create play balance for the beleaguered Texans.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Objective: Be the first racing to position '100'

Typical 'Roll & Move' game from the 1910's in Germany. Roll the die by turns and step forward - until you end on a special field - follow instructions glued on lid inside.

Special rules: players are kicked out / must start again if touched or passed by others. Use 2 dice before passing space 84.

What is it all about?

Two fleets fight against each other during the game. Their aim is to destroy the enemy’s Command Ship. However this is a hard challenge because the game offers several options to obstruct or threaten the enemy’s vessels. Planting sharp mines in the route of the ships is one of them, or you may choose to bluff and plant fake mines that look exactly like real ones...

How does it play?

- Every turn the active player rolls a die. The die shows how many actions you have to take in your round: 1, 2 or 3. 
 - After you roll, you can plant mines on empty fields if you want.
 - Now choose a ship and spend your actions. Turn, move or shoot. Every ship type has its unique weapons, and specific role in the battle.
 - The first player who hits their opponent's Command Ship is the winner.

What this game offers:

- short, simple, easy-to-understand rules,
- quick set up, no more than 5 minutes after the second game,
- relatively short rounds (30-45 minutes), no time to get bored.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Armies boast about their strength and courage, but none are so fool-hardy as to boast that they could fight a dragon. You're tougher than an army, though, right?

More Options

Fires of Fury is a Foe deck with particularly powerful crown Foes. Although you don't have to kill them at first, they inflict damage on their way past and at the climax of the game they all appear at once.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Jedrzej lived to fight, and that meant death for those he fought. After years building a reputation as a combatant to be feared, there was little left that could stand against him. When at last he returned to his home, he found his tribe annihilated and its customs forgotten. In his zeal for the thrill of battle, he had lost sight of its cost. He resolved to keep his axes at his side unless he found a good enough reason to heft them.

More Options

Jedrzej the Hurler is a new Hero deck, fully compatible with the base game. He brings a little of everything to the table - multiple attacks, strong attacks, ranged attacks, Wound removal and Wound denial.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Someone once called Baron Miksa a 'slimy, double-crossing swineherd'. That man disappeared in mysterious circumstances, at around the same time as the foundations were laid for the Baron's new castle. The Redoubt is a stronghold built to be secure on a budget. Alas, the Baron seems to have underestimated the forces it needs to hold against.

More Options

Miksa's Redoubt is a location with plenty of fortification cards, requiring forward-planning to defend effectively. With little on offer to get rid of unwanted cards, you have to take every potential wound seriously.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

When a gang of bandits gets too big for its britches, it dreams of conquest, of over-running an established kingdom and living an easier life. A pipe-dream, naturally, but such gangs can wreak havoc in their attempt.

More Options

The Steppe Bandits is a Foe deck that interacts significantly with Heroism cards, and can sneak up on you with ambushes.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

What better location to defend than a muddy mess of a riverbank near a strategically-important bridge? After all, without the inconvenience of stout walls, gates and so forth, you can see all of the enemy arrows on their way to you. Unless, for some reason, you think that might be a bad thing...

More Options

Traitor's Crossing is a location with plenty of defender cards, meaning you can build a successful deck with relatively few hero cards.

This is a set of three cards to mix into the base cards of CV:

Boardgame designer: spend two happy faces and two light bulbs to receive a happy face, a light bulb, and a relationship icon each turn.

Boardgame collection: spend three dollar signs to receive one victory point at the end of the game and one relationship icon each turn.

Trip to game fair in Essen: spend three dollar signs to receive two happy face icons once.

Inertia is a game of unification in the sense of Claude Soucie's Lines of Action. The first player to unite all of his pieces on the board into a single group wins.

To start, white places one piece anywhere on the 8x8 square board. Players then alternate placing pieces on the board, with the first piece being adjacent to the opponent's most recently placed piece and the second piece being adjacent to no pieces, either orthogonally or diagonally.

Once a player can't place a second piece, the placement phase ends and the next player starts the movement phase. On a turn, a player moves one piece akin to a queen in chess, with movement ending after passing through any number of empty spaces or by landing on an opponent's piece and capturing it. If this move increases the total number of groups on the board — with a group being a single stone or a series of straight- or diagonally-connected like-colored stones — then the move is illegal. Gameplay continues until one player unites all of his pieces into a single group; if he simultaneously creates single groups for both players, then the game is a draw.

Instead of using a chess board, Inertia can be played on a hexhex board with pieces moving like hex rooks.

boardgame: The Game of Merry Christmas Goose Chase
description:
Game manufacturers McLoughlin Brothers produced a group of several games, during the late 19th century, called "The Merry Christmas Series." It is likely that the firm marketed these games especially at holiday time, to take advantage of the gift-buying public.

The Game of Merry Christmas Goose Chase is a simple racing game where players try to catch a Christmas goose. This game is targeted toward young children.

A trick-taking card game focusing on expressions from Bologna's dialect. Players try to avoid taking suits of unlucky events going around the table by either adding a more powerful event of the same sphere, or trumping the suit with an event of a different sphere (the three spheres being home, work or street). If not avoidable, the trick taking can be mitigated by playing a swear word card, or by a Socc'mel card, which transfer the suit to another player. Goal of the game is pushing the other players anger-meter ("incazzometro") beyond 10 to eliminate them from the game.

The game is marketed for ages 6 up, with the explicit proviso that obscenities from Bologna are widely accepted and not considered offensive (which is the case, to some extent...).

There are 5 different ingredients and the raven cards what count as a jokers in this game.
The object of the game is to collect the most cards in one type of ingredient.

In start each player gets 3 cards and the rest of the cards are placed to 5 pile. Put the frog aside to any pile.

In his turn player rolls the dice to move the frog as many steps as the dice indicates. The player takes top card from that pile.
Now he asks any ingredient from any player and also announces the ingredient he must give back. If that player has the ingredient he must give all cards with this ingredient with the current player. 
Game ends if one of the five piles is exhausted:
The player with the most cards with one type of ingredient is the winner.

The Legends Board Expansion includes two additional double-sided board pieces that can be added to Eight-Minute Empire: Legends so players have a total of six double-sided pieces from which to create the modular map.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Theme: Floating islands in the sky

description:
Players take turns dropping their 5 marbles down the channels of the game, trying to get as many marbles as possible down to score at the bottom (hole with different values).

Winner is the first player to exceed 500 points.

Remark:
Published late 1950/early 60's.

Subterfuge is a 2-player, luck-free, abstract strategy game played on a standard chess /checkers board using 12 standard dice (6 each in 2 separate colours).

The game has many similarities to chess but uses d6 dice as the pieces.  Each player starts with their 6 dice in their home territory and they advance into the field to play.  Players alternate moving one of their pieces with the winner being the first player to capture 4 of their opponent’s pieces.

The value of each die face determines the movement and capability of the piece – smaller values move lesser distances but can Capture larger dice – higher value dice move greater distances but Expose smaller dice - equal value dice Influence the value of neighbouring dice, including your own.   Pieces may move forward or backward according to the movement rules defined for each die facing.

Stuterfuge includes many layers of strategic play and brain-burning tactical play, as the dice values change throughout the game.   Game length can vary between 15 to 60 mins, depending on the time permitted for each move.  Multiple variants are also included in the rules, allowing wildly different tactics and play for each game.

Objective: be the first to reach the target (Dresden) moving by turns - "Roll and Move" game.

Move forward according die roll along the red line; if you end on a blue marked space follow the blue line until you join the red line again.

If you end on a green marked space, follow the green line back to the red line.

Equipment: board, 8 meeples (can be extended), 1 die

Probably published during the 1950's.

"Znamenitost" means Celebrity in Russian. It's a quiz game with a unique mechanic that provides infinite questions, and you don't need a game master to play it.

There are 4 different kinds of decks (24 cards per deck, 96 total).
1. Facts: each card represents a fact from a biography (for example, "She wrote a book", "Something is named after her", "She was imprisoned", etc.).
2. Professions, such as "politician", "actor", "soldier", etc.
3. Alphabet: each card is a letter, which should be the first letter of someone's name, surname, pseudonym, or nick name.
4. Countries, such as "USA", "Germany", or "Russia".

Each round one of the players chooses a deck, reveals the top card and reads its text aloud. Then everybody tries to name a famous person that matches the task of the card. The fastest player reveals the top card of one of the remaining decks, and now the players try to name the person who matches two criteria, and so on.

For example, if the cards "C" and "She wrote a book" were revealed, it's possible to name Julius Caesar, Winston Churchill, Clive Barker, or any other person with the first "C" in his/her name (or surname, or nickname) and known as an author. If nobody could name a person after the next card was revealed, than the player who had correctly named the previous person wins a round. You cannot reveal more than 4 cards (one per deck): if someone has managed to name a person with 4 cards, he wins that round.

The game is played till one of the decks is depleted.

Contents:
96 cards (24 per deck)
2 blank cards for your own tasks
rules

boardgame: Es läuft die Zeit... wir laufen mit!
description:
Two different boards:

1. Uhrenspiel
Objective: move the pawns around the board to the "home" section. Movement is controlled by dice. Learn to read a watch. 
The winner is the first player to move all pawns "home".
Ending on a 'white' space move foreward according to the printed watch, on a 'red' space backwards.
Can be played by 2 persons or 2 teams.

2. Snakes and Ladders

Pit of Pillars is an elimination game of placement of men, and moving and capturing stacks. Every capture results in a pillar being erected on the square where the capture took place. Pillars belong to the captor and move as chess queens. They can neither capture nor move over pieces.

Stacks can be split or joined. Whether a stack is vulnerable to capture is determined by the number of adjacent squares. Pillars take away such squares and thus heighten vulnerability of adjacent stacks.

A player wins by eliminating all of the opponent's stacks from the board (regardless of any reserves either player might have in hand) or by leaving his opponent without a legal move. Cooperative cycles are inherently possible, but in regular play the game will seldom end in a draw.

The game can be played using simple generic material: a chess board, draughtsmen, and chess pawns to represent pillars.

Jaywalkers is a two player game, played on the spaces of a 8x8 board.

2 pawns in two colors and 62 path tiles.

SETUP - Place board between players, pawns start on any space of each player’s respective first rows.

PLAY – A player on his turn must take two actions.

1.Place a tile onto an empty space.
2.Move your pawn in a series of one or more rook moves over any number of tiles following any one of three paths shown on a tile you're currently moving over and ending the move on an empty space.

RESTRICTIONS – The tile you placed on your turn must be part of the route your pawn takes. A pawn may not land on a tile. Your pawn may not jump over your opponent's pawn.

ENDGAME – You win if your opponent is unable to take his two actions.

boardgame: Monopoly: Pittsburgh Steelers Super Bowl XL Champions Collector's Edition
description: The “Steel Curtain” has done it again! America’s favorite board game proudly presents the Pittsburgh Steelers XL Champions Collector’s Edition. Buy, sell, and trade the team’s stellar offense including Ben Roethlisberger, Hines Ward and Santonio Holmes and own their tough defense showcasing Troy Polamalu, James Harrison and Aaron Smith, in an effort to own the NFL’s best. Celebrate the Steelers’ unprecedented Super Bowl championship in this American classic, MONOPOLY!

"Our bees are in danger!
In the meadow insecticides dominate. Pollution becomes unbearable.
The climate change disrupts the environment and now the Asian hornet scourge!"

In each round, you can raise or move a bee on the board. You explore the bushes to find the precious pollen. But be careful not to fall into the traps.

The game can be played with or without fighting.

You win the game by beeing the first to collect and place 3 pollen into the bottom of the beehive. The other way to win is to be the first to elimitate the most opponent bees.

Simple game in the 18th Blue Peter Annual. In true Blue Peter tradition, you have to make the game first.

It is a Tiddlywinks type game. Each player gets five 'frogs' and must flip them on to the board. You add scores on to the Flowers and inside the Water-Lilies. Should you be unlucky and land on a fish or into the pond, lose 10 points and 5 points respectively.

Play for as long as you like. The highest score at the end is the winner.
Repackaged Thurn and Taxis with expansions Thurn and Taxis: Power and Glory and Thurn and Taxis: All Roads Lead to Rome included in the same box.
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office
Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
The Magic Journey is a Goose-type game, with players moving via a die roll and obeying the instructions on the illustrated spaces. Whoever reaches HOME first wins.

The Magic Journey was published in a "Tufty" book for children. Tufty games are always about avoiding accidents, and this is no exemption.
publishers: The Royal Society for the Prevention of Accidents

description:
Game featured in the British 1979 Charlie's Angels Annual.

Simple roll and move game for two players. One is the good player representing a member of Charlie's Angels I assume, and the other is the evil player, representing an Enemy Agent.

Players move according to their die roll not forgetting to obey the instructions on special squares, and the first to launch the Rescue Missile (Space 38), wins.

The rules say that the game can be played alone, by 'playing fairly' for the enemy agent's die rolls. It is the first time in a Goose-type game that I have seen a square that only applies to the Charlie's Angel player, who, if she lands on it, she is knocked out of the game. There is no such square for the Enemy Agent so he has a small advantage.

Game featured in the 1978 Black Beauty Annual.

A simple die rolling game. Each player is a rider in a show-jumping competition. As usual with Goose-type games, there are special spaces (illustrated by a horseshoe). Each special space represents faults earned by the rider that round. These fault points have to be written down as they are earned. A slight twist with this game is that the order of finishing also generates time-faults; 1st to finish, none; second to finish, 5 time-faults, and so on.

After all the players have finished, the player with the fewest faults, wins.

A game that featured in the British 1975 Disneyland Annual.

A simple roll and move game. Players represent characters from the Disney animated film about Alice in Wonderland.

Roll a 5 to start. When you land on special square they will often tell you how many blackberries you have picked that turn. Record the number so that each player will have a total once all have finished. The player who picks the most, wins.

A game that featured in the British Disneyland Annual for 1977.

A simple Goose-type game. Mickey Mouse wants to go out in his car but his friends want to go with him. He has to wait until one friend arrives (and he looks very cross as he waits by his car). Players move by a die roll and must obey the special squares. The first player to reach square 30, wins.

description:
Stellar War is based on an old Avalon Hill card game known as Naval War, but rather than simulate a war at sea during WWII, Stellar War simulates a war in space, in the distant future.

The object is to have the most points at the end of play, by destroying enemy spaceships and space stations and preserving your own. Each player begins with a fleet of ships and stations used to attack opponents, and plays cards from their hand that represent offensive and defensive actions and other special events. Ships and stations can acquire special defenses or abilities. Optional rules are included to allow different configurations of play.

Hei Su Hong Su is a battle of wits between two opponents, played on an elegantly crafted board framed in genuine cherry or mahogany. Each game includes an authentic Chinese i-ching coin. In Hei Su Hong Su, when tokens are moved off one end of the board, they return on the opposite side. Additionally, unlike other popular strategy games, players are permitted to move neutral and their opponent’s tokens as their own. Hei Su Hong Su is truly a unique and intriguing strategy game.

In Hei Su Hong Su, one player is red, other is black. At the start of the game, each player has nine pieces set up in a square in opposite corners on the board, with 18 white pieces occupying the other two corners. Players take turns “sliding” rows or columns of pieces by removing a piece from an edge and adding it to the other edge. If a player traps a piece by surrounding it with 4 of his color (including “wrapping around” to the other side of the board), it is replaced with with one of that player's pieces. If a player captures and replaces a white piece, the “tug of war” marker moves 1 in his favor on the scale. If a player captures and replaces an opponent’s piece, the marker moves 2 in his favor on the scale. The game ends when the marker has moved all the way to the end of the scale; that player is the winner.

Each player chooses a pawn and take the 6 pieces of its color.
Put the pawn chosen at the beginning of the board.
Shuffle the 'unexpected' and 'guests' cards in the appropriate spaces on the board.
Shuffle the 24 cards songs and arrange them in the appropriate spaces on the game board.
Insert the jury card inside the bag. If you play in 5 or 6, put them all, or, in the case of 3 or 4 players, only enter the 8 cards numbered from 1 to 2 .
The players alternate in singing receiving the votes of the public (the other players) and the judgment of the jury.
The game is played in 3 rounds plus a final.

Round 1
The current player draws the card song on top of its pile.
Read the songs and decide whether or not to accept the card, otherwise goes to the player to his left until this is chosen by someone.
If all pass, the card is given to the player who had drawn it.
The player who is assigned the card chooses one of the songs written on the front and draws an "unexpected" card.
The player performs the song choice with track on the CD and respecting the rule reported on the "unexpected".
At the end of the performance each of the other players pass a covered card of values.
The player draws a jury card.
The turn passes to the next player. In the first run you must show all at once.
At the end of the performances the players reveal their cards and advance their pawn based on the score received.

Round 2
In the second round the players are performing in duets.
The player chooses a card from the relative number of bunches . Choose a song and a player with which to perform.
The players perform together.
At the end of the performance the other players place a card deck of their color on one area related to one of the two players who have just sung.
Each of the 2-player draws a card and places the jury in your area.
As in the first run will add up your points and advancing the pawn.

Round 3
The last in the standings takes the bunch of songs for the third exhibition and chooses a card based on the type they prefer, without reading the songs.
Then draw a card guest, choose a song and a famous singer and performs alternating a normal singing voice imitation of the chosen guest.
At the end of the performance the other players place a card deck of their color on the area of ​​the board on the turn player.

Final
The two players with the highest score in the final play.
The cards not used are shuffled. Players draw a card and perform the song. The other players vote.
Players then draw a card jury.
The player with the highest score is the winner.

boardgame: Last Night on Earth: Hold the Line!
description:
From Flying Frog's Website:

"The 'Hold the Line' Supplement is a Web-exclusive for 'Last Night on Earth' and features a new scenario and ten new game cards. All pieces are printed on the same high-quality card stock as the boardgame. The cards are exclusive to this supplement and will not be available anywhere else. They feature all new artwork."
family: Series: Last Night on Earth Game Engine (Flying Frog Production)

description:
A basic memory-style game with a NZ rugby theme. Collect pairs of cards to score points, play a tackle token to prevent a player from collecting a set.

Released to coincide with the [2013?] Rugby World Cup.

(n.b.: Aotearoa is the Maori name for New Zealand)

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Rylan Olliven Lieutenant Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Bring your lieutenants to life with Lieutenant Packs for Descent: Journeys in the Dark Second Edition! The Rylan Olliven Lieutenant Pack introduces a sculpted figure and personalized Plot deck for the corrupt politician from the Shadow of Nerekhall expansion. Use the Ryland Olliven figure to replace his lieutenant token, or use his Plot deck to undermine the heroes in any campaign with false informants and double agents. Both overlord and heroes gain new options for strategic play through the threat and fortune tokens included in this Lieutenant Pack.
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Verminous Lieutenant Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Bring your lieutenants to life with Lieutenant Packs for Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition)! The Rat-Thing King from Shadow of Nerekhall schemes in the darkness, waiting for the perfect moment to strike. The Verminous Lieutenant Pack includes a sculpted figure as well as a personalized Plot deck. The overlord can now bring Verminous into other campaigns, using his minions to attack from the shadows. Both overlord and heroes gain new options with the threat and fortune tokens in this Lieutenant Pack.
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

description:
Game featured in the 1976 Rupert Annual.

It is a simple roll and move game. Each player chooses a colour and will need 6 counters of that colour. Only one counter for each player will be in play at any time.

Players throw a die to move their way along a 50-space track. If a player lands on one of the six illustrated spaces, they must put their counter on the correct spot for that character. These spots are marked around the illustration in the centre of the board. That player then takes another counter of her colour and starts at square 1 again. Only one of her counters can be on any spot. The first player to have her 6 counters on the spots in the centre, wins.

boardgame: An Invitation to the Mad Hatter's Tea-Party
description:
Game featured in the 1978 British Disneyland Annual. Various Disney characters are illustrated on the board.

This is a Goose-type game. Players move by throw of a die and must follow instructions should they land on one. The first player to reach square 64 by exact throw, wins the game and can enjoy a cup of tea with the Mad Hatter himself.

NoGo is a combinatorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square or rectangular grid (board). Board sizes bigger than 9×9 are not recommended. 6×7 boards have been used in some human matches.

NoGo takes the Go concepts of group and liberty and uses them in the most minimalistic possible way. The resulting game, with no captures, no cycles and no scoring, is no rival for Go, but remains a fertile ground for tactical challenges in the Dots and Boxes style.

The game was first proposed by John Moore on Sensei's Library on April 1, 2005.

				
				
					DefinitionsA group is a set of orthogonally connected, like-colored stones.

A liberty of a group is an empty point orthogonally adjacent to that group.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point. After your placement, all groups on the board (both friendly and enemy) must have at least one liberty. Otherwise your move is illegal. Whoever makes the last legal move wins.

Game of Tones is a set of fourteen different music games designed to play with tonalities, chords, measures, key signatures, notes on the pentagram and even the location of the notes on a guitar neck. These multiple games can be played at different levels, depending on the musical knowledge of the players. The deck consists of 52 cards representing musical notes and rhythmic figures, with the help of the Circles of Fifths printed on each card, and with different suits or colors, together with some special wildcards.

It consists of many different games, featuring different game mechanics and goals: getting more points or cards than your opponents, be the first to run out of cards, move cards to certain places on the table, etc. In every game there are two different “levels” to keep track: the Musical Level and the Strategy Level, and both are independent from each other. The Strategy Level depends on the particular game, while the Musical Level can be adjusted in most of the games. Some of the games are up to 10 players, others are solitary type ones, for 2 players, and even some of the games can be played in teams or cooperatively.

Pawns are used in some of the games, and Game of Tones also includes two additional board games. These games are called Pianogram, in which the board represents a G-clef staff or a piano, depending on the orientation in which it is placed; and Fretboard, in which the board represents a guitar (or any other stringed instrument) neck.

Designed from an educational standpoint, Game of Tones includes a list of the musical concepts used in every game, and a teaching guide for music teachers. Game of Tones is not language dependent, except for the instructions, which are available in English and Spanish.

Otters is a quick, kid-friendly card game for 2-4 players ages 6 and up. Players take turns sending otters from their hand to the lake playgrounds on the board, playing 2 cards per turn. If you add enough otters to a playground to fill it up, you win that playground! Whoever has the most valuable collection of playgrounds at the end of the game is the winner.

Most of the game is made up of simple otters numbered 1, 2 or 3. Players must decide which 2 of their cards in hand to play, and which playgrounds to play them to.

There are also special otters in the deck, which players can use to try to win playgrounds quickly, plus alligator cards to keep opponents away from playgrounds.

The playgrounds themselves range in value from 2 to 10, making some easy to win with a card or two, but less valuable to your final score. Others are hard to win but worth lots of points! And if you collect all three playgrounds of the same color, you get bonus points at the end of the game.

Otters is simple enough for kids to play, helping them to learn basic addition. But it's also designed to be interesting for adults, requiring strategic decision making.

Ultimately, Otters is a game about playing with adorable otters with your friends and family.

A Decktet Game for 2-players in which you try to buy as many "stamps" as possible for your album in competition with the other player. 
Each player has their own shop and a market place runs down the centre of the table. Players may purchase from any row using the cards in their hand.
For the time being rules etc. can be viewed at http://wiki.decktet.com/game:stamp-albums

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Tristayne Olliven Lieutenant Pack
description:

Bring your lieutenants to life with Lieutenant Packs for Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition)! Necromantic magic explodes out of the Shadow of Nerekhall expansion with the Tristayne Olliven Lieutenant Pack. This expansion includes a detailed figure and a personalized Plot deck that highlights Tristayne's chaotic energy. With his Plot deck, the overlord can also introduce Tristayne into other campaigns, using his dark powers to ruin the heroes. boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Gargan Mirklace Lieutenant Pack

Bring your lieutenants to life with Lieutenant Packs for Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition)! With the Mirklace Lieutenant Pack, the overlord can unleash the full power of the demon mage from the Shadow of Nerekhall expansion. This Lieutenant Pack includes a detailed figure and a personalized Plot deck that highlights Mirklace's scorching presence. The Plot deck also allows the overlord to summon Mirklace into other campaigns and use his evil magic to annihilate the heroes. Both overlord and heroes gain new options with the threat and fortune tokens in this Lieutenant Pack.
